description:
Game description from the publisher:

Top Flopp is a fun trick-taking card game for the whole family. For 2 - 8 players. Aged 10+.

There are three ways to start the game. Based on your cards, you decide and call whether you play: TOP, FLOPP or Chance. If you call TOP you must win each round and all other players will try to trump whereas in a FLOPP round you must lose each round and all other players will try not to trump. If you win a TOP or FLOPP you gain points but if you lose all others get points. When calling Chance you just play a card, which can be trumped or not and everyone has the chance to gain points. The player who trumped then gets to call TOP, FLOPP or Chance… The player who reached the winning amount of points (depending on the number of players) wins the game.

Playing time varies depending on the number of players or the amount of winning points you decide on. reviewers and testplayers played from 15 minutes up to 5 hours (with many rounds and special self-invented challenges for winners or losers ;-).

Reviewers and testplayers say Top Flopp is similar to Swiss/German/Austrian traditional card games like Jassen, Schnellen, Schnalzen... and is most fun with 3 or 4 players.

The German title of the game Spaß muß sein! means "Fun must be!”. This is a simple card game, printed 1958. The game includes 100 cards of two types. The blue one with a ques-tion, and the red one with a answer. You have to through a card with a question. A other player has to choose answer. It’s up to you to decide if the answer is fitting or just fun. 
Example: Question: “Would you like to go with me trough a forest?” Answer: “No, its to dan-gerous.”

Game description from the publisher:

The Cows are back, and this time they brought some back up! Swine Fu add all new creatures, and new Kung Fu event cards you can use to "Up the Steaks".

With 3 new survivors, 3 more locations, and all new items, the steaks just got higher. Take down the Sow-lin Monk, and watch out for the Ham-urai.

Master the art of Kung Fu to draw more cards and add a little Bacon to your hand.

Rhyme: The Rap Challenge is a unique, entertaining party game for the whole family that gives you the chance to discover the rapper in you! Things are simple – choose words from different categories, write your own verses on any subject (as long as they rhyme), rap them on the beat, and collect points. At the end of four rounds you will have created your masterpiece: your 16-bar rap song! The player who accumulates the most points wins.

In Rhyme: The Rap Challenge players are aspiring rappers competing to find out who can create the best rap song.
In order to do that, players have 4 rounds in which they must write 4 verses – 1 in each round. As this is a rap song the 4 bars in each verse must rhyme. At the end of each round players will have to rap their verse on one of the beats, which will be available to choose from the game’s website. Then players will vote, by simultaneously pointing at the player with the best verse in their opinion.

When the 4 rounds are over the player with the most points is the winner.

--description from the publisher

Hockey Cup is a game about building a hockey team. It is inspired by Baseball Highlights: 2045 by Mike Fitzgerald. It uses Games Cards system.

Hockey Cup is like watching TV highlights of hockey games. In this quick and interactive game, two coaches build their teams as they play, combining both strategy (building the team) and tactics (playing the game) without any of the downtime. During each "mini-game", each coach alternates playing six cards to simulate a full game's highlights – 3 defensive and 3 offensive actions. Whichever team wins the most mini-games in the series takes the cup!

Coaches start the game with a team, which consists of 15 1st level players. On his turn a coach sends one offensive player to make 1 trick, and the other coach sends one defensive player to prevent the trick. Next, both players may use one of their special actions. An unprevented trick leads to goal. At the end of third period, if the score is tied, they play overtime, which is followed by a penalty shootout. After every game, a coach uses 3 experience points to raise the level of his players. A 2nd level player can make 2 tricks, 3rd level - 3 tricks.

Tallinn: The Elders is a mini-expansion for Tallinn that consists of three large figures and stickers.

During the game, the first player to have at least five symbols of a faction on their played influence cards (and not their start card) claims the matching large figure, which doubles the associated faction total on their tower cards at the end of the game.

boardgame: Mage Wars: Ring of Healing Promo Card
description:
This promo card was given at Gen Con 2013.

Ring of Healing

Whenever a spell cast by this Mage, or an enchantment controlled by this Mage, provides healing for an object, other than the Regenerate trait, he may increase that amount of healing by one.  He may use this ability once per round.  If he heals multiple object (such as with a Group Heal), he only gets the +1 bonus for one object of his choice.

Tallinn: The Assembly consists of twelve small figures, four each in red, yellow and blue. If you trigger a contest during the round, after resolving those contests you can take a small figure from the supply that doesn't match the faction in which you triggered a contest.

These small figures have no effect during the game, but at the end of the game, each small figure counts as an additional symbol for the matching faction during the final faction row contests.

Home version of the popular TV game show hosted by Craig Ferguson.  Players have to give clues to get their teammates to say the names of celebrities, characters, famous places, etc. before time runs out.  Whichever teams has the most points after four rounds wins!

In The Nightmare Invader you play as an ordinary hotel guest with an extraordinary ability - you can enter other people's dreams.  You will need that power to fight The Nightmare Invader - an evil entity who is trying to take control of all the guests by giving them terrifying nightmares.

To win the game, first you must wake up as many guests as you can before the invader drives them insane with terror.  Then you will take the fight to the invader, trying to drive it out of the mind of its human host, or destroying it completely.  But it will not be easy.  The invader is completely alien, and uses deckbuilding to achieve its goals.  Meanwhile you must use six sided dice to achieve yours.

The Invader gets the first turn.  On the Invader turn you draw 3 cards from the Invader deck, each showing a different fear, such as Ghosts or Spiders.  Any guests afraid of these fears become panicked.  If the Invader can panic a guest 5 times before you wake them, they succumb to the will of the Invader and their card is added to the Invader deck, making it stronger.

On your turn, you roll 6 dice.  Each die can be used once.  If a die roll is high enough, you can use it to calm down a guest and if you calm the guest completely, they wake up and are removed from play.  Dice can also be used to learn extra skills and abilities, such as removing the Invader's health, or adding distractions to the Invader deck.

It's a battle of dice versus cards.  Do you have what it takes to save humanity and defeat The Nightmare Invader?

Game description from the publisher (translated):
Similar to Trigger!/Déclic!?, Déclic Évolution is a (French) party game testing your reaction and coordination abilities, where all the players have to answer a question as fast as possible using their hands. But the answer is no longer "true" or "false" : each card has its own set of themes, one "blue" and one "yellow". Moreover, 2 additional sets add a third possible answer (to be answered with both hands) : set 2 adds "neither blue nor yellow" and set 3 adds "both blue and yellow".

Game description from the publisher:
Comme dans Déclic ou Déclic Family, les joueurs doivent répondre le plus vite possible avec leur main droite ou leur main gauche en tapant sur la cible au centre de la table !
Mais finies les parties où l'on ne répondait que par VRAI ou FAUX. Désormais, chaque carte dispose de deux thèmes uniques, l'un jaune, l'autre bleu. À chaque nouvelle carte, deux nouveaux thèmes !!!
Chaque partie se déroule en trois manches distinctes :
Manche 1 : avec les cartes vertes, répondez par jaune ou bleu.Manche 2 : avec les cartes orange, répondez par jaune, ou bleu, ou ni l'un ni l'autre avec les deux mains !Manche 3 : avec les cartes rouges, répondez par jaune, ou bleu, ou les deux avec les deux mains !
Matériel : 132 cartes Evolution (1584 défis), 12 cartes Numéros, 16 Bracelets, 1 cible rouge, les règles du jeu.

The Bead Game Box is an innovative board game for the modern age. Combined with an online App, it gives you access to more than 100 traditional board games.

The Bead Game Box is all about creativity. You can easily share your variations of well known games using the App or you can even create and share your own games. Users also have access to Print&Play boards for games requiring specific boards.

It doesn't matter if it's Tic-Tac-Toe or The Royal Game of Ur. The Bead Game Box is suited for all kinds of games.

boardgame: Liechtenstein (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride)
description:
This follows the principles of the basic Ticket to Ride game on a special board with special cards and rules. The object of the game is to gain the most points by travelling various routes and outwitting other players. You will need the train pieces and the train cards from either the US or Europe base games (or you can buy them from the Days of Wonder shop.

There is an extra set of Tourist cards each with a value to be earned by visiting the cities and mountain centres. These cards are dealt/picked up alongwith tickets in the same quantity and with the same 'keep/dispose' rules. There are also some Spanner cards which can be good or bad.

The tracks between the cities are all coloured but the tracks to the mountain centres are grey - but you can only travel one way - they are dead ends.  Flights are an add-on from Vaduz.
There are many new options in the rules: exchange all your cards at the start but keep all the new ones: discard 5 cards and pick up 5 new ones, as your turn: earn extra  points: and many more. You can also call time when your wagon stock reaches 3, if you want, giving you the advantage of stopping other players completing tickets.

The game of guessing, guessing and pitching movies

Most box office successes can be described in a single sentence. A perfectly formulated pitch that makes actors and producers alike drool over the opportunity to work with you. It's that magic sentence this game is about!

In High Concept you score points by guessing which movies are associated with what pitch - and you also get to create your own movie pitches with the help of prewritten pitch cards. Winning requires both trivia knowledge and winning pitch rounds.

THE GAME

At the start of the game a moderator hands out a role card to each player, assigning them to either a small team of informed werewolves, who know each others identities, or uninformed villagers, who do not know any player identities. The game then alternates in phases of “night”, in which werewolves agree to covertly kill a villager, and “day", in which villagers debate the identities of the werewolves and vote to kill a suspect. Play continues until all of the werewolves have been killed, or until the werewolves outnumber the villagers.

THE CARDS

There are NO DUPLICATES in the Werewolf House deck of 37 different character cards. Each card features a unique, full color character illustration, so even after dozens of game rounds players will still be discovering new art.

CORE CARDS - The base version of the game can be played by 7-12 people, using 6-10 basic villager cards and 1-2 werewolf cards - all included in the Werewolf House deck.

ADVANCED CARDS - It’s fun to mix it up. Dozens of characters with unique abilities have been carefully curated to add complexity to the gameplay, without burdening the moderator with messy role management tasks.

Ritjakten - A game of drawing and guessing for the whole family.

Adults draw words for everyone to guess, while children instead choose from illustrated cards to copy.

The player that first guesses what's being drawn wins tickets (required to move unhindered on the game board) while the drawer gets to move forward on the circus themed game board. First to the finish line wins the game.

boardgame: Scooby-Doo Fright at the Fun Park
description:
Game description from the publisher:
Jinkies! Can you help the Mystery Inc. gang save the fun park from the spooky ghoul? Whoever figures out where the ghoul is hiding, wins!

Kids will enjoy using the Ticket Cards to make their way around the game board trying to find the ghoul. As they move their favorite Scooby-Doo character mover around the board, they will use the Mystery Decoder to find the answers they need to win the game and save the fun park! They can keep track of all of their clues with the Mini Maps and Wipe-Off Markers.

'Among the Stars: Space Farms' is a promotional Special Location card set available at the Artipia Games booth at Spiel 2016.

The card set includes two copies of the Space Farms card.

This item card also serves as a cross-promotional items for the company's upcoming title, Fields of Green.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Fields of Green: Crop Circle is a promotional Location card set available at the Artipia Games booth at Spiel 2016.

The card set includes three copies of the Crop Circle card.

This item card also serves as a cross-promotional item for Artipia's title, Among the Stars.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
The Cleaner is a Kickstarter exclusive add-on figure for the Mercs Recon: Counter Threat or Mercs Recon: Assassination Protocol games.

It consists of:
1x The Cleaner miniature
1x The Cleaner stat card

This can be used to replace an Op-For event in game.

CPP-209 is a Kickstarter exclusive add-on for the Mercs:Recon Counter Threat and Mercs: Recon Assination Protocol games.

It consists of:
1x CPP-209 miniature
1x CPP-209 stat card

This can be used to replace an Op-For event in the game.

boardgame: Mercs: Recon – Sgt. Pal & Roy McClane
description:
This is a Kickstarter exclusive add-on for the Mercs:Recon Counter Threat and Mercs:Recon Assassination Protocol games.

This contains:
1x Sgt. Pal miniature
1x Sgt. Pal Stat card
1x Roy McClane miniature
3x Roy McClane Stat cards (variable weapons)

These can be used to replace an Op-For event in the game.

boardgame: Mercs: Recon – Assistant to the Regional Manager
description:
This is a Kickstarter exclusive add-on for the Mercs:Recon Counter Threat and Mercs:Recon Assassination Protocol games.

Contains:
1x Assistant to the Regional Manager miniature
1x Assistant to the Regional Manager stat card

This can be used to replace an Op-For event in the game.

Hewns is a modular boardgame, in which players are competing to mine a planet surface which is created differently by the players every new game. The purpose of the game is to mine more Substract (which is a highly valuable liquid with living sub-molecular creatures) than any other player.

Each round, players use bidding drones to bid on Aliens (called Hewns); technologies; scouting advantages; and the Turn order. The alien Hewns all have different skill levels and abilities. However, Bidding costs valuable Substract!

When a player has his/ her turn they can enter their Hewns through common entrances onto the board which is made up of Nodes and ravines. The spaces of ravines and nodes can be either big, medium, or small and these spaces sizes will restrict certain Hewns from entering. But beware! Hewns usually don't survive! They can become damaged, stunned, killed, or even stolen by other players, or even killed by the planet itself!

When Hewns reach the mines on the board they can choose to mine aggressively or normally. The more aggressive the mining the worse the planet will react to the Hewns and also the faster the game will play out.

Players can also attack other Hewns owned by other players but the Substract they have collected will be used up as ammunition for the long-range and/or powerful shooting. Even crazier Hewns and technologies let you take over other players Hewns: but watch out, they might die in the attempt!

Warp Speed is a sci-fi, anime themed, space exploration game, for 2-4 players.

Use your navigation skills or warp yourself through space in order to answer distress signals, take part in battles or trade and recruit new crew.

The movement of the ship is done by using boost green crystals, but as the game evolves you will be able to upgrade your ship and use warp speed to travel a lot faster!

Each player takes the role of a starship captain that is sent into an unknown galaxy, to sweep its sectors from asteroids, discover new planets and by completing objectives, earn fame points and win.

Fame can be earned by completing 2 objectives of the same color. After you get those objectives, you must travel to a corresponding planet and get your reward from the remnants of that planet.

Cards are used to reveal events such as encounters with other pilots, finding new planets etc.

While on your mission you will definitely have to trade as you reach a new planet, otherwise you will be in a tough spot!

This is a WWII-era target game. The targets are cardboard soldiers that are inserted in wooden stands. The shooting mechanism is a wooden cannon powered by an elastic that shoots small wooden darts. While the box shows a twin-engine American starred airplane, the soldiers themselves are still in Napoleonic-era uniforms.

Scoring is simple: whoever knocks down the most soldiers wins.

The all cardboard and wood construction is consistent with wartime publication, and the cannon and stands are similar to the cannon version of Bomb the Navy.

De wakkere Oceaan-Vliegers ("The Brave Ocean Flyers") is a Dutch-language aviation-themed target game by a German publisher (Klee). It is undated, but c. 1930 judging by title's declaration of ocean-crossing as dangerous and by the artwork featuring a combination of biplanes, single-engine monoplane seaplanes and a trimotor.

The game board is a map covering Northern, Central and Western Europe and the East Coast of North America. The board is dotted with 58 small circles with the numbers 5, 10, 15 or 20. Six of these circles are bordered in red, meaning negative points for crashing; the remaining 52 are black and give positive points.

The playing pieces are cardboard planes attached to small wooden balls. The shooting mechanism is a 5"-long cardboard springboard painted with a plane taking off. It has a wooden fulcrum near one end. Players put a plane on the long end and push down on the short end to launch it. It gains or loses points based on where it lands on the board. The player gaining the most points wins.

boardgame: King of Tokyo/New York: Monster Pack – Cthulhu
description:
Monster Pack – Cthulhu includes a new monster — Cthulhu, in case you couldn't guess — as well as eight evolution cards to use with King of Tokyo. You can also play with the cultist tiles, giving players a bonus effect once they obtain it!

You also have eight evolution cards, fifteen cultist tokens (which are also Temple of Cthulhu), for King of New York. You now have new units and buildings to fight for! Defeating a Cultist lets you obtain the tile and use it once to gain either 1 life, 1 energy or 1 extra roll!
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Gigantism is a promo card that can be used in Evolution or Climate by replacing a trait of your choice (all 7 copies) with Gigantisim (all 7 copies).  The art is by John Ariosa and is it available only at the BGG store for a limited time.

Gigantism Text:
At the end of the Feeding Phase, gain 1 Body Size for every 3 food eaten.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Monster Melee: The Monster Mashing Card Game
description:
Desciption from the publisher:

Monster Melee is designed to be a simple game, combining the dueling aspect of a classic card game like War with the betting and bluffing elements of Poker. It's quick to learn and can be played anywhere, anytime you have 30 minutes to spare. All you need is some strategy, a flat surface and 2-4 players.​​

The object of the game is simple: have a higher Power Level than your opponent to defeat their Monster and win gold. Earn enough gold, and you win the game.

But watch out! Fix Cards can secretly change up the fight without your opponent knowing until it's too late.

Monster Melee includes 40 unique Monster Cards and 20 unique Fix Cards.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
User summary:
No to be confused with the two European games with the same name, this is a dice game with a boxing theme. Points are scored for rolling the dice. Additional points are scored for certain combinations of dice. Rolling three 2s, 3s, 4, or 5s results in a knockdown. Three knockdowns and the scoring player wins by technical knockout, otherwise, the player who wins more rounds after a predetermined number of rounds is the victor.
15 new cards for Baseball Highlights: 2045: Starter Team 21 Washington (V9) - Veteran Mix
15 new cards for Baseball Highlights: 2045: Starter Team 22 Cleveland (V10) - Veteran Mix
15 new cards for Baseball Highlights: 2045: Starter Team 11 Minnesota (V11) - Veteran Mix
15 new cards for Baseball Highlights: 2045: Starter Team 24 Seattle (V12) - Veteran Mix

Originally printed as a stretch goal from the Texas Arrows Kickstarter campaign, the Battle Manual was significantly redone and improved for the 2023 Kickstarter.  The new version contains expanded Designer Notes, FAQs, descriptions of units and illustrated examples of both hedgerows and melee. 52 pages.

The extra scenarios E1-E10 are available on the Worthington website

The Table of Contents has the following sections:
1.0 Realism and History
2.0 Module Designer Notes
3.0 Other Designer Notes
4.0 Individual Units
5.0 FAQ
6.0 Hedgerows examples
7.0 Melee example
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: Experience the exciting world of The Dwarves with six new heroes at your side in The Dwarves: New Heroes Expansion! Fight with the True Tungdil or wield the mighty mace of Queen Xamtys II! Champion the power of magic with Andokai and her spells or the power of words with the witty and fabulous Rodario! Perhaps you would rather wield the sharp blade of half-Älfar Narmora? No? Then get out your tool-belt and support the team with Magister Technicus Furgas.

Government Worker is a satirical card game about avoiding bureaucratic work.
 The objective of the game is to get through your work day while performing as little bureaucratic work as possible.
 The game progresses by drawing cards that: assign you work, let you avoid work by dicking off, and let you pass work off to your coworkers.
 The hand ends when a player draws a time card. The player with the least number of work hours recorded on their weekly time sheet is declared the winner!

NSFGW is the "Not Safe for Government Work" adult-themed expansion pack.

Game description from the publisher:

Satirik - Heroic Quest is a fantasy adventure board game for 2-5 players aged 10 and over, playable for 25-50 minutes.
During each adventure where you play one of 5 brave heroes, you will find magical items that you use only once and equipment that give you permanent bonuses.You will meet companions who can help or hinder you in your quest, you shall fight with the guards unconquered region, but also with other heroes, and unforeseen events will dramatically change the flow of your adventures.

The game plays with moves(throwing dice), one player at a time. Move, attack other heroes and regions,use of objects and drawing prospective card only while in the course of your move. It is also not allowed and move and perform some action in the same turn. If during the movement meet with other heroes, you can attack it, do nothing or make a trade. Trump can exchange items, equipment, regions, etc., but not tasks. Battles are resolved classic throwing two dice (with the would-be certain bonuses and minuses), but you automatically lose if you get two aces,while  with two sixes you automatically win the battle. There are also various other accessories that enrich the game: Treasury, shortcuts, dungeons, sanctuaries, teleport, an abyss ...

Despite this wealth of options, the game is very simple and the rules are very easy to learn and leave no room for doubts. Game will even complete inexperienced players quite soon. In addition, Satirik is a very addictive game and it will certainly  hold your attention for a long time. We recommend a game with as many players so you can add more excitement to the party (and last much longer).

Nachhaltige Entwicklung combines education and fun. The game is about sustainable development (that's what the title translates to) in germany. Players act as newsmakers trying to compete for the most important topics to put in their papers.

Step 1:
At the beginning of each round there are two of the two-faced news cards on the table, showing a little headline or news story on the upside, and one of seven colors (political topics) and some symbols unseen on the backside (like social security, health, mobility, education, environment, etc.). Players can acquire those cards by blind bidding of reporter cards. They must try to match their reporter cards to the topic of the news item by assessing which political topic this story is about.

Step 2: reporter and news cards are flipped. Players can only get those cards that they have matched the symbols of. The player with the most stars gets the card. Then, every player gets their reporter cards back, regardless of success in the auction.

Step 3: Players can swap news and reporter cards with each other if they like.

Step 4: Writing an article - scoring points. This is the only phase with a set player order. The first player chooses two or more news cards of the same color (i.e. topic), then draws an event card from the event deck and reads out the instructions that fit the color of the cards chosen. He then scores points based on the event card, symbols on the news cards, and some bonus points about number of news cards or combinations of symbols. He also gets an additional reporter card.

The game ends after a set number of rounds or time, so it can be easily fit into a school lesson.

This game is a reimplementation of a Swiss game called Klartext.
publishers: hep: der Bildungsverlag (hep – the Education Publishing)

description:
The most delicious food combination on earth
《Bubble Tea》&《Chicken Culet》!!!
Chicken Culet always tastes best with an ice cold beverage, preferably Bubble Tea with ice. It's an observation that never changes. They go together like burgers with coke, so extraordinary and enjoyable.
This time players not only have to make a bubble tea quickly but have to get a chicken cutlet as soon as possible!
Idea of the Game
According to the Order Card, players have to put the specified ingredients on the Tea Base Card and win the game. 
In addition to completely matching the quantity shown on the Order Card, players are available to cover the ingredients of the Tea Base Card. 
Whoever finishes the order first gets a Culet Card. The player who gets the most Cutlet Cards wins the game.

Description from the publisher:

With this board game you will experience the criminal world of Penoza. You take the role of person who suddenly receives some packages of coke. Telling the police is no option and you can definitely use some money so selling them would be the best idea. You decide to sell the drugs to clear your debts. In the beginning you try to solve problems on your own, but before even noticing you’re in the middle of the some serious drugs trafficking. Paying Hirelings, money laundering and bluffing are all part of this intense strategy game. Are you capable of getting yourself out of this mess?

Penoza the board game is a strategy game playable with up to four friends. Use your bluffing skills to sell your coke at the black market and use that money to expand your team of Runners, Launderers and Hirelings. While each player tries to sell his way out of the criminal world, police is trying to track you down and competitors are trying to outsmart your deals. In this game you will see a lot of famous characters from the television series Penoza, but you will be able to experience the crime world in your own unique way. Would you go all in to win the highest price at the black market, or do you keep low profile until it’s the right moment to strike. Penoza is a strategic family game which takes a combination of skills and luck to master.

This undated game combines die rolling with memory. Each player takes a bingo-like card with four numbered columns and seven rows of circles and covers each circle with a plastic disk (plack). On each card, 12 circles are blank, and the remainder have instructions such as "Pick-A-Plack"; "Others cover one blank", "Have another turn" or "Cover two blanks".

Players take turns rolling a die. On a 1-4, they may remove one plack from that column; on a 5, they must replace a plack on an open location of their choice; and on a 6, they may remove a plack from any one location. When removing a plack, they obey whatever instructions are underneath. The player who first removes all the placks on his card wins.

Despite the random element of die rolling, the key to victory is using required replacement of placks to re-cover useful spots, and remembering which of these will be most useful in each future situation.

The Death Breath is a terrifying virus which began spreading forth from the eastern enclaves of the Moloch. It infects every living thing, changing them into brainless, bloodthirsty beasts. Victims of these attacks become infected with the virus, and so it spreads. So far, the most effective way of dealing with the infected are strong ammunition, grenades, and a sharp axe.

—description from the publisher

Neuroshima Hex! Death Breath is a new army for Neuroshima Hex! that gives players a new experienceas the units are upgradeable with a special token pool that gives units customizable options. Death Breath also offers a large number of netting units as well as battle tiles.

This is one of the seven "Grandma's" games issued by Milton Bradley in the early 1900s. This one, as the title suggests, is a geographical quiz. It consists of 119 plain cards each with a single numbered question, and a booklet with directions and all the answers.

Players are given at least six cards each, and take turns flipping over a single one of their cards. If that player cannot answer the question, he passes it to his left, and each player in turn has the opportunity to answer. Whichever player answers correctly keeps the card, and the player with the most cards wins.

While the box is undated, Alex G. Malloy dates it to 1910 (American Games, 1999, p. 35, #1081), and the questions confirm the period: Cuba is shown as an independent nation, while the largest Empire in the world is still Russia, with its capital at Petrograd. Interestingly for an American game, 11 of the questions (almost 1/10) deal with what is now Canada.

This undated Chad Valley title is a dexterity game using a wood and metal spring launcher, a hollow celluloid ball, and five target birds, also hollow celluloid, that balance on a wire.

The box is used as the base for the target. A cardboard cutout of a row of trees, ranging from 6" to 8" high, is inserted into two wooden holders fixed at the front of the box. Two 10" wooden poles are inserted in two other wooden blocks fixed to the box near the back, and have a string running between them. The birds are balanced on the string, and players must shoot over the trees to hit the birds. Any number of rounds may be played, with the player having the biggest bag winning.

The instructions inside the box lid show that three other versions of the game were produced: the smallest with just a cardboard backdrop, a larger square version with four poles and strings, and a huge octagonal version with eight poles in two squares at different heights, shown with 21 birds perches and more fallen.

Autodrop is fun game named after famous Dutch candy. It's a simple but fun game where all players race their way to the finish with real candy. Up to four players choose 5 candies they will use as their pawns and with a dice they move forward on the game board. On the way players eat, switch, jump and fight to master the first place so they can eventually finish first! Can you win this race frenzy?!

This 1966 game is based on the Apollo project aimed at putting the first man on the moon. It is a game for 2-6 players, who roll dice to move around the board while building up a full four-stage Saturn rocket and then successfully launching it.

Components include the board, 36 Astronaut (or "Condition") playing cards with period photographs, 20 yellow Chance cards, 8 green Espionage cards, 12 pink Sabotage cards, 4 plastic rockets, each in four stages, 6 wooden rocket playing pieces, money in "mios" or $millions, and 36 markers.

To do so, players must land on squares allowing them to collect research grants, buy rocket parts or "Condition" cards that fill in each player's Control Board, and collect Chance, Espionage or Sabotage cards to help their own projects or hurt their opponents. There is even a funny "Astronaut Training" space where players must get up and do 10 knee bends or pay 100 mio for being out of condition. Players may trade cards or parts by moving into another player's home area.

Once all four stages are ready and the Control Board is full, a player may attempt a final Count Down. He must roll 2D6 three times, and if he gets a cumulative total of 25 or more, he wins the game. He may roll again on succeeding turns unless of course his project is sabotaged.

Grab some peanuts and Cracker Jack and pull up a seat to The Old Ball Game!

TOBG is a head-2-head, traditional base ball game, featuring real-time pitching (no charts or die rolls to determine results). Players can enjoy a full 9 innings or set a score and play only the 9th.

The emphasis in on the heart of the game - pitching, with timely hitting sprinkled in and solid fielding (except for the occasional error!)

TOBG moves along smoothly and quickly, with the time of play dictated by the time to pitch. Meaning you can take your time or play fast - it's up to you. There are special rules for stealing and bunting; and old-fashioned score boards keep track of the count and score, for that Authentic feel.

Pitching: The pitcher has 7 pitches to choose from. The pitching happens in 2 stages with the batter saying 'Take' or 'Swing' at each pitch. The result is a ball or strike. As the count goes deeper, the batter gets the advantage and knowledge will prevail over luck.

Hitting: When the batter swings at a Strike he gets a potential hit - he flips over a batting card to reveal a single, double, triple (or home-run!), hit to various areas of the ball park. (There are also infield flies.)

Fielding: The fielding team will select which fielding card to turn over to make the play. Results are Out, Hit or Error.

Introducing an exclusive collection of pieces created to commemorate the 112 years at the Boleyn Ground.
In summer 2016, West Ham United will say goodbye tothe venerable Boleyn Ground, home of the club for over a century.
Situated in Newham Borough's heart in East London, The Boleyn Ground was opened in 1904, when the Hammers were still a Southern League club.
In 1919, the stadium hosted its first Football League match and became a successful Premier League team when the competition arrived in 1993.
Special Edition "Farewell Boleyn" Monopoly.
Gives you the chance to invest in stands and stadiums and trade in landmarks and players!

Buurman & Buurman Cardhouse Game is a game where players try to build a house out of cards. Each team (existing of any number of players) starts with a floor and places two "wall cards" on it. After that, both teams try to place more floors and more walls to obtain points and create the biggest card-house. When one of the teams houses falls, the other team wins, or when both players finish their house by placing the roof, the teams count the points they obtained.

A base game expansion adding the British Army to the Allies.

The expansion includes a total of 120 cards:
-    62 units of 20 different types 
-    50 Support cards
-    2 summary cards
-    4 Terrain cards
-    2 General cards

Game description from the designer:

This is a new semi-cooperative card game of political negotiation, deception and voting in which players are politicians from the two big parties but who also try to push forward an individual hidden agenda. This game was initially designed as a game-base learning tool at the London School of Economics and Political Science to introduce students to collective decision-making, game theory and market regulation. The game scales well at different player counts and plays under 1 hour. Gamers and non-gamers alike will enjoy Kill the Bill. It rewards experienced players who manage well their hand of cards and use them in the appropriate way and at the right time. However, the game can be also thoroughly enjoyed as a party game with a strong take-that component in which negotiation and bluffing skills may compensate the lack of experience playing the tactics cards.

Overview
In Kill the Bill, players are members of a high profile Committee in the U.S. Congress who try to pass legislation benefiting both their political party and their own secret individual agenda. Each year 3 bills are discussed and voted by the Committee. The goal of the game is to become the most popular politician by the end of the fourth year (round) so to become the front-runner in the next presidential campaign. 
Players obtain points by passing bills which are favorable to the interest of their party (party scoring track), or favorable to their individual agenda (agenda scoring track). Moreover players get (and lose) popularity points throughout the game as effect of the political tactics cards played. Skillful persuasion and negotiation are important to success. However, in politics things are never straightforward. A wide range of political tactics (cards) are available to players who can unexpectedly turn decisive votes in their favor. Building alliances across the party lines, having a long-term strategy in mind and showing tactical flexibility are important in "Kill the Bill" too. Players must be ready for blackmail, bribery, deception and treason. Sometimes the end justifies the means.

Turn order
Each round consist of 4 phases:
1-Preparation (new tactics cards are dealt -draft in the first year, Committee Chair is selected and bills presented)
2-Logrolling (negotiation) phase: players try to persuade each other to support or oppose certain bills
3-Political tactics phase: tactics cards are played
4-Voting phase: bills are voted and scoring tracks adjusted.

description:
2GM Tactics Wargame is a tabletop game that lets you recreate battles between the US – Allied nation – and Germany – Axis nation – during the late years of the WW2.

Players will create and customize their own decks in advance in order to complete the military feats assigned. Army decks are comprised of Unit cards (Infantry, Artillery, Armor, Transports and Aircraft) and Support cards.

Support cards complement each army deck and provide special, one-time abilities and perks to our units during a battle. Use Support cards to hamper the enemy and their strategy.

Terrain cards are double-sided cards that are placed in the battlefield and provide extra cover to our units.

Additionally, you’ll also find General cards, Scenario cards and Player Aid and Game Summary cards.

There are two different game modes:

1. Battle Royal, in which the goal is to destroy the opponent’s HQ.

2. Scenario, a pre-set scenario with its own set of deployment rules and predetermined configuration. The final goal is established by the specific rules of the scenario itself.

A key factor to take into account in the game is the ability a player has to build up their own army deck before entering the battle. Depending on the objectives and the restrictions inherent to the scenario, the player will be able to choose what units and Support cards to include.

The goals to achieve the victory in a given game are totally diverse depending on the battle or scenario being played. Goals Range from destroying enemy’s HQ to seize the battlefield or inflicting a specific amount of casualties; to bomb a building or seizing it; raze a specific objective or steal supplies from the enemy; rescue a key position or standing on it for a given number of turns, among others.

German Reinforcements is an expansion to the base game in which we add new Unit and Support cards to Germany. Also new Terrain cards, such as Factories or Debris.

This expansion consists of 76 new cards, 42 units of 18 different types and 28 additional Support cards. In addition, we include 4 new Terrain cards, 1 summary card and two new General cards: Von Bock and Von Leeb.

Build your own town in Design Town! In this deck-building game, you have no hand at all; instead, you play cards directly from the top of your deck. Winning the game requires delicate strategies...and some luck as well!!

A turn consists of two phases: card playing and building.

• Players play cards directly from the top of their deck and may choose to continue unless they have three cry-face icons in play, which ends their turn immediately. When they choose to stop, they move to the building phase.

• During the building phase, a player can use the coins gained from their previously played cards to buy a new card or to upgrade a card in their personal discard pile. To upgrade a card, pay the cost and flip it over; upgraded cards remain in the discard pile.

Since all cards in this game are double-sided, be careful not to flip them over accidentally when placing cards into your discard pile or when shuffling your deck. Whenever a player gains eight or more VP during a single card playing phase, that player wins immediately!

This edition of Design Town consists of the original Design Town from Homosapiens Lab as well as the Design Town: Reuse expansion that adds two more double-sided cards to the game.

description:
The cities of the future continue to expand…upward! As buildings get taller, skyways are constructed to connect buildings above street level to allow for easier foot traffic between them.

In Skyways, you and your opponents are building the city, floor by floor and skyway by skyway. By placing tiles of the same color, you can score large numbers of points. By placing a building capital on top, you finish a building and claim a colored area for yourself for bonus points at the end of the game. Do you want to score more now and risk giving your opponent a larger area connected to their capital, or do you want to get your capitals out as quickly as you can and enlarge your own areas for the end game?

The player with the most points when the tiles have been placed wins!

Galleons overloaded with gold are the desired catch for sea wolfs. The most influential pirate lords have gathered at the port to put together a team of sailors to board the traders' fleet. Seizing the gold is just half of the business, however; it’s much harder to…share it.

In Pirate Lords, you gather masters of saber, steering wheel, and pistol under your flag — then passionately bargain with other players for the booty. Only in unity can pirates overpower a trader's defenses. Will you show greediness or generously share rewards? Your victory in the game depends on timely decisions.

Each player is dealt 7 white cards, each including a word or phrase. Each round, players take turns serving as the judge, drawing one black card containing a question or fill in the blank sentence.

Players answer the question or fill in the blank sentence by choosing their favorite white card to fill it in. Each player takes turns judging, choosing their favorite option and the funniest answer wins the round.

Love Will Tear Us Apart
A sad little game about dividing your stuff after a divorce.

How to play: 
    
1. Hold the paper up between each other for 5 seconds. The side you are looking at shows your goal items. You then flip the paper over so each player is looking at the other player's side. (maybe draw a little stick figure example?)
    
2. Each player takes turns trying to rip out a single item at a time from the items on their goal side WITHOUT looking at it while the other player holds the page up.
    
3. Each item successful ripped off in full, with no pieces of any other item on the same piece is worth one point. Most points when all the items are taken or damaged wins.
    
4. Check PrettiestPrincessGames.com for a rules video and more details and variants.

You'll find that in the process of trying to get the most stuff, you'll not only end up destroying what you want by accident, but also knowingly trashing the stuff your ex wants.

Part of the Prettiest Princess Games Consumable 1-Sheet line.

Roll it. Spark it. Score it. Do you have what it takes to get the HIGHEST score?

Rules:

1. Roll it. Roll this paper into your best interpretation of a jazz cigarette; score side on the inside.

2. Spark it. Light it on fire. Literally. Be safe. Or don't. Whatever.

3. Score it. You can put out the fire whenever you choose, however you choose. Score the burn line furthest from the start. HIGHEST score wins or play multiple rounds to 420.

Note: Don't be a moron and actually set yourself or your stuff on fire.

Part of the Prettiest Princess Games Consumable 1-Sheet line.

A game of trivia, teamwork, & campaign strategy. Ages 10 and up, for 2-8 players.
ELECTION! The Presidential Campaign Game
2nd Edition
Overview:
Citizens vote to elect our president. In this game, you can lead the campaign! ELECTION! is a game of trivia, teamwork, & campaign strategy. Players compete to be the first party to claim the most electoral votes in an American Presidential election campaign. Players lead their party’s campaign for president by raising funds and purchasing Town Hall, Advertise, and Charisma cards to earn votes. Then with votes in hand, they strategically claim State's electoral votes and ensure their party's victory. Game Play:

Each turn involves three actions.
1 Roll dice
2 Buy Cards
3 Claim States
ACTION 1: Players can choose to roll the dice to raise funds. ACTION 2: They can opt to buy cards to build their campaign and earn popular votes. For example, buy a Town Hall trivia card to test your knowledge of history, State Capitals, State nicknames, and more. A correct response earns the votes. ACTION 3: With popular votes in hand, the players use them to win selected States on the score ballot.

Players may also "endorse" a teammate after their three actions. This is done by forwarding cards to a teammate's hand, pooling cards and votes to claim more States during their turn. Can the party now claim enough electoral votes to win the election?

Game progress is kept on the score ballot. The score ballot lists all 50 states and DC, and is sorted based on presidential election results from 1988 through 2008. There are states that lean red, states that lean blue, and swing states in the middle. States that lean toward the player's political party require fewer popular votes to claim. The swing states are equal for each party, and are more difficult for both. By claiming states with earned votes, players win electoral votes for their party and work toward victory. The electoral votes listed are current for the 2012, 2016, and 2020 presidential elections.

Exiles is a fast-paced, frenetic, buzzword-filled game of gritty Wild West adventure. The game plays as a "miniatures role-playing game" because it runs like an RPG and plays like a skirmish wargame. One player acts as the game master, or governor, and the others play against a scenario of his or her creation. The game ends when the players win or lose depending on the scenario's conditions or if the players survive.

boardgame: Marvel Universe Miniature Game: The Dark Avengers Starter Set
description:
This game is a small scale skirmish level game where Superheroes and Villains face off against each other in a 3D tabletop set up. It could be a city, the Wakandan desert, the Savage Lands, the blue area of the moon or anything you like. In Marvel they fight anywhere.
It is played on a 36" x 36" board so will fit on most tables without a problem.

In a game you will usually have between 4-7 characters on your team. Each of these characters act individually using their own Power to carry out different attacks and use Special Powers.
Many can help or hinder each others though with boosts and actions like a Fastball Special.

The aim of the game is to win set scenarios by completing different tasks and scoring Victory Points. These could be anything from take down / save the highest Level model in the team or rescue and keep a Cosmic Relic.

Each character comes with their own Stat Card which shows everything they can do. Attacks, Defence, Skills, Power, Damage and such.
These are then used in a 2 D8 mechanic during the game. For example, 2D8 plus Attack Stat vs Defence Stat or 2D8 against your own Agility.

Reformed supervillain Norman Osborn, now calling himself the Iron Patriot, was given leadership of the espionage agency S.H.I.E.L.D. He reformed that agency into H.A.M.M.E.R. and created a new Avengers team under its aegis. The line-up consists of former Thunderbolts members and new recruits Sentry and Ares, as well as disguised super-villain Moonstone who is portraying Ms. Marvel. Using the Dark Avengers as a cover Norman declared war on the Asgardians, and has the Dark Avengers prepare for the Siege of Asgard. Norman considered The Sentry, specifically his dark side, known as The Void, his secret weapon.

"IT'S CLOBBERIN TIME!" The famous words bellowed from the mutated human Ben Grimm known as "The Thing", protected by his rock like body The Thing can shake off almost any blow that would kill a mere un-mutated human.

boardgame: Marvel Universe Miniature Game: Dr. Doom
description: Victor Von Doom known to many as Doctor Doom is a criminal mastermind, Ruler of Latveria and arch nemesis of the fantastic four. He has spent his life studying both Science and the Dark Arts. Doom is a brilliant inventor and scientist. While at the same time, he is one of the most powerful sorcerers in existence, making him easily amongst the most dangerous villains on Earth. Having fought with almost every superhero in the Marvel Universe, Doom is a force to be feared, as time after time it takes teams of Superheroes working together to foil his evil plots.

Game description from the designer:

Figment is a cooperative, narrative card game for children ages 6 and up. Players are required to work closely together and use each other's individual strengths to defeat classic opponents from medieval fantasy tales and, as a team, prevent the destruction of the mythic kingdom!

Each player chooses one of ten classic and recognizable characters: a knight, a barbarian, an elf, a wizard, a princess, and so on. Each character has a specific combination of skills, represented by colorful icons, that can be used to defeat monsters along the way; stars represent physical might, while books stand for lore and hearts embody social skills. As the right combination of skill icons is required to defeat monsters and eventually dethrone the main villain of the piece. By doing this, children not only see that they differ from the other players, they even need to make good use of the other players' strengths — and help them cope with their weaknesses — to succeed.

Players start off in the idyllic medieval kingdom, where they'll confront weaker monsters drawn randomly from a stack of encounters while also traveling from one location to the next. When they feel up to it, certain locations give them access to the evil Shadowrealm, where stronger monsters roam but ancient, powerful magics can also be discovered. In this realm of darkness and shadow, they eventually have to defeat the one, powerful creature who has been organizing the monsters for an all-out assault on the kingdom...

Figment is ideal for young children and was developed in cooperation with educators and elementary school teachers.

This 1890 game from Milton Bradley uses the Happy Families/Authors mechanism of building sets by asking for cards, but complicates strategy through a set of bonus cards.

The game consists of 95 small card slips, roughly 2.5" long by 0.5" wide. Of these, 18 contain the name of a Longfellow poem, 72 have the name of the poem and two lines from it, and five contain the names of styles of edition bindings: Tree Calf; Full Morocco; Half Morocco; Cloth; and Paper. There also are six larger reference slips with the names of all 18 poems.

Players take turns asking for a poem or selections from a poem, and when they complete a set, play it down in front of them. They also may ask for an "edition slip", and if the asked player has one, he must hand it over. If he has more than one, he chooses which to hand over.

All but the Paper slip provide a bonus that applies to all sets scored at the time it is played. Bonuses are cumulative, so a doubled pair plus two more sets then tripled equals 18 points. However, whichever player is left with the Paper slip at the end of the game cuts his total score in half. Highest net score wins.

The game is listed by Alex G. Malloy (American Games, 1999) at p. 34, #1033.

Legenden des Schwertes ("Legends of the Sword") is a strategy board game for 2-4 players at the age of 12+.

Each player takes control of a samurai family that competes for the title of the new Shogun. Players must manage their province, villages and build castles, recruit units and complete missions.

The game is aimed at all strategy fans who love the setting up and managing of a little kingdom. The uniqueness of the game is that the players are able to gain victory over a lot of different strategies and every match is unique. There will never be a game that is similar to a previous one! In addition, the game offers an exciting battle system in which players must tactically operate and not just roll dice.

Game description:

Legenden des Schwertes is a round based strategy game. At the start of the game, every player gets a set of  mission cards and event cards. The mission cards are required to get victory points. The event cards can be used to manipulate combat between armies. The game is played in clockwise direction and every player has one turn per round.

The player's turn consists of five steps:
1. Receive income
2. Built and convert buildings
3. Hire units
4. Move and fight with your armies
5. End your turn and get victory points for completed missions cards

The game ends if a player has twelve or more victory points.

May the greatest strategic genius win!

What Just Happened is a competitive/cooperative storytelling game, similar to Cards Against Humanity, for 3-6 players that takes 30-45 minutes to play. Each player picks a character, plays an item on a character, and then tells the story that they feel transpired using the cards in play. The catch? You only have 60 seconds.

Each card has a points value that is based on how difficult the card is to use in a story with a little bias towards cards children can use. As each card is used, you "turn" it to denote that you have used that card in your story. At the end of your story you add up all the cards that you used correctly. The person with the most points each round wins the round. After you win 3 rounds you win the game!

This is a dependent expansion for the Band of Brothers series. It contains a double sided 22"x34" map. Map 27 is of a large hill formation and Map 28 is a winter map with woods and a village.

A counter sheet includes extra counters for really large scenarios as well as new units, including the M26 Pershing and Waffen SS counters.

The Battle Pack includes a mixture of 8 scenarios from small to large.

boardgame: Sky Kingdoms: Good Evil & Primal Forces
description:
Establish dominance of the skies with armies of battle-hardened warriors who attack from ancient machinations of war called Sky Kingdoms. Take control of the Good, Evil or Primal forces in this fast paced, expandable card game. Decimate opposing forces, and lay waste to their floating realms, then claim enough gold to build a new flying fortress capable of not only winning the battle, but the war itself.

At a glance
Sky Kingdoms is a light strategy game intended to keep every player engaged and active at every point of the game. The round-robin game mechanic (think Poker) keeps turns fast, and a single stat (think Blizzard's Hearthstone) makes for simple yet strategic gameplay.

Object of the Game
The goal of Sky Kingdoms is to collect as much Gold as possible by battling for control of the Skies. The first player to collect enough gold wins the game.

Card Battle mechanic
Each turn is made up of a series of Combat Rounds, where each player has the opportunity to use over 48 unique Unit Cards and Battle Cards in combination to produce the highest Combat Value, win Combat and collect Gold.

Game description from the publisher:

It is impossible to imagine the life on our planet without plants! They serve as food and shelter for animals, as their foes and protectors.

The plants evolve and ‘invent’ toxins and spikes, while animals develop antidotes and specialized feeding organs. Animals and plants develop complex symbiotic links with each other – as well as with organisms of another kingdom – mushrooms.

Evolution: Plantarum expansion allows you to play the game in the conditions of ever-changing plant life!

Evolution. The Origin of Species or Evolution. Random Mutations is required to play this expansion.

description:
This is a simple roll and move game. The players start at space 1 and advance according to the die roll. The first player to reach space 60 wins the game. Every few spaces depict another olympic sports competition. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players.

The game includes two dice, but the rules don't mention how many dice are actually used. So it probably depends if one wants to play a short or a long game.

description:
Eduskuntapeli is roll and move game from the sixties, where your want your party members to be elected to Finnish parliament (Eduskunta).

On your turn you roll the die and move one of your party members in the game board. Some areas in the game board are electoral districts, where you collect random amount of votes (1000-4000). 10000 votes gets you candidate to be elected to parliament. When you have gathered at least 10000 votes for your current candidate, you can move him/her to parliament. Then you can start moving and collecting votes for your next party member. Every player has 16 candidates.

Of course there are some squares where you have to pick up a random event card. Event cards might give you votes or get your party member to be elected as a minister or get the whole government to be moved to some parliamentary committee.

Other way to get your party member to government is to move one of your already elected members of parliament to government squares on the board. You want you MP's into government because then you get extra votes.
 Game ends when one player gets all his/her party members elected to parliament. Winner is the player with most votes, when the game ends.

boardgame: Cats Against the World (fan expansion for Cards Against Humanity)
description:
Cats Against the World is a parody game for horrible people... who LOVE CATS.  Unlike most of the party games you've played before, Cats Against the World is as awkward and sassy as you and your 40 plus cats at home are.

This deck (36 Cards: 27 White & 9 Black) is an expansion pack for Cards Against Humanity.  This means, you need to have or buy a Cards Against Humanity deck before using these cards.

100% OF PROFITS GO TOWARD SAVING THE LIVES OF HOMELESS & SICK CATS IN CALIFORNIA.

Each deck sold supports SOXrescue's No-Kill shelter initiatives within Kern, Los Angeles, and San Bernardino County; adoption program; Trap-Neuter-Release (TNR) project; and their emergency medical/disaster relief funds.

THE SALE OF ONE DECK CAN PROVIDE FOOD, SHELTER, AND MEDICAL TREATMENT FOR ONE HOMELESS CAT FOR ONE WEEK.

Unfortunately, although this product is still advertised on the Cats Against the World website, and orders can still be placed, it seems to be no longer be shipped, and the seller no longer responds to purchasers' messages sent via any contact method.

User summary
捕込 (Tokkome) is a facility where semi-farmed wild horses were captured and picked as military horses in Edo period of Japan.

Tokkome is a card game with a theme of horse-capturing.

Each card is numbered from 1 to 7, and depicts that many horses. There are two piles players can play cards on: Tokkome and Maki. Each turn, you may play one or three cards on top of either pile if the cards being played can form a sequence with the top card of the pile, or take all cards from Tokkome.

After taking all cards from Tokkome, if you have three cards of the same number, you discard them and score victory points, but taking all cards is risky because it increases your cards in hand, which you have to get rid of. Afterwards, all cards in the Maki pile go to Tokkome.

When a player gets rid of all their cards in hand, each other player loses victory points for their remaining cards in hand, and the highest scorer wins the game.

Publisher's summary
「捕込」は野馬を捕まえつつ手札を減らすゲームです。
ただし、野馬を捕まえる際に余計な手札も増えてしまいます。
いかに無駄なく野馬を捕まえられるかが悩ましいゲームです。

YANKS contains the entire United States Army order of battle for ASL, including five different squad types, their half squads, leaders, and all the varied vehicle, ordnance, and support weapon and crew counters, thus enabling you to command every type of combat unit used by the American Army in every theater throughout the war.

YANKS now includes the essential elements of PARATROOPER and 24 additional scenarios.

This is the 2nd editon.

Chapter H contains over 20 pages of detailed notes on the guns and vehicles, complete with some minor updates to the original. Also included are eight mapboards: 16-19, 24, 40-41, and 46, which will help you play many of the 40 scenarios inside YANKS: the 16 original scenarios from YANKS and PARATROOPER (all re-balanced) as well as 24 more out-of-print scenarios, re-numbered and re-balanced, all with existing errata incorporated. (New errata have been issued for the old scenarios to reflect the balancing changes made in these updates.)
