    Parker's Crossroads
    A Breezeless Day
    Ace In The Hole
    Broich Bash
    Hunters From The Sky
    Hitdorf On The Rhine 

    



 Contents:

    eight mapboards (16-19, 24, 40-41, and 46)
    six countersheets (1/2” and 5/8”)
    American Chapter H (U.S. vehicle and ordnance notes)
    40 scenarios


(from MMP website and user's description)
The Front Nine: Slow Play is a single promotional card for The Front Nine that features a poorly-driving zombie. Designer Nick Case notes, "This will be the last offering for The Front Nine game."

boardgame: Stevepunk (fan expansion for Council of Blackthorn)
description:
Stevepunk is a mysterious character representing the farthest reaches. He tells wild stories of incredible flying machines and steam powered weapons. Everyone loves him, but no one really trusts him.

This is a fan made expansion for Council of Blackthorn. Copies of this were handed out at Gen Con 2016 by Stevepunk himself.

Stevepunk claims: This expansion is banned in 7 states and 2 Canadian provinces.

Stevepunk adds 1 additional Council Member to choose from at the beginning of the game with unique actions and abilities.  Player tokens and council member card included.

7 Seize is a speedy battle of pirate wits that anyone can play with common bits & bobs found around the home along with a standard deck of cards. The Kickstarter includes deck up playing cards with custom art, 49 pieces of booty, and a box that transforms into a balance scale.

Players have 7 rounds to collect as much booty as they can. At the end of the game they weigh their pieces and the heaviest collection wins!

In each round starts with players wagering for the powerful role of Captain. Then the Captain divides some booty into 2 piles. Finally, players play another card to try to seize the best booty. They take turns grabbing pieces in order of rank from Ace to 2.

Gen Con 2016 promo. Publisher description:The Arcane Scrolls...are worth a whopping 12 gold, and, while they will provide a definitively large amount of gold at the end of the game, they prohibit you from gaining set bonuses at the end.  Risk it all for future riches or make what you can make now and get these scrolls past the Sheriff?  The choice is yours!

Card description: You cannot collect bonuses at the end of the game.

boardgame: Spider-Man Miniature Game: Superior Spiderman Starter Set
description:
The Spider-Man Miniature Game is  an expansion annexed to the BMG rules based on your favorite Web-slinger!
With this rules you will be able to build a crew with the most famous heroes and the worst maniacs from New York City!
In addition to the new releases, old heroes and villains will join the ranks of the new crews and gangs!

A copy of the Batman Miniature Game rulebook is needed to play.

Just when things started to go good for Peter Parker (for once), something bad happened. While visiting a dying Otto Octavius, Peter suddenly had his mind swapped with Otto's, allowing Otto to live and run around in Peter's body. But just before Peter died, Otto discovered the "true" meaning of "With great power comes great responsibility" via Peter's harrowing memories, so he decided to live up to Peter's legacy and become the Superior Spider-Man.

boardgame: Spider-Man Miniature Game: Goblin Cult Starter Set
description:
The Spider-Man Miniature Game is  an expansion annexed to the BMG rules based on your favorite Web-slinger!
With this rules you will be able to build a crew with the most famous heroes and the worst maniacs from New York City!
In addition to the new releases, old heroes and villains will join the ranks of the new crews and gangs!

A copy of the Batman Miniature Game rulebook is needed to play.

The Green Goblin Cult, a group of individuals who secretly 'worshipped' Norman Osborn and his worldview.  This cult would play heavily in getting Norman removed from incarceration and they should not be confused with Norman's other cult-following, the Order of the Goblin.

Great Comics Adventure is a tabletop card game celebrating the history of Comic Book Characters from the Golden Age. The deck includes 90 Triumph Cards and 18 City Cards. Several major cities are represented in 'The Great Comic Adventure' (GCA) all featured as post-cards. On the back of each post-card is the sequence of cards required to 'claim' that city. Once the five cards in your hands match the post-card, it is then claimed. Once you claim a city you will grab another, and once you claim three cities you win the round!

All the characters and images used in this game are from the Public Domain, so they belong to all of us! In general, works published after 1977 will not fall into the public domain until 70 years after the death of author, or, for corporate works, anonymous works, or works for hire, 95 years from the date of publication or 120 years from the date of creation, whichever expires first. Pre-1978 works what are in the public domain because they were published without proper copyright notice, or, in the case of pre-1964 works, were not timely renewed, do not receive retroactive protection under the CTEA. Similarly, works published before 1923, do not receive retroactive protection, and remain in the public domain. All of these comic books were published in the Golden Age of Comic Book History which all started with one super hero, Superman. The Golden Age could be given a specific start time thank to Superman #14 (Feb. 1942), this is when we ushered in the Golden Age of Comic Books. The Golden Age of Comic Books describes an era of American comic books from the late 1930s to the early 1950s. During this time, modern comic books were first published and rapidly increased in popularity. All the comics in the game feature comics from the 1940's and 1950's along with the post card designs featured as well.

Hästspelet is action-packed, eventful, fun and exciting game for everyone.

Hästspelet is a board game where you compete against each other to become the first to achieve three milestones. 
These three milestones are: building up the horse farm, win races and to collect a certain amount of money.

The game can be played at three levels: local, regional or national.
The levels affect the playtime and requirements of the milestones.

Some of the things that can happen during the game, competing in dressage, jumping and trot, chasing money, questions and fun and less fun "Mockor"
Mockor are random events that can affect both you and others of good and evil.

The questions for the game are mixed in different categories and divided into three age groups: children, youth and adults. 
This means that you will compete on the same terms.
The questions and "Mockor" are on an APP that is available for free download for both Android and Apple.
The APP requires no internet connection after installation.

You don’t have be a horse person to play this Hästspelet, likewise to you don’t have to be a broker to play monopoly.

Preparations
It requires the free app Hästspelet downloaded to a phone or tablet.
The app contains the questions and mockor needed to play. The download and installation takes a few minutes. The app requires no internet connection after installation. In the meantime you can prepare the bank with banknotes and read the rules. Unfold and place the game board so that everyone reaches it. Before the game begins, all players must agree on what level they will select.

The start
Start by placing the buildings "house and stables" in all the blank squares on the game board. This area represents each player's horse farm. Then distribute the SEK 50 000, a horse farm card, a holder for the game piece and a horse for all participants. The horse should be the same color as the two boxes labeled "Inackordering" on the board.
All then select the respective game character piece, attach it to its holder that has the same color as the horse. Place the game character piece in the starting box. Let a roll of the dice to decide who starts playing.
Always roll two dice during the game (with the exception of mockorna). The order is always clockwise. The bank pays out 30 000 SEK each time a player passes the starting box.

The milestones
Milestone 1 - Build a horse farm
Horse farm built by landing on the "Mäklare” (Broker) ", where the player can buy buildings to their horse farm. You can buy several buildings at the same time. Read on horse farm card for more information.

Milestone 2 - Gain Diploma
Diplomas obtained by winning races. The Bank manages the distribution of the award to the winner of a contest, with the prize money standing on diploma card.
The winner and runner-up parts of the money in the pot. If the pot is uneven it is awarded to the winner advantage.

Milestone 3 - Cash
Cash acquire it through: to win races, coming second in the competitions, the boarders at your horse farm, answering the question right, pass the Start box, some mockor and on the horse market where you can sell.

There are special Contest Rules for "jumping", "Dressage" and "racetrack" because it is a competitive element of the game that often occur. Much because of the desire to win money, which in turn generates that one can buy buildings to their horse farm. Last but not least, you will gain a diploma when you win.

There are rules for three and two players and rules for five and four players that is slightly different from when there is 6 players.

What makes this game a little unique is precisely the balance of the questions and mockorna, racing adrenaline and the crazy events that may occur in mockorna. And also fun with an APP for a board game, it makes it feel new and fresh.

Welcome to the Orctoberfest, where all creatures of the fantasy world are invited to have a beer and a sausage before the next bumpy rollercoaster ride. This yearly fair is pretty crowded, though, and long queues await the visitors before they are able to quench their thirst.

In the role-selection game Orctoberfest, the players try to satisfy their characters by reducing their waiting time in the queues for bratwurst, beer and pretzels. The sooner a character gets served, the happier he is, leaving the queue for the beer hall. The player with the most happiness at the end of the game wins — but not everybody plays fair and wants to wait for their turn, trying to jump the queue, or worse, using their body odor to scare others out of the waiting line.

Different character abilities provide interesting decisions for the players throughout the game. Playing the right character at the right time, assessing the situation on the table correctly, and predicting the plays of your opponents are the keys to victory in this fast-playing queue-building game.

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – Muratas Expansion Pack
description:
Star Trek - Attack Wing: Muratas Expansion Pack is part of the Wave 29 expansion packs from WizKids Games, and this expansion comes with one new ship and a new set of cards for Star Trek: Attack Wing.

The Muratas is a Xindi Reptilian Warship class ship.

A 2-player abstract game, designed by C. W. Cowsley and distributed by Applied Technology in 1989. The game consists of twelve top-hat shaped tokens (10 yellow numbered from 1-10, 1 red and 1 blue), the instructions, and a green cloth bag which doubles up as a double-sided game board.

One player plays as Red, the other as Blue. The object of the game is to capture the highest total of numbered yellow tokens, without your own token being captured by your opponent. The tokens are designed so that they can be easily pressed together, so that the players' red and blue tokens gradually accumulate a stack of yellow tokens underneath them as the game progresses.

The board consists of ten circles, connected by lines in a geometric pattern. There are two variant configurations, one printed on each side of the cloth bag. The game is played in two phases: firstly, each player alternately places one numbered token on a circle until all ten have been placed; secondly players take turns moving their red or blue token along the lines from circle to circle, capturing tokens as they move, until either all ten numbered tokens have been captured - or one player has blundered allowing their own token to be captured! The advantage of going first is mitigated by placing an upper limit on the first token that the starting player may capture - in the basic game, this is fixed at 6; in the advanced game this is decided by auction.

Welcome to the Artworld: The Card Game! Artworld is playable as art critic who rates art, and art collectors who buy art through auction and sell the art to the critic for money and points. To win, the art collectors must excel in reading the critic's taste and predicting what kind of art the critic would like, and what kind of art the critic would dislike. It is also possible for art collectors to make a set of artworks (3, 5 or 7 cards) that have same traits, in order to get points. First collector who reaches 10 million dollars or 5 points wins the game.

For this expansion, a new meeple is added: a black camel. During the game, the owner of this camel is known as the Caravan Leader.

At the beginning of the game, the black camel is placed on the Caravansery (6); it does not have an owner at this point.

If you execute the action in the Caravansery, take the black camel at the end of your turn and put it in front of you; you are now the Caravan Leader. You remain the Caravan Leader until another player executes the action in the Caravansery.

If you are already the Caravan Leader before you execute the action in the Caravansery, you receive the bonus of the Caravan Leader: After taking 2 bonus cards, you don´t discard any cards from your hand.

You are taking a trip to Venice, where the famous carnival is held.
While walking around, a masked lady challenges you to a gem hunt in the middle of a canal.
Use the planks to go from pile to pile to find your way through this labyrinth!

Each player picks a colour and has to collect four gems: rubies for the red player, diamonds for the white player, emeralds for the green player and topazes for the yellow player. Use your stock of planks to go from one pile to another.
A player wins the game if he/she is the first to collect all 4 gems of his/her colour.

The game can then continue to determine who comes second, third and fourth.

In Hand of Manus players take roles of craftsman guild leaders, competing to construct and present the best hand for valorous warrior Manus, who lost his hand in a battle.
Each round players design and then test their constructs in a test room. A hand consists of three parts - shoulder, forearm and wrist and each part has affinity to different elements, so it's performance depends on testing conditions. Every round players bet on expected performance for their hand and then secretly influence testing conditions. Whoever fulfills their bet scores points accordingly, and a first player to get 12 points wins.

AbrakaRun is a game in which we will play the role of a wizard.  He will have to move on a board to get first to the finish line, the King's Castle.
We will cast spells (yes, you are right in reading "spells") in order to move around the board because we want to become the greatest wizard of this Kingdom.
We will have "hidden" tokens to cast a spell and it will require a good memory on our part to use them.  These important magic words are needed not only to get ahead, but also to fight the other wizards whose aim is to reach the goal too.

Description in Spanish:
ABRAKARUN, un juego donde interpretaremos el papel de un mago que deberá avanzar por un tablero para conseguir llegar el primero a la meta, el castillo del Rey.

Para avanzar, usaremos hechizos (sí, has leído bien, hechizos) porque queremos ser el mayor mago del reino. Para invocar esos hechizos necesitaremos ingredientes, que encontraremos en forma de tokens ocultos por lo que necesitaremos una buena memoria para poder utilizar los hechizos.

Estos hechizos no sólo nos permitirán avanzar, sino que también podremos atacar al resto de magos que intentan llegar al castillo y perjudicarles en su loca carrera hacia la meta.

boardgame: Star Wars: X-Wing Miniatures Game – Sabine's TIE Fighter Expansion Pack
description:
Game description from the publisher:

The ubiquitous TIE fighter was one of the Empire’s most common symbols of oppression. When one of these nimble crafts fell into the hands of Rebel saboteur and artist Sabine Wren, she knew exactly how to customize it for use against the Empire. Brilliantly repainted and retooled, the TIE fighter from the Sabine’s TIE Fighter Expansion Pack for X-Wing comes with four unique ship cards and six upgrades, including the Sabine’s Masterpiece Title, which allows your Rebels to outfit the ship with crew and illicit upgrades.
family: Game: Star Wars X-Wing Miniatures Game

boardgame: Star Wars: X-Wing Miniatures Game – Quadjumper Expansion Pack
description:
Game description from the publisher:

First appearing in The Force Awakens, the quadjumper arrives to X-Wing as a Scum and Villainy ship, full of new maneuvers and dirty tricks. In addition to its pre-painted quadjumper miniature, the Quadjumper Expansion Pack allows you to militarize this typically unarmed ship with four Scum pilots and eight upgrades. These include new crew members, illicit technologies, and a modification that grants extra versatility to your tractor beam tokens. Finally, a unique elite pilot talent and associated condition help your elite pilots pay back their debts against those who have done them wrong.
family: Game: Star Wars X-Wing Miniatures Game

boardgame: Star Wars: X-Wing Miniatures Game – Upsilon-class Shuttle Expansion Pack
description:
Description from the publisher:

Make your opponents fear the First Order and the dark side of the Force with the Upsilon-class Shuttle Expansion Pack for X-Wing! It features one pre-painted, large-base miniature of the sleek, predatory shuttle that the First Order typically used to escort its high-ranking officers. Boasting both powerful weapons and shields, the shuttle also comes with a full complement of fifteen upgrades. These include advanced new technologies as well as the dark side warrior Kylo Ren, who allows you to hinder your foes with his associated condition, I’ll Show You the Dark Side.
family: Game: Star Wars X-Wing Miniatures Game

description: Explore Alexander's decisive battle in a card-based system designed by Richard Berg. This is not a "card-driven" game, nor one with a traditional mapboard, but one in which the units and leaders themselves are cards that capture the abilities and appearance of the two armies. Cards face, move, and fight within their own grid. Game options include a "historical" battle deployment or allowing you to "do better" on your own.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: History: Wars of Alexander the Great
Series: Twenty Decisive Battles of the World (TPS)

description: 2-3 player strategy game with a 2 team 4 player cooperative variant. Set in Ancient Greece, players try to reach the Golden Fleece and bring it to any of the 4 cities at the corners of the board. Opponents try to trap or land on the player with the Golden Fleece. Trapped players are teleported to the corner squares minus the Fleece. The winner is the first player to get the Fleece to a corner square / city.

boardgame: Tanto Cuore: Private Maid – Aino Svensen
description:
This card was given as a promo at Gen Con 2016.

Card Description-  During your starting phase:  You may discard a card of your choice from your hand.  If you do, you may immediately draw 1 card.

Mystic Vale: Vale of Magic introduces 54 new advancement cards and 18 vale cards to the Mystic Vale base game, expanding player’s options and opening up new crafting combinations. This expansion is designed to be mixed straight into the base game, introduces effects that trigger "when bought" on some of the vale cards, and has a slight emphasis on Guardian power on the card advancements.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description: An expansion bundle that included More Cosmic Encounter, Simply Cosmic, and two additional player colours (hexes and base tokens): dark green and brown.

boardgame: The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game – Race Across Harad
description:
From the publisher:

Race Across Harad is the second Adventure Pack in the Haradrim cycle for The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game.

Freshly seated atop the oliphaunts they captured and tamed, a small band of Middle-earth's greatest heroes begin their journey through the deserts and dangers of Harad. However, they don't get far before they hear the distant howling of wargs. And where the warg howls, there also the orc prowls.

The new scenario in Race Across Harad pits the heroes against these wargs and their riders, all within the treacherous wilds of Harad. Their towering oliphaunts may give the heroes an advantage, however, even their oliphaunts cannot keep the heroes safe for long from the warg-riders and their barbed arrows. Instead, their only choice is to quicken their pace and race toward the Ford of Harnen. There, the heroes' massive oliphaunts might be able to crash through waters too deep for the wargs to cross…

Featuring scenario-specific rules that allow the Orcs and wargs to pursue the heroes from their own staging area, Race Across Harad brings this frantic chase to life in dramatic fashion, as you and your friends need to progress through your quest stages more quickly than the Orcs progress through their own.

Simultaneously, the Adventure Pack introduces a new Dúnedain hero in the Lore sphere, as well as three copies each of nine different player cards that enhance a wide range of the game's existing keywords, Traits, and mechanics. You'll find support for everything from Mounts to side quests and from the Doomed keyword to the cycle's new focus on events that offer powerful rewards when you can target specific pairs of Traits.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: The Lord of the Rings – The Card Game (LCG)

boardgame: The New Game of Divers or Hidden Treasure
description:
This game from Parker Brothers takes a concept used by early fishing games and re-themes it to divers trying to hook sunken treasure.

The game includes a fold-out cardboard "tank" about 9" square and 6.5" high. Each wall contains a mesh-covered arch through which players can see to some extent what they are doing. There are two foot-long wooden poles, each with a string dangling from one end leading to a 2.5"-tall deep-sea diver figure with a small metal hook at its feet. The treasure consists of small cardboard rectangles, each with a metal loop projecting from the back, and the type of treasure (gold, silver, jewels) printed on the bottom with a value.

The treasures are placed value down at the bottom of the tank, and players must dangle their divers over the wall, twisting the rods to try and hook the treasures. The game can be played as a race between two people that lasts until all the treasure has been retrieved. Or it can be played by more people, with each player getting three minutes to hook as much as possible. Either way, the largest value of treasure (not number of catches) wins the game.

This is a fantasy-themed roll-and-move game with a three-dimensional board. The board comes in three segments: two of these form a colorful truncated pyramid when pegged together, and the third forms a square ivy-covered stone tower that fits into the top of the pyramid.

Each side of the pyramid has 35 holes for pegs on a winding path up to the tower; each side of the tower has a further 11 holes leading to the window where the princess presumably awaits rescue.

Each player rolls a die to climb one side of the pyramid and then up the adjacent wall. There are no shortcuts up, but eight spaces have slides back down. First player to rescue the princess wins. The rules suggest a party variation, in which four "prizes" are placed in the tower, and players get to choose a prize in order of finish.

The box is not dated, but the game is listed in the 1946 Catalog of Copyright Entries.

This is a game of global travel and trade. It consists of six miniature ships, two different spinners, and a four-section map of the world measuring roughly 35" x 20". The map bears a variety of different routes available (Panama vs. Cape Horn, Suez vs. Cape of Good Hope, etc.) that can be sailed in either direction.

The objective is to complete a trip around the world while picking up the greatest value of cargo. Players each start with a ship in New York and spin for their initial cargo. They then announce a direction and destination, and when they arrive at that port, can add a cargo based on the chief exports of that port as listed on the board.

When leaving a port, players who spin greater than 3 move quickly but face a financial penalty (for being delayed in port). The winner is not the player who completes the round the world voyage first, but whoever finishes with the highest total value of cargo. If a ship is destroyed by landing on a storm, all cargo is lost, the player gets a spin for insurance to offset the lost, but starts again from New York.
Two sets of playing pieces to make the game playable with 4 instead of 2 players

This is a quiz game regarding the Greek cinema. With this game you share beautiful moments with your friends by putting in test your memory and knowledge about famous greek old and modern movies. The game components: Board, 6 pawns, 484 cards, a die and the rules

User summary

This is the fifth extra Tragedy Set to Tragedy Looper, but it's numbered to be "2B", as it's in the same ideas as the second extra set: Horror.

This set is based around the Cthulhu Mythos, and contains everything needed for the tragedy set, including script creation guide and 6 sample scenarios.

boardgame: Mauritius & Réunion (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride)
description:
This follows the principles of the basic Ticket to Ride game on a special board with special cards and rules. The object of the game is to gain the most points by travelling various routes and outwitting other players. You will need the train pieces and the train cards from either the US or Europe base games (or you can buy them from the Days of Wonder shop.

There is an extra set of Tourist cards for the cities, each with a value to be earned by visiting that city. These cards are dealt/picked up alongwith tickets in the same quantity and with the same 'keep/dispose' rules.There are also up to 9 bonuses for the end-of-game scoring.

The board does not have any grey tracks or parallel tracks - but there are spanners in the works which may help (or hinder) you.

There are many new options in the rules: exchange all your cards at the start but keep all the new ones: discard 5 cards and pick up 5 new ones, as your turn: earn extra points: and many more. You can also call time when your wagon stock reaches 3, if you want, giving you the advantage of stopping other players completing tickets.

boardgame: ATS Gallabat: The War in East Africa 1940
description:
Gallabat: The War in East Africa 1940 is a set of 10 historical scenarios taking place around the borderlands between Kenya and Eithiopia, pitting the forces of colonial Britain and Italy against each other in fierce, early-war battles.
Besides the 10 all-new scenarios there are four 11" × 16" geomorphic boards to play them on, as well as 368 color, die-cut counters including new soldier art; all the combat counters needed to play! 
Ownership of the ATS Rulebook and standard system markers are required.

This Advanced Tobruk System module contains maps & scenarios for two separate invasion points during the D-Day operations.

First, King Red, and the amphibious landing by Commonwealth forces at the eastern portion of Gold Beach, where the 5th Yorkshire were faced with Weapons Nest 33 (Wn33), a Nazi emplacement bristling with weaponry.  This aspect of the game features modified Churchill and Sherman tanks, the "funnies", such as the anti-mine Crab variant and the emplacement-busting AVRE.

Second, Operation Deadstick was the codename for an operation by airborne glider troops of the British Army that took place on 6 June 1944 as part of the Normandy landings. The objective was to capture intact two road bridges in Normandy across the River Orne and the Caen Canal providing the only exit eastwards for British forces from their landing on Sword Beach. Intelligence reports said both bridges were heavily defended by the Germans and wired for demolition. Once captured, the bridges had to be held against any counter-attack until the assault force was relieved by commandos and infantry advancing from the British landing zone.

This set includes new scenarios, maps and all the combat counters needed to play out King Red and Operation Deadstick.
Ownership of the ATS Rulebook and standard system markers is required.

The objective of The County Fair Game is to have the most fun at the county fair by spending as much of your money to win prizes, be awarded ribbons, take photos, eat food and experience the fair.  Players begin with 85 play dollars to spend at the fair.  Before entering the fair, they must first find a parking place (roll to land in spaces around the outer edge of the board.) Upon entering the fair, players roll a die each turn and can choose which space to land on (usually from several options.) The player with the most points from accumulated prizes, and experiences wins.

boardgame: Das kleine Gespenst: Spuk auf Burg Eulenstein
description: Every night in Das kleine Gespenst: Spuk auf Burg Eulenstein, the little ghost awakes at the witching hour to run through the castle rooms to play. Its favorite game is cannonball bowling — but where did it hide the cannonballs last time? Who can help the little ghosts find them again? Who has been paying attention to which chests hide which cannonballs?

boardgame: Mille Bornes: Edition spéciale Sécurité Routière
description:
Small version of the old french classic game given for free during  2016 summer holidays on french highways. This was for educate drivers on road safety.

Only 32 cards in this version:
16 mileage cards 
4 dump hazard cards (1 classic - red light - and 3 specifics for this version: Alcohol, Phone call, no motorcycle equipment)
8 remedy hazard cards (green light, negative ethylotest, end the call, motorcycle equipment)
4 safety cards (emergency vehicle, SAM - the guy who drives don't drink, hands free kit, full equipment and airbag

Other changes from the usual rules of the game:
The goal is to reach 500 km
No need of a green light at first before playing mileage cards

Imagine a 10-day trip through Switzerland. During your journey you visit the cantons and lakes that have a common border. You eventually will have to ride a train, bus or a seaplane to connect two destinations. With a little luck, you will draw a card that is perfect for your trip. In the end, 10 regions and means of transport must be aligned in the right order, Simple rules that guarantee fun and entertainment for children and adults.
The goal is to be the first player to reach the top of his mountain. To do so, each player draw a tile picturing something. If the first letter of the image is on your mountain, you "climbed" it.

boardgame: Бестиарий Сигиллума: кузница хаоса (Bestiary of Sigillum: Forge of Chaos)
description: This expansion brings 9 new characters and adds a little bit of uncertainity to the game via random events or fluctuations of damage.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Bolt Action: Campaign – Battle of the Bulge
description: The Ardennes, 1944. Driven back by the Allies since D-Day, Germany launches a surprise offensive on the Western Front. This assault against the unprepared Allied lines is the opening move in one of the largest battles of World War II. This new Campaign Book for Bolt Action allows players to take command of both armies in this desperate battle, fighting it as they believe it should have been fought. New, linked scenarios, rules, troop types and Theatre Selectors provide plenty of options for novice and veteran players alike.

The year is 1940, and the German invasion of Britain has begun. With this new campaign book for Bolt Action, players can fight the battles of World War II's greatest 'what if' scenario. Defend the cliffs of Dover and the beaches of Kent from wave after wave of German landing craft. Parachute into the Home Counties in a surgical strike to capture Winston Churchill. Rally the Home Guard in a last, desperate attempt to keep England free of the Nazi invaders! Containing new rules, scenarios, and unit types covering all of the unique features of this alternate history campaign, it offers a chance for all Bolt Action players to truly rewrite the history of World War II.

Game description from the publisher:
Samurai are fiercely guarding their secrets and daymyo does not forgive slightest offense. However not even that can stop ninjas from stealing mystical scrolls for their masters. Now thieves face most difficult task – escape from village surrounded by samurai!

Ninja Cats are fast tactical game for 2-4 players. Use your cards to maneuver your pawn in such manner so you will be not surrounded by enemies or angry samurai.

Game description from the publisher (Polish):
Samuraje zazdrośnie strzegą swoich sekretów, a daymyo nie wybacza najdrobniejszej zniewagi.
Lecz nawet to nie powstrzymało ninja przed wykradnięciem tajemnych zwojów w imieniu swoich panów.
Teraz przed złodziejami stoi najtrudniejsze zadanie – ucieczka z osady otoczonej przez samurajów...

NINJA KOTY to szybka taktyczna gra dla 2-4 osób. Przy użyciu kart manewrujemy swoim pionkiem w taki sposób, aby nie zostać otoczonym przez przeciwników oraz rozzłoszczonych samurajów.

Build your own army, destroy your enemies and most of all, have fun! Tiny Armies is a funny card game for 2-4 players with strategy and a bit of luck. It plays in 20-40 minutes depending on the number of players.

You draw cards to help build your army, consisting of various different units. Units are then recruited from hand, spies are potentially played and you attack enemy units to score stars.

The objective is to gain the most stars with the game ending at a pre-set number of stars. You gain stars by destroying enemy units and stealing them from other players.

Game description from the designer:

Optimo+ is the reimplementation of Optimo!  It contains the base game and an expansion for younger players (7+) with different cards and rules, but using the same mechanism.

Optimo! is a fast-paced word game in which you have to be the quickest to find the best word with each round's setting. In each round, you have a combination of six cards with constraints (letter or lexical). These constraints can be a requirement (i.e. you must use the letter "E" or you must have six letters in the word) or a ban (i.e. you can't use verbs or you can't have a word with 4 or less letters). Then you must find a word that corresponds to at least 4 constraints, then trigger the timer to give the other players 30 seconds more to find a better word than yours. Pondering if you should find a 5(or 6)-constraint word or go fast with a 4-constraint word to get the fastest-player bonus but risk losing it all to a better word is the core mechanic of the game.

Optimo kids is an easy and fast-aced word game in which you have a combination of 4 cards with constraints (letter, lexical and theme) which can be requirements or bans. Players must find a word that fits the 4 constraints or launch a "dare" to the other players, leaving them 30 seconds to come up with a word or else, the "darer" wins the round.

One set of 5 different colored discs for each player participating and the "Violetta" piece are placed (one piece on one space) in a random pattern on the game board. The playing pieces start on the corner spaces. Movement is determined by a die and is allowed in any direction, but not diagonal. If a playing piece gets on a space with a disc it needs next, it collects the disc. The discs may only be collected in the right order, beginning with the largest (red) and ending with the smallest (blue). The playing pieces may only enter empty spaces or spaces with the next disc they Need. When a player has collected all 5 discs, he further needs to collect "Violetta" to win the game.

description:
Description from the publisher:

Is your goblin horde more of a goblin clique? Not for long! Swell your goblin ranks with Munchkin Pathfinder: Truly Gobnoxious, a booster pack with ten new monster cards and five goblin-related treasures for use with Munchkin Pathfinder or any other Munchkin title.

Game description from the publisher:

Ostfront is the result of 5 years of research and play-testing. Its a comprehensive World War 2 system aimed at historical accuracy, balance and fun. The rules have been streamlined over the years, and we would consider it "fast play" - Its quick, brutal and rewards bold commanders with sound tactics.

Ostfront covers Infantry, Light Vehicles, all varieties of Tanks and Self-Propelled guns, Artillery in the form of Field Guns and Howitzers, Air Support and Air defense, as well various other forms of support and fortifications. It can be played in any theater of WW2, from North Africa to the Pacific. Aimed at 1/72, 1/100 or smaller scale miniatures. It would also work fine for 28mm.

The Ostfront system is all about combined arms - it plays at a Company to Battalion level, with the smallest unit being the Infantry Platoon. Infantry are deployed in bases, each one representing about 25 men for regulars, and about 10 men for the veteran Assault Infantry units. At 1/72 scale we usually use 3-4 miniatures per base, meaning you can make a large amount of bases from any cheap infantry box - one airfix box of 48 miniatures would give you 10 bases.

Tanks are of course a large part of Ostfront battles, with the average game fielding anywhere from 1 to 20 of them per side, sometimes more in larger games. Light vehicles play an important role, and reconnaissance has a tangible effect in-game - he who scouts out the area first can gain a tactical advantage.

We Split WW2 into 3 periods, allowing balanced battles to be fought and still capture the feelings of operations like Barbarossa and Operation Torch. Its not always about who has the most heavy tanks - in fact heavy tanks are somewhat expensive in the points system, due to the difficulty with which they were produced, maintained and transported. During early war players will need to rely on light tanks, Infantry, Artillery and air strikes to secure their objectives. Mid war covers Kursk style battles - with the Tiger, Panther and Sherman all making an appearance, but before heavier Soviet tanks like the IS-2 or T-34/85 had come into action. Late war covers all the late war toys, from the JagdTiger and Tiger II to the Pershing, to the aforementioned IS-2, among many others.

Ostfront also has a campaign system for combination map-and-tabletop play, allowing you to get that glorious combination of grand strategy and tabletop tactics. The campaign allows up to 5 players to go head-to-head on a map of Europe and North Africa, from Spain to Russia, Scandinavia to Egypt. Each faction fights to control production centres - where they produce more forces each turn, and to spread their supply lines out to the places they need to conquer. A technology race is also implemented, with players who control Tech Centres able to produce slightly more advanced units - even extending to post war tanks if the game is currently in late war - ever wanted a good reason to use a Maus or IS-3? Well here's your chance.

Every time forces make contact on the Campaign map, an Ostfront tabletop battle is fought, with the amount of points on the campaign map dictating how many points you can use in your table top battle. After the battle the results are applied to the Campaign: if you lost 50% of your forces during the game, your force on the Campaign map will also lose 50%. If you were able to win the objectives and cut off your enemies supply train on the table, you will be able to force him back on the campaign map.

This connectedness makes each tabletop battle have a purpose, and adds a lot of flavor to the game as a whole. You might know you're horribly outnumbered, but you need to give the enemy force a bloody nose while you wait for your main force and your allies to come to your aid, and so you will build a specific list for that game, and that purpose. The campaign also allows for combined attacks, out-flanking maneuvers, encirclement and cutting off of supply lines, and a free-form diplomacy system is also implemented.

Two Campaign maps are available for free download, one is a balanced map for a slightly ahistorical but balanced campaign. The other is a realistic map for a  more accurate WW2 campaign.

description: This was an educational game from Selchow and Righter that was part of a series of at least 3 "Our USA" games.  The cover states it is for ages 7 to 14.  The game includes a map of the US with a winding path, a spinner, and cards corresponding to resources that could be collected along the path (e.g. Wyoming -- wood).  The object is to complete a full circuit of the path.

Dice City: Scrap Builder is a promo card for Dice City with the following ability:

Deactivate an active location in your city to get 1 resource of each different type in its cost.

Scrap Builder is an economic location with a build cost of 3 Iron, a Defense value of 4 and VP value of 2.

Spazz! The Card Game is a card game that tests your concentration and coordination. You know you have finally got the hang of it when you look completely ridiculous!

Gameplay:
All the cards in the deck are dealt out to the players, and they keep their hand face-down, each taking turns placing a card face up in the pile in the middle of the table. When two identical cards are on the top of the pile, all players must perform the correct action. The last player to do so must take the cards from the pile into their hand.
First one to get rid of their hand wins!

Audience:
Suitable for players ages 6 and up, playtime is approximately 10 minutes. Replayability infinite.

Contents:
110 cards
rules booklet

Lixso is a new and original logic game, somewhere in between Sudoku and a puzzle. Fill a grid entirely using L-shapes with the 4 colors (or matching symbols I,X,S & O), respecting the pre-filled cells, and in such a way that L-shapes with the same symbol/color don’t touch – neither on a side or a corner.
It sounds easy, but there are many ways to fill a 9 by 9 grid with L-shapes.  Each grid has only a single correct solution, you will need to use logical reasoning to find it. There are 6 difficulty levels. Try level 1 or 2 to learn the game – one can easily see which symbols fit or not, and with each added L, others become clear. But for level 4, 5 or 6, it is more difficult. Sometimes you have to imagine or place a whole chain of L-shapes to find out if it leads to the solution. First, place those L-shapes of which you are certain. Do observe the entire grid, as sometimes you have to continue on a whole other part of the grid.

Lixso can also be played with 2 to 4 players – rather than solving the puzzle, this becomes a competition to place the most L-shapes on the grid.
The symbols/colors are divided among players. With 2 players each has 2 symbols, with 4 players each has 1. With 3 players each has one symbol, and the remaining symbol is shared among players. 
The purpose of the game is to place the most L-shapes.
Place L-shapes in turns, while possible – and keep the count for each player. If you can’t, skip a turn. The game ends when no-one can place another L-shape. The Player who placed the most L-shapes wins.
Rules number 1 and 2 apply: respect the pre-filled cells, and L-shapes with the same symbol cannot touch. (an L-shape also cannot touch a pre-filled cel of the same symbol!)
Rules 3 does not apply – the grid does NOT need to be filled, empty cells can remain.
And herein lies the whole interest of the multiplayer game: rather than placing the L-shapes to solve the puzzle, you place them competitively, trying to limit available space for the other player/s. You can try to place tiles in such a way that parts of the grid (with the symbols/colors) of your opponent/s) become impossible to use.
As a variation, all players can play any of the symbols/colors. (don’t forget to keep count while playing!) This way it is very hard to gain an advantage over the other player.

Ordleg ("Word Play" in English) is a word dice game with game play the same as Ad-Lib Crossword Cubes. On your turn, you roll the 15 letter dice and arrange them into a crossword puzzle structure within the one minute time limit. Your score is based on the value of letters used. The player with the highest cumulative score after 10 rounds wins.

Two dice have one blank side. The game comes with two sheets of blank stickers so that you can cover these blank sides (or any other side of any die) and change the letter distribution to suit your preferred language.

Although the game comes with rules for 20 languages, including Greek and Russian, the game uses only the Latin alphabet. However, it comes with 100 blank stickers, enough to cover the sides of all 15 dice.

boardgame: Danger Mouse Secret Agent Card Game
description: Play a card with the same same symbol or colour as the top card of the discard pile. First player to play out all their cards and shout 'Danger Mouse!' wins.

A fun & challenging game with a messy room & chaos of night and day. Look out the window, is it sunny outside? Is your messy room full of lights and colors? If so, hurry up and find the COLOR that appears the most. But if it’s night then everything changes! The room is dark and full of shadows, no colors can be seen, only the shapes of the objects. This time try and spot the OBJECT that appearS the most. Hey! Pay attention! Two more objects might be hiding among your mess! a Flashlight and a curtain. They switch things around and turn day into night and night into day creating a FUN MESS OF A CHALLENGE!

How to play?
1The youngest player starts by flipping the first card of the deck and placing it next to it so that the objects side of the card is face up. Now there is the deck of cards with a window card on top and an objects card next to it. If the window card shows day then the players must try and spot the COLOR that appears the most on the card. 
2If the window card shows night then the players must try and spot the object that appears the most on the card. Then another card is flipped from the pile by the next player and the game continues.

3
Beware! There are two special objects that change things around. When they appear it doesn’t matter whether its night or day in the window because they switch things around! The winner of the game is the player who collected the most cards.

Description from the publisher:

Farce is an innovative new word game with an inventive step which creates new possibilities for players to outwit each other and have fun.

The inventive step in Farce divides the English Language into 3 elements (3 types of card) so that multiple cards may be combined to create new and original witty sentences. This hasn’t been done before and is an original feature of Farce.

Farce cards contain fragments of comedy which have been designed to combine with other fragments to generate new and original witty sentences. For each round of Farce each player combines up to 12 cards (comprised of the 3 elements) to make a witty sentence which they then read out to the group. Players use their own creativity to produce something uniquely funny with their cards which they then share with the group. The player who gets the most laughs wins the round.

The group decides on each round which player received the most laughs and awards a Farce Point. If there is any dispute then the dealer for that round has the final say. The dealer rotates after each round.

Farce includes 22 Wild Cards which allow players to insert the name of a fellow player into their farcical sentence, and thereby direct their sentences at each other and make fun of each other. This makes the game all about the players.

Farce is aimed at players aged 17+ as the outputs of the game can include adult humour.

The number of possible card permutations playable is virtually infinite. Players can always create something new with Farce, something original and funny which no one has ever heard before.

A resource gathering game where you are paying off the various noble houses of Dayrm to become the successor of the dead regent to the city of Farsley.  You will move your agents to each of the different merchants to gather the fine goods that these noble families desire in order to back your claim to become the next regent.

The modular board allows each game to be unique.  Each of the different payoffs can be played randomly under each of the 7 noble houses.  The order of the merchants is never the same as they populate around and create the game board.  Be the first to gather all the required items and become the next regent of Farsley.

boardgame: Secret Weapons of the Third Reich: Ahnenerbe Expansion
description:
A mini-expansion that introduces several new projects and powerful event cards.

Discover new weapon projects developed or though of by the Germans, such as the Silbervogel (a sort of shuttle), the Himmelsturmer fligh-pack, the Messerchmitt Me-262 and the Me-163 "Komet", the flying-wing bomber of the Horten brothers and more!

This expansion consists of:


     a instruction card
     6 new project cards
     3 new technology cards
     8 new event cards
     2 white cubes

Be sweet! Be cute! Try to please the humans and be the most cuddled cat! Roll your dice, see how the human is feeling, be mischievous, sweet, or silly! Fun for the whole family!

Each turn players roll their cat dice to determine if they take cuddle tokens from the pool of human cuddles, put cuddles back into the pool, are silly, steal cuddle tokens from other players, or roll the special human die. When there are no cuddle tokens are left in the middle, players count up the number of cuddle tokens they have. The player with the most tokens wins the round.

Players can play until someone wins one, two, or three rounds!

Cassette Juego was a very popular late 80´s spin and move series of portable boardgames that were created in Spain by CASA CHICO, but later was very popular in Venezuela and Peru,among other South American countries.(produced by Loklinka and Maxi S.A.)

The game itself was portable with shape of a 80´s old audio cassette, that come with it cassette box. The cassette was displayed in three sections. It was made ​​of metal, for small magnetic chips (usually four, but this varied depending on the game) could adhere securely and not dropped or lost. When not being used, the chips were placed in a small metal plate located on the upper right of the board.

description:
INTRODUCTION AND PURPOSE OF THE GAME

In several parts of the world there are many unsolved mysteries, with the presence of mysterious artifacts, the so-called O.O.P.Art. (Out Of Place Artifacts) and other evidence left by ancient entities.
Scientists from different disciplines and different places in the world, to solve these mysteries, should work together to formulate hypotheses that may initially seem bizarre.
This game is a journey through the O.O.P.Art. and in the mental faculties, to formulate a fantastic hypothesis of solutions to the many still unsolved mysteries. The U.F.O. and the Crop-Circle (Crop circles), represent the last still undeciphered signs to have a possible interpretations.
In the role of "Illuminati" men with particular mental powers, you will have to travel to the world sites where Crop-Circles have appeared and find the Artifacts (oopart), increase your mental energy, discover the key to understanding them to accumulate Knowledge Points.
.... before the "Ancient Astronauts" return to Earth !!!
Man uses only 5% of his mental abilities. What man perceives of the world with his senses is only a small part. Some men are gifted, or have developed particular mental faculties for which they have a more accentuated way of perceiving, Gustavo Adolfo Rol was an enlightened example, and we dedicate this game to him.

INTRODUCTION TO THE GAME
A stormy period of the election to the City Council has just finished. The current model of managing cities has been hugely criticized. Many of the changes that took place in Poland and in the world resulted in the search for new ways of dealing with the problem.

The project CreaCity takes place in a number of big and small places scattered all over the world. Your city has become one of the participants of this great plan aimed at fixing the world.

The project assumes forming a Small Council for one term of office, which will deal with critical issues and those demanding immediate action. Your task will be to take decisions which satisfy the residents and indicate the direction of the development of the city, and at the same time avoid bringing it to bankruptcy.

This experiment may reveal new future leaders, so, even if you do not succeed, this game might be the beginning of something fantastic, both for you and the entire world.

CreaCity is a live action board game, during which players take on the role of councillors during the meetings of the Small City Council.

Each player will receive their character card stating their objectives and the committee they belong to. Each round of the game means very intense 45 minutes, during which committee members will be entrusted with Small and Big Challenges. It is these Challenges that CreaCity will be based on and, depending on the decisions taken by the players, the situation in the city will be changing.

The city is symbolized by a map divided into 8 regions. There are two dice placed in each region: a satisfaction dice (blue) and a dissatisfaction one (red).

Every time players take a binding decision, its effects will be transferred onto the board – satisfaction in the region will change (by changing the values on the dice), general satisfaction in the city and the available budget will change as well, and special buildings will appear.

Players start with a high degree of social trust and their decisions are aimed at gaining the largest general satisfaction possible. Af ter 5 rounds the game ends and the players check who accomplished the greatest number of objectives. Depending on the condition of the city, the entire Small Council receives appropriate number of victory points

GAME OBJECTIVES
For a period of 5 rounds you must not let the city go bankrupt. It happens when:

     on the board, there will be 5 region bankruptcy tags.
     The Dissatisfaction Tag will cross field number 30.


What is more, fight for your personal result and accomplish your objectives.

When you leave the airship for treasure and the captain succeeds in the next leg of the voyage you may shoot your grappling hook and get back on board. Obviously playing this special card means you'll have to leave the treasure from the previous city behind. You can't hang on to a rope without letting go of your heavy load.

Grappling hook:
Can be played by:   a passenger who just left the ship
Can be played when: the captain announces that the journey will continue
Has for effect:     that the passenger who plays this card may climb back on board of the air ship (whilst leaving the last claimed treasure behind)

Similar to Hex, the players race to connect two opposite sides of the board. Each player has 30 small pieces, 1 long piece and 1 extralong piece.

Pieces can be stacked creating bridges, but cannot cross over an opponent piece.

Koth is a two-player, fantasy-themed duel card game in which players assume the roles of the two attendents (Sina or Ames) of the god of war; Koth of the game world, to decide the outcome of a battle between Orcs and Humans.

The game consists of a single deck of 52 cards. It is shared by both players to draw cards. There are 20 different kinds of units in the deck and these units are commanded onto the battlefield. Each unit has an ATTACK and a DEFENSE value. These values are used to dominate (attack) and hold (defend) a column. Some units also have ABILITIES (band, flank, rage, rally, and support) which give units additional skills than these two.

The battlefield is divided into five sections. Each section is called a "column" and players compete to dominate those columns. To dominate a column a unit's ATTACK must be at least equal to the unit's DEFENSE that is holding it.

Players have 10 Command Points (CP) for each round and they can command as many units as they please -provided they don't go over CP. Each unit has a CP cost equal to its ATTACK value.

Players start the game with three cards and draw one card at the start of their turns, and they take turns to command units onto the battlefield.

To win a game, one player must either control all of the five columns. In cases which this is not possible (i.e. when there's no more cards to draw and/or play) the player holding the most columns win the game. And whenever a player wins two consecutive games s/he wins the battle.

The official board game of MasterChef Junior TV Show.
You can play the kids or the family version.
KIDS
Play 5 challenges with the ingredient cards and tiles: the challenges are based on pattern recognition, fast catch, memory and deductive skills.
FAMILY
Create a dish using some ingredient cards drawn from the deck.
The players vote the best and the more original dish, and score points accordingly.

The player with the most points will win and can wear the chef hat!

Give the wrong answer on purpose! In Brain Flip, one player picks a card and the other players try to guess which card it is by asking questions. The twist: every answer given will be the opposite of the truth. Train your brain to do mind bending gymnastics and you will be the winner!

Display a number of cards on the table. Mentally pick one and prepare to answer the players’ questions. The players will try to guess the card you picked by asking questions that have to be answer only by YES or NO. You must always answer the opposite. It looks simple, but it will trick their brains. Don’t get confused! Play smart and find the hidden card! Tally the points to see who is the winner!
