boardgame: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Turtle Power Battle Game
description:
Game description from the publisher:

"Become a Turtle or one of the bad guys and challenge your friends to an exciting duel."

Simple dice-based combat game based on the Nickelodeon cartoon.

Each player starts with ten numbered power cards. On your turn you roll two dice and can take power cards from your opponents equal to the total rolled. In addition, you roll the character die and take a bonus card if it comes up with your character.

When you take a player's last power card, a you roll off with that player in a duel. If you eliminate a player, you take their character card for use in future rounds. The last character standing is the winner.
family: Comic Books: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles

description:
Tank Hunter is a simple yet engaging card game featuring all possible tanks from WW2. The players start with a line-up of hidden tanks and can place them in the front line or the back line. Cards can be flipped to be revealed, and revealed tanks can attack other player's tanks.

Tank Hunter 2e Monster is a standalone game that also serves as an expansion for Tank Hunter.

boardgame: ATS: The Fight For Hue – Apex of The Vietnam War
description:
The Advanced Tobruk System returns to Vietnam with this recreation of the bitter street fighting in the city of Hue, January 31, 1968.
An all new, super detailed map was commissioned for this project, on which tactical-level gamers will battle tooth and nail over The Ming Ca Garrison, The Tay Loc airfield, The Imperial Palace...all the landmarks are there.  A selection of all-new scenarios and all of the combat counters required to play out this epic struggle are included.
Ownership of the ATS Rulebook and standard system markers are required

A hungry panda has escaped the zoo in search of better food and the only way to get him back is to gather the bamboo that he likes best.  That's where you come in!

In Panda Pursuit, each player is a zookeeper trying to lure Panda back to his enclosure and keep him happy.  You'll have to watch what he eats and make sure you stock up on his favorite bamboo to stop him from escaping again.  Meanwhile, a sneaky photographer is also chasing Panda to get the perfect set of photos.  Could Panda secretly want to be a star?

Each turn players will roll custom dice and apply their results to Panda and their Zookeeper or Panda and the Photographer.  When Panda stops on bamboo, he eats it.  When a Zookeper stops on bamboo, he collects it.  And when the Photographer stops in the same row or column as Panda, he snaps a photo.

Race the other zookeepers to the best bamboo and help or hinder the photographer trying to capture Panda in a variety of poses.

Find the right combination of food and photos to keep Panda happy and home in his enclosure in Panda Pursuit.

Skewered and Roasted is an Apples-to-Apples style points based game. There are two decks Events and Memories. Each round, the active player draws a Memory card (which features a situation like "What was my favorite classroom activity") from the deck, then the other players each secretly choose the Event card in hand that best matches that player's history and plays it face-down on the table. The active player then reveals these cards and chooses the Event card that, in their opinion, best matches the Memory card, which he awards to whoever played that Event card. This player earns 1 point, and the active player marker is passed down to the next player.

Once a player has won a number of points, selected from 3 gameplay styles, that player wins.

Battleblade is a skirmish-level fantasy wargame.  Usually using less than 10 figures per side, players pit their party of warriors against their opponent's in one of several objective-driven scenarios.

Battleblade uses the same Command & Cohesion mechanic introduced in Laserblade but shifts the focus more towards close combat as well as adding rules for magical attacks, all driven by the roll of a single D10.

Offering a point-based system for character creation, players are free to create the battling party that best matches their playing style.

With games lasting as little as 20 minutes a time, it's possible to complete a whole campaign in an evening, or to play a quick warmup game before settling down to play your mass-battle game of choice...

This expansion provides 4 new character cards.

Expert Promo Cards:

     Bell: play the game as usual.


This expansion has not been released separately,

This expansion provides 4 kinds of new tile.


     Pirate Smuggling Tiles


During game setting, choose place side A or side B of this tile facing upward on the game board where the pirate icon is marked. Now when executing the pirate smuggling action you will have the additional choice of executing the promo main action.


     Expert Card Discard Pile Tile


During game setting, place the expert card discard pile promo tile beside the expert card deck. Draw 5 expert cards and place face up on the tile to make the expert card discard pile. When executing hiring expert action, player may choose to hire action from the top of the discard pile.When end of game scoring, sort the cards in the discard pile according the type (Phoenix, Tiger, Horse) and calculate the numbers, for every remaining card of the same type that player has, receive honor points equal to the number of cards of the same type in the discard pile.


     Bao-Chao Banknote Tile


Game Accessory: Bao-Chao Banknote, the front have a value of 5 coins, the back is bonus points calculated the end of the game.
During game setting, place Bao-Chao Banknote face up in the coin supply.Anytime when a player receives 5 coins or more, player can choose take a Bao-Chao bank note instead of 5 coins. Player must immediately decide to flip to the back side for scoring and place below the player board. Flipped tiles cannot be used as 5 coins.
1.Receive 2 honor points for every 3 coins
2.Receive 2 honor points for every gold
3.Receive 2 honor points for every 2 basic resource of any kind.


     Calculation goods Tile


Player can calculate the quantity of goods more.

boardgame: Star Wars: X-Wing Miniatures Game – TIE Striker Expansion Pack
description:
Game description from the publisher:

The sleek and agile TIE striker was developed primarily by Imperial forces stationed on planetary installations, where its adaptive ailerons and potent weaponry made it an excellent safeguard against Rebel incursions. The TIE striker and its adaptive ailerons come to life with the TIE Striker Expansion Pack for X-Wing. It contains one highly detailed and pre-painted miniature TIE striker, along with six ship cards and three upgrades. These reflect the ship’s exceptional atmospheric maneuverability, as well as its use in Imperial swarm tactics.
family: Game: Star Wars X-Wing Miniatures Game

boardgame: Star Wars: X-Wing Miniatures Game – U-Wing Expansion Pack
description:
Game description from the publisher:

The Rebel Alliance used the U-wing to deploy troops under the cover of darkness or into the midst of dangerous situations. You can use the U-wing Expansion Pack for X-Wing to transport Jyn Erso and the other heroes of Rogue One: A Star Wars Story straight to your tabletop. It features one highly detailed and pre-painted miniature U-wing with articulated wings that can pivot back and forth, along with four ship cards and fourteen upgrades. Conduct espionage. Promote Rebel bravery. Pivot your ship’s wings to real effect. The U-wing Expansion Pack makes it all possible.
family: Game: Star Wars X-Wing Miniatures Game

description:
Soccer: The Board Game is a fast-paced 2-player sports board game that captures the thrill and excitement of soccer.  You, the Coach, can lead your team to victory by using real soccer practice and strategies that give your team more chances to score. This board game is pure competitive soccer fun.  Challenge someone to a friendly match or start a tournament and take your team to the top!

In this game, each player controls opposing teams.  Movement is determined by rolling a die and moving the plans and ball a total number of spaces as determined by the die roll, all within 30 seconds.  When a player can attempt a goal, the players determine if it is successful by having a roll off.  Penalties are resolved with cards.  The game has an included timer so the turns can be kept to 30 seconds and the game to 30 minutes overall.

boardgame: Sexy Red Flags: A Seductive Expansion to Red Flags
description:
Sexy Red Flags adds 75 sexually explicit, but definitely desirable perks to your game.

Here are some heavily censored examples:
Always brings a friend to bed.
Having [CENSORED] with them is equivalent to eight hours of sleep.
[CENSORED] can grow or tighten at will.

This deck isn’t for the prudish or easily offended. We apologize in advance for the awkward situations you're about to put your friends in.

boardgame: Nerdy Red Flags: A Geeky Expansion to Red Flags
description:
Nerdy Red Flags adds 75 nerdy perks and Red Flags to your game.

Here are some examples:
Owns real Pokemon
Is your favorite Doctor
Looks exactly like Jabba the Hutt

This deck adds all kinds of nerdy goodness to your game, and has sources all across the popular and classic sci-fi, fantasy, and video-game worlds.

The Mission: Costa Rica Starter Set comes in a cigar box made from recycled paper. Includes an oversized playmat with an eco-map on the back, 43 cards (4 Endangered, 14 Rare, 23 Common, two limited-edition sketch cards) for a 2-player game, giant learn-to-play cards, bonus CD, and a rulebook Xeko cards are printed on recycled paper with eco-friendly inks. Xeko donates 4% of game net sales to Conservation International.

Additional Mission: Costa Rica cards could be purchased in 9-card booster packs from displays. Mission: Costa Rica added a total of 96 new cards.

Outlawyered is a law based card game that puts players in the the suit and lawsuit of an attorney. Each player is given three clients and must do what they can to be the first to win 5 trials.

Players must use their given Briefcase cards to play various types of Evidencezxc (Ex. Expert Witness and Mistress' Testimony) and Lawyer Tools (Ex. Objections and Outlawyered cards)in order to prevail. Each round sees one player's client get sued by the deck. The player must fight off opposing players to secure their client's freedom, and a trial win. But be careful, just because you win at the trial level does not guarantee a victory at the Super Ultimate Court!!!!

To win at trial the player must keep their client's Trial Score above the designated Trial Card's Conviction Score. Evidence cards can either decrease or increase the Trial Score while Lawyer Tools can dramaticallyasd>< impact the game in various ways, including negating a strong piece of evidence or even changing out which player is on trial.

No legal experience is needed nor will any be gained by playing.

boardgame: Quartermaster General: Alternative Histories – Draft Cards
description:
A set of six cards sent to backers of the 2016 Kickstarter campaign for Alternate Histories, A Quartermaster General Expansion.

Consisting of six cards:

MERCHANT MARINES!

Use once per game during your play step. Instead of playing a cards, discard a card from your hand and build a Navy.

PRESS ON!

Use once per game when you battle a Land space. Discard a card from your hand ; then take a Land Battle card from your discard pile and add it to your hand.

INTERCEPTORS!

Use once per game when you are the target of Economic Warfare. Subtract 2 from the cards required to be discarded.

REGROUP!

Use once per game when your Army is battled. Discard a card from your hand; then take a Build Army card from your discard pile and add it to your hand.

SPARE CAPACITY!

Use once per game. You may Reallocate Resources without having to discard any cards.

FORAGE!

Use once per game during your Supply Step. Do not remove a piece that is out of supply.

boardgame: In the Trenches: Through Fire and Flames
description:
Game description from the publisher:

Through Fire and Flames is BY FAR the largest battle pack Tiny Battle Publishing has ever released for its popular In the Trenches system. The third battle pack for In the Trenches, Through Fire and Flames brings FIVE new battle maps and SEVEN scenarios to the table, including Whittlesey’s Brave, The Lost Battalion, The Kaiserschlacht, the first tank on tank battle, and more. The seven historical scenarios include the British, French, Germans, Americans and let’s not forget, the Bulgarians!  The game’s namesake is born from the German flamethrowers that are added, raising the corrugated lethality of the counter mix. Battle with Schneider, MKIV, and A7V tanks. Bombard enemy positions with mortars and artillery, rake the advancing infantry with machine guns, and infiltrate enemy lines with Stosstruppen.

Designed by John Gorkowski, In the Trenches enables players to game historically accurate simulations of tactical operations from the Great War. ITT challenges players to move and fight with company-level formations made of separate platoon-sized units during five minute, impulse-based turns. The historically accurate maps are scaled to 100 yards per hex. This approach recreates WWI command and control problems and inspires historically accurate play without forcing decisions.
family: Series: In the Trenches (Tiny Battle Publishing)

description:
Game description from the publisher:

Version 2 of the Platoon Commander series, with improved art and thicker counters, Tiny Battles introduces India-Pakistan War 1965. India-Pakistan War is a Platoon Commander module simulating the conflict during August and September of 1965 between India and Pakistan in the Chawinda region of Kashmir.  The module designer, David K. van Hoose; has brought to life some of the largest armor battles since the Battle of Kursk. The module includes five scenarios that will take the gamer from small skirmishes to the slugfests that produced what has become known as the Grave Yard of Tanks. Fight with Chieftains, Easy Eight Shermans, Pattons, and AMX-13 tanks, insert Guerillas behind the enemy’s lines, and call devastating artillery support. The historically accurate forces are at your command. Can you lead them to victory?

Platoon Commander is a Mark H. Walker designed, platoon-level game system depicting armored combat from 1936 into the near future. The counters represent everything from tanks and infantry to armored personnel carriers and, as the technology progresses, even missile teams. The game uses an innovative sequence of play; a card-assisted system that is partially impulse driven, partially turn-based, and all fun. Morale plays a decisive role, and the period’s equipment is understandably depicted with colors and numbers on each counter. From the designer of Lock ‘n Load, World at War, and Night of Man, Platoon Commander is an exciting, accessible game of strategy and tactics.
family: Series: Mark H. Walker's Platoon Commander

boardgame: Slush Fund 2: Secret Powers Promo Pack
description:
This is a special item that is only available to backers and members of Dr. Finn's Kickstarter Club (which is only possible for backers of this project or a future Dr. Finn's project). The promo pack will not be available in the retail version nor will it be distributed to retail stores.

It consists of three cards. Each card provides a special power to be used once during the game. Each player is given one randomly at the start and the card stays secret until use.  The three promo cards include:

FBI - Prevent opponent from using Slush Fund for 1 turn

Windfall - Draw 3 more cards, return 3 to the top of deck, and then take your turn

Encryption - Place on a politician to prevent hacking

Randomly choose the Event cards based on player count and add them to the deck per the instructions. They take effect when they are drawn from the top of the draw deck.

These promotional cards will not be available in the retail version of the first edition of the game. It is possible that they will be available during future Kickstarter campaigns for Dr. Finn's Games and, in rare cases, at some special promotional events.

Based on the popular french children series Wickie The Viking.

A set-collection game which used a curved mirror mounted on a 3d game mat. Looking at the reflection of tokens on the mirror players have to decide whether take or not the various tokens.

For 2-4 players.

boardgame: Imperial Settlers: The Dice Tower Inn
description:
Dice Tower 2016 Kickstarter campaign promo tile:

"During the Production Phase, place 1 (wood) and 1 (stone) on this tile.
All players may activate this Location once per turn:
Action: Spend 1 (population), name a color, and discard the top card of the common Deck. If the color matches your choice, take all Goods from this card and add them to your supply."

Hogswallow is an open ended, mythical storytelling game for 2 to 6 players. Players use the primitive imagery on the cards to guide the creation of a cohesive tale. The glyphs on the cards do not have any fixed meanings. They are purposefully pliable, designed to jump start the imagination, and may be interpreted differently from player to player and game to game.

Hogswallow is wonderfully adaptable and suitable for any age and any group, as the stories told are created completely by the participants. The rules provide the three most common variations of play, including one cooperative method, and two competitive ways to play.

In the cooperative version "High Hogswallow", the players take turns adding an element to the story, building off what has come before until one of them wins by bringing the story to  satisfactory conclusion.

In the competitive versions, players play only with their own hand of cards to either trump the single card that was played before them in a storytelling battle (Dilly Dallying) or to tell the best story using a limited hand of cards, argue it's merits compared to the other stories, and ultimately vote on whose was the best (Low Hogswallow).

In Priate Theory (Pirate Theory with a deliberate misspelling), players are pirate captains dividing resources and plundering ships and treasures. At the end of game, the player with most treasures becomes the Pirate King.

In each round, players are going to divide several cards. One player make a suggestion of some cards that they should take as their share. Each other player can agree or disagree. The player who disagrees must cut the share, making it smaller. The TRICKY part is that the one who takes the share is the player who makes the last cut, not the player who makes the suggestion at first.

How do you get the cards you really want? How to cut a share with above average value and make others hard to cut? These problems are all parts of Priate Theory.

•••

在《海盗大会》中，玩家扮演各大海盗船长瓜分物资、劫掠船只获得宝藏。游戏结束时获得宝藏最多的玩家成为海贼之王。
每一轮将有一些牌供玩家们瓜分。一名玩家提出自己想要的一份份额，其他玩家可以同意或不同意他的提案。不同意的玩家必须将这一份方案切小一些。有意思的是，方案中的牌将由最后做出切分的玩家而不是最初提出方案的玩家获得。
如何顺利得到自己想要的牌而不被人切走，如何切分大于平均价值的份额却使其他人难以下手，都成了一门学问。

Back in 1400 Europe had other borders. Hosts in each country are located and begins the conquest. Can you take possession of other countries and make you strong and powerful?

Purpose of the game:
Fulfill the mission contained in Secret Mission card.

Preparation:

     Each player chooses the color of his army.
     Distribute the 20 territory cards.
     Submit a Secret Mission card to each player.
     Place the armies spread across territories.


Turn:

     Gaining reinforcements.
     Choosing a contact territory to attack the enemy with dice.
     Removing the destroyed armies and moving the conquerors.



(Game description in Spanish)

Allá por el año 1400 Europa tenía otras fronteras. Se sitúan los ejércitos en cada país y empieza la conquista. ¿Podrás apoderarte de otros países y hacerte fuerte y poderoso?

Finalidad del juego:
Cumplir la misión que figura en la carta de Objetivo.

Preparación:

     Elegir cada jugador el color de su ejército.
     Repartir las 20 cartas de países.
     Entregar una carta de Objetivo en secreto a cada jugador.
     Colocar los ejércitos repartidos en los países.


Turno:

     Ganar refuerzos.
     Escoger un país enemigo en contacto y atacar con los dados.
     Retirar los ejércitos destruidos y mover a los conquistadores.

Description from the publisher:

Ferali is a disk-flicking game system that comes with nine different game variants that can be played on a octagonal game board with different disk types that come with the game. 
The variants are described in more detail below.


    Rainbow: pocket seven disks in the colors of the rainbow in the correct order (violet, indigo, blue, green, yellow, orange, red). The first player to pocket on set of all seven colours wins the game.



    Race to 16: this game uses red and black numbered disks. The first player to pocket exactly 16 points wins the game.



    Poker: each player gets a set of cards and has to pocket the disks with the same numbers as the cards. The player to do this first wins the game.



    Snooker: use to the Ferali game table to play a variant of Snooker using numbered disks.



    Zero is hero: similar to Race to 16. But now a score of zero has to be achieved by adding and subtracting the numbers on the disks.



    Word builder: this game uses disks with characters. Each player pockets disks, writes down the letters, then returns them to the board. Players compete for a predetermined period of time, then they make as many words as possible with their noted letters. Whoever has created the most words wins.



    Cricket: using red and black disks, a cricket game is played on the Ferali board.

Iron League:Black Stadium is a brutal tactical game utilizing Mechs, miniatures and interactive scenery for action-packed game play.

Configure your team with giant machines called Iron Mechs with interchangeable arms and weapons and warriors: the hordes of Claws, the heavy Zimman, your mechanic to repair your mechs. You can recruit mercenaries on your team.

Use drums full of gas in your favor, use walls to protect your warriors and be careful of fire squares. Throw the enemy into the air, or throw them at burning drum. Shoot down the Iron Mech pilots and steal their mechs, or destroy them.

With 4 game Modes: Match, Death Match, Arena and Cooperative. Nuke Tracker is a Expansion full Co-op supplementary to Iron League:Black Stadium.

Match: a tactical and ferocious sports game. Your team can be repaired after each goal.

Death Match: a competitive, destructive combat, if you survive you win. If your team dies, they cannot regenerate.

Arena: Inspired by MOBA style video games, if you destroy 2 enemy black holes or steal your enemy's plasma ball, you win. If your team dies, they can regenerate.

Coop: Survive waves of attacking enemies.

The game consists of 4 teams.

Each team contains 3 Iron Mechs, 3 Claws , 1 Zimman Warrior and 1 Mechanic. Iron Mechs are controlled by pilots with special abilities, but can be taken over by any warrior (mechanics are not warriors). If a warrior takes control of an Iron Mech, he loses his abilities, armor, and special weapons. So, he becomes a pilot but with limitations. There are also 4 mercenaries that can be hired during the game.

boardgame: Thing Two and Thing One Whirly Fun! Game
description:
Thing One and Thing Two are running around the house making messes! Work together to clean up the house before Mother gets home. If everything is tidy when Mother comes in the door, you all win!

This cooperative game is played inside the box, in which a small model of the house from The Cat in the Hat is built. Through random card draws, the house is messed up by the Things (a spinning top) and re-cleaned by the players.

25th anniversary edition of the famous Trivial Pursuit: A new Disney Family edition with many many questions from Fantasia, Mary Poppins, Snow White and the seven dwarfs, Prince Caspian, Wall-E and many more. Every card has a beautiful picture of the corresponding movie.

The questions vary from easy to difficult and the game contains a new feature; action cards.

Kelsier’s revolution has succeeded. The Lord Ruler is gone. Your story is just beginning...

Following the Lord Ruler’s downfall in Mistborn: The Final Empire, the surviving noble Houses face a brave new world filled with great opportunity and even greater danger. With the empire in disarray and the populace liberated from the Steel Ministry’s iron grip, the nobility is free to pursue new agendas and ancient vendettas.

Protecting the capital is the provisional government of King Elend Venture, scion of House Venture and beloved of Vin, a powerful skaa Mistborn. Together with the survivors of Kelsier’s crew, Elend and the Luthadel Assembly spearhead a fledgling republic led by commoners and noblemen alike. They are supported by lesser Houses like Penrod and the Church of the Survivor — skaa faithful revering Kelsier as a god — and the people are taking their first wobbly steps toward self-rule.

Outside the city walls, threats abound. Minor kingdoms squabble and divide the empire into lots. Bandits terrorize the countryside. Hulking koloss — devastating engines of fury and destruction now freed from the Lord Ruler’s control — wantonly lay waste to villages and towns. Amid the chaos, Houses Cett, Lekal, and Venture amass armies to impose order and seize control of this new world. These Houses now march on Luthadel, their eyes set upon the crown jewel of the Empire and its trove of the precious Atium.

Beyond this mounting conflict, strange supernatural forces are stirring. The mists that dominate the night now come during the day as well. Whole villages fall to a strange sickness, and fearful rumors persist of a spirit wandering the mist. It’s up to Vin, the scholar Sazed, and their allies to learn why this is happening — and save the world before it all falls apart.

In The Siege of Luthadel, the noble Houses that survived the Lord Ruler’s fall are rebuilding and addressing the myriad Problems of the aftermath to curry the Favor of the people. In this desperate time, a new and bitter rift has developed: should a new Lord Ruler rise to power, or should the people determine their own fate? This disagreement leads to a military conflict between the Houses, and a siege of the capital city of Luthadel.

Through their actions, the leader of each House supports either the Attackers at the city walls, or the city’s beleaguered Defenders. Their decisions win the Allegiance of those who believe in their cause, and the Impact of their choices will determine which faction is victorious.

Key to this burgeoning war is control of the Koloss, a terrifying horde of savage brutes Rampaging through the countryside, massing their Spoils of war as they march towards the capital…

Vin’s journey to discover the truth of the mists terrorizing the world, and the truth of the Hero of Ages, becomes a race against time as the inevitable Battle of Luthadel approaches. Will she rescue the city before it collapses beneath the weight of the siege?

This is where you come in…

After several centuries sleeping, the giants Monta and Monty have awakened. To their surprise, the beautiful
valley that they enjoyed looking at is now a big city, which has brought foul smells, pollution, and too much noise
even for two angry giants. Their first visit to the big city is going to be rough.

The game is divided into 2 phases: The “Build the city” phase and the “Destroy the city” phase. During the “Build the city” phase, players will build a city by playing their city tiles to create the map for Monty and Monta to roam about. During the “Destroy the city” phase, the players will take turns to move the giants and will play cards to try to destroy as many city tiles as possible. But be careful, if you forget which ones are your own player tiles, you could end up destroying them and losing points!

Introduces Veteran game rules and Hero Cards to Epic Roll. Consists of Veteran Rulebook and three Hero Cards (Dara, Greff, and Orric).

Each Hero gains a unique Passive Ability and an Activated Ability which can be used during opponents' turns. Each player's maximum hand size is increased to three cards, and defeating the toughest monsters (Skeleton and Banshee) reward players with an additional treasure card. Players also gain the ability to play treasure cards in pairs to achieve 'Focused Effects'.

Packs are included with game purchases, and available for free at shows/events. The components are also available for download from EpicRoll.com

A spin-off of the cute and casual card game Tiny Swords, Tactics is a completely new game. Flip, slide, and toss other player's tiles away to be the last one standing.

Keep your tiles close to the Heart of the Battle, and toss your foes away. If a tile is disconnected from all other tiles, that player has until the end of their turn to get it back to the battle or risk losing it forever! Tiles that are face-up can be pushed and tossed, while face-down tiles can act as walls to contain the ever-increasing chaos. Every time you toss a tile, they accumulate more Toss Tokens and get flung further each time, pushing any face-up tiles in their path!

On top of that, each team has their own Captain with unique powers. Utilize each Captain's ability to turn the tide of battle and knock the other players off of your actual table. The whole table is your stage!
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Classic game of Monopoly with a geek twist.

Publisher's description:

ALL HAIL THE GEEK!

Although it may not be good for their skin, geeks are enjoying a day in the sun. A seismic shift has taken place in our culture and geeks are moving toward world domination. Once a disparaging term, geek is now most often used to indicate someone of higher intellect with superior knowledge of a subject. It's a salute to genius!

Celebrate Geekatude with Geek-opoly. Gather a group of like minded individuals who are up to code and advance to Go Geek! Buy your favorite geek chic properties and up the rent with Gigabytes and Terabytes. It's all fun and games until you're sent to Internet Down, and you're out for three turns as other players roll on to Geekdom.

Who knows? You may soon be elected Alpha Geek... or you may be busted for trolling the Internet. Whatever happens... be eccentric, enthusiastic, and intelligent, you know... a geek.
publishers: Late for the Sky Production Company

description:
Game description from the designer:

Bomb Arena is a party game. Friends can team up to fight against each other. Players work together to put bombs to rival's field and do their best igniting them.  Boom!!

It's fun and exciting to use bomb cards and accumulate explosion power with skill cards and team member's help.  On the one side, you have to do your best to deal damage to your opponent's team, on the other side, you have to rack your brains removing the bombs in your field.

Players of Bomb Arena usually love to have one more match, two more matches right after the 1st game because it is really fun.
Players keep mesmerized when they play Bomb Arena because every player's movement would affect each other dramatically.  You would be excited during the whole game.

HOw to ignite bombs?

There are 3 ways to ignite bombs devil
1. 11 kings of Bomb Cards : No.2 ~ No. 12
We deal 3 bomb cards and 2 skill cards to each player.
Each player take turns placing one bomb card on enemy's field.
When players work together and put 5 bomb cards in a row on enemy's
field, !!BOOM!! It will deal 2 damage to enemy's team.

for example, when you and your team member put
No.2, No.3, No4, No5, No.6 bomb cards on your enemy's field, it explodes.

2. Skill card: Time Bomb
A player can put a time bomb and attache it on a Bomb on enemy's field.

for example, a player can put time bomb on No.7 Bomb card which is already on the filed. then every next player needs to throw 2 dice to
check if it will explode or not. Every player needs to throw 2 dice during the beginning of his turn until the time bomb explodes.

3. When 3 bomb cards of the same number are piled up.
the 3 bomb cards explode immediately can cause 1 point damage.

Players can use counterattack, remove, provoke, designate or other skill cards to make the game more interesting.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Ed, the Cat: Wer fährt seine Krallen aus?
description:
Ed the cat, Wer fährt seine Krallen aus? is an easy to learn short little card game. The object of the game is to get rid of your hand cards faster than your opponents and collecting as few points as possible. On your turn simply play one of your cards, if one player manages to get rid of all his hand cards at the end of his turn the current round ends and you start a new one. If one player collected 99 or more points at the end of a round the game ends and the player with the fewest points wins.

Game description from the back of the box:

Dieses Kartenspiel hat es in sich:
Es ist so einfach wie Mau-Mau - und doch so listig wie ein Miezekätzchen.
Wer verpasst seinen Mitspielern die meisten Strafpunkte, ohne selbst welche zu bekommen?
Und wer wird als Ersters seine Karten los?

boardgame: A Game for Good Christians: The Blessings of God – A Kinder, Gentler Expansion Deck!
description:
This expansion deck is comprised of Biblical blessings (and curses on other people which, let’s be honest, are blessings). It includes promises such as

The grace of God offering salvation to all people. (Titus 2:11)  
God holding your enemies’ heads under the water until the bubbles stop. (Exodus 15:1-12;19),  and
All the sex! (Genesis 1:28)  
This expansion comes with 54 Cards: 12 Creed & 42 Canon.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: A Game for Good Christians: The Red Letter Deck – The Words and Actions of Jesus the Christ
description:
This expanded expansion deck is based on the words and actions of our Lord and savior, Jesus the Christ including

"That time Jesus was a racist" (Matthew 15:21-28)

"Fishing for men" (Matthew 4:19)

"Demon-castin’, tongues-speakin’, snake-handlin’, poison-drinkin,’ hand-layin’-healin' in the name of Jesus" (Mark 16:17-18)

and of course

"God so loving the world that he gave his only Son, so that everyone who believes in him may not perish but may have eternal life."  (John 3:16)

This BIGGER deck comes with 70 Cards (20 Creed & 52 Canon) in a custom tuck box.

–description from the publisher

boardgame: A Game for Good Christians: Things That Make The Baby Jesus Cry
description:
THIS NEW UN-THEMED EXPANSION DECK INCLUDES SUCH FONDLY REMEMBERED BIBLICAL MOMENTS AS:

A present of golden mice and hemorrhoids sent as an offering to the Lord. (1 Samuel 6:1-18)

Sarah wondering if she'll ever be "abundantly wet" again. (Genesis 18:12)

The Lord shaving your pubic hair. (Isaiah 7:20)

THIS EXPANSION COMES WITH 54 CARDS: 12 CREED & 42 CANON.

—description from the publisher

9-Masu Shogi is played on a tiny three-by-three square board using only eight of the original Shogi’s types of pieces.

Movements and the goal of checkmate remain, but unlike regular Shogi there are 40 different starting positions to choose from.

boardgame: Réunion & Mauritius (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride)
description:
This follows the principles of the basic Ticket to Ride game on a special board with special cards and rules. The object of the game is to gain the most points by travelling various routes and outwitting other players. You will need the train pieces and the train cards from either the US or Europe base games (or you can buy them from the Days of Wonder shop.

There is an extra set of Tourist cards for the cities, each with a value to be earned by visiting that city. These cards are dealt/picked up alongwith tickets in the same quantity and with the same 'keep/dispose' rules.There are also up to 9 bonuses for the end-of-game scoring.

The board does not have any grey tracks or parallel tracks - but there are spanners in the works which may help (or hinder) you.

There are many new options in the rules: exchange all your cards at the start but keep all the new ones: discard 5 cards and pick up 5 new ones, as your turn: earn extra points: and many more. You can also call time when your wagon stock reaches 3, if you want, giving you the advantage of stopping other players completing tickets.

The Terrible Monster: Desperation expansion consists of twelve cards to add to your game of Terrible Monster. The additional monster and spells can just be mixed into the original game. The new heroes, however, are a brand new gameplay feature of this expansion; instead of playing a card, a player can choose to use the ability of their hero. These abilities cannot be countered by counter tokens!

Players travel a path from the top of the board to the bottom by playing cards.  Cards can also be used to impede opponents.

Cards either have a number (1-5) of squares to move in one direction, a "star" that lets a player advance to a corner or fork, a "circled star" that allows a player to move another player's piece to a star square, or an "X" that forces a player to skip a turn.

Jungle is the name given to the first expansion of new cards and Theme Decks for the Pokémon Trading Card Game. Are you ready to explore the jungle? If you look hard, you`ll find 48 new Pokemon Trading Card Game cards in the exciting Jungle expansion for the Pokemon trading card game.

----

Collect all the Jungle cards and you`ll discover awesome new holographic cards with super attacks - a must for every Pokemon collector! Each pre-constructed theme deck contains 60 cards, a cardboard holographic foil Vileplume coin, 10 damage counters, and an english rulebook.

Fossil (Japanese: Mystery of the Fossils) is the name given to the third expansion of cards and Theme Decks for the Pokémon Trading Card Game in both America and Japan. The set's booster pack designs feature Lapras, Kabutops, and Zapdos.


With this Pokemon Fossil Lockdown Pre-Constructed Theme Deck comes with 60 mint Pokemon collectible trading cards. You also a cardboard holographic foil Aerodactyl coin, 10 damage counters, and an english rulebook.
Team Rocket (Japanese: Rocket Gang) is the name given to an expansion of the Pokémon Trading Card Game. As its name implies, it was themed around the exploits of Team Rocket.

This game is an homage to the great designer Alex Randolph. Basically, it is Ricochet Robots made with a postcard. A player throws a postcard at the center of a table, all the players try to figure out what is the minimum amount of overturn on its side the postcard must do to touch the edge of the table, the player that said the smallest number must check the claim. If he/she was right the player cuts the first border of the postcard (see the image) and keep it as a token of his/her points. Otherwise the player cuts the border and crumple it up as a negative point. the postcard is now more irregular and smaller, therefore is harder to guess right. If a player collect 2 crumpled up tokens is out of the game, the game end when everybody is eliminated or the card cannot be cut anymore (end of the dotted lines. The survived player in the first case or the player with the most stars in their token in the second case is the winner.

At a time when people were once again yearning for natural diversity and healthy food, they decided to leave behind the era of monocultural wastelands, poisoned fields and industrially produced nutrients, and to give everyone a small piece of land to encourage self-sufficiency. To make the best use of the land, the smartest among them resorted to a cultivation method that incorporates the dimension of height and creates naturally grown floors: agroforestry.
And so the forest gardeners were born. The players assume this role and are faced with the task of transforming their fallow land into a productive garden of culinary trees and shrubs.
When selecting woody plants, they must take into account the light and space requirements of the plants, as well as the timing of their flowering and fruit maturity.
A garden that provides leafy vegetables, colourful flowers or diverse fruits can also be beneficial in this regard.
It is also important whether one or the other specimen needs a partner for fertilization, because otherwise the desired fruit harvest will not come...
Whoever has put together the most valuable forest garden at the end wins.

Gaining the right cards, and placing or discarding them at the right time will lead to your horticultural success!

A card game of courtly politics and intrigue
His Royal Majesty is dying. Stricken with sudden illness our king failed to anoint a successor. Now a ruthless game begins between his sons, daughters and bastards.

Outmaneuver your rivals.
Gain support of key figures at the court.
Become the next king.

In Interregnum, 3-5 players strive to build a coterie of influential nobles, courtiers and soldiers who will support their claim to the throne. Important members of the court can help persuade others to join your cause, but even lowly servants often hear rumours that you may use as leverage.
Other contenders will use whatever influence they have to stop you, so rotten compromises and short-term alliances will be necessary on the way.

On each of his turns the active player chooses one character card he wishes to recruit and one or more cards that strengthen the bid. Other players may then decide to forfeit cards from their hands in order to block the recruitment.

On a successful recruitment the character (card) stays in play and provides ongoing benefits as well as Power (VP) to the owner.

Once a player gathers enough Power he or she may proclaim victory.

Only if you play your cards right and outsmart the competition - the crown will be yours.

boardgame: Above and Below: Watch It Played Building Promo Card
description:
A promotional card given out during the 2016 Watch It Played Indiegogo campaign.

This promo introduces a new Building Card that you can use in the game.  The building card enables you to receive a cider, once per round, when you do a harvest action. At the end of the game, the card is worth one Victory Point, when the card is added to your village.

Pep McDonald and Rodney Smith are depicted on the card, with the Watch It Played logos on barrels.

boardgame: Mutiny: A Pirate's Jolly Game of Dice and Back-Stabbing
description:
In Mutiny: A Pirate's Jolly Game of Dice and Backstabbing, players take on the roles of 2-6 pirates, trying to horde gold while attempting to escape a steadily sinking ship. In this case, the captain may not go down with the ship, but you can bet everyone else will!

Mutiny was introduced in concept during the 2014 rpggeek.com VirtuaCon digital convention event. At that time it was called "Pirates of the Sinking Ship".

The rules for Mutiny are simple: Using five, or perhaps six, standard dice, players roll for values of 3-5, hoping to amass wealth, 6's to board the lifeboat on which the gold is stored, while attempting to avoid 1's and 2's, which sink the ship.

When the ship reaches a water level of 20 fathoms, the ship sinks, with all aboard except whichever clever pirate has snuck aboard the lifeboat and made away with the treasure.

This game also includes Rum cards, which can be spent to gain initiative to roll dice. However, when a player's number is rolled, his rum card must be spent. Therefore, all players will receive an opportunity to roll the dice (and hopefully board the lifeboat) at least once.

Mutiny is played over a course of 3 separate games. Player character abilities shift each game, and are related to numbers also used to initiate a player's turn.

1) When this player is aboard the lifeboat, 1's rolled increase the water level by 2 fathoms.

2) This player may reroll any 2's rolled during his turn.

3) This player may keep his Rum card until he chooses to spend it to take his turn.

4) This player adds 2 gold to the lifeboat whenever he rolls a 4.

5) This player acquires 1 gold for his own personal pocket whenever anyone rolls his number during turn initiation. (The gold is added to his score at game end, provided he has won at least one of the three games.)

6) This player rolls six dice, instead of 5.

May the best backstabbing mutineer win!

Pakjesavond is a micro game about Sinterklaas and the missing presents. Up to five players try to find the location of these presents using the powers of the Pieten (played by children in the images) and Sinterklaas himself. The player who finds most presents by the end of the game is considered to be the best helper by Sinterklaas and wins the game.

All the ants want to return to their home in Cross Roads, so they jostle back and forth among  the other crawling critters, but the way home isn't easy because if a strong scent crosses your path, you'll fall back and lose valuable time.

Game description from the publisher:

This (2016) is a very special year  for GRANNA - we are celebrating our 25th anniversary! Throughout all this time we have tried to provide all of you with the best games - the kind we would like to play ourselves. We would like  you to celebrate  this very special anniversary together with us. For this occasion we have prepared a little surprise. Open the box and enter the land of good games. Thank you for being with us!

The game 25 consists of a deck of 54 double-faced cards. They show pieces of the covers of 6 games published by Granna. Each cover picture is divided into 6 pieces. Each card's 2 sides show pieces from the same position of 2 different cover pictures. Each specific piece of a cover appears on 3 different cards in the deck. In a 2-player game, the goal is to make 2 complete pictures on the table. With 3 and 4 players, the goal is to make 1 complete picture on the table and collect 1 piece each of the 5 other pictures.

Apokalypse was a miniature wargame set in a dystopian future, the aftermath of the final war. It simulated the desperate skirmishes, firefights and encounters taking place in the last 120 days of humanity. The players took the role of powermongers, leaders and warlords of what armed forces still existing in the postapocalyptic wasteland, using remaining survivors as tools to enforce their agenda.

It was stated Apokalypse used 25mm miniatures but since that scale later came to be known as (non-heroic) 28mm you could generally say that 28mm is the default standard for the game. The length measure unit, "meters", was intentionally meant for being adapted to whatever scale of minis you had access to, making the game playable with all scales of miniatures.

When Hardcore Media was liquidated, around 1998-99, the game faded into obscurity and very much stayed that way.
In 2016 it was made available online, as a freeware document with the creators' consent, through a fan-made PDF of the original rulebook (available in the Web Links section).

There were occasional references to expansions throughout the core rulebook. The material for all of these was sadly destroyed when Hardcore Media folded and its creators moved on to different projects. Despite this, Apokalypse was a fully finished product and contains all the rules and information needed to play.

boardgame: Het Stratégisch Spel, Of Militaire Uitspanning
description:
Wargame published in the Netherlands and Germany in 1819 for teaching military strategy. It was sold in a wooden box containing a rulebook, 2 dice, 52 rectangular wooden playing pieces representing military units, and an 18.5 inches square topographic map.

Units move from crossroad to crossroad on the point-to-point map. Terrain affects unit movement and combat, and players also need to use their mobile magazines to provide supply for their army. The goal of the game is to capture the enemy capital. A player can also win by surrounding or eliminating a large enough portion of the enemy army.

Combat is mostly deterministic with rules for how many of different types of units are required to successfully attack a position, modified by map terrain, but players under some circumstances roll the dice to resolve combats.
publishers: De Weduwe J. Allart en Comp

description:
Game description from the publisher:

Scratch Wars is close to a collectible game, but in certain game modes it's closer to a board game and can be also played online through a phone application for iOS and Android.

In more detail, to start playing Scratch Wars you need only two cards: one hero and one weapon. The cards are unique in several aspects. You can scratch them like lottery tickets, but by doing so you are not destroying or devaluing them. Each hero card is unique; each hero has a unique name and value generated. No one else in the world will have the same card! Slowly uncover the card by scratching it and in combination with unique values, it is pleasantly thrilling. Cards can even be bought individually. (Would you rather have a hero or a weapon?)

With an increasing number of weapon cards, you can try a sophisticated one-on-one duel in which you equip your hero with up to five weapons, then think strategically to find out which one to use when. The duel itself is very interesting; you spin a special circular weapon: turntable paper on a stick. The hero has certain levels of energy which you spend using various weapons. Heroes can change weapons during the turn and their proper use is the focus of the game. The game is risk management; you are trying to spend the hero's energy for the best possible effects. According to the composition of effects, you choose weapon, strategy and risk. You can attack the opponent's weapon, his permanent effects, or the hero directly. You have to decide when to repair a weapon, when to save energy for the hit with more powerful weapons, etc.

Once you get into the basic game, you can try a more comprehensive and strategic mode in which teams of heroes compete against each other. Then you can move to the top mode where entire armies of heroes collide. With more than two players, it is possible to play in teams or each player plays by themselves with modified rules. The game is perfect for tournaments.

If you want to play with just one player, simply scan the card into your phone and you can play online. There is a varied range of options at your disposal, from tournaments and hero trainings to territorial fights. The goal is to level your hero up and obtain spells for him. As the first game in the world, Scratch Wars presents digital ownership so that what you have physically, you also have digitally. If you later want to replace or sell any of your heroes, you can change them digitally with all that is recorded on the card, i.e., with all the spells and experiences.

If you would rather play the game more as a board game and add more strategic options, you will be able to expand the game with "trade" cards or hero's "abilities".

Game description from the publisher:

Flipped Off is a tactical card game where you play a plotting Mastermind aiming to defeat your rivals and seize control of the city for yourself. You must manage your Minions - the Pirates, Ninjas, and Robots that do your bidding, play actions that can improve your situation or wreak havoc with your enemies, and use your Minions to launch attacks and hope that your rivals can’t turn the cascade of results to their own advantage.

Each player has a set of Minion cards that they can use to attack other players, and a set of Action cards in their hand that they can use to affect either their own minions, or other player's minions. The objective of the game is to use your Minions and Actions to attack the other players and reduce their health until only a single player remains.

A Minion attacks by triggering another active Minion on the board. When a Minion is triggered it has an effect - it can deal damage to players, it can heal a player, or it can add flip counters to another Minion. But be careful! Attacking a Minion can cause a chain reaction that is out of your control. Each Minion has 2 different effects on either side of its card - each time it triggers it has a different effect on the game, and then it flips to reveal the opposite side. Minions also have a limited number of flips, once they're spent they are removed from play.

Can you out-think your opponents? Can you take advantage of the Minions on the board in order to come out on top? Eliminate your rivals and seize control of the city. Or at the very least, take all your rivals out with you and have the strongest army of Minions left who can secure the city for your successor.
The Imperator module provides a new action location to the game. Your aim is to capture one of the 5 regions, thereby gaining a reward at the end of the round.
The Consul expansion adds a new card action location to the game. There is a new type of card to be purchased and a new resource, gold is introduced.
If you decide to use this module with the game, remove the area scoring card from the game. The scoring of areas is changed, and in addition you can gain different rewards.

Magistratus gives each player a special ability that he/she has throughout the current game. Before the beginning of the game, shuffle all the cards and hand out two to each player. Each player should keep one, the other one goes back to the game box. Choose a side on the selected card and place it in front of you, next to your player board. Proceed with the game setup as described in the rulebook.

Using these scoring cards, you are entering a mini race with each other in every round. Shuffle the cards at the start of the game (since they are double sided, you may want to randomize sides too) and draw all cards, placing them right to left half covering each other. The left card will be the one used for scoring the current round, while the one underneath/right to it will be used in the next round.

boardgame: Champions of Midgard: Despised Promo Card
description:
Promo Destiny Card for Champions of Midgard.

"Have the most BLAME at the end of the game"

Citizen Heroes is a fully cooperative card drafting and community resource management game where players take on the roles of support personnel such as 911 Dispatcher, Field Operations, Crisis Coordinator, and Structural Engineer. Players must work together to save their city and its citizens from an imminent Disaster (such as a Flood, Earthquake, or Inferno). 
Recruit First Responders, rally them, and use them to rescue citizens,  all the while ensuring that the Disaster does not destroy the city.

• Tight action economy.
• Hard decisions can have lasting effects.
• Tension escalates after every turn. 
• Constantly evolving game state.
• Danger Level Track pushes the end game.
• High player interaction through a community pool of resources and card sharing.

boardgame: Rage Quitter or: How I Learned to Stop Worrying and Flip the Table
description: In Rage Quitter, each player competes in a dexterity challenge to see who can collect the most game pieces from the scattered ruins of table. On each players turn, after collecting game pieces, they will flip a table(card) from their deck with two pieces on it and try not fling them off the table. After flipping a table, the player then gets to throw a table(card) to cover up the pieces on the table, so no other player can get to them.

Game description from the publisher (Translated):
Relive your youth. Souvenir is the second expansion for L'osti d'jeu. It makes you relive the best and the worst from the 60's to the 90's in Québec. As a bonus, 10 blank cards to write your own sillyness.

Game description from the publisher (French):

Revivez votre jeunesse! L'extension souvenir est la deuxième extension de L'osti d'jeu. Elle vous fait revivre le meilleur et le pire du Québec des années 60 à 90. En prime, 10 cartes vierges pour écrire vos propres niaiseries.

A strategic faction based die stacking and siege game. Take control of an elemental faction waging war over ancient ruins. Earn points by rebuilding ruins as high as you can; however, be wary of your adversaries who will try and knock them down with their siege engines.  As seen at PAX West 2016 in the Indie Megabooth.

Each turn players perform an action to explore new territory, move or fire their siege engine, or take control of territory with their leader. Then they roll dice to build up the ancient ruins on the board. The taller a ruin is, the more points it is worth but the bigger target it is to be knocked down by an enemy siege engine. Dice knocked out of play are captured and worth points at the end of the game.

At the end of the game, whoever has the most points currently on the board wins. There are five different scoring objectives (ruin height, color matching, captured enemy dice, faction objectives, and your faction leader's control) which can all contribute to your victory.

boardgame: Legends of Andor: Garz, the Dwarf Merchant
description:
A promo card for "Legends of Andor: The last hope" for legends 12-17.

"Kaum ein Chronist erwähnte den kleinen gerissenen Handelszwerg und es scheint auch mehr als abwegig, dass er sich dem Tross der Andori anschloss. Unwegsames Gelände ohne Städte oder sonst jemanden der mit ihm Handel treiben könnte.
Nun, der Legende nach gab es großartige Edelsteine in den Höhlen des Grauen Gebirges. Und ja, es könnte sein, dass die Gier nach diesen Gemmen die Furcht des Handelszwergs bezwang ..."

English translation: "Hardly any chronicler mentioned the small, cunning merchant dwarf, and it seems highly improbable that he joined the Andori's entourage. The terrain was rugged, with no cities or anyone else with whom he could trade.
However, according to legend, there were magnificent gemstones in the caves of the Grey Mountains. And yes, it's possible that the lure of these gems overcame the merchant dwarf's fear..."

Available at SPIEL'16 or as download the KOSMOS website after the fair.

Promo card for "Legends of Andor: The last hope".
For the legends 12-16.
Randomizes the Sunrise box sequence.

Incantesium is a card game where 2-5 wizards face each other in a magic duel through elemental spells.
Each player starts with 10 cards and the purpose is to terminate the opponent cards.
Each turn a player throws a spell following the magic flow of the card on the table: if the flow is correct (same or opposite element of the last played card) the spell is active and he can cure himself or attack an opponent.

Welcome to the ancient and wondrous city of Jadid. Its streets are a bustling hive of activity. Traders and merchants doing business and how do tehir goods get transported? A network of flying carpet couriers deliver these goods from one location to another!

In this magical, fun game you play delivery men trying to fulfill contracts. Really? Yes, but you're not ordinary delivery men! Your delivery vehicle is a genie-powered flying carpet and you're roaring through twisting and turning city streets in a frenzied race to make the biggest profit.

Grab the most lucrative contract or be sneaky and steal one from a rival! Ram your opponents' carpets into buildings or target them with genie power-ups! Spend coin to boost your carpet's abilities and avoid the mischievous genie wandering around town!

In Genie Express: 1001 Deliveries, players move to a location on the modular board to pick up contracts and then plotting a course to the drop off location. Movement is determined by rolling a pool of special movement dice. Completing a contract gains you gold. A player wins by getting to a pre-determined amount of gold first. Players compete to pickup the limited number of contracts so you better be quick otherwise your rivals will beat you to the winning goal!

Phineas and Ferb have a mission: They want to build the coolest rollercoaster in the world. With tactival thinking and luck, you can make it together. But beware: taddle-tale Candace and the mean Dr. Doofenschrmirtz will do everything to destroy the build!

The player flips a few coins. If they land with the right side up, they get to move their token. This way they can collect pieces of their own puzzle, which shows a picture of the Rollercoaster! The first one to collect all the different pieces, AND makes it to the House, wins!
There is a risk, though. Candace can also move, and when she gets home before one of the players, she will tell mom and the plan is foiled!

Concordia: Gallia / Corsica is an expansion for Concordia that contains two maps for new strategic challenges:


     In Gallia, you start moving around with only one land colonist and have to settle inside one of the harbor cities before you can use your sea colonist as well.



     In Corsica, you will play on the tightest map ever published for Concordia, containing only twenty cities in eight provinces.


Both maps can be played at all player counts, but Corsica is especially meant for two or three players.

A travel game set in Scandinavia. Players must play travel cards in order to travel around Scandinavia, trying to collect a post card from each of the five countries. First one to do that and then return to the starting city wins the game.

There's also some trivia involved, but it's just purely optional flavour. The game play is very simple play a card and move a space.

Game description from the publisher:

In the early 1970's an innovator and ordnance expert named Harold "Hal" Hock got his hands on a document unseen outside of military circles: BRL 1192 (Ballistics Research Lab, Document No. 1192). The data within became the core of the ground-breaking design, Tobruk: Tank Battles in North Africa 1942.

Years later, an MIT-grad and devotee saw the potential of reverse-engineering the 'programmed instruction' rules; the result is something that brings back the approach to tank vs. tank warfare, and keeps the approach true, creating a truly unique system of recreating the war in North Africa and beyond.

BRL 1192: Tobruk Deprogrammed is an enhanced edition of the original Tobruk; the heart of the game remains the same, but now it can be enjoyed with a deprogrammed 24-page rulebook, revised play aids, a comprehensive selection of counters representing the men, weapons and machinery of WWII North Africa, plus a collection of geomorphic maps and new terrain overlays.

description: This 54 card expansion deck includes new Special, Icon and Event cards to add even more variety to your game play. French and Italian territories are also included, giving greater variety of bonuses as well.

Halfling Harvest is a simple take-that set collecting came for two to four players.

Every year our Halfling friends have a little party to celebrate the end of their harvest. The last few years have been quite extravagant and the problem is, this year they have drawn the attention of the various monsters that live in the mountains around the shire with their little shin-dig.

As the Halflings drink and dance on into the night, these monsters arrive all at the same time and (as one would expect) they do not wish to share the spoils. The fight amongst them grew ugly fast and I fear that our friends will all be hauled off one by one in the impending battle.

What we need is a distraction. The sun will rise soon and the monsters will scatter, so why don't we trick them into having a contest for the Halflings. Have each group take a recipe and the first to gather all their ingredients, gets ALL the Halflings. With a little luck this will delay them long enough that the sun will come up and our little friends can make it home in one piece.
 Goal of the game/objective

Players take on the persona of one of four monsters and scramble to sort through the pile of ingredients to gather what they need to complete their recipe. This is done by drawing from the pile and playing cards from your hand; laying them face up on the table in front of you. The first person to collect and play all the items in their recipe wins. Play again to delay as long as possible!

At the end, the winner of the most games can decide whether they are a friend of the Halflings in disguise and was able to delay long enough to set them free, or is the real Monster and EATS THEM...

Component list

•72 Poker sized cards, 12 Jumbo sized recipe cards, and a Rules sheet in a two piece box
•4- player cards: Ogres, Giants, Goblins, Trolls
•12- recipe cards and 68- component cards  
•13- Halflings  
•42- Ingredient cards:
•7 each of: Spice, Dragonberry, Mutton, Fungus, Dwarfish Eel, & Elf Bread 
•9- Attack Cards:
•1 Take Two Halflings, 1 Take Two Ingredients, 1 Wild Attack, 3 Take One Halfling, 3 Take One Ingredients 
•2- Wild Ingredient Cards 
•2- Defend Cards

Game Play

1.At the start of each turn, if the player has any number of Halflings, they MUST play all into their recipe by laying them face up in front of them.  
2.Next, the player may play any one ingredient card into their recipe if desired, by placing it face up next to the Halflings in front of them. 
3. If the player has an attack card, they may then choose to play one against another player. *note: stolen items may not be put in your hand, but must be played immediately.* 
4.After the attack, the player may discard any number of cards still left in their hand into the discard pile.    
5.The player will then draw from the component deck into their hand back up to a limit of five cards.
6.Play continues to the left.

Notes:

-Stolen items must be played directly into the recipe
-“Wild” cards can be played in place of any ingredients, but not Halflings
-The “Wild Attack” card can steal any card, Ingredient or Halfling, and is the only card that can steal a “Wild” card. 
-The “Take two Halfling” and “Take two Ingredients” attack cards may both be taken from the same player, OR may take one from two different players.  
-You may play more ingredients or Halflings into your recipe then are required.

Overview：
This game is about assembling mainframe. There are 6 parts to assemble the mainframe. This is a game for 4 players.

Game components:
12 red cards (computer cases)
72 blue cards (9 CPU, 9 motherboards, 9 memories, 18 GPU, 27 hard drives)
18 green cards (function cards)
Function cards
20 Change 1 component: update a hardware which has been installed.
18 Replace 2 component cards: discard 2 cards and draw 2 components cards.
6 Boom: Choose one player to uninstall 2 components on the deck expect computer case.
4 Robbery: Rob one player of one component card in hand.
1Rule book

Rules:
1.Each player is allotted three computer case cards, and each player need to choose one from three cards.
2.Player need to collect all parts of mainframe(computer case, motherboard, CPU, memories, GPU, hard drives).
3.Different computer case need different numbers of GPU and hard drives.
4.Each player only can install two hardware in one turn.
5.Each player in each turn can make a choice, one choice is draw two blue cards and one green card, the other choice is draw one green cards and one blue card.
6.Every function card should be used in the next turn,and every player only can use two function cards in one turn.
7.On the desk, the green cards need to face up 7 cards, player can decide draw facing up card, or pile. If the facing up cards are drew, the facing up cards will be supplemented.
8. Every player can trade with other players.
9.Each player must face up the cards which they decide to install on the deck. If they do this action, they can’t change them unless using function cards.
10.If one player finishes all the parts, the game still need to finish next round.
Procedures:
1.Each player will get three computer case cards, and each player must choose one of three cards.
2.Players need to use Rock Paper Scissors to decide who is the first, then the game is on a clockwise.
3.Before players draw cards, they can use only one function card.
4.Each player in each turn can make a choice, one choice is draw two blue cards and one green card, the other choice is draw one green cards and one blue card.
5.If player want to install a part, he/she need to face up the card on the desk.

Win state: 
If only one player finishes installing mainframe in this round, he/she win.
If more than one players finishes installing mainframe in this round, they will compare who get higher points in the game. And the player who get higher points win.

The theme：On the Thanksgiving Day, a family need to vote who sings a song to celebrate this festival. Then, they write red egg, yellow egg, green egg, bad egg and rainbow egg. And if one person get the less score, this person will sing a song.

Game components:
11 cards ( 3 Red Egg cards, 3 Green Egg cards, 3 Yellow Egg cards, 1 Black Egg card, 1 Rainbow Egg card)
1 Pen
1 Blank paper
1 Rule book

The gameplay:1. Shuffling all cards and putting it on the table.
2. Each player need to pick one card place it on their forehead which each player only can see other cards and can’t see his/her own card. 
3. The first player is the youngest player, then play clockwise.
4. One player is responsible for recording the votes. And the votes need to run two rounds.
5. Rainbow egg card always gets 1 score, and Black egg card can’t get any score.
6. The game need to be finished in three turns.
First turn:
The color eggs which are voted the middle time will get 2 score. The color eggs which are voted the highest time will get 1 score, and the color eggs which are lowest time can’t get any score.
Second turn:
The color eggs which are voted the highest time will get 2 score. The color eggs which are voted the lowest time will get 1 score, and the color eggs which are voted the middle time can’t get any score.
Third turn:
The color eggs which are voted the lowest time will get 2 score. The color eggs which are voted the middle time will get 1 score, and the color eggs which are voted the highest can’t get any score.

Goals:
The player who get most score will win.
The player who get least score will sing a song.

Anomaly is a 4x sci-fi board game experience about deep space exploration, resource management, space stations and combat which plays in 30-60 minutes.

Anomaly is a science fiction board game for 2-4 players about deep space exploration, resource management, space stations and combat. Take control of one of four alien races, each with their own unique abilities and tactics. At the helm of your civilization’s deep space explorer fleet you must explore the universe, expand your territory, exploit every resource and, if it comes to it, exterminate your opposition.

Anomaly is designed as a full 4X experience that plays out within a time frame of 40-60 minutes. Anomaly offers high-grade components and a simple set of rules that allows for both tactical and strategic decision-making as well as fluid, immersive game play.

As you explore the universe, you will discover Anomalies that generate the win conditions for each particular game. Four unique player races, 6 Anomalies, 38 Upgrades and a random combination of 19 different space Sector tiles will grant you many hours of deep space exploration!

-- description from the publisher:

Description from the publisher:

Where are the Ten Commandments? With whom was the life story of the Reformer filmed? What did he call his wife? And how many theses were there actually? Martin Luther: Das Quiz is a trivia game centered around the reformer Martin Luther, the Bible, and films on the subject.

boardgame: Holy Roman Empire Expansion 2: Battles of the English Civil War
description:
Game description from the publisher:

The English Civil War (ECW) Scenario Pack is intended for use with the tactical battle map and Battle Board Rules that come with Holy Roman Empire. Just like its cousin, the Thirty Years War Scenario Pack,this expansion is designed to allow players to set up and game historical battles from the period. This is a game, not a simulation, and while intended to provide an insight into the struggles of the era it is meant to be fast, fun, and challenging rather than an in-depth recreation of events. This pack covers selected battles of what is known as the First Civil War, from the first major engagement at Edgehill in 1642 until the kings decisive defeat at Naseby in 1645. The designer hopes that playing these scenarios will generate interest in the history of the period and will encourage gamers to read more about the era both for education and entertainment.

A half sheet of die-cut mounted playing pieces is also included, as is version 1.5 of the main Holy Roman Empire rule book.

description:
扮演檢察官與證人，找出關鍵案件真相，起訴嫌犯！
本遊戲將模擬檢察官偵查辦案流程，用各種方式訊問證人，抽絲剝繭，找出案件真相。遊戲中精選推理小說與刑法教科書案例，無不讓人拍案叫絕。
究竟檢察官能不能成功找出真相，突破難關？現在，這個任務就交給你了！

User summary (English):
Players take on the roles of prosecutors and witness.
There are still 3 questionable points in the murdering case.
The prosecutors have to find the truth for indict the defendant.
Prosecutors could interrogate the witness,read the documents,command the police for getting evidence.
After the investigation is concluded, the player with the most Victory Points is the Top Prosecutor and win the game!

Morphos is a connection game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the intersections (points) of an initially empty square grid (board). The top and bottom edges of the board are colored black; the left and right edges are colored white.

Black plays first, then turns alternate. On your turn, place a stone of your color on an empty point or replace (flip) a weak enemy stone with a stone of your color. A black stone is weak if it is part of at least one of the following patterns or their translations, rotations or reflections:


     Black stone on b2 and white stones on b3, a3, a2 and b1.



     Black stone on b2 and white stones on a4, a3, a2, b1 and c1.



     Black stone in a 2×2 area orthogonally adjacent to eight white stones.


Likewise, with colors reversed, for weak white stones.

Passing is not allowed, but, if you have no legal moves available, your turn is skipped.

You win if there is a chain of orthogonally connected stones of your color touching the two opposite board edges of your color. If neither player has a move available and neither player has won, Black wins if there is at least one pair of vertically adjacent, like-colored stones, and White wins otherwise.

To make the game fair, White will have the option, on their first turn only, to swap sides with Black instead of making a regular move.

				
				
					NotesOn an n×n board, the number of deadlocks, including symmetrical copies, is 2^(n + 1) - 2, i.e. two less than four times the number of compositions of n. Note that, in a deadlock, there can be vertically adjacent, like-colored stones or horizontally adjacent, like-colored stones, but not both. If there are no adjacencies of either type because the stones form a checkered pattern, White wins.

The following mutators can be applied to alter the character of the game:


     Minimorph: The last weakness pattern is not used, and the other two have the stone in the top left corner removed from them. This requires a larger board than the standard game, as it makes blocking quite harder. On the other hand, deadlocks are impossible. All other mutators on this list work best when combined with this one.



     Deepmorph: A stone that was flipped by your opponent on their latest turn still counts as a friendly stone for flipping purposes. In this case, the winning condition must be checked for a player at the start of their turn, and it is illegal to flip a stone if the opponent could flip it back on their next turn.



     Duomorph: After placing (not flipping) a stone on your turn, you must, if possible, place a second friendly stone on an empty point orthogonally adjacent to the first one.



     Remorph: When you remove an enemy stone and there are empty points on the board, place the removed stone next to the board. On their next turn, your opponent must place the stone back on an empty point of the board and then make a regular move.



     Metamorph: The game starts with the board filled with stones in a checkered pattern.

'AireCards' is a game first played at the AireCon Board Game convention.

Players fill out their AireCard by distributing 1000 points between 5 categories, although no two categories can be the same value. Collect 3 lives (tokens) and a custom AireCon dice (the 6 replaced by the AireCon logo) then you're all set to go!

The rules are easy, walk up to another player, challenge them and then roll your dice - then compare values in the matching category on your AireCards. The highest value wins and takes a life from the other player.

You can't challenge someone that just challenged you, and you can't challenge the same person twice.

At the end of the convention, hand in your AireCard and all the lives you've collected, whoever collected the most lives wins!

A combination sports and gambling game where each player plays as a team owner, competes through an entire season, and attempts to make the most money. As the sales sheet states, "Follow the season schedule and win or lose millions playing football games against your friends. Your luck will also be tested by picking Safe or Risk cards when you are the Home Team."

Dragon Kings is a deck building game consisting of 55 cards. In Dragon Kings, players develop a deck by using mana to purchase and cast new Spells/Dragons from the Dragon Pit. The Dragon Pit is the set of powerful neutral cards that belong to neither player, placed in the center of the play area. With the aid of the Dragons and Spells purchased from the Dragon Pit, the players' ultimate goal is to reduce their opponent's life to 0.

Dragons Kings is a 2-player game (or 4 players with 2 decks) that introduces players to deck building.
