boardgame: Vampires & Villagers: The Curse of Christoph
description:
Build your crypt, destroy the village, and defeat your rivals in Vampires & Villagers: The Curse of Christoph!

The Vampires have grown displeased with the villagers of Askwith’s ongoing challenges to their dominance. The Vampire Lords set out to destroy the town, while also battling each other for supremacy. You are a Vampire Lord in control of a crypt of creatures. The goal is to end the game with the most powerful crypt.  The game is played in a series of rounds as rival vampires build up their forces and deplete the village.  Every time the village square is emptied, the round ends and an event occurs. Many event effects are lasting; some favor the humans, while others favor the forces of darkness. The fourth time the square is cleared, Askwith is destroyed and the game is scored.

There are four character classes: Villagers, Vampire Hunters, Thralls, and Vampires, including Master Vampires, Lesser Vampires, and Turned Hunters. Players place their Vampires and Thralls in the “crypt” in front of them in order to be able to use them in play. The creatures in the crypt can be face up and active or face down, in shadows. Active creatures can directly attack the villagers, while creatures in shadows use stealth attacks. Thralls and turned hunters/slave vampires, when applicable, can also be used to defend a creature who has been targeted by a crypt attack.

Each turn, players draw 1 card and play 1 card, choosing to build up their crypts or use a hunter or strong villager to attack one of their rivals. During the next phases, players can use the skills of face-up cards and attack the village with direct attacks and stealth. Finally, crypt attacks and other residual actions from previous turns are resolved. During a crypt attack, the Hunter is supported by all of the Villagers in the square.

After the end of the fourth round, the game ends and the power value of all creatures in the crypt in front of each player are scored, along with any giant bat victory points. The player with the highest score is the reigning Vampire Lord!

Vampires and Villagers introduces an unusual semi-cooperative element. Players both attack the village in order to gain victory points and use the villagers against one another. A strong village is a useful weapon, but it can be used against you, as well. Vampires and Villagers is all about managing opposing goals – the successful player balances bringing about the end of rounds and the game quickly with strengthening their own crypt, while simultaneously preventing their opponents from doing the same.  Keeping your opponents off balance as you subtly drive the flow of the game is key to victory.

One additional card for the German 2016 version of Pentos.
The cards are for founders on Spieleschmiede which founded the Black Pocket package. That means all three games PENTOS, ZERO and GHOSTS of Asyncron games

Politicks is a fun, tongue-in-cheek card game for 2–4 players featuring Maltese politicians and events from Maltese politics. Not that you need to know anything about Maltese politics or even understand Maltese to enjoy the game. The game was designed by Gordon Pace and illustrated by Mark Scicluna.

You have just been appointed as a political party’s strategist with the task of winning the upcoming election. You know that the best way to win votes is to gain the support of organisations and lobby groups by sending your best politicians to convince them and by directing the course of events to be in your favour. Will you lead your party to electoral victory?

In the game, each player represents a political party. The game is played over a number of rounds, in each of which a faction (a lobby group or organisation) is willing to meet up with the parties to decide who to support in the upcoming election. Players play politician cards to convince the faction to support them, and event cards to affect politicians and the faction. At the end of the round, the votes represented by the faction are gained by the player whose politician is most influential. The first player to gain 9 votes wins the game.

boardgame: Arkham Horror: The Card Game – The Dunwich Legacy: Expansion
description:
Game description from the publisher:

The Dunwich Legacy is a deluxe expansion for Arkham Horror: The Card Game.

Several months ago, three Miskatonic University professors armed themselves with secret knowledge, traveled to Dunwich, and brought an end to the strange and terrifying creature that had been terrorizing the secluded village. Their heroic efforts brought peace to the region—for a while—but also left the men scarred by the horrors they had witnessed. Now, one of those professors, Dr. Armitage, has need of your assistance. His colleagues have gone missing, and he fears the worst…

With five mini cards and 156 full-size cards, The Dunwich Legacy challenges you to find the missing professors and launches you into a campaign that leads to Miskatonic University, the notorious Clover Club, and beyond. Five new investigators arrive to offer their services, armed with fifty-nine new player cards (including a complete playset of each) that allow you to pursue new deck-building strategies and new styles of play. Finally, you'll find all manner of new terrors arrayed against you—from backroom thugs and bad luck to arcane hexes and wholly unnatural abominations.

description:
Game description from the publisher:

An educational game racing around the USA using the 50 Capital City Key Cards. 2-6 players start at their home towns state capital and pick from the Key Card deck to determine the prop plane route. Player picking the most key cards at end of game wins the round. Each player can check off the score pad the capital they claim. In event of tie player with their home capital wins. Ties are very rare in the 252 test games from our 2 working prototypes. Only a few. The game is very exciting and has 6 special effects that can turn the game even more exciting. Ages 7-seniors even a 4 yr old can play. Very popular with children that when they got the first prototype they play 30 times in a few week period and most rounds last 45-60 minutes average. Special effects occur 20-30 times a round when the two effects match on the dice. 2 regular dice, 2 special effect dice, 6 prop planes on stands, 50 key cards, score pad of 50 states check list. Great thing about the game is not needing to answer the questions but a memorization of the key card(s) of each state capital. It is a fun race game to collect most capitals. Object is to stay away from the long routes needed to get to the capitals. It is normal to have more than one card in your hand due to the extra key and steal a key effect.

This expansion to Shadowrift 2nd Edition introduces two new monster factions: the Restless Dead, featuring poltergeists; and the Cult of the Fang, featuring spiders! A Hunter hero and four new hero cards are also slated for the expansion.

boardgame: The Eye of Judgment: Biolith Rebellion – Series 2
description:
All pre-constructed decks contain 30 Summoning Cards and the Summoner's Companion - 32-pages containing tactical hints, card lists, images and stories. Booster packs consist of random summoning cards. Set/series 2 added 100 new cards to the base game.

Two-player competitive matches can be played online. As a prohibitive measure against cheating the card draw, players' summoning decks are registered and saved by the game. In online matches, the random draw is handled virtually using the registered deck data. The cards are then played as normal.

Every legend has a beginning and every famous skeleton like Johnny the Skull had to start somewhere. Where would that be if not at the graveyard!

To play this game the first thing you should do is find a place with dim light. We suggest going to the nearest graveyard at night to play, but we feel that might be too creepy! A darkened location is best because the skeleton bones glow in the dark.

On the count of three, both players should start excavating to become the fastest digger on earth. One player is collecting green bones and the other player orange bones. Use the shovel to move the dirt and uncover the elusive parts of our friend, Johnny. When you find a bone of your color, seize it with the eerie tweezers and put it next to the other pieces you have found on the glow in the dark diagram.

The winner of this unusual game of digging out a skeleton from a graveyard is not the one who finds all the bones first, but the one who can put all the pieces where they belong. The first player to build Johnny's skeleton will be the winner.

Villa Musarañas Great Race is going to start. Each school chooses a child to participate. What about you? Who are you going to run with?

OBJECTIVE OF THE GAME
Arrive at the finish line before your rivals. For this purpose you will have to overcome all kind of obstacles. First player who reaches 200 metres wins.

BOX CONTENTS
98 playing cards and 2 more cards with the rules.

CONTENTS TO PLAY
Cards, six-sided dice (take one of any other game or download an application on your smartphone browser that simulates one), notebook and pencil.

PREPARATION
The game can be played by any number of players from 2 to 6.
Write down in your notebook each one of the players in order to add the metres reached.
Divide the cards into three stacks:
1. Race (map-greens)
Overcome obstacles, dodge monsters and answer the wise men’s questions correctly to get metres in the race.
2. Equipment (open coffer-purples)
There are two kinds of objects:
Those ones that help to overcome obstacles (blues).
Those ones that are used for hindering the rest of runners (reds).
3. Runners (cup-yellows)
Each one of the runners has a physical skill (frog jump, lion strength, rabbit speed) that helps against monsters. There is also an intellectual one (language or mathematics) that helps to answer the wise men’s questions.

CARD MANAGEMENT
Shuffle the three stacks separately.
Each player chooses a runner that is put face up on the table. The discarded cards are kept.
Deal four equipment cards to each player. It is necessary to keep them out of sight. You can never have more than four equipment cards in your hand. The unused cards are put face down at one side of the table.
The race stack is put face down in the middle of the table. The race starts when putting face up the first card of the stack. When the stack has no cards left, shuffle and put face down again.

BEGINNING OF THE GAME
Roll the dice and the player with the highest score, starts playing. Each turn spins in a clockwise direction and starts showing a card of the race stack. Each card has a challenge with a score that must be overcome by rolling the dice. If you get it you get more metres or more equipment. If not, it is likely to lose metres (never under zero), equipment or the position. Each turn does not finish until each and everyone of the players try to overcome it.

STAGES IN EACH TURN
Each turn is divided into two stages:
1. Use of the equipment. You can use equipment cards only once.
2. Overcoming the challenge. In order to get it you will have to roll the dice and overcome the score of each challenge. It will be kept in mind if some equipment card has been used.
(Before starting each player’s turn, it will be also kept in mind if some equipment card has been used for hindering).

END OF THE GAME
The player who reaches 200 metres wins Villa Musarañas Great Race Cup.

Description from the publisher:

Cards and dice board game half-cooperative, based on the Shadow World universe.

Vampires, werewolves and unusual humans fight to gain control of Dark City. To achieve that they should cooperate to overcome the adversities of the environment and compete with each other in order to become the first to complete its mission. Collect a balanced band, arm them with sophisticated equipment or powerful spells and face any kind of danger. Use the other players to your advantage. Prove you're worthy of being the leader of the dark city.

MECHANICS AND TYPE OF GAME
Shadow World: Arena is a game of cards and dice, mixing the dark world of the Shadow World universe with a complete but flexible system.
In it, players will create their bands through the Character cards, Allies and Accessories. The dice are your resources, and limits what skills can use each turn the members of our band, providing a random point in the game.

Then everyone will try to accomplish the mission being assigned. To do this they will face unexpected events, and controlled by artificial intelligence enemies of the game itself and the interests of other players, because in the dark city, only one can claim victory.

Chinese people are very stressed and busy due to the high cost of living. As a result, a lot of young people don't have much time to meet people and start a relationship, which leads to blind dates being a popular way to find the other half of their lives.

In many situations, young people don't know how to start to talk on a blind date, so this game is a helpful tool for them to show their charm right from the beginning.

100,000 Blind Dates is based on Chinese research on the experience of one hundred thousand blind dates. The game uses the keywords from the research to help people talking on blind dates.

The game is very easy. Players use the keyword cards and event cards in their hands, along with the charm keyword cards on the table, to profess their feelings to the people in which they're interested. Then, both people score accordingly.

At the end of the game, the player with the highest score wins, and they might be the most successful mate with whom others will want to start a relationship...

User summary (Chinese):
是一款支持3-8人的欢乐聚会桌游，游戏时间在15-30分钟，适合玩家年龄在14岁以上，
大家在游戏中分别扮演拥有四个不同魅力属性的角色，玩家们会因为彼此角色的魅力属性而心生爱慕，从而进行表白和各种针对表白的反应动作。游戏中，玩家有很多方式来获得暧昧分数，暧昧分数被视为真诚，勇敢，魅力和智慧的象征，在游戏结束时，拥有最高暧昧分数的玩家获得胜利，他被视为是在相亲情场上最成功的人。

如果你喜欢一个人，应该和他玩《十万次相亲》，在游戏里，你可以向他表白；
如果你不喜欢一个人，应该和他玩《十万次相亲》，在游戏里，你可以拒绝他的表白；
如果你不了解一个人，也应该和他玩《十万次相亲》，在游戏里，你们可能是相爱的；

Zupper Dolls is a family playing game, which contain many special paper components, such as magnetic board, magnetic hero card, magnetic item card, easy to play with cute characters and drawing art, hope you and your family will like it.

It is a racing game, use difference team to fight again the boss, when processing the map, player team can get difference hero, action card and weapon,which have difference function to help player, who is the first finish the game is winner!

SudoKount is a colorful, fun and brain challenging card game inspired by Sudoku and magical squares that renew the genre of number placement puzzle game making the experience touchable and multiplayer (both competitive or cooperative). It's also educative, decorative, and it can be brought everywhere...

SudoKount is very simple to play: draw a puzzle card and then use number tiles to solve it. Puzzle cards indicates an initial configuration for 5x5, 6x6 or 7x7 grids. Solving the puzzle consist in adding or removing number tiles so that the sum of each row and each column is equal to the goal indicated on the puzzle card.

Pulled from the lore of Layman Kingsford's Living Empires stories, 7 Sign Circle is a deck of round cards - The Deck of Fey - with 8 “suits” of 11 cards, one for each signMark. Some people believe in the oracular potency of the deck while others simply use them for gambling and recreational games of chance.

Test your wit at any of the games possible with this deck of cards, a 7-pointed star mat and eight signMark tokens. Games include variations on trick-taking, set collecting and bluffing mechanisms familiar from traditional card games, but each of these games comes with the twist that the suits relate to each other in variable ways from hand to hand and game to game.

boardgame: Rodrigues & Mauritius (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride)
description:
This follows the principles of the basic Ticket to Ride game on a special board with special cards and rules. The object of the game is to gain the most points by travelling various routes and outwitting other players. You will need the train pieces and the train cards from either the US or Europe base games (or you can buy them from the Days of Wonder shop.

There is an extra set of Tourist cards for the cities, each with a value to be earned by visiting that city. These cards are dealt/picked up alongwith tickets in the same quantity and with the same 'keep/dispose' rules.There are also up to 9 bonuses for the end-of-game scoring.

The board does not have any grey tracks or parallel tracks - but there are spanners in the works which may help (or hinder) you..

There are many new options in the rules: exchange all your cards at the start but keep all the new ones: discard 5 cards and pick up 5 new ones, as your turn: earn extra points: and many more. You can also call time when your wagon stock reaches 3, if you want, giving you the advantage of stopping other players completing tickets.

boardgame: Blitzkrieg in the West: Northern Campaign
description:
Game description from the publisher:

This six-scenario pack revolves around battles of  the German invasion of Fortress Holland and is compatible with MMP's ASL™ System and modifiable to be used with other miniature Wargaming systems.

Blitzkrieg in the West - Northern Campaign scenarios can be played separately, or the six scenarios may be linked together to form a campaign game by playing each scenario in order. At the conclusion of each scenario, both sides total up their Victory Points (VP); and at the end of the last scenario these VP are totalled, and the side with most VP wins.

The scenarios are designed with replayablility in mind. Each scenario features a base force and variable attachments to that force. Base forces and variable attachments are based on historical availability for that scenario. At the start of each scenario: Base Force + Variable Attachment = Scenario Order of Battle.

boardgame: Guards of Atlantis: Sabina & Ignatia Character Pack
description:
A box containing 2 models for the expansion characters for the game and their corresponding decks of cards.

This expansion allows playing the Guards of Atlantis with 10-11 players.

The players are animal rights activists, who have the goal to save as many endangered animals from extinction. Only when they work together on strategies and skillfully utilize the available resources, they succeed to retake the habitat of the animals. Animal welfare is teamwork!

The cards represent the threatend species from three different habitats (green: land, blue: water, orange: air). 
There are 5-6 (depending on the number of players) animals laying face down in the middle of the table, so every player can see the habitat, but not the needs of the animal (like "2 air and 1 money"). 
Now the players have to choose a "strategic card", which gives one player a special move (like switching a card on the table, or looking at a card).
After that, the players have to play ressources from their hand face down on an animal of their choice. When each player has played their card (now communication allowed at that point), the animals and ressources are revealed and all animals, where the ressources match the needs are saved.

boardgame: A Song of Ice and Fief (fan expansion for Fief: France 1429)
description:
A fan based mod of the Fief France 1429 game.

This mod changes the setting of the game from France to Westeros and includes new rules that attempt to simplify the original vanilla Fief rules.

In this game you will play as one of the 9 main houses in Westeros, each house has an special ability and a defined starting space in the map.

Each turn you will expand and try to gain more glory for your house by getting titles for your house either nobility titles such as a fief or the king or military titles such as guardian of the north, a small council position etc...

This version includes extra mechanics such as Seasons (Winter is indeed coming) which make natural disasters that appear every turn affect more areas, or the Wall mechanic which forces all the players to collaborate sending funds and troops to the wall to prevent the wildings and white walkers from ravaging westeros and then making all the players lose the game.

The cards mechanics from FIEF has been protected and enhanced making assasinations, betrayals, kings justice killings and kidnappings more prevalent through the game.

Players will also be able to build different structures with more variety than in the base game such as mills, septs, maester towers etc...

This mod has been done wihthout any intellectual property from either the Fief 1429 publishers or George RR Martin or any other third party who owns or may own the Song of Ice and Fief series IP.

This game is freely downloadable as Print and Play :
All PnP assets: http://www.mediafire.com/download/qtl7eddl4q2aej7/A_song_of_Ice_and_Fief_PnPv2.rar

A PDF of the rules in English: http://www.mediafire.com/download/whav330t53pdnda/A_song_of_ice_and_fief_english_rulebook_v04.pdf

A PDF of the rules in French: https://www.dropbox.com/s/z3q7h9nztr862hl/Trone_de_fief.pdf?dl=0

A printer studio card set: http://www.printerstudio.com/design/dn_show_parse.aspx?producttype=772E45C74E337CF36906FDBEFF230590&flow=868A4695A58AE09DFCB8D0AE100A93FF163EC7F6C8151841&id=5C0281D46639765F356BE34168854B028CE8392DAA7C044CADD83CDD5F0AC72C7A9EE6AD3C2A2744&projecttype=EFAE81EBDA27CF77EB9E00D7A1ACC4D5&projectfrom=EFAE81EBDA27CF77EB9E00D7A1ACC4D5&orderNo=&sellID=&ssid=49CEFF8DFE9D4622B41BFA6460445337

Game description from the publisher:

Challenge your friends with this exciting quiz famous from the Dutch television. Try to match the right descriptions with the correct answers before the time runs out! Try to outsmart your friends and use your tokens at the right moment. Are you able to think under a lot of pressure?

Per Seconde Wijzer is a quiz game for up to 4 players older than 12 years old. One of the players reads 6 descriptions and all the other players try to connect the right answers within 60 seconds. For every correct answer the player is allowed to move a step forward. The first one to reach the goal is the winner.

boardgame: Mascarene Islands (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride)
description:
This game is a combination of the three games: Réunion & Mauritius, Mauritius & Réunion and Rodrigues & Mauritius.  The rules for each game are consistent and apply to this game also.

You will need all three boards (side by side in that order) and tickets plus each player will need three sets of wagons (one for each board).

You will start with your markers at zero on each board.  Begin play on one island (Rodrigues, Mauritius or Réunion) and, if you wish, stay on that board throughout the game.  However, when you reach the coast, you may take a ferry to another island – now you may still play on that board or you may continue your journey from the port of arrival on the next board.

You may travel in any direction from board to board, always travelling to a part-island and then moving to the same port on the main island.  Or stay on one or two boards.

2349:  Space Prospectors takes place in the year 2349.  A cluster of Asteroids have been discovered near a distant colony.  These asteroids are rich in a rare mineral called Unobtainium and a 24th century version of a “gold rush” has begun.

To begin, players flick asteroids, including a home base asteroid onto the playing field.

Players take on the role of Prospectors.  Controlling up 4 ships, players expend fuel cards to fly ships from their home camp asteroid to unexplored and unclaimed asteroids.  Ships land on the asteroids and, using the fuel cards, drill into these lumps of rock to extract the Unobtainium.
In the spirit of the Wild West, each player wants to lay claim to asteroids with the largest Unobtainium amounts.  Beware, opponents can wrestle control of your asteroids using show-downs and claim-jumps.

Worm hole cards are drawn at the end of each turn that can cause mechanical failures, alien encounters, navigation errors, fuel caches, gamma radiation and disruption of the asteroid field as new asteroids are flicked into the playing area.

The Game ends when a Cruiser carrying the Colonial Governor arrives to establish Law and Order. Whomever controls the asteroids with the highest Unobtainium values wins.  Beware the Alien Invasion where controlling 2 Alien Asteroids wins the game!

In 1846, the Donner Party got snowbound in the High Sierras. Only half survived the winter. Who will you eat to be one of them?

Each player has a family of settlers (color coded), and a few hirelings or settlers outside that family. While individually named, all of these actually represent several people (you are not playing all 81 settlers).

You get points for each settler you have alive at the end of the game, with bonus points for healthy settlers, oldest settler, youngest settler and an intact family. Things you do in the game cause you to accumulate "Shame", which are cards with random values that you draw but do not look at, and whoever has the highest Shame at the end of the game loses, regardless of their score.

Play proceeds in rounds, moving through a chronologically stacked Forage deck that represents the winter of 1846. Each round a player has a personal forage step, and then there is a group forage step. In the latter, players bid to acquire extra food and the bid is how much Shame they are willing to draw to get that food. The number of group forage cards available in a turn is always less than the number of players, so someone gets left out every time. Additionally one of the group forage cards is dealt face-down, so you do not know what it is. Ties in bidding are broken by the party leader, a role which rotates around the table. Players who do not get a forage card in the group step get to draw an Action card instead.

Then, everyone feeds their settlers, with weakened settlers costing less food than healthy ones. But weakened ones also do not forage as well. Losses from hunger have to follow a few simple rules, like none of your settlers can starve to death until all of them are weakened.

In addition to forage, there are action cards that players can acquire through the foraging process. These are the nasty things like murder and grave robbing and such. These can be played at any time and generally give you food, but unlike Forage cards, have an inherent Shame cost to play them.

This process continues until either the Forage deck empties or the "final rescue" card is drawn during March 1847. Since some cards are randomly removed from each game before play, you never know exactly when the game will end or what the weather will be and thus cannot plan for it.

Big naval battle is waged on the Caribbean Sea. Every captain wants to win and bring precious treasures to their port. To win, you have to use either your wits or great firepower. Which tactic will you choose?

At the beginning of the game, each player gets 6 cards representing ships and their abilities, and 6 tokens representing ship upgrades.
Every turn all players simultaneously choose 1 card and up to 2 tokens to play. Then the fastest and strongest ships sail safely to the harbour and other ships are sunk (they score no points). Game ends when all cards are played. Player with the most treasure chests in port wins the game.
family: Series: Dobra gra w dobrej cenie (Egmont Polska)

description:
In this Moomin-themed pattern recognition game players turn over two cards depicting Moomin characters or some items. Players must find pictures depicted in both cards from the board and place their fingers on the pictures.

First player to do this is rewarded with a fruit tile. If both cards show a comet symbol, the player also gets a comet tile. First player to reach five fruit tiles or two comet tiles wins the game.

The board is assembled from pieces, so it changes between games. The tiles are double-sided: one side is plain, the other filled with jungle, making it harder to find the pictures.

boardgame: Monopoly Card Games: Free Parking and Water Works
description:
Free Parking
Feed your parking meter and run your "errands" before time runs out. Visit the health club, renew your driver's license, take flying lessons... But watch out for Officer Jones, because if your meter's time "expires" you could be in deep trouble. Feeling lucky? Draw a second chance card and maybe Mister MONOPOLY will help you turn panic into points. Score the most points and you win!

Water Works
Be the first player to complete your own "pipeline" from valve to spout by connecting enough pipe cards in between. But beware - your opponents are building their own pipelines and will be delighted to ruin your best laid plans by springing "leaks" on your pipeline. You'll have to repair those pipes or make detours. Call for the plumber (Mister Monopoly)! Complete your pipeline first and you win!

(NOTE: This expansion is now part of the base game, so it is no longer available independently)

New enemies threaten the Tetrarchy!

Do you think you and your co-Emperors can handle already the enemies of the Roman Empire? Then beware, new enemies arrive to breach the walls of Rome! From eastern Europe, the Gothic army crosses the Danube unstoppable. From the sea, Pirate ships challenge Roman rule over `your' Mare Nostrum. Hopefully, a young Roman general, the Dux, may come to your aid!

This extension of Tetrarchia includes three independent modules, the Dux (a new orange Roman meeple), the Pirates (a new black fleet), and the Goths (a new black Barbarian meeple). They can be combined in any number with the base game and with any of the five official variants, providing and endless list of new challenges. But don't worry, the rules remain simple and the playtime fast!

The greatest leapers of the animal kingdom have gathered for a jumping competition, but nobody brought lunch! You need to launch to get your meal and grab the food that you like the most. Use special powers that are unique to each animal to help along the way.

Launchtime is card-flipping family dexterity game for 2 players of almost all ages, and plays in 15 minutes just about anywhere that you can fling a few cards around.  Each player has a rabbit, kangaroo, flea and frog card. Using your reference card you flip your animal cards onto cards in the center of the table that have different food highlighted (targeted), you want your animal to land on cards that highlights the food your animal eats. You take the card as a reward AND if your animal and its food match, you gain bonus points. As an added twist, if your animal cards land face up to gain special abilities that are unique to the animal.

boardgame: Advanced Song of Blades and Heroes: Axe and Brimstone
description:
Axe and Brimstone is an expansion for the popular Advanced Song of Blades and Heroes game system. Axe and Brimstone is a narrative campaign that tells the story of the Possessed (a group of evil, demon-worshipping dwarves) that are trying to summon their dark masters to the material world. In order to gather enough magical energies to open the gates to Zo, the region of the Netherwhere (Hell) where the demons are confined, the Possessed must offer a number of innocent souls in sacrifice. And what is more innocent than a village full of peace-loving, land tilling Halfling farmers?

Axe and Brimstone is a campaign supplement for Advanced Song of Blades and Heroes. In its pages, you will find:

     Background information and profiles for the Possessed, demon-worshipping dwarves who aim to unleash their dark masters upon Norindaal.
     The valiant, peaceful halflings who oppose them.
     A complete campaign in five scenarios.
     Plenty of new Traits: demonic seals, hellfire chain weapons, hellfire muskets, hellfire grenades
     New group action: Dark Ritual
     New spells
     Rules for halfling banquet magic and wise women’s remedies.
     Profiles for all characters for Advanced Song of Blades
family: Series: Song of Blades and Heroes

description: Creation: The land and human is a strategy card battle game. In the game, players play as the god of the new world, they are competing to each others by collecting 5 kind of elements : METAL,WOOD,WATER,FIRE,EARTH. By using different combo of elements, they can play various mysterious creation card in order to destroy their rivals or increase their citizen or army. Players are able to battle directly with other players by using army card. Additionally, players can even build famous wonder by using multiple combo of elements to get stable benefit. The player who get most citizens win the game.

The game consists of 15 cards. There are three colours and three shapes. The colours are Red, Blue and Yellow, and the shapes Circle, Triangle and Square. Some cards have coloured shapes, three of the cards heve colours with out shape, and three of the cards have shapes without colour.
Each of the two players gets 7 cards and the last one is put face up in the middle. In turns, the players play a card with either the same shape or the same colour as the one played last on the pile.
When the players have no valid card to play, they pass.
When both players pass, the one who passed first can start afresh, playing any card she/he chooses.
Only a card with the same shape can be played on a card with a colourless shape.
Only a card with the same colour can be played on a card with a shapeless colour.
The winner is the player who first empties his/her hand.

Protect your Great Depression-era property from The Hole-in-the-Boot Gang, a clan of absurdly destructive hobos that has invaded your town! Use "The Hobo Code" to direct the hobos away from your poperty and towards your neighbors’ properties instead.

The Hobo Code is 2-6 player, ~30 min game of strategic hot potato if instead of one potato, there's a bunch of them and instead of potatoes, it's hobos!

The complete rules can be found here:

https://drive.google.com/open?id=156a0isQI_PN5WIrbdK3FQuBoyYSbgvzhZ60tL1tc03o

boardgame: Play Ball! A Baseball Game of Skill
description:
This 1930's title is a physical-dexterity baseball game. The cardboard playing field is inset in the bottom of the box and has 23 holes punched into it, each with a result ranging from Out to Home Run. A 3" wooden bat is mounted on a wooden peg beside home plate, which has a small hole in it.

The At-Bat player puts a marble on home plate, and then flicks the bat to propel the marble across the field. If the ball fails to fall into any of the holes, it is a strike, with three strikes as usual causing an out, and three outs ending the inning.

This is an action-dexterity version of baseball, with a pressed-metal field mounted inside a wooden frame. The infield and foul territory are at a higher level than the outfield. There are two foul-out holes in the higher level, and 38 holes across the lower outfield, of which 13 are outs, 1 is a double play, 12 are singles, 8 are doubles, 3 are triples, and 1 is a home run.

There is a thin springy metal loop near 2nd base that the pitching player uses to send the ball toward home plate. There is a spring-loaded 4" wooden bat that the batting player holds back and releases to strike the ball. There is a small bell behind home plate labeled "Automatic Umpire": if the batter does not swing, a pitch hitting the bell is a strike, while one missing it is a ball.

While the game is undated, the box is labeled "A Lynn Pressman Original", suggesting that it was published after her husband Jack Pressman's death in 1959.

ENGLISH: Tired of fighting each other for religious supremacy and that these wars have caused many human deaths and the need to remain toghether against the threats of evil, the good neurents have agreed to face each other in a peaceful competition. In this event can participate up to six of the world's great religions. The winner will prevail as the only world religion and thereafter guide the designs of human beings until you play again. Instead of acting directly on humans, they have decided to conquer their sources of survival: earth, water, air, fire, flora and fauna. The first to achieve to conquer any of them will win because humans depend entirely on it to survive. Each religion will have four participants in the competition shall strictly comply with the following rules. You will be responsible for guiding your steps. Hopefully your gods help you in this mission.

Using their pawns, the players (as individuals or teams) must conquer areas that are not initially of their own color. When anyone has managed to conquer all tiles of a given resource will become the winner.

ESPAÑOL: Cansados de luchar entre sí por la supremacía religiosa, de que estas guerras hayan causado tantos muertos humanos y de la necesidad de estar unidos ante las amenazas del mal, los neurentes buenos han acordado enfrentarse entre sí en una competición pacífica. En la contienda podrán participar hasta 6 de las grandes religiones del mundo. El ganador prevalecerá como única religión mundial y guiará a partir de entonces los designios de los humanos... hasta que se vuelva a jugar. En lugar de actuar directamente sobre los humanos, han decido conquistar sus fuentes de supervivencia: tierra, agua, aire, fuego, flora y fauna. El primero que logre dominar alguna de ellas será el vencedor porque los humanos dependerán completamente de él para sobrevivir. Cada religión tendrá cuatro participantes en la competición que deberán cumplir estrictamente las siguientes reglas. Tú serás el encargado de guiar sus pasos. Esperemos que tus dioses te ayuden en esta misión.

Utilizando los peones, los jugadores (individualmente o por equipos) deberán conquistar zonas que no sean inicialmente de su color. Cuando alguno haya logrado conquistar todas las casillas de un recurso determinado se convertirá en el ganador.

Game description from the designers:

The Period Game strives to turn a typically uneasy situation into a fun, positive, learning experience. It is designed to teach participants about what is happening within the female body and how to "go with the flow."

Every turn begins with a player turning one of the two ovaries, releasing a marble into the tray below.

A red marble came out? Congrats! You got your period. A clear marble? Better luck next time.  Depending on where your marble takes you, play a protection card, preparation card or go to the nurse’s office.

Make it around the board before anyone else to win. Either way, you’re a pro at handling your period.

This game is a re-theming of Snakes and Ladders to the web comic Looking for Group: the snakes are replaced with the warlock Tiny Dick's fwooshes, and the ladders with the kraken Scooter's tentacles.

It is also an extension of Snakes and Ladders in two ways: 

     Characters: Each pawn stands for a different LFG character, with its own unique ability, such as rolling twice and choosing the higher number, or reversing the direction of the fwooshes and tentacles. 
     Hit Points: Each character has hit points, which can be lost by going down a fwoosh, or losing in a combat when one pawn passes another. They are gradually healed by skipping turns.

This 1887 card game was designed to teach children the names and images of animals. The deck has 40 cards, each with the name and large ink drawing of an animal on top, and four small drawings and animal names underneath, forming sets of five cards to be collected. Each card also bears a number from 1 to 8 to identify the set. This is intended to allow children to call for cards even if they don't yet read well.

The game uses a "go-fish" mechanism, with five cards initially dealt to each player. Players ask for another animal from another player, get another turn if they are successful, and draw from the top of the deck if they are not. Player collecting the most sets wins.

The cover of the box notes the patent registration date of Aug. 23rd, 1887 and displays the price of 25 cents. The side of the box declares: "Natural History Made Easy and Amusing: Fun, Wit and Knowledge combined."

The actual U. S. patent application was filed on 19 January 1888 and granted 4 December 1888 to Asa M. Fitch of Seymour, Indiana: US393969A.

The ancient reptilian civilization of the Sarens disappeared millions years ago. They knew all about matter manipulation and hyperspace travel. The remains of this civilization plunge into oblivion are spread across Heos’s realms and are full of powerful relics that defy the imagination.

Expeditions have engaged in an archaeological journey on behalf of powerful factions and their members will use all of their abilities to achieve their ends. For prophecy is that when enough relics have been found, a dimensional portal will open to go on the moon Wym, the cradle of the Secret of the Sarens...

Each player leads an expedition in order to collect Sarens relics. Expeditions are made of 3 type of cards: Regions, Classes & Factions: 

     On each Region card, you will find 2 to 4 relics.
     Each Class card has only 1 relic, but it is balanced by abilities like looking at the other players cards or destroying a type of card.
     Each Faction has a permanent effect that can reverse the situation.


Players will activate their cards in order to find more Relics than the other players and try to guess the other player's score in order to end the game at the right time.
family: Versions & Editions: Board Game Versions of Role-Playing Games

description:
Nodes is a strategy game for two or four players. The game board is octagonal, and each space is the intersection of a horizontal, vertical, and two diagonal lines. There are thus eight lines emanating out from each space in the manner of a compass rose.

There are two types of pieces: nodes and units. Nodes are akin to the king in Chess. They only can move one space, and a player wins when they surround their opponent's node. Units are akin to pawns in chess. They are the main troops in the game and form the basis of each player's strategy. Each player has one node and eight units throughout the game.

Unlike chess, however, the placement of the nodes on the board also determines how the units can move. The lines that extend in eight directions from the space a node occupies are called "lines of communication." If a unit is located along a line of communication the unit can move, whether it's the player's node or their opponent's. If the unit moves into a space that is also along the same line as a node, it can move again. But if a unit moves out of line or starts a turn out of line, it can't move.

A player's turn involves moving as many pieces as they want as many spaces as they want in order to defend their own node and attack their opponent's node. Every turn asks the players to make strategic decisions about when to attack, when to defend, and how to do both with only eight units. At the end of a player's turn, they move their node, and the other player makes their move. The game ends when one player is unable to move their node.

boardgame: The Beast at the Gates: Drewry's Bluff 1864
description: Beast at the Gates is a hex and counter brigade level game simulating the Battle of Drewry's Bluff, published in Yaah #7. It uses old school mechanics but features leader personality ratings, a streamlined activation system, random events, and simplified artillery rules. Six scenarios allow players to explore the possibilities of this often overlooked battle.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Description from the publisher:

The Fast-Action Packed Drawing and Guessing Game for Outdoor Sporting Enthusiasts! "Ready, Aim...Draw!", is the zany fast-paced action game of sketching, drawing, doodling, guessing and yelling. Entertaining words and phrases from all outdoor sporting activities including fishing, hunting, camping, boating, first aid, sporting safety, wildlife, trapping, wildlife conservation and nature test and challenge the drawing skills of the Sketcher and the perceptive guessing skills of the Guessers. As Players sketch, individuals or team members attempt to guess the word or phrase with correct guesses being rewarded with points or colored poker chips. Collect all four chips and play resumes at the "Roulette Wheel" where with a little luck the player can win the game!

boardgame: Our America: A Game for All Generations
description: A family game of fun facts about the U.S.A. Fun for all generations! Learn about the history, heroes and heritage of the U.S.A. as you play. Roll the color die to see which of the six different categories of questions is yours to answer. Have fun following the state flags around your own game board. First player to collect all six category cards and reach the finish square wins!
A 36 Card Expansion for Shindig Machine with new artwork that expands the storytelling possibilities of the core set.
The aim of the game is to answer the rhyming riddles correctly. The correct answer is shown on the other side of each card.

The official board game of Bake off TV Show.
In turn the players play the role of judge, while the other players are competitors and perform 2 tests:

     Technical test: each competitor receives a card depicting a sweet and must put on his plate the ingredients that he thinks are appropriate to realize, quickly taking them from those places in the center of the table. The first to complete their own sweet takes the whisk on the table. Depending on the correctness of the collected ingredients, players gain prize chips and cards for the next test.
     Creative test: each competitor creates a sweet using the ingredient cards in your possession. The best recipes according to the current judge gain prize chips.


Players insert their chips within their paper cups.
When everyone has played the role of judge the game ends: who has the most chips is the winner.

boardgame: Alla ricerca di Dory: Occhio al tentacolo
description:
A board game based on the Disney/Pixar film Finding Dory.
Each player takes a Dory figure and tries to meet his four friends, avoiding the tentacles of the octopus Hank, and go home with them.
The current player spins the spinner and decides to move based on the number or color of the arrow: if the destination square is occupied by another player, the current player moves further.
After the movement, the player flips the coin of the target square and, according to it, he can: meet one of his friends, turn the spinner again, move to a square he likes or rotate Hank tentacles 90 degrees. It is useful to remember what is depicted in the chips, so you better choose whether to move by color or number.
If the spinner lands on octopus Hank, the player rotates 90 degrees clockwise or counterclockwise: if its tentacles hits one or more figures, they come back to the starting square.
The winner is the first to meet all 4 Dory friends and returns to the starting square.

This rare 1937 game from obscure publisher Ray-Fair Company uses two special dice to generate its results. Both are six-sided, but are marked with specific results, each with a line and number underneath. The rules say the dice are designed to "give results almost IDENTICAL with the averages of corresponding plays in REAL BASEBALL."

The game's dice, rules and red and blue wooden player tokens came in a small box with a separate 19" square board.

This pocket-size wartime football game consists of a block of wood less than 5" a side that is wrapped in simulated leather. Included in a cardboard drawer in theblock are an unusual 48-space spinner, four wooden pegs and a rules sheet with results table. The board has 100 holes on the field punched diagonally between each printed 10-yard division on the field. There also are holes to mark "Yards to Gain" (1-10) and downs (1-4).

The results table is laid out in columns. The first two columns handle kick-offs and returns. The remaining six are the play call options for the offensive player: Line Plunge; End Run; Trick Play; Forward Pass; Lateral Pass; and Punt.

There are 48 rows of results, each indicating the yards gained or lost on the called play according to the result on the spinner. Field goals may be attempted within 30 yards, and succeed on spins of 1 or multiples of 5; points after touchdown score on an even number.

Ypsilonie: Cesta do vyjmenované země is an accessible light family game about an expedition to a magical kingdom. The game is mainly roll and move while trying to teach children(as well as adults) a little bit about czech grammar rules. The game is one big adventure (cca 50 minutes playing time) and three mini games (each around 25 minutes).

Each player is a City Planner in New Orleans. All players must begrudgingly work together building levees to keep the city from flooding during the next big storm. During a turn, one player will be designated as the City Manager and select 3 levees for the City Planners to bid on. The levee that gets the most money, will be built. Unfortunately everyone has a different vision of the city's future and wants some districts to be destroyed for future revitalization.

In this semi-cooperative game, players will make and break promises to save and destroy the districts according to their secret goals. The game ends when the great storm comes and floods the unprotected districts. Players earn points for successfully fulfilling the their secret goals, and lose points for failure.

Darkrock Ventures: Expansion Pack #1 includes ten new cards to enhance your Darkrock Ventures experience!

Contains ten brand new, unique cards to add to your Darkrock Ventures base game:

     5 New Research Cards
     5 New Corporate Sponsors card
     1 Proof of Purchase card (not used in gameplay)

A strategy board game of thinking and drinking. This game plays on an old world tavern theme where you try to capture an opponents glass of ale using a set of stone game pieces. The players can utilize augments such as the sword, shield, horse, and war ram in order to gain an advantage.

On your turn you may move any two stones up to a number of spaces equal to that particular stones designation. The Fate stone's maximum movement is determined by rolling 1d6 at the start of each of the players turn. Once two stones have been moved, play moves clockwise among the players.

On their turn a player can attack an opponents stone, sending it back across the board to it's starting position within it's stockade. However, this causes the attacking stone to become knocked prone and vulnerable to being pinned by an opponent moving on top. The pinned stoned cannot move until the start of a turn where it is not longer pinned.

Using augments such as the horse or the shield gives you a bonus to movement or defending against attacks, receptively. Use of the war ram and sword give offensive bonuses.

The game ends once a players moves three of his five stones into the stockade of an opponent, effectively capturing his glass of ale and winning the game.

The game incorporates optional rules for playing with your favorite beverage.

This is a 'car' promo token for the game Dream Home. The first player to complete a two-card garage receives the 'car' promo token, which is worth 1 point.

The promo token is treated as every other furnishing token.

A book with 200 printed Trivial Pursuit cards, offering 1200 questions and answers from the following categories:

Jazz, Blues & Big Band
Popular Music
Rock
Folk & Country
Classical, Opera & Ballet
Popular Lyrics

boardgame: Trivial Pursuit Questions: 1200 Brand New Questions and Answers
description:
A book containing questions for use with Trivial Pursuit in the following categories:

Geography
Entertainment
History
Art & Literature
Science & Nature
Sport & Leisure

This is a set collection game using a deck of cards with historical figures who played important roles in the Bakumatsu period (1853-1867) on them (these cards also serve as a standard deck of cards). Each player is dealt five cards. On their turn, the player draws one card and discards one card face up in front of them, trying to build one of 43 sets, which are thematic (people from the same domain, people involved in the same incidents, etc.), require 2 to 5 cards, and are rated from one to fifty points. When a player is one card away from completing his or her set, they say "Iza! Jinjo ni!" (Roughly, "Let's fight properly), and discard their card sideways. If they see the last card they need in front of another player, they may take it and finish their set. When a player finishes their set, they say "Bakumatsu," and discard their last card. They are done. The game ends when the deck runs out, at which time the sets are scored, highest score wins and deals the next game.

boardgame: I Racconti del Libro della Giungla
description:
A racket wakes you up in the middle of the night! A lightning set a tree on fire: we have to stop it or it will burn to ashes the entire jungle.. How will you deal with it? Will you use sand or water to cover it or you will howl to the moon to ask your animal companions for help? Everything you will do to save your pack will be told for centuries as a legend to the young wolves around the Rock, during the long and wild jungle nights.

I Racconti del Libro della Giungla ("Tales from the Junglebook") is an innovative storytelling game thanks to the impartial scoring system. It has an engine made of 27 element cards as objects and moves. A few of these cards will be given to players and will be their hands.
During you turn you tell a story starting from a quest card incipit. You can mention 3 elements at most in your tale. If another player has the corresponding element card, he has to show it to take a point but help you gaining points at the end of the quest. 
When you reach the story end throw a die and add to the result the number of element cards shown by other players. If the result is the same as the quest card's at least, you take points from the quest card.
Allies like Baloo and Bagheera and enemies like Jakala and Shere-Khan will give bonus and malus to the quest.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Movie Plotz: The Sequel is a standalone expansion to 2014's Movie Plotz. All new Movie Plotz cards!

Players begin with a simple plot (a simple sentence) then draw cards to modify the movie concept. With each draw, players get to redo the pitch and make it even more exciting. In the end, the final plot will be a guaranteed hit!

The game lasts for approximately 15 minutes, until all cards have been played. Depending on the rules variation that you choose to play, the player with the most points will win.

Game description from the publisher:

Narcos is a game that will allow you to play a part in the creation of the Medellin drug cartel. Travel with your associates along the road from Medellin to Miami, avoiding police controls to complete your job as smoothly as possible. For every failed delivery there’s someone who’ll pay and you’d better hope it’s not you. In NARCOS each player will have to play his role for everything to go as planned. But be warned, double crossers hide behind every corner an under cover cop could catch you red-handed, or worse, one of your associates might as well decide to cheat you.

Game preparation
Shuffle the character cards and distribute them face down ,one to each player. Each player will check the character he has been given and put the card in front of himself,careful to keep  it face down all the time. Shuffle the Delivery deck and give 5 cards to each player. Put the deck in the middle of the game table in order to allow every player to easily reach it. Leave some free space for the discard pile.
At no time during the match should anyone ever reveal his character identity by any mean, not should anyone give details of the cards he has in his hands or those who have been given or received during the match. However, it is possible for the players to talk during the INTERROGATION phase to try saving their own neck and to avoid being eliminated. 
Once every player has his own character and his own hand, you can continue to the next phase of the game.
END OF THE GAME:
The game ends when one of these conditions occurs:
Drug traffickers victory:
The DRUG TRAFFICKERS win if they manage to successfully complete the expected number of deliveries before the end of the turns (based on the numbers of players)
Or
The DRUG TRAFFICKERS win if they eliminate EVERY federal agent
Or
an individual DRUG TRAFFICKER can decide to starts his own business and win the match on his own(VICTORY BY MONOPOLY)
Federal agents victory:
FEDERAL AGENTS win if they manage to prevent the drug traffickers to successfully complete the expected number of deliveries.
Pablo Escobar victory:
PABLO ESCOBAR wins if during any delivery round he manages to obtain FOUR PARLAMENTARIAN cards AFTER haing passed the four cards of that turn to the player on his left side. Or PABLO ESCOBAR might also get a VICTORY BY MONOPOLY.

Alkisah  (Eng: Once upon a time) is a game of connect the dots that could be played by 3-6 players. At each turn, a player makes a word beginning with the letter that are printed on Letter cards taken and continued the story to be answered by the next player. The game continues until one The End card open then the next player opens a new Character card. Each game consists of 3 Character cards and 3 The End cards. The game ended after all 3 The End cards opened. For each Letter card successfully taken player gets 1 point. There is a bonus round where each player puts together a word from the successfully-taken Letter cards to add points.

Alkisah does not include the hourglass in the sales package, but the player may use the hourglass that lasts approximately 1-2 minutes to limit the time each player in finding the right words. If a player is not be able to do so, then at the end of the game, minus 1 point for each letter card that is missed.

There are some Effect cards mixing together to add more joy and fun of the game.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Promo card given out during the Kickstarter for the Dice Tower.

If another player plays a Mischief card in your room, flip this card. They must play the Mischief card into a different players' room. (you may not use this card's ability again once it is flipped. If there is no other player to choose they must play another card.)

Description from the publisher:

Fast-play solo stat sports sim dedicated to the early years of European Soccer knockout competitions. Rulers of the Continent permits you to replay entire tournaments, with scorers, in just a few dice rolls per match.

Individual sets are available, each including the base rulebook necessary to play, for the European Champions Cup, Cup Winners Cup and  UEFA Cup.

For each match, teams are rated based on the number of goals they scored and conceded in the historical tournament, and these ratings are compared to identify a column on a chart.  Along with a 2d6 roll, this determines the initial number of goals scored.

Teams have a "third dice" stat ensuring that those that did well in the actual tournament have a better chance of advancing, while still allowing for shock results and giant killings.  A d10 roll determines whether or not each team gets to adjust the initial goal results, with the "better" teams more often gaining this advantage.

A percentile dice roll provides the name of the scorer of each goal, again based on the historical results of the competition.

With just a handful of rolls per match, an entire Tournament can be completed, and a Champion crowned, in a single sitting.

Builds on the game engine of the designer's World Cup soccer game, Dream To End All Dreams.

boardgame: Marvel Dice Masters: The Defenders Team Pack
description: Marvel Dice Masters: The Defenders Team Pack features Hulk, Iron Fist, Jessica Jones, and other members of the Defenders roster on 24 cards and 16 dice, and it can be paired with any Marvel Dice Masters starter set.

The dungeons of Doomgloom are said to be treacherous, in fact, those “brave adventurers” who’ve said “Doomgloom Dungeon? No problem.” have never been seen again.

No one knows what lies in Doomgloom Dungeon as no one has ever returned to tell the tale but you’re about to change all that. Having trained for many years and been through many adventures, you think it’s time you tackled the ultimate adventure that is Doomgloom Dungeon.

Doomgloom Dungeon is a light and quick solo 'card dungeon crawler' that will take around 10 to 15 minutes to play. You will be playing cards and rolling dice in order to beat the various Dungeon cards that appear.

Game description from the publisher:

Scratch the planet, discover the planet!

Many people often imagine what is in the universe and on alien planets. Planet Surprise is a game in which you can fulfill your dreams of exploring the universe. Perhaps you can find out what's hidden on the planet in the shape of a tennis ball, or what's in the mysterious flying box?

The game is wonderfully minimalist as each player has only one scratch card (similar to a scratch ticket). On your turn, you can land on a planet, which means that you scratch the scratching layer of one planet (with a fingernail or a coin) and look at what is hiding underneath. You will always find a point value underneath, positive or negative, and also certain surprises in the form of funny graphics. The goal is to uncover the planet with the highest score. (During ten actions, you will uncover about ten planets out of sixteen.) Each set includes different deployment of points and different jokes.

Instead of scratching, you can navigate the opponent to land on a planet of your choice, without doubt the most beautiful one of those that you discovered. Some actions will allow you to gain insight into your opponent's game so that you might know better on which planets it pays to visit and which to avoid.

Planet Surprise originated as a little brother of a much larger project, a fantasy offline/online game with scratch cards called Scratch Wars. All you need to play Planet Surprise is one scratch card for each player, but they need to be all from the same set of cards (as players need to explore the same universe).

This might be described as a minimalist baseball game. It consists of a single plastic-laminated sheet which includes a small baseball diamond, a scoring box, rules of play, and a remarkably detailed results table that aims to reflect the probabilities of a wide range of plays.

The playing mechanism is a regular pack of cards, and each batting player turns over cards and records the outcomes until reaching three outs for an inning.

The table shows a specific result for each card in the deck, including contingencies such as tagging up on a fly out. The 52 cards produce 7 singles, 2.6 doubles, 1 triple and 1.4 home runs (7 of hearts = HR in innings 3, 6, 7 and 9, otherwise a double). There are 3 walks and 2 errors, while the rest are outs of various kinds.

The special situations apply to the three double play cards along with the 7 of spades and 3 of diamonds, where the outcomes vary depending on combinations of runners on base.

All players are dealt the same letter cards. Players simultaneously try to find and write down as long words as possible using only the letters available. Letters are divided into two categories, red and black. Each black letter used scores two points and each red letter three points. Bonus point die rolls are awarded when players reach certain thresholds on the score track. Game is played over four rounds with increasing amount of letter cards. First round with seven cards, then eight, nine, and finally ten letter cards.

Who can find the longest word? Whose word brings most points? Young players learn spelling during the game.

Contents: board, 288 letter cards, 2 sand timers, player pawns, paper and pens, die, rules.

boardgame: Tanto Cuore: Arisa Hayakawa – Tabletop Day 2016
description:
Cleaning Maid - Arisa Hayakawa

Cost of 5. Allows paying -2. If you do, select an Event card in your Private Quarters, and return it to the Town. Gain Love equal to half (round down) the Employ cost of the card you returned this way.

This was originally given away to celebrate TableTop Day. Unfortunately with this being a new general maid, you would need 10 copies to actually be able to use her in a game. With the International Kickstarter, she was included in a pledge level and an add-on and each place included 10 copies of her.

Domino Castle is played on a 7 x 8 grid with standard domino tiles. The game is for two players. At the beginning in two opposite corners is one tile the 0/0 and 6/6. Tile 0/0 is the starting tile for a player and tile 6/6 for the other player. All other tiles lie face up on the table.
 In your turn you choose one of the open tiles and put it at your starting tile. One side of your new tile have to fit to your starting tile. If you start with the 6/6 tile you have to choose a tile with a 6.

In the second round you can choose where you place your new tile. It is not allowed to place a tile with different numbers close together; at the corner it is ok. If it`s possible you could place a new tile in the second (or even third) level. In this case both sides of the new tile must fit with the sides of the two tiles beneath.

A player who can't move passes. The other player continues. To determine the winner, the numbers of stones in the first level are multiply with the number of stones in the second level.

A storytelling card game. Includes 55 unique cards (no duplicates) of photographed wildlife, mostly of raccoons. The game is "creative and non-competitive". Game play variations abound. For example players alternate drawing a card from the face down draw pile and place face up, and then describe the scene in a single sentence as it relates to previously drawn cards. A sequence of cards, left to right thereby tells a story. A variation has each player offer a scene description and then all players vote on the best one. Another alternative has players maintain a hand of cards and on their turn offer a card to extend the story, or all players offer one card and then vote on the best story extension with that one card added to the card sequence. Although not intended as a competitive game, it could be played where the winner is the first that runs out of cards (discarding your last card requires coming up with an ending better than any other players' story extensions), or collects the most rejected cards. A variation of "Go Fish" can be played where one player turns over a card, and then all players attempt to turn over the best matching card. The player with the best match as determined by vote takes the two matching cards, all other cards are turned back over, and that player turns over the next card to be matched.

This undated dexterity game consists of a pistol that shoots wooden darts, a wooden pot and post containing 8 round wooden beads (the beans), and a target area, with 13 holes valued 5 to 25, inset in the box bottom. The pot is filled with the beans and placed on top of the post, which is inserted in a hole at the front of the target area.

The rules say players should shoot from a distance of 8-10 feet, and get three shots each. When they hit the pot, it spills the beans onto the target area. Any that fall into holes count that many points. Any that remain loose are replaced in the pot for the next shot.

Winner is the first player to accumulate 250 points. Since the maximum possible score from a single round is 100, a game will last at least three rounds.

This is a Monopoly-style election game with some add-on trivia. Each player starts with $5.5 million and rolls dice to move around the 62-space track. A player landing on a state space may pay $20,000 times its electoral votes to win it. If he doesn't want to or can't pay, the state goes up for auction. And if the state is already owned, the player pays $10,000 times electoral votes into the Campaign Fund Treasure Chest. Any player landing on that space collects all money there.

There are three kinds of random event cards and spaces: National Election Commission, National Party Headquarters and Press Conference. Each may grant or cost money or votes, and may offer the opportunity to answer a trivia question from a Presidential Seal card. Each card has two questions, one of which is chosen and read by an opponent. Answering correctly provides benefits.

The first candidate to 270 electoral votes wins, but a player may win sooner if all opponents go broke. There is a time-limit elimination process for more than two players. After 60 minutes, there is a ballot, and the player with the lowest EV total drops out and returns his states to the banks. Further ballots are held each 15 minutes until the game is down to 2 players.

Subtitled "The Trucker's CB Radio Game", this 1976 title is a roll-and-move affair that riffs off that era's fad around Citizen's Band radio. The game takes players around a lavishly illustrated map of the United States, with occasional dead-ends and many bear traps and other distractions along the way.

The inside of the box top has an extensive glossary of "C.B. and Trucker's Lingo", and many of these terms along with nicknames for states and cities are sprinkled across the board.

The box says the game was distributed by Priority Marketing Communications, a subsidiary of National Consumer Products Inc. based in Chicago.

boardgame: Die Legenden von Andor: Die Rückkehr
description:
Die Rückkehr ("The Return") is a freely-downloadable legend for two heroes. This legend tells of the return of Chada and Thorn back to Andor as a prelude to Legends of Andor: The Last Hope. It is designed specifically for the heroes Chada and Thorn and two players.
You need material from Legends of Andor: The Star Shield and Legends of Andor: Journey to the North.

German description
“Die Rückkehr“ ist eine trickreiche Mini-Legende für 2 Spieler.
Sie kann als Prolog zu Legends of Andor: The Last Hope verstanden werden und spielt auf dem Andor-Spielplan.

boardgame: Hands in the Sea: Kickstarter Expansion

description:
Dicey is a new demon dice game, with a unique feature. Rascally shaped dice make a mockery of throwing a six in this fast, fun and furiously tricky game of chance!

The result of Dicey's unique shape means the odds of throwing a 6 is now about three times harder (1 in 18) and throwing a 1 that much easier (about 1 in 3). Throwing the other four number has about the same odds as for a normal dice (1 in 6)

There are 4 ways to play depending on the number of players.

The first game named Dicey is a 3-6 player game has the objective of winning all your opponents' counters. You take one dice and 8 counters.

All players roll in real time and when someone throws a 6 they win the pot.

As you roll the following numbers

1 - Add a counter to the pot
2 - If someone spots you roll this, they can take a counter from you
3 - no action
4 - no action
5 - Must stop rolling for 5 seconds
6 - Win the pot

The second option is a two player game named Double Trouble with the objective of winning all counters.

You each take 10 counters and two dice.

You both ante one token the pot and make a single roll in turn

If you roll

A single 1 - place 1 token into the pot
A double 1 - place 2 tokens into the pot
Double 2,3,4,5 - You lose the round and the pot goes to the other player
A single 6 - You win the pot
A double 6 - You win the game automatically.

The third game is for 3-6 players named Last Man Standing, with the objective of not being the player to put the last token into the pot.

You start with an agreed number of counters each, three for a quick game or more for a longer game.

Each player in turn rolls 3 dice

1 - Put a counter into the pot
2 - Give a counter to the person on the left
3 - Give a counter to the person on the right

If you have no tokens you are not out as you may get some back.

When only 1 token not in the pot  the dice actions change

2 - move a counter left
3 - move a counter right
6 - place a counter in the pot and lose the game!

The fourth and last way to play is named The solo challenge and is for a single player. This is a challenge mode with the aim of rolling specific faces in the least number of rolls.

War & Pieces is the ultimate family game of building, tactical skill, and destruction. Each player builds a fortress with blocks and must knock the other player(s) fortresses down by launching a catapult.

Each player begins with 50 building blocks and 5 special soldier blocks, and constructs a fortress on a game mat. Rules are simple, solider blocks cannot touch the mat and building blocks that touch the map must be sideways.

Players take turns launching the stone catapult, or the ballista at an opposing player's fortress. If a solider block gets knocked down, that unlocks a special power that may benefit or harm the shooter. Players who lose all their solider blocks are eliminated. Last player standing wins!

War & Pieces is created by YouTube Superstar and family man, Roman Atwood, in conjunction with Studio71 and Justin Gary, founder of Blade Entertainment.

French cycling game with a disctintive 1970s flavour.

As is typical in this kind of games, it combines overambitious features with rules that are  quite obvious in general, though they may feature some surprises.

This game is big (the box measures 65 x 28 cms) and heavy, specially due to the seven (!) boards included. Five of these boards are just different finishes for various stages. Another board ("parcour montagne") can be either the end of a stage or a stage in itself, while the bigger board "("parcours plaine") is the one used in mosts races.

A maximum of six players manages a team of two riders each.

The riders are seventies' Cofalus (5cms, smaller than the ones featured in later games like l'Echappée Infernale or Les Géants de la Route), and two extra riders are provided for the yellow and green jerseys. 
The riders are a bit oversized for the board (which is nevertheless quite big), and there is also a trailing car (voiture balai).

Most of the time you throw two dice and move your two riders. You decide which rider you move with each dice, considering that you are not allowed to move to a field occupied by another rider, and that there are certain fields that give you advantages or disadvantages.

An interesting/unusual rule, though, is the one for the trailing car. It starts its movement when the first rider reachs km. 47, and stops at km 100 of flat stages. If there are two or three players in the game, the trailing car moves 5 fields forward in each movement. For 4 or 5 players it just moves 4 field. With 6 players, it just moves 3 fields. All the riders caught up by the car are out of the race, though they may take part in subsequent stages.

For mountain stages, movement rules change. Before a climb, riders have tos stop compulsorily in the "Plateforme" field before the climb (in which there can be no more than three riders at a time). Then, during climbs, to progress to the next field riders must throw exactly the number inscribed in that field. If the field ahead is blocked by another rider, you must throw the number corresponding to the first free field, to which you will move. As a result, riders tend to form pelotons in the climbs, and these pelotons disband quickly in the descents.

By the way, there is no trailing car in mountain stages.

Another unusual feature of this game are the different stage finishes. Riders have to arrive to the neutral zone (last field) and then they move onto that particular stage finishing board. What is most unusual is that every finish has different rules. For instance, in end of stage G you have game-of-goose rules, while in end of stage F you move forward with even numbers but backward with odd numbers. In the velodrome, you have to fall exactly on the finishing stage or else ride another lap. A flat stage can end with a climb, using the B' part of board B.

At the end of the game (the players can decide how many of the stages they want to play) the player who's riders score best in the general classification wins the game.

This is a game made by Marco Rossi, from Genoa (Italy). It is a clever middle point between a simulation replay game and a board game. You can play it by yourself, with a lot of tables, individual characteristics for each rider and a some of dice-throwing and table checking like a replay game, but you still have a board and riders, and the game can be played by several players.

There are (or maybe I should say "there wiill be") six versions of the game, corresponding to six historical periods  (one for each post WWII decade: mine is the 1966-1975 edition). There are ten teams for each historical period, plus a bonus "world championship" special set.

There are ten team (of three riders which you can chose among four fdifferent ones). That makes thirty riders, and they all take part in the race, regardless of whether there are enough players. In the (most probable) case of having less than ten players, there are rules to manage the virtual players, to take decisions for them as whern to attack and when to follow other riders, etc...

You can play the game by yourself against nine virtual teams, in which case the game is almost a replay game (thouigh you still take your decisions for one of the teams). I wonder which would be the better balance between the number of human players and the number of virtual teams for the game playability.

An individual race or stage consists of six game turns. The winner is not the first to reach a certain point in the board, but the one to ride further in these six turns.

Before the race you set up six tiles in a special window in the board , which will define the race profile. (In the case of the picture below, a mountain stage, though one starting with a "pavés" zone). You can design your own race profile, but you can also use one of the many included in the game rules. You can chose between Milano-San Remo,  Gand-Velvegem, Flèche Wallonne, Gold Race, Paris-Tours, Ronde van Vlaanderen, Paris-Roubaix, Liege-Bastogne-Liege, Paris-Bruxelles, Giro di Lombardía, a World Championship, a Time Trial World Championship, as well as 10-stage long Giro d'Italia and Tour de France.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Vuelta Ciclista a España is a dice driven race game, but it has an unusual rule.
There are five teams of five riders and a car each. The riders are numbered from 1 to 5 in each team, and  the winner is the first team with three riders crossing the finish line. 
On their turn players throw the die five times and the first throw is for rider number 1, the second for number 2, and so on...

However, at any time a player can decide to move the team's car instead of the designated rider.

There are also positive squares, marked AV (for "AVituallamiento", that is, feeding zone). Riders falling on them have an extra throw.

There is also an intermediate sprint ("Meta Volante") which also gives an extra throw to all the riders who pass it (regardless of whether they fall on it). If the result of this extra throw makes the rider fall on a C or P square, the throw is not valid and the rider may repeat the throw.
