 After square 35 there is a parking place for the cars, which cannot help riders in the final part of the race even though there is a square marked P and one marked with a C.

The game also provides classification sheets in case you want to play a stage race. 
All the stages are similar and the classification is a points classification, with 40 awarded to the winner of the stage, 36 points to the second and one point to the 25th rider.

Cycling board game produced in the DDR in 1956 about the Prag-Berlin-Warschau cycling race which was known as the Peace race, also known as Der Friedensfahrt.

The race begins in Bratislava and ends in Warsaw (as did the 1953 edition of the race). The race is divided in 12 stages. The game is played with 3 dice. The first rider to reach the even stages receives 5 points, and the second receives three points. For odd stages there are only 3 poinys awarded to the first rider to pass the city and one popint for the second.

Blue points are mountain passes; the first rider to pass through that point receives three point (none for the second).

Red lines are sprints; players who fall on these play again.

Red points with letters are race incidents (fall, punctures, injures). Players who fall on these get negative points or have to wait for some turns.

The rider with more points when all have reached Warsaw is the winner of the game.

Inland Water Transport was produced around 1950* as a promotional piece for the transportation companies Macneill & Barry Ltd and their subsidiary Kilburn & Co Private Ltd. The companies, along with their associates the Joint Steamer Companies, were involved in the transportation of goods throughout India (and, post partition, Pakistan). The game is a simulation of this regional logistic operation, featuring elements of roll-and-move with some mitigating choices, pick up and deliver, and the acquisition of routes and ports.

In the game up to 6 players each have a steamer and a train which they can move along the waterways and railways of North East India and East Pakistan (beginning in Calcutta), with movements based on dice rolls. At each port or rail depot they can collect cargo or deliver cargo. Cargo items are unique and are to be delivered to specific destinations, with varying payments on delivery. At certain points players collect "report" cards, which function similarly to "chance" cards in Monopoly, bestowing small benefits or losses on the player. Players can also purchase ports or railways, levying charges on other players that use them. The game ends once all cargo has been delivered, with the player with the most money winning.


    Publication date is an educated approximate - no date is given in the manual but historical research of dates when the involved companies were merged and subsequently changed names dates it must be between 1949 and 1956.


Designers are sadly unknown.

Game description from the publisher:

Do you know which people founded Copenhagen? Or what the former name of New York was? BrainBox Cities of the world contains 71 beautifully-illustrated cards showing famous cities of the world!
The object of the game is to study a card for 10 seconds and then answer a question based on the roll of a die. If the question is answered correctly, the card is kept and the person with the most number of cards after 5 or 10 minutes is the winner.
Made from a minimum of 70% recycled material, the BrainBox range is fabulous fun. Find out who the BrainBox is in your family – it may not be who you think it is!

boardgame: Sentinels of the Multiverse: Void Guard
description:
The team known as the Southwest Sentinels were in trouble. Chokepoint had blindsided them in their headquarters in Fort Adamant. It was a rough fight. The fact that the heroes were getting no military support in this fight, and that no alarms had been set off by Chokepoint’s entirely unstealthy arrival was not immediately concerning to them, but the Southwest Sentinels began to realize things were not quite what they had assumed.

They fled through the military installation, narrowly dodging the attacks from Chokepoint. As they entered the lower levels, Chokepoint tore through a wall before them, and then was incapacitated by a blast of power. The wall she had just destroyed housed a secret room the heroes had never seen before. In that room were four large glowing crystals, each radiating a different spectrum of energy.

Behind them, Chokepoint groaned and began struggling to her feet. She would be back at full power in no time - the heroes had a choice to make. And they choose survival.

Now, Doctor Medico, Mainstay, The Idealist, and Writhe harness the power of Oblivion Shards and fight against the end of all things. They hold back the Void when no one else can.

Sentinels of the Multiverse: Void Guard is a box set of four hero characters, complete with their own hero character cards and decks.

Classical Petteia (Poleis, Cities or the Game of Pebbles) was an ancient Greek game traditionally believed to be played by Ajax and Achilles during the Trojan War (c. 1194-1184 BC).

Petteia XXI is a variation of the ancient Greek game, expanding on the classical rules and adding new tactics which allow a modern 'XXI' (a.k.a. 21st) century player to force a win on his/her opponent, while minimizing the number of draws or blocked positions.

Petteia XXI is played with light and dark pieces, checkers or stones initially lined up on opposite sides of the board. It can be played in various size boards: 8x8, 10x8 or 12x8.

The objective is to stalemate the opponent or to capture his entire army.

Classical captures (custodial and corner) apply to Petteia XXI in addition to the following capturing tactics:

     Push and Crush Attack
     Flank Attack
     Phalanx Attack


Aditionally, Petteia XXI has locked soldiers (similar to blocked pawns in Chess) which strategically limit pieces mobility to increase capture opportunities, and to minimize the chances of draws.

http://www.chessvariants.com/invention/petteia-latrunculi-xxi

Boosting Bike is a cycling game, with a special focus on making the movement of a peloton of cyclists on the board realistic, to have a "peloton feel" in the way riders move.

The number of riders in the race is fixed to sixteen (four teams of four riders). If there are less than four players, some of them have to take more than one team.

The rules say that the game is for 1 to 5 players. Of course, in the case of playing solo, the player takes all four teams. This lets clear that, although this not just a roll-and-move game and there are a fair number of decisions to be taken at each turn, there is not a complex strategy involved in the game.

The game does not include rules for different types of roads or riders. It represents a race on a flat circuit.

The winner of the game is the player who's riders arrive first at the finish.

boardgame: Cyprus (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride)
description:
This follows the principles of the basic Ticket to Ride game on a special board with special cards and rules. The object of the game is to gain the most points by travelling various routes and outwitting other players. You will need the train pieces and the train cards from either the US or Europe base games (or you can buy them from the Days of Wonder shop.

There is an extra set of Tourist cards for the cities, each with a value to be earned by visiting that city. These cards are dealt/picked up alongwith tickets in the same quantity and with the same 'keep/dispose' rules.There are also up to 9 bonuses for the end-of-game scoring.

The board does not have any grey tracks or parallel tracks - but there are spanner cards to collect which may help or hinder you.

There are many new options in the rules: exchange all your cards at the start but keep all the new ones: discard 5 cards and pick up 5 new ones, as your turn: earn extra points: and many more. You can also call time when your wagon stock reaches 3, if you want, giving you the advantage of stopping other players completing tickets.

Classical Latrunculi (Ludus latrunculorum or the Game of Little Soldiers) was an ancient strategy game played throughout the Roman Empire (27 BC - 1453 AD).

Latrunculi XXI is a variation of the ancient Roman game, expanding on the classical rules and adding new tactics which allow a modern 'XXI' (a.k.a. 21st) century player to force a win on his/her opponent, while minimizing the number of draws or blocked positions.

Latrunculi XXI is played with light and dark pieces, checkers or stones to represent the soldiers, and a 2nd piece to identify the Dux (Leader or King). It can be played in various size boards: 8x8, 10x8 or 12x8.

The objective is to immobilize the opponent's Dux, to stalemate him, or to annihilate his army.

Classical captures (custodial and corner) apply to Latrunculi XXI in addition to the following capturing tactics:

     Push and Crush Attack
     Flank Attack
     Phalanx Attack


Latrunculi XXI has rules that limit the Duxes' mobility when they are in combat, as well as locked soldiers (similar to blocked pawns in Chess) which strategically limit the pieces mobility to increase capture opportunities, and to minimize the chances of draws.

http://www.chessvariants.com/invention/latrunculi-petteia-xxi

Wizards and Unicorns is a fast-paced card game about battling unicorns, wizards, unicornwizards, elves, and pegasus set in the city of Unicornopia. You dive into the role of one of them trying to gain control over the Stone of Sigma. In order to achieve this, you try to gather fans among the wizards in town.

In a match of Wizards and Unicorns, you battle with your foes for fan markers by playing showtime cards and hoping to keep them on stage. As the players take their turns, the draw pile will soon run empty. The game ends as soon as a player manages to get a certain number of fans or the draw pile runs empty.

The game consists of roughly 50 uniquely styled and funny cards, each featuring many different effects, offering you the possibility to play the game with many different strategies. The vast options of combining them and the growing intensity as the draw pile shrinks lead to many new and exciting situations.

boardgame: Pathfinder Adventure Card Game: Mummy's Mask – Adventure Deck 5: The Slave Trenches of Hakotep
description:
From the publisher:

Watch the sun-parched skies in this thrilling Mummy’s Mask adventure! You and your allies are called to defend the city of Wati after a massive flying pyramid appears in the skies above it! To save the citizens of Osirion, you must venture deep into the monument vault in the far reaches of the Slave Trenches of Hakotep to discover a way to bring the Sky Pharoah’s tomb crashing down to earth! The Pathfinder Adventure Card Game: The Slave Trenches of Hakotep Adventure Deck is a 110-card expansion that adds 5 new scenarios to the Pathfinder Adventure Card Game, and also includes new locations, villains, monsters, curses, traders, loot, and more.
family: Series: Pathfinder Adventure Card Game (Paizo Publishing)

boardgame: Pathfinder Adventure Card Game: Mummy's Mask – Adventure Deck 6: Pyramid of the Sky Pharaoh
description:
From the publisher:

Your Mummy's Mask adventure concludes in high-flying style! As you bring the sky pyramid down to the ground for the first time in 6,000 years, you must venture into four elemental crypts and defeat the mummy lord Hakotep's generals. Should you live through this experience, the Sky Pharaoh himself will stand against you. The Pathfinder Adventure Card Game: Pyramid of the Sky Pharaoh Adventure Deck is a 110-card expansion that adds 5 new scenarios to the Pathfinder Adventure Card Game, and also includes new locations, villains, monsters, curses, traders, loot, and more.
family: Series: Pathfinder Adventure Card Game (Paizo Publishing)

boardgame: Rising Sun over China: The Sino-Japanese War, July 1937-Nov 1941
description: Rising Sun over China( RSOC) simulates the Japanese invasion of China prior to World War II (1937-1941) on a table top map scaled at 50 miles per hex with division-level playing pieces and monthly game turns.  The simple I-go-U-go game mechanics keep play fast and focused while additional rules evoke the feel of 1930s China. Two player game as written, but can be played solo like many wargames, as there is little hidden information. Game comes in Paper Wars #83.

A game based on mimicry for two to four teams of players.

In each team's turn, one of the team's players attempts to silently mimic a specific thing, with the goal of enabling their team partners to guess which thing it is before the timer runs out. The things to be mimicked fall in one of four categories, each one of which has an associated mask to be worn by the player that will attempt the mimicry: actions (mime mask); places (Indian mask); animals and things (tiger mask); and school (teacher mask).

Teams advance their tokens along the game board by rolling one die, as long as they keep succeeding in guessing the mimicked concept after each throw of the die.

The goal is to be the first team to reach the last space in the game board (number 36).

Game contents:
-Game board.
-Hourglass.
-4 masks.
-144 word cards.
-12 joker cards.
-4 team tokens.
-1 die.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: El gran juego de Hobby Consolas
description:
Players attempt to buy famous video games for several contemporary consoles, and also the consoles themselves. Income is gained by other players renting games and consoles from them, and money is spent when they rent games and consoles from other players.

Gameplay
On their turn, a player rolls two dice and moves that number of spaces around the board. If the player lands on an as-yet-unowned game, they have the opportunity to buy it and add it to their collection. If a player owns all the games for a specific console, they may then buy consoles to rent with each game, generating even more income from opponents who land there. If they land on a game owned by another player, they must pay that player a rental price according to the value of the game and any consoles coming with it. There are other places on the board which cannot be bought, but instead require the player to: draw a "News" card and perform the action on the card; draw a "Question" card and answer a question about video games in order to win a prize; collect income; or fix a broken console (which can keep them from moving for as long as three turns).

Player elimination
If a player does not have enough money to pay for a debt, not even by selling all of their video games and consoles, they will be bankrupt and out of the game.

Goal
The goal of the game is to be the last player remaining with any money.

Background
This Monopoly-inspired game was gifted to readers of Spanish video game magazine Hobby Consolas with their numbers 54 and 55. Publication of the game was sponsored by Spanish video game retail chain Centro Mail.

Game contents:
-Game board.
-26 video game cards.
-26 console cards.
-20 News cards.
-31 Question cards.
-310 point notes.
-10 Centro Mail discount coupons.

(Additionally, two dice and one token per player are required to play the game.)
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
The game consists of 300 image squares and a modular game board. The game board has 300 different items, which must be incorporated to find image squares. Image squares also have interesting additional functions, which make the game even more exciting. The game can be played in many different ways, both basic and advanced rules. Therefore, the game is suitable for different age groups - from tiny kids to adults.

Players pull the picture-tiles from the bag and must find the needed pictures from the board. First player gets the tile as a point. First player who finds the required number of pictures first is the winner.

Shuffle: Twister is a take on the traditional game of twister with a card based shuffle. In this version you start the game sitting in a circle on the floor with a single card between each player, color side up. Then players take turns drawing a card from the remaining cards in a deck that is placed in the middle of all the players. The card you draw will tell you to place the card on another player somewhere on their body and it may tell you to touch them with a head, hand, or foot. If you're given a card you must keep it in place and not let it fall even while you're placing cards on other people. If you do drop a card you lose a life, if you lose a second life you're out of the game. Last player in the game wins.

Fishery is an abstract strategy game in which 2 players place ships and cast nets, each trying to have the largest area in their fisheries.

MATERIALS

Gameboard (7×9 grid)

12 Ship pieces (6 white, 6 black)

60 Net pieces (sticks: 30 white, 30 black)

SETUP

The gameboard starts empty. Each player selects a color, and Black goes first. Players alternate turns during the game.

PLAY

Phase 1: Place Ships

On your turn, place 1 of your ships in an empty square space on the gameboard.

Once all 12 ships are on the gameboard, continue to the next phase.

Phase 2: Place Nets

On your turn, either place a net on one of the net spaces between the squares according to the rules below, or pass:

1. A fishery is an area completely surrounded by nets, including the pre-printed blue nets at the edge of the board.

2. Each fishery must contain at least one ship, but not necessarily your own ship.

3. Each fishery must use at least one of the pre-printed blue nets at the edge of the board as part of its border.

END OF THE GAME

If both players pass consecutively, the game is over.

SCORING

For each fishery, the player with more ships in it controls that fishery, and scores 1 fish for each square space in it, including the spaces with ships. If both players have the same number of ships in a fishery, neither player controls that fishery, and neither gets the fish.

The player with the most fish wins! In the event of a tie, the second player (White) wins.

Tallinn: The City Guard is a postcard expansion for Tallinn that consists of four tokens that need to be cut from the card.

The player (or players) who first builds a tower takes one of these tokens. Another player can take a token only if they have more towers than whoever currently holds a token, but if this happens, the previous token holder must return their token to the supply. At the end of the game, token holders receive 3 points for each other player who does not have a token.

Baumland, a.k.a. Bauland, is a communication and negotiation game in which the parties involved — industry, agriculture, construction, ecology, and politics — all try to nab the most valuable sites for themselves. The first four parties try to grab the best ground, with certain locations having different values for certain parties, while the politicians try to win together with one party (without being too obvious about it in order to avoid new elections).

Different versions of the game make play more complicated through the introduction of secret collaborations. Be as honest or as mean as you like — it's up to you...
Jets Erweiterung consists of six 3D jet figures for use with the Jets board game as well as a large game board.

Description from the bottom of the box:

AEG's Big Game Night has been a hit event at Gen Con Indy for the last three years. Now you can also have all of the same excitement without buying a plane ticket! Enjoy these great AEG games, available only in this box, this year. In this box you will find 4 games from acclaimed designer Seiji Kanai (Love Letter, Lost Legacy, Cheaty Mages):

     1 copy of Love Letter -- Kanai Edition with oversized cards
     1 copy of Say Adios to the Villains
     1 copy of Eight Epics -- Kanai Edition
     1 copy of Regality and Religion -- Kanai Edition


You will also find:

     2 boxes fitted to contain up to 7 games from AEG's pocket line!
     3 empty pocket line boxes to store gaming components such as dice, tokens or even other games!

[sic]titious is a party game for adults who love word play. In the game of [sic]titious, you define just how clever or crass you want to be. We’ve loaded the deck with more than 100 humorous altered words. Just flip a card, flip the timer and come up with a definition. Players award their [sic], [sic]er and [sic]est point cards for what they think are the best (or funniest) definitions. Or, the card flipper can be judge and jury and award the word card in play for their favorite definition.

In Princesses & Unicorns, players use their identical decks to bid on three types of prizes — crowns, gowns and frogs — because a princess needs as many of these to pick from as possible. The Fairy Godmother will let you choose a prize whereas the witch will cost you a point...

Sheepdogs is a trick-taking game in which the sheepdogs are trump and four types of sheep are the different suits. Players bid to predict the number of tricks they will take and must be exactly right to gain any points. However, the player who collects the most black sheep always gets a point. Mind the wolf and the penned sheep as these cards can derail the best made plans.

There is nothing quite like a Bavarian Holiday of sampling good beer and enjoying the local scenery. In this dice game for 2-5 players, Alt, Pilsner and Weiss beer are represented by different colored dice and are each represented on the score cards. Choose the number and color of dice you wish to roll in the hopes of crossing off the empty spaces on your score sheet. Choose carefully or you will spill too much beer and be cut off or eat too many pretzels and get full. Are you the first to leave the pub, buy flowers and go home or do you push your luck and try to get one of the two really big beers?

—description from the publisher

This is a card game based on the stress of living in the Big Apple. There is a deck of 98 cards. Of these, 19 are I Survived New York! cards, which when played in front of a player, open that player to getting stressed out. There are 34 Stress Cards, of five types: Romance, Bucks, Shelter, Nine to Five, and Why Me? There are 38 Relief cards, each of which stops a specific type of stress. There are 5 Quick Fix cards, one for each kind of stress, that both provide relief and prevent that player from receiving any more stress of that kind for the rest of the round. Finally, there are 2 Reversal cards, which of course allow a player to dump his current stress on another player.

Players receive 5 cards each to start, and each turn draw one card and either play or discard one card. They may play their cards on any player including themselves. An I Survived New York card opens a player to receiving stress, one card at a time. A Relief card "closes" a stress pile, at which point that player then adds up his accumulated stress cards and notes the total on a scorepad. All the cards in the closed pile go into the discard pile, and that player is now free of additional stress until someone opens a new stress pile with an ILNY card.

As soon as one player has reached 4,000 stress points and closed his current pile, the round ends, and all cards are returned and reshuffled. When a player accumulates more than 12,000 stress points, he burns out totally and must move to Cleveland. The last survivor wins the game, unless a player accumulates exactly 12,000 points, at which point he wins instantly and becomes Mayor of New York.

This Depression-era game involves manipulating a string to move cardboard dogs. There is a sheet of very thick cardboard that is folded into a three-dimensional row of four kennels with a starting ledge in front of them. A thick cardboard dog is attached to each kennel by a string.

Players stand in a line equally distant from the kennels and with strings of equal length. They line up the dogs by leaning them on the edge of the starting ledge, and then wiggle or shake their strings so that their dogs move toward them across the playing table. First dog across the finish line wins. Any player touching a dog is disqualified.

This is an asymmetrical abstract game of movement. The game board is a grid of squares six deep by four wide, with the four squares in the upper left excluded and replaced by the game label. The result is two columns of four squares and two of six squares, with stars at each corner numbered from 1 to 31.

The stars are connected by pathways orthogonally and along alternating diagonals, so a given star may have as few as three connections on an edge and eight in the middle of the board.

One player takes the Chuco, a single large black plastic figure, and the other player takes 12 smaller blue hunters. Pieces may move one space along any path to an empty star, but the Chuco can jump an adjacent hunter if there is an open space behind it.

The Chuco moves first, starting on star #13, third rank in the middle. The hunters start across the first two ranks and on the two edge stars on the third rank. The hunters win if they can immobilize the Chuco by surrounding it on star 27. Since doing so requires all 12 hunters, the Chuco player wins by jumping a single hunter.

boardgame: Siege of the Citadel: Brotherhood Expansion
description:
Game description from the publisher:

Siege of the Citadel: Brotherhood Expansion adds the Brotherhood faction to the game including a sixth player board (and rules to expand the game to six players) with Nikodemus and Crenshaw as your heroes.

boardgame: WCW Nitro Trading Card Game: Hardcore Expansion
description:
This expansion adds 114 cards to the original game, including 8 new wrestlers:

"Big Poppa Pump" Scott Steiner
Brian Knobbs
Hollywood Hogan
Norman Smiley
Ric Flair
Tank Abbott
Terry Funk
Vampiro

The focus of this set was allowing players to take the action outside the ring and perform special moves that could only be used on the arena floor.

Game description from the back of the Slap Pack booster box:
Get in the Ring! The high-flying action of the WCW Nitro trading card game spills out of the ring and into your hands with the Hardcore expansion set! Grab that folding chair and jump into the melee with the newest hardcore Wrestler cards. Create crushing decks with all-stars Funk and Vampiro or ring monsters like Tank Abbott and Big Poppa Pump. Collect them all and watch your opponent beg for mercy.

Adventure Land: King & Princess features three new scenarios, as well as new components, for Adventure Land that bring even more variety into play. There are various challenges awaiting heroic adventurers: The princess has been kidnapped, the super fog creatures are attacking the capital city, and the king needs to be protected. The three new and very different scenarios make it continually varied and always exciting.

1. Kidnap of the princess
Who will be the first to collect all the keys and have enough combat strength to rescue the princess?

2. Uprising of the fog creatures
The players fight together against the super fog creatures in order to survive this cooperative adventure.

3. Rescue of the king
The full game board entices you with herbs, swords, and companions. Who can protect the king and collect the most of each item?

Meine große Obstgarten Spielesammlung is a gigantic game compilation of memory, card, tactile and dice games that includes both the original Obstgarden game and nine other games in the same box.

German publishers info:
Riesige Spielesammlung rund um den Obstgarten
• 10 in 1: original Obstgarten-Spiel UND 9 weitere, abwechslungsreiche Spielideen in einer Packung
• mit Memo-, Karten-, Fühl- und Würfelspielen
• umfangreiches, hochwertiges Spielmaterial aus Holz und Pappe

Miniatures wargame played on a square grid, attempting to be more realistic and less chess-like than older similar efforts, without the complexity of map-based free movement wargames. Infantry and cavalry are represented by tiny painted metal figures placed in holes drilled in wooden tiles. Figures are removed to show losses from combat, although the exact number of soldiers remaining in each unit is tracked using pencil and paper. Artillery and various supply and naval units are represented by metal or cardboard figures.

Wooden blocks are placed on the board to show heights, and players can optionally build cardboard terrain following illustrations in the rulebook to place on the board instead of just using colored cardboard tiles to represent different terrain types. The cardboard terrain is flat and placed standing along the edges of map squares so that units can still be placed in the squares.

Description from the publisher:

Oh dear! The moon has lost his radiant magic staff! Without the mystical staff the moon cannot wax, it can only wane. But if the sparkle sprites can build a ladder along the stars before the moon vanishes behind the cloud, Paul may be able to climb up the ladder, returning the magic staff to his friend, the moon. Who would like to help Paul and the sparkle sprites? A cooperative memory game that also includes a competitive version.

Description from the publisher (German):
Oje, der Mond hat seinen leuchtenden Zauberstab verloren.
Ohne ihn kann er nicht mehr zum Vollmond werden, sondern nimmt immer weiter ab. Doch wenn die Funkelfeen an den Sternen entlang eine Leiter Richtung Mond bauen, bevor er hinter der Wolke verschwunden ist, kann Paul an ihr hochklettern und dem Mond seinen leuchtenden Zauberstab zurückgeben. Wer möchte Paul und den Funkelfeen helfen?
Ein kooperatives Memo-Spiel mit Wettbewerbsvariante.

     toller 3-D-Spielaufbau mit abnehmendem Mond
     in 3 Schwierigkeitsstufen
     plus Gute-Nacht-Geschichte zum Vorlesen

boardgame: A la Conquista del Reino Cobra
description:
Game Objective: Conquering the Kingdom Cobra, dominator of all Tribes; in each of the four tribes they have been chosen the three best warriors in order to go and fight and kill Cobra, so grab your kingdom and dominate the other tribes.

How to play: Each player will choose three tiles of the same color, and place them in the corresponding color Tribe.

Each player may draw the first tile with any number, the following can only put them into play when you take out a 1 with the dice, once you have 3 chips in play, you can move on every play you prefer.

Each time a player take out a 1 with the dice can repeat turn, but if you take a 3 consecutive results dice with 1 on the dice, will have to return to the Tribe box that matches your color and wait to get out again, can meanwhile play with the other tiles.

When a tile falls into a square in which there is already a tile from another player "kills" it, forcing her to return to her "tribe". The player who has "killed" advance 10 squares with any of your tiles (in the boxes marked with a circle can not kill another tile).

Two cards of the same color in a square form a barrier that can not be overridden by other tiles. These barriers can also be formed in the boxed circled by two different colored tiles. These barriers have to be dissolved when the player or players that have formed check out a 6 with the dice.

Each player will have to go around the board with each of his tiles and end its route (by its corresponding color) in the center of the board to get the "Kingdom Cobra". Each tile will introduce each player will have to fight the "Cobra". You will have to throw the dice once by its tile and again by the "Cobra". If the number drawn by the Cobra is higher than yours, your chip will return to his tribe, having it put back into play. But if the player takes the number is greater than remove the Cobra, the "Kingdom Cobra" will stay.

Winner: The first player who wins their three tiles at the Cobra, gets overcome, takes over the "Kingdom Cobra, and winning the game.

boardgame: Warhammer Quest: Silver Tower – Arcane Heroes
description:
This box set contains five plastic Citadel miniatures, providing a collection of new heroes with an emphasis on wielding arcane powers.


     Chaos Sorceror Lord
     Skink Starpriest
     Dark Elf Sorceress
     Skaven Grey Seer
     Stormcast Knight Heraldor

boardgame: Warhammer Quest: Silver Tower – Hero Cards
description: A collection of 44 physical stat cards, featuring all currently available characters in the My Heroes app, to allow the use of a range of new heroes within the Silver Tower. Does not come with any citadel miniatures.

Stuff the Universe is a family friendly, turn based strategy game where players explore the universe to amass the most victory points. No game is ever the same as the universe unfolds while the players explore it, the Ancients arrive based on player actions, and the difficulty of navigating the sectors is affected by the battles that occur within them.  
  
Equip your ship with crew and weapons to safely navigate the universe, colonize new worlds, and combat aliens that get in your way. The fate of your faction is up to you; what edge will you find that leads them to victory?

SECAN is an innovative number puzzle game designed to challenge your mental arithmetic and combinatorical skills. Add, subtract, multiply, or divide two adjacent number tiles to solve the puzzle. Play one-on-one or in teams of two or more in this fast-paced game of number strategy.

Easy to Play
Players choose beginner, intermediate, or advanced level.  Based on the level, they randomly draw a set of tiles and arrange them in a matrix. When all teams are ready, the first of three 5-minute rounds begins.  Players add, subtract, multiply, or divide numbers to solve the puzzle.

The team with the best two out of three rounds wins the game!

boardgame: Batman Miniature Game: Suicide Squad Game Box
description:
We're bad guys, it's what we do...

This fantastic new 2 player box set from Knight Models allows you to assemble a team of some of the most dangerous villains in the DC universe, and take the fight to the streets of the Batman miniatures Game. Play as Harley Quinn, Deadshot, Captain Boomerang, El Diablo, Killer Croc, Katana and Slipknot, or use the Jared Leto Joker and his crew of psychos and misfits.

You and your scientist crew are battling against industrial fishermen, oil companies and resorts trying to exploit the resources from a newly discovered coral reef located in an archipelago of 7 islands. In this cooperative adventure, you will have to work together and help each other with your unique abilities to be able to save the coral reef from destruction!

To do so, you need to show the government that the life's diversity in the coral reef is unique enough to justify changing the area into an aquatic national park!

On their turn, players will have 4 actions to help discover new species. They can either dive and explore, exchange information, go on an island to close a hotel, switch their coral tiles for new ones or get the help from friends and animals in the reef. The game is mostly played by moving your character, placing new tiles, discovering new species and giving the right information to your partners at the right moment.

With a good plan and a lot of cooperation, players have the possibility of saving enough species from extinction, thus showing the government that the area is worth saving! If too many species fall and disapear, your team of scientists will have failed their mission and the coral reef will be destroyed forever ...

Will you be fast enough in your discoveries or will the coral reef be forgotten before you achieve your goal?

Crazy Turf is a crazy race of animals.

You are going to bet on the first three ones (a secret objective given to you at the beginning of the game).

Each turn you will draw a card from the animal deck ( You have one in hand at the start of the game), and you will play one of those cards.
You can either use it to:

     move the animal shown on the card
     move any animal on the shown field


And variant add some powers to the animals.

The elements are at war for the throne ,Choose your side and race to the crown. The main goal of the game is to get to the crown first in order to win, do all you can to stop the others.

Games consist of choosing your element and character. Each character and element has special abilities and different characteristics , for example: water does extra damage to fire element, fire does extra damage to nature, nature does extra damage to electric, and electric does extra damage to water. During the game you will battle against enemies and players, find random encounters, events and much more.

Game play:
Path will be tile based, placing cards to reveal the path ahead. D6 Dice will be rolled in order to determine where to move. The path has four colors (each element) and the dice will have the color you will move onto or the number of spaces.

Battles will consist of dice rolls to win, each player has a total life points. Dice is rolled and based on image on die is the action taken (defend or attack)

All players have same base damage and HP, it can be increased by item cards like weapons, armor and occasionally events.

Weapons:
Will be bought with gold or found along the way. Some weapons contain special abilities. Use them wisely.

Events:
In game you will events and depending on your choice it will change the outcome. Careful one choice can help you or damage you. 
The events will go from finding a chest with treasure with gold or items,a trap or even finding a rare egg than will hatch into a powerful ally. Some events will not only affect one player but many such as earthquakes or gravity increase.

There is special event cards that will rarely play in game, such as: swap places, a tornado (which makes you loose weapons or armor) hired thieve (steals and item from one player and give to player who drew card) and many more.

S.P.A.C.E. (Space, Planets, Asteroids, Conquer, Explore) is a game about the exporation of the Solar System.
It has some didactic contents (such as planetary features, rudiments about the probes etc.) but it's a "real" game in every aspect.
In each round, a player must choose one of these actions:
a: scientific research (i.e. draw 2 cards)
b: build a part of the probe by spending cards
c: start a mission toward a planet, once the probe is complete
d: play a mission card
e: play an ALERT card (normally against other players)
f: send an already built part of probe to supply the ISS station.

Each time a player explores a planet, they can choose the Discovery side of the planet card (full amount of Victory Points) or the Technology side (less points, but they'll gain a special power for the rest of the game. Each planet has a different one).

It's also possible to explore asteroids through mission cards.

The game ends when 3 planets have been totally explored (i.e. when 3 planet decks are finished) or when all the asteroids have been explored.

At the end of the game, the players will score points according to the explored planets, explored asteroids and contributions to the ISS station.

In the box there is also an atlas with infos about the Solar System.

The Saber-Toothed Cats are on a scavenger hunt across the tar pit.  Watch out!  The tar is sticky, and sometimes the cats get stuck. The Saber Toothed Cat that can remember which tiles to avoid will be the fastest tar tracker and win the game!

In this game, tiles (some of them secretly magnetic, some not) are laid out on a board. Each player's task is to move their animal to the 6 numbered tiles on the sides of the board, in a given order (blue saber-toothed cat has to visit blue 1 first, then blue 2 etc., until they reach blue 6). Player pieces start in the middle. An animal can move to adjacent tiles as long as they are not "stuck in the mud" which means they don't step on magnetized tiles. When they do, the player's turn ends and it's the next player's turn. Whoever reaches their 6 first wins the game!

The hungry animals have gathered around for snack time! It's up to you to feed these animals and quiet their rumbling tummies. The active player rolls the die and moves the corresponding animals from one player to the next until they roll a snack. Then all players get to feed their animals! The first player to feed all of their snack to the animals wins the game.

Pure luck game to teach the youngest players on following rules, matching (colors and animal pieces) and taking turns.

boardgame: Dixit: "The Werewolves of Miller's Hollow: The Pact" Promo Card Pack
description:
Three cards that were included in some boxes of The Werewolves of Miller's Hollow: The Pact.
"Witch's brew " card could be also found in Dixit: Tabletop Day 2015 Promo Pack.

User summary
High!High?High!!

In the game of High!High?High!!, players try to collect cards so that card values are arranged in ascending order, while avoiding collecting the same value as an opponent's.

Each turn, you draw a card. You may place that card face-down in front of you if the card's value is higher than the highest card you have in front of you, or lower than the lowest. You may collect up to five cards in this way, but players may end collecting earlier because bonus points are added according to the order they finished.

At the end of game, reveal all cards players have collected. You add up total value of your cards from the lowest, but if one of your cards has the same value as one of an opponent's card, you must stop counting immediately. Add the bonus points previously mentioned, and that's your score. The highest scorer wins the game.

Backs of cards are color-coded according to their values. Cards with values 1-4 are blue, 5-8 are yellow, 9-12 are red. You should consider the color of the opponents' cards and decide if you take the drawn card or not.

Publisher's summary
数字が被らないように推理してカードを獲得していくバッティングゲームです。
裏向きに置かれたカードはターンが進むにつれ明らかになっていきます。
カードのデザインにもヒントが。
他のプレイヤーが置いたカードを推理しつつ、いち早く自分の場を完成させましょう。

boardgame: Blood Eagle: Skirmish Wargaming in the Legendary Dark Ages
description:
Description from the publisher:

Have you ever wanted to replicate the bloody feats of heroism you see in The Thirteenth Warrior movie and the Vikings! TV series, or recreate the many great stories from the Norse Sagas and Eddas or the Anglo-Saxon Chronicle and poetry? Be faced by an enemy shieldwall and then hurl yourself at it regardless of the cost? Jump onto the deck of a rival’s longship while at sea and wreak your revenge on the man who destroyed your village? Harry the coast of a foreign land and return laden down with silver and slaves? These rules allow you to fight such encounters across Northern Europe, Scandinavia and the British Isles in the 9th and 10th centuries.

Perhaps you would also want to replicate the heroic feats of such characters as Lord Uhtred, Ragnar Loðbrok, the shield maiden Lagertha, Egil Skallagrimsson, Erik the Red or the mighty Beowulf himself. These rules give you all you need to create and play heroes of legend.

You may even want to face trolls and frost giants, lead a Sidhe warband to raid a Christian monastery, master the darkest of arts and become a deathless Draugr chieftain, or even test your courage against the mighty Grendel himself. These rules also include the tools to run games set in a legendary past.

You can do all of this if you play Blood Eagle, skirmish wargaming in the legendary Dark Ages.

This undated baseball game from Fairchild seems have evolved from the company's 1946 Baseball game. This game consists of two separate decks of cards: one of 28 Pitching cards and the other of 30 Hitting/Fielding cards. Also included are a small glossy baseball diamond and scoring box, erasable crayons for marking the score and wooden discs for baserunners.

To play, the Pitching cards are shuffled and placed face down between the players. The Hitting/Fielding cards are shuffled, and each player is dealt a hand of five. The batting player must discard and replace any Pickoff card, while the fielding player must discard and replace any Error card.

For each turn, the fielding player turns over the top Pitching card, which shows either a Ball, a green Fastball, blue Curve, yellow Changeup or a red Slider. The Batting player draws a card from Hitting/Fielding deck and then must try to play a card that matches the color of the pitch. Each of these cards has a number from 1 to 4. The fielding player then draws a card from the Hitting/Fielding deck and tries to match the color of the hitting card. A color match with a number equal to or higher than the batting card is an out; if the fielding card is lower, the difference is the number of bases hit/advanced. A card that doesn't match in color counts as a zero.

The batting player may use an Error card to automatically advance the batter and all runners by one base. The fielding player may use a Pick Off card to remove a runner on base. A fielding card 2 higher than the batting card is a double play if a runner is on first; 3 higher allows a double play of any two runners.

English summary:
Stack cards for the princess to sleep upon. But beware the wobbling bed. Don't let the princess fall out of bed.

Description from the publisher (German):
Enni, die kleine Erbsenprinzessin, kann wieder einmal nicht richtig einschlafen. Liegt etwa eine Erbse unter ihrem Prinzessinnenbett? Vielleicht kann Enni besser schlafen, wenn sie noch ein paar Matratzen, Daunendecken und Kissen in ihrem Bett übereinander stapelt.
Aber aufgepasst, dass die kleine Erbsenprinzessin bei der ganzen Stapelei nicht aus dem Bett fällt.
Ein märchenhaftes Geschicklichkeitsspiel.

     Spiel in der Dose mit Wackel-Effekt
     plus Märchen zum Vorlesen
A set of 15 invention cards players can use once during the game to perform an ability.
family: Organizations: Game Designers of North Carolina

description:
Description from the publisher (translated):

Minikorona is a space-saving version of its big brother Korona. With just a 30 cm × 30 cm playing area, players need to have pinpoint accuracy to be able to get the discs into corner pockets and thus win the game.

Unlike the original Korona, this mini-sized version is only for two players.

boardgame: Crazy & Horrible Rabbit: Volume One (fan expansion for Cards Against Humanity)
description:
A third-party unofficial humorous expansion pack

Compatible with Cards Against Humanity.

Includes 80 White, 32 Black Cards

boardgame: Oh My Goods!: Longsdale in Revolt
description:
Riots are spreading in the capital Longsdale as well as rumors that there could soon be a war. The king needs your help, and your decisions might even be able to turn fate in a new direction...

Oh My Goods!: Longsdale in Revolt, an expansion for Oh My Goods!, consists of five chapters that together tell a single story. The length of the game is now not fixed, but depends on the current chapter, which has an event deck that will be compiled differently each time you play. The expansion also includes four character cards, 34 new buildings (in seven types), 22 event cards (in both German and English), and 14 chapter cards (ditto).
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Description from the publisher:

Mr. Hu is full of wisdom. Well, almost full. He needs you to
fill-in-the-blanks of his Ancient Chinese Secrets with your own words of wisdom. The question is 'who said what' and who is the 'wisest' of them all. Enjoy!

Player score points by writing the favorite answer and matching which player wrote which answer. The player with the most points wins the game!

boardgame: Falling Sky: The Gallic Revolt Against Caesar – Ariovistus
description:
Gaul, 58 BC:  Competing Celts have invited mercenary Germans and then an ambitious Roman governor into their divided land as protectors. Before Caesar’s audacious claim to the whole of Gaul leads his legions even farther north to war with the Belgae, a great Sueban chieftain will call ever more warbands out of Germania’s forests to launch his own bid for dominion.…

Ariovistus expands Falling Sky to take 1-4 players back to Caesar’s first entry into Gaul, with a Helvetian migration challenging the Aedui and Germanic Tribes pressing close behind. New components and Faction enable one player to take command of the Germanic Tribes, while veteran Roman, Aedui, and Belgic players face unfamiliar strategic dynamics and deal with new events. For the heartiest, an extended scenario covers Caesar’s full time in Gaul—nearly a decade of resistance and revolt….

To this prequel to the popular COIN Series volume, the expansion package adds tactically refined, more aggressive Non-players and extra-sturdy Forces mats usable in both original Falling Sky and Ariovistus scenarios. (A copy of Falling Sky is necessary to play.)

COMPONENTS

        25 embossed wooden pieces
       52 playing cards
        5 deluxe 5x8” player mats
       6 player aid sheets and foldouts
        Quarter-sheet of counters
        Expansion rules booklet
        Expansion playbook
        Upgraded Non-player rules booklet
        Zip-lock bag


Time Scale: 1 year per campaign (15 cards)
Map Scale: Area Movement in Gaul
Number of Players: 1-4

(from GMT website)
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
This is a small manual dexterity game produced by French game publishers Villard et Weill in the late 19th Century as part of a large box of mixed games marketed by the company.

The game consists of a small wooden top and a game board set into the box. The board is a 4 x 4 grid of squares with a diamond in the middle. The 17 resulting spaces are numbered and separated by raised cardboard ridges except for a gap between each square and its numerical neighbors. Players start the top spinning in square 1 on the upper left corner and must tilt the box to guide the top through the gaps up to the 17 (diamond) space in the middle.

The rules printed inside the box lid sets three possible victory conditions. The winner can be the player who reaches the highest number with a single spin, the one who can record the highest total from three spins, or simply the first to succeed in reaching the centre space.

boardgame: Election Frenzy: the Presidential Card Game
description:
Description from the publisher:

Election Frenzy is a new card game, based on the wild world of Presidential politics. Win the game by collecting endorsements, popularity, and media points. Attack your opponents with attack ads and scandals. Manage your campaign funds and win the Presidency!

In Election Frenzy, there are 3 rounds, and each round is split into two phases.

In Phase 1, the Campaign phase, players try to build the strongest hand possible, by collecting cards in the Popularity, Endorsements, and Media categories (suits); similar to poker, Uno, and Sushi-Go.

In Phase 2, the Attack phase, players use attacking cards to either burn or steal desired cards from their opponents; similar to Go Fish, and Bang!

The winner of each suit receives 2 electoral points, while the runner up receives 1. (Example: 1st place in Popularity = 2 pts. 2nd place in Endorsements = 1 pt. 3rd place in Media = 0 pts. TOTAL = 3 pts)

After 3 rounds, the player with the most electoral points is the President!

There are some really fun nuances to the game. Each character has strengths and weaknesses, based on the real life politicians who are being parodied. For example, Hillary has a bonus each round for the Endorsements, while Donald has a bonus each round for Media.

Each character also starts the game with a fixed amount of money. Money is needed to launch attacks. There is also money scattered throughout the deck, so even if you spend all of your cash, there is still a chance to replenish your funds by drawing the right cards.

Dominion: Update Pack contains the seven new kingdom cards introduced in the second edition of Dominion, thereby allowing owners of the first edition to obtain these new cards without needing to repurchase the entire game.

The new cards: Harbinger, Merchant, Vassal, Poacher, Bandit, Sentry, Artisan

The following cards included in the first edition should be removed when using the second edition update pack.
Chancellor, Woodcutter, Feast, Spy, Thief, Adventurer

Part of the Dominion series.

description: Simple strategy wargame on a map of central England in the 1640s. The winner after fifty turns is the player controlling the most towns. Combat uses 2d6. Published in a limited edition of thirty copies.

Exlibrium is a magical seal, which helps secret order of mages-bookworms protect the barrier between narrative and objective realities. 
The game challenges players in medieval fantasy setting - mages of seven colors will transform into barbarians, bards and assassins. Up to 5 players can fight in a battle, but there is only 1 champion.

Card-drafting game with original twist - all cards are double-sided, and a player must decide, which side he want to use this time. The game took place over three rounds - each round players will simultaneously choose one of cards from the hand to play and then pass remaining cards to the next player. Each card is double-sided and each side provides a player one of 7 characters to add to his tableau. At the end of each round the player activate all of the characters - but the character power is granted only if player has the majority. At the end of the game all characters are scored and the player with most vp earned from majority of characters and their powers through the game wins.

—description from the publisher
Spaghetti: alla Granna is a small promo - expansion to the dexterity game Spaghetti. It consist of a special blue pasta strand worth 5 points and one recipe token - alla Granna.

From the publisher:

Arcane Locations features scenarios focused on some of the more infamous ruins to be found in the Frozen City, such as Brycho’s Celestium, Walenton Manor, and the Arena Without Walls. Also included are new options for developing a wizard’s base in the ruins of Frostgrave.

boardgame: Jiffy Pop Chef Boyardee Crunch'n Munch Home Team Baseball
description:
This is an undated promotional game by packaged food giant ConAgra that pushed three of its brands. The game comes in a 9.5" x 14" envelope, and consists of a rules sheet and two folded and perforated sheets of glossy cardboard.

One sheet is made up of the playing field, stadium and 8 player cards, while the other has 18 player cards, 16 fly ball result cards, 16 ground ball result cards, 16 batter and runner figures, two dice (one red and one blue), and assorted signs and markers. The baseball stadium, scoreboards and the dice need to be assembled after separating the pieces.

Players draft their teams from the pool of 9 red and 9 blue players. The red players are long ball hitters and use the red die; the blue players hit more ground balls and use the blue die.

For each at-bat, the batting player rolls the appropriate die, which produces ball, strike, foul, fly ball or ground ball. When the ball is hit, players take one of the fly ball or ground ball cards and follow the results, which range from home runs to double plays. The usual rules of baseball apply.

"The dice game for the cunning and sly".

Each player rolls a secret "hand" of five D12 dice which they reveal simultaneously one by one, with the highest value winning that round. Points for a round are determined by rolling a D8 before players reveal their chosen die. Playing high and low dice at the right time will maximize the number of rounds and/or points won. Trying to win higher point rounds while sacrificing lower point rounds is key to maximizing points. But beware points from previous rounds can be swapped/stolen when presenting a die roll of one. The player with the most points after 10 rounds wins.

Enter a map enchanted by Unicorns, Phoenixes, Fairies and tormented by Dragons, Minotaurs, and Cthulhu!
  Woven in Myth is a quick and easy game to play with plenty of tactics and strategy.  The map, a 6x6 grid of cards, is full of adventure where no two games are alike.  Face creatures that allow for special moves, and exit the map before your opponent does.  As you move your Avatar through the corridors, you may flip and rotate cards to change the map.  A draw pile allows you to replace or resurrect cards when your corridors have vanished.  
  Be the first to move your Avatar across a map of mythical creatures while flipping and rotating cards to victory!
The Deep Space expansion adds 38 ship cards to the game. (9 unique ships)

The Dingo Ate the Baby is a fast-paced game for 2-5 players, lasting 20-30 minutes. The goal each day is to collect enough cards within your ranch in an attempt to reach the total value needed for that day, without going over. This seems simple enough, but other players will attempt to sabotage your ranch, using animals, babies, sunlight and darkness to scare, chase, overpopulate, and even eat things within your ranch.

Setup:
Deal a Player Starting Card, face-up, to each player. Set the matching player score markers on the Start/Finish space of the game board. Shuffle the 60- card play deck and place it face down on the game board in the space labeled “Draw.” Randomly determine the first starting player, who will also deal. The starting player changes each day, rotating clockwise.

Play:
At the beginning of each day, roll the 10-sided die and set it in in a position where all can see. Add 10 to this die roll. This is the target value your ranch needs to obtain for the day. For example: If a 7 is rolled, the target value for the day is 17.

The starting player deals 1 card face-up into each player’s ranch and 2 cards face-down to each player’s hand. A player’s hand is always hidden from other players.

Ranch:
The initial face-up card dealt to each player is set directly to the right of the Player Starting Cards. This signifies the beginning of each player’s ranch. The card directly to the right of the Player Starting Cards is always protected. This means that it cannot be removed by effects of other players but may be removed by a player’s own effect. Any card played on a person’s ranch is placed to the right of all the cards currently in that player’s ranch. Whenever cards are removed, the remaining cards slide left to fill in any gaps.

Actions:
When it is your turn, you can perform 1 of the following 4 actions:
1. Play a card of any color into your own ranch. This card is placed to the right of all the cards currently in your ranch. If the card has an effect, the current player may resolve the effect but is not required to do so.
2. Play a card into an opponent’s ranch that matches the color of that player’s starting card or the color of a card already in that player’s ranch. This card is placed to the right of all the cards currently in that player’s ranch. If the card has an effect, the current player may resolve the effect but is not required to do so.
3. Destroy your own protected card. This can only be done if you have more than 1 Playing Card in your ranch. When the protected card is destroyed, the remaining cards slide left while the new leftmost ranch card next to your Player Starting Card becomes the new protected card.
4. Discard a card. This allows you to draw a new card and also speeds up the current day.

At the end of your turn, you draw back up to two cards.

Special Rules:
If a player plays a card in their own ranch that matches a color already in their ranch, they may choose to destroy their own leftmost (protected) card as part of the play.

Some card effects remove other cards from a ranch. When these effects resolve, the player playing the card chooses the target. The target can be a card in ANY player’s ranch, not just the ranch of the player where the card was played. The target card may not be a protected card or the card immediately to the left of a special Moon card. Important: The player playing the card is not required to resolve any card effect and may choose to ignore that effect.

The following keywords all remove cards into the discard pile:
• Scare
• Chase
• Destroy
• Eat
• Stalk
• Stomp

Ending a Day:
As cards are removed or discarded, they are collected in a discard pile in the center of the game board. When the number of cards in the discard pile equals twice the number of players in the game, the day ends. For example: In a 3-player game, the day ends when the 6th card is placed in the discard pile.

Scoring:
When the day is over, the player with the highest ranch value, without going over the target value for the day, scores 1 point for each player they beat. In addition, each other player also scores 1 point for each player that they beat, so position at the end of each day is important. Players that exceed the target total when the day ends score no points.

Winning the Game:
A standard game is played up to 15 points. When a player exceeds 15 points at the end of a day, the game is over, and the highest total (or tie for highest) is the winner. In the case of a tie, the players may share the victory or may play an additional day as a tiebreaker.

2-Player Rules:
When playing with 2 players, it is recommended to not end a day until 6 cards have been removed or discarded instead of 4.

"Something’s afoot. The steward smiles at you like he has a secret, or like he thinks you have a secret, or like you think he thinks you have a secret. There are secret plots brewing, you’re sure of it. At the very least, there are yours. A passing servant murmurs, “The eggs are on the plate.” You frantically search your codebook for the translation before realizing he means that breakfast is ready. Excellent. Everything is going according to plan."

Dominion: Intrigue (Second Edition), an expansion for Dominion or Dominion (Second Edition), contains 26 Kingdom card types that can be used with the base game. It no longer has Basic cards and thus is no longer a stand-alone expansion; to expand to 5-6 players, use Base Cards.

Dominion: Intrigue (Second Edition) replaces six Kingdom card types from the first edition with six new types of Kingdom cards, while also replacing the blank cards in that item with a seventh new Kingdom card; these new cards are also available on their own in the Dominion: Intrigue Update Pack. In addition, the rulebook has been rewritten, one card has had a mild functional change (Masquerade skips players with no cards in hand), and other cards have received updated wording while remaining functionally the same.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Two rulesets are included:
"The Families" where You may ask missing cards of You set from any player if he has he must give it to You. If You have all 4 cards of set You can place them inf front of You. The winner is the one who collects the most animal families.
"The Trumps" where players take turns reading off the requested
information. Whoever has the highest value wins the other players’ cards and puts them under his pile. If someone runs out of cards the player with the most cards is the winner.
More versions come with the app.

An expansion for Quizduell: Das Spiel.

This book contains more than 500 additional questions and answers from 19 different categories for the base game.

Each of the 2 players have their own deck with red or blue backgrounds.
In his turn player try to play cards in a horizontal line in front of him so that the ball matches the positions of the previous cards. Are there 5 cards in line? Now is time to play the goal card and score.
Player who scores more goals is the winner.

A second after the Big Bang, the matter and antimatter that had just been created annihilated into light. Hopefully, a tiny excess of matter survived, about 1 particle per billion, providing the building blocks of the Universe we live in. Find your antiplayer and re-fight that clash between matter and antimatter. Physics is the only rule!

Big*Bang is an abstract game with a strong theme, the first minutes of our Universe. On an elongated hexagonal grid, one player tries to fuse protons and neutrons in order to form Hydrogen and Helium, while the other fuses antiprotons and antineutrons in order to form antiHydrogen and antiHelium. However, every turn they will have the choice: fuse their own nuclei, or annihilate the opponent's?

The three victory conditions (produce the most light through annihilation, form the most Helium, and form the most massive star) force players to find an equilibrium between the different options, that quickly shrink with the board as nuclei fuse and annihilate. The rules are simple, the playtime short, and you might learn some Physics!

NB: The turn sequence has been streamlined in March 2018, please download the new rulebook from the publisher page if you bought the game before! Also note that now there are several puzzles that allow solitaire play.

Description from the publisher:

Tripol is a logic game that combines simple rules, original graphic design, and interesting tactics. Your goal is to score as many points as possible by matching appropriate triangle sides, and the rules include multiple ways to play:

1. Colorful polygon
2. One color only
3. My color 
4. Three for a start 
5. Experiment (combination of the triangles)
6. Blockade
7. Barricade

boardgame: Bulgaria (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride)
description:
This follows the principles of the basic Ticket to Ride game on a special board with special cards and rules. The object of the game is to gain the most points by travelling various routes and outwitting other players. You will need the train pieces and the train cards from either the US or Europe base games (or you can buy them from the Days of Wonder shop.

There is an extra set of Tourist cards for the cities, each with a value to be earned by visiting that city. These cards are dealt/picked up alongwith tickets in the same quantity and with the same 'keep/dispose' rules.

There are also spanners along some tracks which may help or hinder you. The board does not have any grey tracks or parallel tracks - but there are some options in the spanner pack.

You can ride the bullet train for extra points.

There are many new options in the rules: exchange all your cards at the start but keep all the new ones: discard 5 cards and pick up 5 new ones, as your turn: earn extra points: and many more. You can also call time when your wagon stock reaches 3, if you want, giving you the advantage of stopping other players completing tickets.

Promo card "Sauna / Avanto" first distributed in Essen 2016 by German publisher ASS Altenburger.
Later available through the BGG store and again in a pack of promos for backing Game Boy Geek 2017 Kickstarter campaign

It's a split pile.  Contains 5 copies of Sauna, 5 copies of Avanto, and 1 randomizer/placeholder

You may play an Avanto from your hand.

        Avanto (Action - Cost 5 [coin])

    
    
         +3 Cards
You may play a Sauna from your hand.

    



 


    
        Sauna/Avanto (Action - Cost 4[coin])

    
    
         This pile starts the game with 5 copies of Sauna on top, then 5 copies of Avanto. Only the top card of the pile can be gained or bought.

    


Part of the Dominion series.
