family: Series: Arkham Horror Files (Fantasy Flight Games)

description:
Become COSMIC bounty hunters. In a SCI-FI, Board & Card game Hybrid; created by Aaron Bonkowski.

The craziest, toughest bounty hunters the galaxy has ever seen will collide in this game. They will risk it all to collect some of the most villainous criminals in the pursuit of cold hard space cash! And a relaxing retirement.

The objective of the game is to be the first hunter to collect 3 bounties. Collect a bounty by being the first to eliminate one with a variety of attack and survival cards. Choosing when to strike and when to defend is key because you may just win by being the sole survivor.
Jolly & Roger: Cannonball! is a mini-expansion for Jolly & Roger that consists of two cannonball tokens (that you need to cut out) and rules for how to use them in the game.

Make 'n' Break Architect Mini follows the familiar Make 'n' Break formula of players being given a challenge — typically a building or constructing challenge — then racing to complete it first in order to claim the card or score the points for the challenge.

In Make 'n' Break Architect, each player has a colorful folding ruler, and the challenges demand that players create particular shapes with the ruler or (more difficultly) create particular shapes with specific colors in specific locations. Whoever does so first can slap the card to claim it, and whoever claims six cards first wins.

Make 'n' Break Architect is a miniature version of the original Make 'n' Break Architect with components only for two players and with twenty cards showing forty tasks that was available with a McDonalds Happy Meal during September 2016.

Description from the publisher:

In Copacabana, each player is a contractor at the beginning of the 20th century, helping to turn a peaceful beach into an international icon of glamour and chaos.

On each turn, you must build a new quarter to expand Copacabana, be it hotels, apartment buildings, nightclubs, movie theaters, beaches and hills. Each one has a different effect that you must use to your benefit.

Your aim is to get victory points by placing your dwellers in the most valued streets and building the famous landmarks of Copacabana.

Easy to learn and lasting around 60 minutes, Copacabana is a tile placement game that can hold from 2 to 6 players and is sure please experienced gamers and beginners alike.

This board game is based on the 1990's television cartoon series "Stickin' Around."

The box contains two games that are played one after the other.

The first is called "Mix and Match the Illustrations." In this game, players attempt to assemble complete characters by collecting the correct heads, bodies, and feet cards. The more characters collected in this game will help players in the second game.

The second game is called "Turn the board for explorations." In this game, players roll dice to advance on a board towards the goal, planet Uranus. This game is a combination of luck and skill, as the characters that you collected in the first game can allow you to affect the game, such as stealing an opponent's character, or switching positions on the game board.

Förbjuden Zon is a dystopic semi-cooperative board game, with a Stockholm setting. From the start of the game is good for the players to cooperate with the other players, but in the end of the game it is better to steal items from them. The board has four special sectors: Tunnels, Junkyard, Ark, and Mekano. You can only visit those one time in each game. The players need to pass through all of these. A player can be one of five characters in the game, who is better or worse at the four abilities (negotiate, habit, technology, and combat). The players roll two D6 and moves, draws Ruin card, one event cards and then a item card. Each item card has a "treasure value" from 1-3. If a player lose of lifepoints he is out of the game. The winner is the player that can escape "the zone", the board, with most total value on item cards.

boardgame: Not Alone: The Red Artefact Promo Cards
description:
The Red Artefact promo cards are composed of 1 survival card, 1 hunt card and 4 new place (#10) cards.

The survival and the hunt cards may be added to their respective stack.

The new place card is a new version of the artefact and replaces the one of the basic game.

Description from the publisher:

Epic Roll: Eclipse is the first stand-alone expansion for the Epic Roll series, combining a dice-based battle system with card effects and character abilities.

Eclipse challenges players to don their black hats (helms and cowls are also acceptable) and lead the forces of evil against the Paladin King and his loyal followers. Make your friends jealous and your parents proud(ish) by being the first to plunge the kingdom into darkness. Trampling do-gooders on your way to victory is only part of the fun…further enjoyment comes as you thwart the efforts of your fellow players and spoil their dreams of glory. All while grinning behind steepled fingers.

Eclipse allows 2-3 players to compete in games averaging 15-30 minutes. Combined with the original Epic Roll, games expand to support up to 6 players, competing in teams or individually (with 6 players, expect games to last 40-60 minutes)

Two small punch boards, one with the first two Heroes of the Labyrinth (Ariane and the Minotaur), the other with counters for Provinces at War (5), the Statue of Zeus peace (2), and Odysseus (Ulysses).

The German Kickstarter edition also includes the third Hero of the Labyrinth (Theseus). English Kickstarter subscribers also received Theseus.

description:
Special Committee is a bluffing and hidden identity game featuring current US politicians.

Each player has two concealed Senator Cards. Players need to pass legislation that is important to their own Senators; HOWEVER, if other players figure out both of your Senators, and correctly accuse you, you're out of the game.

The game has similarities to both Machi Koro and Coup. Machi Koro, because players pass Legislation Cards that then give them returns (in the form of votes, favors and coins) on certain dice rolls, and Coup because of the two concealed characters in each players hand.

Trick or Treat is a micro card game about pressing your luck to grab as much candy as possible before too many Trick cards show up. This is a SHORT micro Print and Play game for 1 to any number of players.

Description of a turn:
1. Shuffle the deck.
2. Draw a Card
3. Keep Treat cards and return Trick cards to the deck.
REPEAT steps 2-3 until you decide to quit or you lose. Then pass to the next player.
You gain 1 point for each Treat card that you take and you lose the game when you have drawn any Trick card 3 times.
Objective: Press your luck and see who can grab more candy!

With Rattus: Academicus, an expansion for the Rattus base game, you can upgrade the base characters to make their actions even more powerful!

The added universities and event cards provide even more diversity to the game.

The game contains 25 plastic moustaches with hooked ends.

Pick up a moustache and hook one end around the end of a second moustache. Now hook your second moustache to a third. Make the longest chain of moustaches without dropping any and win!

Aye is a cooperative pirate board game where the player will the crew of a ship under attack by a Kraken. One player will assume the role of captain and will be in charge of planning the round strategy, while the other players (the crew) will execute that strategy to try and complete four different missions: fire cannons, attack with swords, set sails and navigate away from the Kraken. As the mission unfolds, players will win and lose reputation and, if the captain's reputation is too low, crew members with high reputation will be able to declare a mutiny to nominate a new captain of the ship.

Aye is a dice rolling game with a high social component and push-your-luck mechanics. It has 12 custom dice which are dealt by the captain to the players, who will roll them to complete the missions. But receiving damage and losing to much reputation can make you lose those dice.

boardgame: Forgotten Battles: The Campaign for Overloon – September 25th to October 19th, 1944
description:
Forgotten Battles is a low to moderate complexity game on the largest tank battle fought in The Netherlands during World War II. The Allied Market-Garden offensive in September ended in a frustrating loss and the Germans occupying a salient in the Allied lines east of Nijmegen. Seeking to destroy the German threat that was centered on the crucial crossroads town of Overloon, as well as possibly resuming the drive to the Rhine, the British 2nd Army launched a series of offensives that took place throughout the last week of September and the first three weeks of October. The battles that occurred were often fought in torrential rains and marked by heavy casualties on all sides. Can you do as well or better?

Forgotten Battles is designed by Paul Rohrbaugh with graphics by Ilya Kudriashov. The game uses the same design as Breaking Into Valhalla/Zhukov's Final Victory and although designed for two players is very solitaire friendly.

Contents
One 10 page rulebook
Two 11” by 17” map sections
One sheet of 220 single-sided, unmounted units (an optional mounted countersheet can be purchased).
Two sets of charts

Players will also need one or two six-sided dice to play the game.

After an Environmental Crisis, Human kind left the Earth to its doom. Now, they have developed the means to time travel and save the earth by completing 12 time-changing missions.

Takyon is a time travel game in which players will have to adjust time to try and gather religious, cultural, scientific, warfare, environmental and future technologies, so they can bring them to other places in time and space. The game missions can be of the kind of giving nuclear bombs to French Revolutionaries or teach the Roman Empire the ways of Pastafarianism!

In this strategic worker placement game, each change to the time continuum will create a paradox that can block a continent for further time travel, until it is cleaned (or it has so many paradoxes it is blocked permanently). With their time machine pieces, the players will be able to perform actions like move time, move the agents between continents, sell technologies to the Time Museum, clean Paradoxes, acquire new missions o gather future technologies. Moving time will affect all players simultaneously, as their time machines are time-locked.

The game can end in either of these conditions: all players have completed 12 missions overall, which will trigger final scoring to declare a winner, or if three continents get permanently blocked due to an excess in paradoxes, in which case all players will lose and the Earth will be erased from hist...

Game: Arkham Horror – The Card Game
Series: Arkham Horror Files (Fantasy Flight Games)

description:
Description from the publisher:

Neolithic: Advanced Cultures is an expansion for Neolithic.

This expansion introduces new Tasks, brand new cards (and card types), and new ways to play Neolithic, including solo play.

Dedicated Forage cards add additional paths to streamline Village scoring strategies and boosted Task turns.  While a new, revolving card pool changes the cards that are available each game, adding additional variety and replayability to Neolithic.

boardgame: Tiny Epic Galaxies: Beyond the Black – Drones Mini Expansion
description: The Drones Mini Expansion to Tiny Epic Galaxies: Beyond The Black introduces Drones, which integrates with the pilots, and includes six additional pilot cards.

6 promo cards for the Baby Blues game.

Every player gets one card, devil side up.

When you trade a card with another player you have an opportunity to flip your card to the angel side when you make an honest trade. Though this is only possible outside your turn (so you need to help the active player to become an angel).

When the angel side is visible the player receives a few benefits:


     At the end of your turn you can draw up to 6 cards if the angel side is still visible.
     During your turn you are allowed to play 2 negative cards instead of 1.


But you don't stay an angel forever...


     When you lie about the card you trade when the angel is visible you must immediately flip your card to the devil side.
     Also when you decide to play 2 two negative cards in one turn, you must immediately flip your card to the devil side. At the end of this turn, you only draw up to five cards again.

Description from the publisher:

In Derelict, 3-8 players are exploring an abandoned space ship. What is the purpose of the ship, and the rooms that the crew discover along the way? That is yet to be discovered. But with a bit of knowledge and some luck, you may be able to survive.

Derelict is a tile exploration, worker placement board game for 3-8 players. Players work together to explore the derelict ship and gain resources along the way. At some point in the game, the purpose of the ship becomes clear. At that point, it is everyone for themselves as they try to escape.

With dozens of scenarios, Derelict can be played again and again, and each time will be different.

Description from the publisher:

TAKARO is the fast-paced anagram exchange game where players earn points by using game tiles to spell words and then exchange those tiles with the other team to spell a completely new set of words.

In round 1, each team draws any combination of 10 game tiles – consonants and vowels.  Teams then roll the dice and choose a designated number of additional game tiles.  To boost their scores, teams may decide to select a Power Vowel tile.  This tile can be used to earn 10 additional points each round if used.  When the round starts, teams will spell as many words as they can within five minutes.

At the completion of round 1, teams exchange all of their game tiles with the other team.  In round 2, teams must spell unique words using the other team’s game tiles.  Teams may not repeat words from round 1.  The highest cumulative score wins!

Description from the publisher:

Croopo is a fun, strategic, two-person trading card game where players battle each other by carefully choosing Creature, Power-up, and Terrain cards, and rolling dice to determine the outcome of each turn.

Croopo can be enjoyed by kids as young as six years old, and by adults of all ages. For younger kids, rules may be modified for simplification, for example, by omitting Terrain or Power-up cards from the deck.

Each game takes around 30 minutes. All you need to play Croopo are two decks of cards (one for each player) and two six-sided dice. A game board is also available to help beginners place cards correctly. Players can build custom decks by choosing different combinations of cards from their collection in order to develop new strategies of game play.

La Muerte has a job: kill people that has finished their time on Earth.

But not everyone wants to die. Some people try to run. But La Muerte don't give up.

La Muerte starts the game with Wrath value 1. Every card has a "Run" and "Wrath" values. In your turn, you can play until 3 cards and sum the Run value to be equal or greater than La Muerte's Wrath. If you run, La Muerte get's more anger and its Wrath value is increased, according with the played cards. If you can't, you died. As ghost, you only increment La Muerte's Wrath, and helps it to kill!

When only one player is alive, he/she has one turn to stay alive and win! But if La Muerte, with the ghosts' help, kills this last player, nobody wins, except La Muerte!!

In Manimals: Zoo Leipzig, which features animals found in that particular zoo, players compete to find as many animals as possible with a certain characteristic. To start play, shuffle the animal cards, then spread forty of them on the table so that everyone can see them. Flip over one card, see the characteristic identified on this card, then race to pick up all animals that feature this characteristic. Once everyone has finished picking up cards, check all the cards collected to see whether anyone made a mistake; if so, that player must return the mistaken card and one correctly collected card.

Players repeat this process six more times, first laying out three more cards from the deck each time, then they see who has collected the most cards and won!

Manimals: Zoo Leipzig includes rule variants that allow for play with children as young as four, and for each copy of Manimals sold, Adlung-Spiele will donate fifty cents to "für den Freundes- und Förderverein des Zoo Leipzig e. V." for animal protection.

In Arrass, players quickly count how many parts of the parrot are the same on two revealed cards, then lay the appropriate number card down on the table as quickly as possible. The quickest player to lay down the right number card is awarded points, and the player with the most points wins.

Arrass includes rule variants allowing for players as young as four to compete.

In NextPress, all players reveal cards simultaneously each round, with the cards having letters on them. As soon as a player discovers a word, they call out and claim it on their victory pile — but because all visible letters are allowed to be used by all players in future rounds, the topmost card on each victory pile is not safe from being stolen!
Find all the necessary ingredients as quickly as you can in the speedy memory game Speemo, and hurry! The guests are hungry! Whoever prepares the most meals wins!

In Turn A-Round, players lay their cards simultaneously in such a way as to have more of their own colored cards showing than those of their neighbors. But watch out! You are not allowed to lay cards on your own colored cards!

boardgame: Arcane Warfare Excel: AWE – Field Battle Rules 300BC – 500 AD
description:
Arcane Warfare Excel (AWE) are rules for miniature wargaming in the Antiquity, from 300BC to 500AD.

They are the top evolution of the Arcane Warfare Project, run by Jerboa Wargames.

AWE is a miniatures game without randomizers, like dice, cards or equivalent, instead it is a true mind game.
The rules have been developed using '15 mm' miniatures on 40 mm frontage bases.
Each base represents a cohort size unit, that can fight independently of as part of a large formation, a phalanx.    
The game is played over rectangular templates, by default 20 cm x 10 cm, but smaller and lerger areas are also used.
A battle with 32-34 bases per side can be confortably concluded within 90 minutes.

Features

     Fast play, because of the grid movements
     Basic armies around 32 stands
     Scalable
     Miniatures combat system based on dialog, decison/reaction
     Decisive combat
     Combat Tiles concept
     Decided by player skill
     Steep learning curve
     Unforgiving


No dice, rulers, cards, bookkeeping or written orders.

Requirements: rectangle templates; magnetic sheet and metal steel paper, such as those found in hobby stores.

Warning: heavy base work required!

AWE version 1.1 can be obtained from Wargame Vault

description: Similar to Six Card Golf. This game is played with 9 cards that start face down in 3 rows of 3. At the beginning of game play one card is upturned (any card). On your turn you can take a new card from the draw pile or one that has already been discarded. There are cards ranging from -3 to 9, a few wild cards & also a lose your turn & a reverse order card. The object of the game is to have the least amount of points. If you get 3 cards in a row (diagonal, horizontal or vertical) all 3 cards count as 0 no matter what their face value. Game ends when one player has all 9 of their cards face up.

Immerse yourself in the world of James Bond.

This set contains 600 questions and answers on 100 cards from the following categories:

- Cast & Characters
- The Films
- Vehicles
- Gadgets & Weapons
- Locations
- Crew / Behind the Scenes

This set can be used as an expansion along with any Trivial Pursuit base game.

100 Swords is a sword based dungeon crawling micro deck builder! The Multi-User Dungeon Expansion or (M.U.D. Expansion) adds support for additional players. (This expansion requires two of the base dungeons to play!)

IntroductionLear is a finite territory game for two players: Black and White. It's played on the intersections (points) of an initially empty square board. The suggested board sizes are between 7x7 and 10x10 points.

				
				
					DefinitionsTo flip a stone means to replace it with a stone of the opposite color.

				
				
					PlayBlack plays first, then turns alternate. On their turn, a player must place a stone of their color on an empty point. If the placement is part of one or more uninterrupted orthogonal lines of stones made of

a) two friendly stones and
b) an uninterrupted line of enemy stones,

said enemy stones are flipped. The two friendly stones can be both at one end of the enemy line or one at each end.

x+oo ... +xoo ... +ooox ... +xxooo

In the first three diagrams above, an x play at + flips the o stones; in the fourth diagram, it doesn't.

When a placement fills the last empty point on the board, all enemy stones that would normally be flipped as a result of it are removed instead, after which the game ends.

The winner is the player with the highest score in the final position. A player's score is the number of stones with their color showing that there are on the board, minus half a point for the player who made the last placement.

This is a very early automotive-themed card game. It consists of a deck of 56 cards, divided into four colored suits with card values ascending from 1 mile to 14 miles.

Each player takes a well-shuffled suit, and the first player then turns over the top card. If it is a 1 mile, he starts his "Speed Pile" and can turn over the next card. If not, he places the card in a "Delay Pile", and the turn passes. No player may have more than three Delay Piles.

Each turn, a player then has the choice of turning over the top card in his deck or adding the top card from one of his Delay Piles to his Speed Pile. Each time a card is added to the Speed Pile, the player gets another play. When a deck runs out of cards, all cards in that player's Delay Piles are shuffled together and become the new draw deck.

The first player to complete his Speed Pile in sequence from 1 to 14 wins the hand, and gets points equal to the number of cards left in the other players' hands or Delay Piles. First player to 50 points wins the game.

This is an early but undated shooting game from Parker Brothers. It consists of an 11.5" long metal and wood spring-loaded rifle, 0.6" long wooden bullets, 9 cardboard ducks held upright by small metal stands, and a decorated platform with 9 spots for the ducks. The spots have point values: 2 x 5; 2 x 10; 2 x 15; 2 x 25; and 50.

The box lid is placed upside down on the floor. The box is inserted vertically to form a backdrop, and the target platform fits in front of that. Standing "a few feet away", players get three shots each, and score points for each duck hit equal to the value of its spot. The targets are reset after each player turn. The first player to reach 500 points over multiple rounds wins.

boardgame: The Road to the White House
description:
This a simple area-control, card-based U.S. election game aimed at younger children. It comes in a ziplock bag with a plasticized map of the United States, 4 crayons (red, yellow, green and blue), and a deck of 56 cards (50 state cards, a Washington D.C. card and five color-coded wild cards).

Players are dealt hands of five, and start by choosing one of their cards as their home state. Each player colors that state with his crayon. Each turn thereafter, a player starts by filling his hand to 5 cards either by drawing from the remaining deck or by stealing blind from another player.

To claim additional states, players must play cards for states that are adjacent to a state they already hold. In addition to playing from their hands, players may play the top card of the discard pile at any time. Players color in states as claimed, and only discard at the end of their turn if their hand still has five cards in it.

The states are color-coded by region, and there is one wild card for each region, plus a yellow wild card good for anywhere in the country. These allow players to claim non-adjacent states within the corresponding regions. Once all states have been claimed, the player with the largest number of electoral votes wins, with number of states held is a tie-breaker.

Cheese is a game of observation and quick reflexes.
Each player gets a card depicting a slice of cheese. At the same time, each player tries to be the first to complete its scheme: to do this you have to quickly take the pieces on the table and place them on the card, making them fit with the pattern depicted on the card.
The player who successfully complete the scheme first, takes the 3D piece of cheese in the middle of the table: he earns the pieces of cheese taken and draws a cat card.
The cat cards are penalties to be applied in the next round. Examples: use only one hand, use only thumb and forefinger, do not bend your elbows.
When the cheese cards end, the game ends: the player who earned more pieces of cheese wins.
The rules provides various game modes, with different levels of difficulty.

Mini-expansion for the successful Ophidian 2360 Futuristic Gladiator Combat Game!  Original mechanics, great art, with nods to the 2016 US presidential election.

This small expansion adds additional Minions, Quick-Hits, Pumps, and more, including an entirely new keyword-based subtheme!

Play Letters contains 400 cardboard letter squares and has directions for 10 word games:
1. Anagrams: 2-6 players. Object is to create words of three or more letters.
2. Build-a-Sentence: 2-6 players.  Similar to Anagrams but you create sentences and you can take words from other players if you transpose letters to form new word.
3. Scrambled Letters: Players take letters and form a word and then scramble letters in their hands to pass onto next player to unscramble and correctly guess word.
4. Word Solitaire: Take all the letters in your name (or another place or name) and rearrange to form other word(s).
5. Ghosts: 2-6 players - Object is for each player to add a letter to letters already played by preceding players without completing a word of three or more letters.
6. Heads and Tails: Similar to Ghosts except played with words of 4 or more letters.
7. Add or O-U-T: Similar to Ghosts except you want to create words of 4 or more by adding letter to previously played letters.
8. Crosswords: Players alternate in adding words to crossword layout.
9. Tell Me What: Storytelling game where player starts a story and the stops the story and selects a letter.  The first player to say an appropriate word to continue the story beginning witht he letter wins it.
10. Barnyard Hunt: 8 or 10 players.  Leader hides letter squares in defined area (indoors or outdoors).  Everyone goes off to find letters and when all are found each player uses letters to spell out as many barnyard related items as possible.  This can also be played with other categories.

The game Casper & Lazarus is a board game for 2-6 players. Each player moves his ghost through the mansion trying to gather the treasures of Casper. Depending on the space on which you land, you can get the various treasures, advance or otherwise retreat, and some of them indicated a punishment. In his turn each player rolls a die which will indicate the number of spaces that must move. Win the game the first player (ghost) reaches the last space with the "wonderful potion", allowing him to become human.
family: TV Shows: Casper the Friendly Ghost

description:
This game is best described as a card based co-operative dungeon exploration.

Heroes of different roles and powers join together to defeat different challenges before them, and unlock the more difficult ones beyond. Every turn each hero can join in if they have a card that allows them to contribute to that particular task, pooling their dice with the ones the previous hero chose not to use. If they succeed the challenge is removed, but if you fail, you discard cards as damage.

Be careful though, the cards can only be used 3 times, and after that the heroes run out of time.

This expansion includes 4 additional building plans and 30 extra stones. Building plan boards include Schloss Schonbrunn, Sphinx, Louvre, & Kolner Dom. This package is exclusive for Kickstarter and fairs.

Description from the designer:

Introduction
Our enemy is rich in resources, but weak-minded. Many of their Mechs are bigger, faster, and stronger than ours. But challenge their mercenary pilots and they give up quickly and leave their machines.

We control 3 of the 6 sectors around their base, and we’re moving in for the kill. As their Mechs retreat to defend their base, we will attempt to surround and capture them. Capture them if you can, destroy them if you must.

We are closer than we have ever been to a major victory against the Federation. It is all in your hands now. Don’t fail us.

Game Overview
Mech Capture is a solitaire print and play card game using 36 cards - no dice, cubes, or other components needed.

As enemy and friendly units appear on the battlefield, take charge of friendly units and deploy them in pairs to surround enemy Mechs, or in triplets to surround enemy Mechs, Transports, or Long Range Guns at their base.

A limited number of friendly units may be placed in your Reserves for future deployment. Deploy these units to match the enemy’s displayed characteristic and number of pilots, to capture the Mech and gain its Victory Points. If you exceed their displayed characteristic, the Enemy unit is destroyed so that the Enemy (or you) cannot gain its Victory Points.

Winning the Game
By game end have more Victory Points than the Enemy. Compare your victory points from captured units to all remaining enemy units at their base, three sectors, and Enemy draw pile (if any).

description:
The Battles of Khalkhin-Gol, the first installment in Days of Wonder's second series of Memoir '44 Battle Maps, offers a campaign of six standard scenarios that can be enhanced with the two pre-printed Breakthrough battles provided in this expansion.

In more detail, The Battles of Khalkhin-Gol is a series of battles fought along the Halha River (Khalkhin-Gol) on the Soviet-Japanese border at the very beginning of World War II. The Japanese Sixth Army was facing the Soviet 57th Special Corps, including Mongolian cavalry. At first, the battles were only short skirmishes, but it soon escalated into open war. The whole campaign lasted five months and ended with a Soviet victory. The six standard scenarios of this expansion can be played separately or as a campaign, with each scenario outcome impacting the following one. The pre-printed Breakthrough scenarios — Bain Tsagan Heights and Tightening the Noose — can be played in the campaign as well as one of the two Overlord scenarios, Encirclement at Khalkhin-Gol.

The second Overlord scenario, Cape Torokina Landings, highlights the fierce battle for Cape Torokina between U.S. Marines and Japanese forces that took place years later; therefore, it is not part of the campaign.

This expansion also includes six Soviet BA-10 Armored Cars and six Japanese Type 97 Te-Ke tankettes that players will find useful for many of the scenarios included.

The Battles of Khalkhin-Gol is designed for 2 to 8 players. Each oversized map is ready-to-play with all terrain, obstacles, and unit positions pre-printed so players can simply add the figures and play. The various scenarios of the expansion require one copy of Memoir '44 (two for the Overlord scenarios), as well as the Terrain Pack, Eastern Front, and Pacific Theater expansions. The Equipment Pack and Winter Wars expansions are also recommended.

—description from the publisher
family: Game: Memoir '44 – The Battle Map Series

description:
Falling Stars is a Role-Playing and Tactical Game of political and military conflict unfolding amidst an alien intervention.

What is a Tactical Game System?  A tactical game system allows players to skirmish one-on-one with each other using the Falling Stars Opposed d10 game system. Players can take a break from role playing (or completely ignore that aspect of the game) and simply enjoy fighting other players. Players can select teams or elect to play free-for-all or anything else in between. The maps in Falling Stars are scaled perfectly for 28mm miniatures, or players can make their own maps using half-inch grid squares or as a miniature gaming system with ranges measured in half-inch increments.
 Maybe that tough talking UPAS marine thinks he can take your brawler. So take a break for the RPG and let the two players settle the argument with the Tactical Game System. Bragging rights goes to the winner, and then the group can get back to playing the role playing game. The possibilities are endless!

This box contains everything you need to learn the basic mechanics of Falling Stars. It includes Quick Start Rules, eight pre-generated characters, the first three chapters of an ongoing sci-fi adventure campaign, “Into the Long Dark Night,” characters and tokens mounted on high-quality cardboard and full-color printed maps for the adventure.

Lock 'n Load Tactical: Solo is the next evolution in our LnLT series. LnLTS is a solitaire system that provides players with a way to play scenarios and generate battles on their own. We wanted to take that a step farther and provide to have one solitaire system that would work for the entire Lock 'n Load Tactical series past, present, and future games. Gamers will be able to use LnLTS to play any complete Lock ‘n Load Tactical game including any expansions, in either World War 2 or Modern eras. We also wanted players to be able to play the scenarios that come with the games, so LnLTS makes this possible. There will be a few scenarios that won't play as smoothly as most of the scenarios due to events or special rules. LnLTS will work with any scenario generated by our LnLT Battle Generators. Included with LnLTS is a system is a random events generator that is optional to add a little something special to your games and could be used even in two-player games.
         
Lock 'n Load Tactical: Solo was developed by Sean Druelinger and David Heath and is based and licensed from Academy Games's Solo game design. We like to thank Uwe Eickert for allowing us to create this edition for the Lock 'n Load Tactical series.

UPDATE: All of our manuals and guides can be Downloaded, Viewed, and even printed right from our LnLP Online Library: We now have an Online Library of all, well almost all of our manuals.  We add some every few weeks.  You can find all the LnLT manuals at the link below.

LnLP Online Library: http://library.lnlpublishing.com/

description:
Description from the publisher:

CONTENTS
100 Playing Cards (20 Food, 20 Water, 20 Shelter, 10 Weapon, 10 Beast, 20 Trash)
21 Life Tokens

OBJECTIVE
In Survive the Card Game the players represent one of the final pockets of humanity desperately trying to stay alive in the face of a ceaseless and brutal nuclear winter.

Players will need to spend one of each resource – food, water, and shelter – to survive through each round.  For each resource they don’t have at the end of the round they lose a life token.  Lose all life tokens and that player is out of the game.  The last surviving player is the winner.

STARTING THE GAME
Shuffle the deck.  Deal each player five cards, and give each player three Life Tokens.  
Place the deck in the middle of the table.
The player’s Hand is kept concealed.
The player’s Stockpile will be placed face up on the table in front of them.
Cards are drawn from the Search Deck.  Discarded cards go on the Trash Pile, which is placed next to the deck.
There are six card types: Food, Water, Shelter, Weapon, Beast, and Trash.

PLAYING THE GAME
Each round is broken up into two phases: Search, and Negotiation.

SEARCH PHASE
In the Search phase each player takes it in turn.  The player who hasn’t eaten for the longest time goes first.
First, you can place any number of cards from your Hand face up into your Stockpile.  There is no limit to the number of cards you can have in your Stockpile.
You can also discard any unwanted cards onto the Trash Pile.  In the first round, discard any Beast cards in your Hand before you draw from the Search Deck.
You can now draw a number of cards from the Search Deck equal to five minus the number of cards in your hand (e.g., if you have two cards left in your hand, draw three cards).  You may never have more than five cards in your Hand.  You can only draw once per round.
Any Beast cards that you draw from the Search Deck must be placed face up in front of you.  This indicates that you have been attacked by one or more beasts whilst searching, and must defeat them to avoid losing any Life Tokens.
Once you have drawn from the Search Deck play moves to the next player on your left.  Once every player has had a turn the phase is over.
If at any point the Search Deck is exhausted, shuffle the Trash Pile and start the deck anew.

NEGOTIATION PHASE
All players now negotiate at the same time and with anyone they choose.
Players with missing resources should do their best to attain them from other players to avoid losing any Life Tokens.
The only rule to negotiation is simple: you can’t steal cards from another player’s Hand or Stockpile (actually, there is one exception to this which is explained below under Weapons).  
You can trade, haggle, swindle, beg, coerce, threaten, lie, manipulate, make promises, break promises, form alliances, betray alliances, seek revenge, avenge others, be helpful, be unhelpful, do nothing, or do anything that you think might help you to win.    
Once a card has been played into your Stockpile it can’t go back into your Hand.  Any cards you receive from other players must be placed into your Stockpile and not into your Hand.  The phase is over when the majority of players agree that they’ve finished negotiating.
NOTE: Trash cards are essentially worthless.  That said, they can be useful for tricking other players into believing that you have more resources in your hand than you actually do, believing that you have a Weapon card in your hand, or even accepting them for trade (if you can get away with it).

WEAPONS
Weapon cards can be used in one of four ways:
1. Remove one Beast attack from any player including yourself.
2. Remove one Life Token from any player.
3. Steal all or part of a Stockpile from any other player.
4. Counter one Weapon card played.  You must play the Weapon card immediately after the other player.
You can play any number of Weapon cards during the Negotiation phase.  
Once a Weapon has been played it is discarded onto the Trash Pile.
HINT: You can threaten to use a Weapon on another player before actually playing it, or even to avoid having to play it.  You can also pretend to have a Weapon card in your Hand when you don’t, in order to bluff other players.

END OF THE ROUND
Each player must now discard one of each resource – Food, Water, and Shelter – onto the Trash Pile.  These can be discarded from the player’s Hand and/or Stockpile.
For each resource not available to discard, that player loses one Life Token.  
For each Beast card still in play on the table in front of them, that player loses one Life Token, and then the Beast card is discarded onto the Trash Pile.  
Once a player loses all of their Life Tokens they are out of the game.
Players are encouraged to continue vocal involvement even after going out.

END OF THE GAME
The game ends when only one player is left and is declared the winner.
If every last player is removed from the game in the same round, then nobody wins.

What Is Inside It is a full action packed, very thrilling & very exciting card game. This game is of high intensity and mafia like situation. It is truly a Russian Roulette. It is highly enjoyable and can be played with a number of family members and friends. This is a great game if you are looking to have a great time. This is also a perfect party game as well as a source of great entertainment and joy. If you love Action, Thrill, Big Sum Of Money and Heavy Explosions then this game is a must for you.

Background Story & Synopsis :

Imagine your self in a crisis situation and in an urgent need of cash. You end up getting in front of a loan shark who is also a crime boss, asking him for a million dollars. He places 3 briefcases in front of you and asks you to "pick one and open it". He further mentions that "only one of them has the million dollars in cash, one has the trash and one has the bomb. If you end up opening the case with the cash, you walk away rich, if you open the case with the trash, you walk away with nothing but the stink and if you open up the case with the bomb then you are a roasted meat". You take a good look at all the 3 briefcases, put in front of you to choose from. They all look exactly same, they all weigh exactly same, they also feel exactly same, but the only thing in which they differ is, what they carry inside. All the briefcases are completely odorless and produce no sound upon shaking them. They are completely sealed and locked. You may only choose one briefcase and you only get to know what is inside it, by opening it right there and right then. You finally choose and grab one briefcase to open it while wondering, what is inside it ...

GAME PLAY :

What is inside it is an exhilarating and an exciting game. A number of players can play it against each other. This game of odds, chances and strategy unfolds when a number of players are confronted with a number of cards with briefcases pictures on it. To open a briefcase, the playing card is flipped upside down so that the content of that briefcase is revealed. Without knowing what is inside of each card, the players pick up and flip one card at a time, upon their turn in each round. The risk element kicks in when all the players must instinctively choose and flip a number of cards, which are to be opened one at a time upon their turns, and the content of the card is revealed. As each card is flipped or turned, the likelihood of the players having a valuable cash amount such as a million dollars in the card or something else, such as a BOMB. The pressure mounts in each round, as the number of cards are turned or flipped. The players may also come under pressure by what might be contained in their chosen card. What is inside it ? cash or bomb or something else... The players will always be curious and puzzled, but they also know that as long as they do not intentionally or accidentally open the card with a BOMB or come under attack by any other opponent player, they are fine. There are multiple variants of this game, which can be played.

description from the publisher:

boardgame: Huscarls: A Game of Conquest in Dark Ages Britain.
description:
INTRODUCTION
Huscarls is a semi-historical game where one to four Players assume the roles of minor Saxon Warlords in Britain during the Dark Ages of around 980-1080 AD. There are many rich pastures, thick forests with deer and boar and rolling hills, all ready for the taking as the riches of Medieval Britain can be yours. All you need is to demonstrate a firm hand and fruitful estates. A strong sword and loyal followers are also required to maintain your lands and ensure the villagers are providing the proper taxes. You must also be able to defend your territories from rival Lords and invading Normans and plundering Vikings.

GAME OBJECTIVE
Players assume the roles of Saxon Warlords in Dark Ages Britain (AD 980-1080) with the goals of expanding and improving their territorial control. Warlords do this by raising Retinues and conquering neighboring territories. Warlords can, also, improve their holdings by building Hamlets, Manors and Castles. A Game consists of up to 10 Campaign Seasons. Players may agree to play fewer or more as desired. The Player, who at the end of any Campaign Season (Spring, Summer or Autumn) controls the greatest Settlement Value from their Hamlets, Manors and Castles, is the Victor.
