description:
Jumbalup plays with words, syllables and sounds, and combines observation, memory and strategy with a bit of luck.

Make strange and fantastic creatures in a race to complete your animals first. The winner is the first player to collect four real animals with matching heads and tails.

Players take turns to draw a card adding a head or tail to their collection or saving a special card to play later - using a Jumbalup card to steal a head or a tail from another player or playing a dodo to slow another player down.

The cards can also be used to play a matching pairs memory game (with twists) for younger players (3+).

A pack of Jumbalup contains 56 standard sized playing cards and a printed rule sheet.

A card game for mature audiences. Players take turns playing cards on themselves or an opponent to affect their scores in Money, Health and Dilation points. The first player who is broke, dead and f*cked wins the game.

Springo is a classic roll-and-move game in which players race with their pawns to the middle of the board.

Each player controls 4 pawns. At the beginning of the game each pawn is on the outer ring. The goal of the game is to move the pawns to the inner rings and finally to the middle. Every turn a player throw a dice and choose which pawn to move clockwise around the circle that the pawns is currently. If the pawn ens its move in the same place as the other pawn, the other one goes back to the starting place. If a pawn finishes a move in a horse spot, then the next move that pawn will do is going to the inner circle. If it is the most inner circle then that pawn finishes his game in one of the six spots in the middle of the board.

When there are six spots in the middle occupied the game is over. The player that lead the most pawns to the middle is the winner. In case of a tie the values that pawns are standing on in the middle are sum up and whoever has the most is the winner.

A Game: Black Stories edition with riddle stories without a special theme - except maybe the unlucky number 13.

Black Stories are fiddly, morbid and mysterious riddles for teenagers and adults. The players try to reconstruct the crime by asking, guessing and fiddling about. Only yes/no answers are allowed. A spooky card game just right for any party.
A single card and solo rules given for free at Essen 2016

boardgame: The Oracle of Delphi: The Pillars of Delphi Variant
description: Variant of the game with 6 Pillars offers for the first edition of the game at Essen 2016

A Major League Baseball simulation game, which has individual cards for each player and a set of result charts for each of 8 base situations (bases empty to bases loaded), rare plays, base stealing, base advancement, advanced pitching grades.

After setting your lineup, players roll dice, obtain a result number from the player card, and then refer to charts for results. This will result in an out, or getting on base, with details about each outcome. Then you move on to the next batter. Standard rules of major league baseball apply.

Although the cards are designed for play with all APBA game boards, it's highly recommended that you purchase the SkeeterSoft Boards and Advanced Pitching System.  All options in the SkeeterSoft world are intuitive, fully explained on the lineup sheet and an Options sheet furnished with each set, and have a relatively slight learning curve.

Almost every season is available back to 1901.

Notorious is a strategic dice drafting, resource management, set collection game. You'll draft new crew members and complete heists to earn the most Notoriety points to win the game.

You started as a small-time hood. But you know to become king of this city, you’ll need a crew. Assemble the right team, complete heists, and gain notoriety. As you finish more jobs, the heat on you becomes stronger. If one of your squad gets pinched, you can always work the system with a bribe, or just let them go to the slammer. Outlast your rivals and see if you have what it takes to become the most NOTORIOUS.

Gameplay:
As a new boss, you will complete small jobs by rolling dice. You’ll use the earnings (jewels) from these small jobs to buy new members to join your crew. These Crew members will combine their abilities together to allow you to complete larger Heists. Every Heist you complete will earn you Notoriety points to win the game.

On your turn, you can do any 2 of the following:
1.    Roll dice (completing small jobs)
2.    Draft up to 2 crew cards
3.    Complete up to 2 heists

Rolling Dice - If you roll dice, you roll your choice of 2 of the 3 colored dice (red, blue, and green), and roll 1 of the 2 black and white dice. You can keep and/or re-roll any of the dice once, so that’s a total of 2 total rolls.

Dice representation: Blue die = Sapphires. Green die = Emeralds. Red die = Rubies. The diamond shape represents a Diamond. The Flame shape represents Surveillance. The Star on the Black die represents the player earning a Jewel of the player’s choice.

Drafting Crew Cards – The cost of the Crew Card is detailed in the upper left portion of the card. You can pay the jewel cost (earned from rolling dice), and that crew member is now part of your crew. You will play that Crew Card on the table in front of you. A new Crew Card replaces the drafted Crew Card on the table. - Each crew member has attributes which will help players complete Heists. Some crew members have special abilities which will improve your abilities, or hinder the play of your competitors.

Completing Heists – If you have enough attributes from your collective Crew Cards, you may complete the Heists. The Heist requirements are detailed at the bottom of the Heist Card. You will add your Crew Card attributes together, and if those attributes total to at least the minimum required for each Heist requirement, you may complete the Heist. You will take the Heist card and keep it on the table in front of you. A new Heist Card is placed face up on the table to replace any Heist Card taken by a player. Each Heist Card has a Notoriety point value at the top right. The first player to earn at least 21 Notoriety points wins the game.

To Windward!: The Lunar Islands is a 2-4 person competitive, historical Euro-style tabletop game inspired by the Golden Age of Sailing.

It is a competitive, strategic tabletop game which realistically captures the excitement, danger and unpredictability of life at sea. Players command their own fleet of ships in an effort to establish themselves as the dominant naval power in the Lunar Islands. The scales of power can shift suddenly all with a change of wind or an enemy's skills as a master gunner, skilled sailor, or clever scoundrel.

				
				
					How to play				
				
					Set upEach player gets a Home Island a sturdy Sloop to begin their quest for adventure and riches and the race to gain the most Power & Influence over the islands.

				
				
					Player turn				
				
					1- Check WindCheck wind timer and reset wind direction if necessary.

				
				
					2- Perform actionsDuring their turn a player gets 4 actions.


     Sail up to 2 steps leeward or straight to leeward
     Sail 1 step to windward (may not sail straight to windward)
     Use Gunnery card
     Use a Sailing card
     Dock/Go to town
     Upgrade a ship
     Buy a new sloop
     Relaunch a sunken ship
     Dive for sunken treasure
     Buy up to three cards (Sailing and/or Gunnery) for 2 gold each
     Buy 1 Power and Influence token for 8 gold (once per turn, not allowed during final round)


				
				
					3- Collect Power and Influence tokens & claim accomplished Missions
     +1 Selling cargo for 2 gold per ton during a docking action
     +1 Buy Power and Influence for 8 gold (not allowed during final round)
     Sink an enemy ship
         +2 for a Sloop
         +3 for a Frigate/Barque
         +4 for a Indiaman/Man o' War
    


All awards doubled during final round

				
				
					4- Draw and carry out 1 Event card				
				
					The WindThe wind can be an unpredictable ally or fierce opponent. It dictates how far and which directions ships can sail and a change in the wind change quickly shift the balance of power.

				
				
					Sailing & FightingA players hand consists of Sailing and Gunnery cards which are used then discarded to move across the board with ease, to attack other players ships or perhaps fend off an opponent. The diversity and strength of these two decks can turn vulnerable seeming Sloop into a fierce or wily opponent.

				
				
					TradingBy filling a ship's hold with one of 3 commodities in the game and trading it to the neighbouring islands, players can begin to amass gold and gain Power & Influence.

				
				
					UpgradesThis gold can be used to add ships to their fleet or upgrade their Sloop or Sloops to be fierce fighting ships like Frigates and Man o' War or rich merchant vessels like Barques and the famous Indiaman and take on enemies to win Power. But be careful, as a ship becomes laden with treasure, it will become targets for other players.

				
				
					EventsEvents add another wrinkle of unpredictability in the game. At the end of each players turn a random event occurs which may effect their fleet, or the entire board with positive or perhaps disastrous consequences.

				
				
					MissionsMissions set out tasks that players can accomplish during their turn for a significant payout. Be sure to store as much treasure on your home island as possible because treasure still on a ship at the end of the game only counts for half of what gold on shore is worth.

				
				
					End GameIn the final round the stakes are raised as the race to get the most Power & Influence over the Lunar Islands nears an end. Players who have thought strategically and planned can turn this to their advantage and sweep victory out from under their opponents.

				
				
					Victory				
				
					End of game awards
     +1 for each Sloop in your fleet
     +2 for each Frigate/Barque in your fleet
     +3 for each Indiaman/Man o' War in your fleet
     +1 for first Mission, +2 for second, +3 for third, etc.
     +1 for every 10 treasure


At the end of the game the player with the most Power and Influence wins.  In case of a tie whoever has the most treasure is the victor

This is a murder mystery party game for 8-14 guests.

Setting / Theme:
This annual party banquet takes place at Gonagen Trailer Trash Park, where the residents have gathered to guzzle beer and box wine, and chomp down vittles while they celebrate nothing in particular "just a good ole time for all of us to get together," says Big Hoss Beaver, the host. However, this year the event was dampened by the unforeseen murder of one of the residents! Who, at this totally unfashionable party, created this trailer trash tragedy? All guests will have fun playing out their individual characters and investigating this unpredictable event. Whodunit and why, are the mysteries the rest of the trailer folks are determined to solve before the murderer strikes again!

The Gonagen Trailer Trash Park is the setting for this murder mystery game for 8-14 player guests. All of the residents come together to celebrate this grand event, the Annual Trailer Park Smorgasbord Dinner and Dance party. However,the party is overshadowed by the murder of one of the residents!

How to Play
Planning:

     Host of the Murder Mystery picks the day/time and location for the game which will take 2-3 hours and finds out how many guests can attend.
     Host creates guest list and assigns each guest one of the characters to play.
     Host sends official invite with their character background to each guest.
     Guests create / purchase a costume based on their character.  Thrift stores are good for this.
     Host decorates and plans for food for evening.


Playing the game:

     As guests arrive, the host gives them their instructions for the first round which will include things to say/do/ask.
     After all actions are completed, instructions are given to guests for Round 2.
     Repeat again for Round 3.
     In between rounds you can eat or have activities based on the theme.


Ending:

     Everyone discusses and says who they think the murderer is.
     Each guest reads their final clue in order leading up to the murderer.
     Winner are those that guessed the murderer.  
     If nobody guesses correctly the murderer wins.

Description from the rules:

The year is 1400 BCE. The fabled Labyrinth of Konoso, better known as the Labyrinth of Knossos, has suffered a catastrophe! Parts have been burned, other bits have collapsed, making it nearly impossible to escape. All you have to guide your way are the scribe's tokens, which match certain doorways, allowing you to pass. Will you be able to make it out of the labyrinth alive?

At the start of the game, you roll to see how many of each type of scribe's tokens you receive.  There are six types of tokens, each one associated with a different Linear B Ideogram.  If you ever roll for only one of a particular token type, you can choose a rotation token.  There are six unique rotations tokens, which move the hex tiles in different ways.

You start the game at the center start hex, encircled by two concentric circles of hex tiles, the first circle containing six tiles, the second circle containing twelve tiles.  The goal is to make it to one of the six exit hexes, located at various points beyond the second circle.

To move through the labyrinth, you match the token you have with the doorway you walk through.  The token only has to match the doorway of the tile you are on - not the tile you are traveling to.  Once you run out of a type of token, you can no longer walk through doors of that type.

Will you be able to make it to the exit before your tokens run out?

A micro-card game played in your hand.  You take the role of the Jack, who is out to steal as many pies as he can before the Kings can catch him.  Played with a unique deck of 17 cards, you have to use the abilities of your Jack to steal pies and avoid the Kings.  If there are ever two Kings in a row, you have been caught!  At the end of the game, tally up how many pies you managed to steal.

This game is based on the old poem "The Queen of Hearts".

Munchkin Valentines comes with five valentines and envelopes to give you five chances to make someone like you. What's more, five small packs of Munchkin cards that fit inside the valentines are included so that you sweethearts will have something new to play together.

You have heard of a wondrous artifact, the Sampo, which will bring great prosperity to your country. Unfortunately, it has not yet been created! In order for the great smith Ilmarinen to forge the Sampo, you must bring him the finest wool of a lamb, a single grain of barley, white iron, and a white swan feather. However Louhi, the Sorceress of Pohjola, also wants the Sampo to bring her prosperity! Will you be able to gain the Sampo and return to the Stone Fortress of Kivimäki before Louhi can steal the Sampo?

There are four stats in the game: Brawn, Wits, Magic, and Luck. At the start of the game, Brawn, Wits, and Magic all start at 1, and Luck starts at 0. You have 9 points to distribute between the 4 stats. Each point means you roll an additional six-sided die when using that stat.

These stats are used for the encounters at each location.  Each encounter is fixed, and requires certain stats or combinations of stats for you to succeed.  These encounters are how you gain better stats, gain the items you need to make the Sampo, and once you have the Sampo, how you avoid Louhi and her minions.

Inspired by the story of the Sampo from the Kalevala.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
The box includes both games Werewolf & Mafia. Werewolf & Mafia are simple party games for 7+ players. Both games are a masterful creation of bluffing, paranoia, and mob rule. Mafia was created by Dimitry Davidoff in 1986. In 1997 Andrew Plotkin turned the game into werewolf. We love playing both of these games and have published a regular size deck but with the mini deck its even more discrete and portable.

Whats in the Box?

28 Werewolf Game Cards
28 Mafia Game Cards
Size: 1.75" x 1.25" (63mm x 44mm)
Finish: Magic (similar to a deck of Bicycle playing cards)

Ogre Reinforcements includes new units (including 5 new armor unit types), 8 scenarios, map overlays, and associated rules for use with the Ogre 6th Edition.

Reinforcements is fully compatible with Ogre Designer's Edition, but does not contain anything not already present in the Designer's Edition except that the unit colors are slightly distinct.

boardgame: Lock 'n Load Tactical: Compendium Volume 1 World War 2 Era
description:
Lock ’n Load Tactical Compendium. Volume 1 is the WW2-Era edition, and it collates, in a re-edited and updated format, all of the essential WW2-Era LnLT articles, maps and scenarios from every issue of Line of Fire magazine, plus some new content, including four new scenarios.

In the Compendium Volume 1, you can read indispensable articles on infantry and tank tactics—it’s like attending advanced combat training in 10 pages! Learn how to best place obstacles when you are on the defensive, and how to tackle the difficulties of the Fog in Noville, Bastogne’s Outpost. Read about the history and tactics of the battles for Kellam’s Bridge and the Norman village of Cauquigny and then play the two scenarios. Detailed descriptions of the Japanese forces in Heroes of the Pacific and the British forces in Heroes of Normandy also provide background and tactical insights on how to use them. Plus, fiction from the East Front and a history of the US Armored Divisions.

Scenarios comprise the bulk of this Compendium, and more scenarios equal more gameplay for your existing games. From the desperate moments of the Blitzkrieg in 1940 to North Africa in 1942 to Normandy in 1944 and across France to Holland and into the Hürtgen Forest of Germany, in four new scenarios played on redesigned maps. With over 110 pages, 22 scenarios and six maps, this Compendium has something for everyone who’s a fan of the Lock ’n Load Tactical series’ WW2-Era games. And it’s all right here: One game system, one era, one place!

In 2784, the departure of General Alexsandr Kerensky and the remnants of the SLDF from known space leaves the Inner Sphere balanced on a knife-edge. With no Star League to keep the House Lords in check, greed, ambition and old grudges come to the fore once more, propelling the five remaining Great Houses into a war that threatens human civilization itself: The First Succession War.

The First Succession War describes the most devastating conflict ever fought by mankind as the five Great Houses battle for supremacy in the ruins of the Star League. Covering the collapse of the Star League, the militarization of the Inner Sphere and the horrors that ensued, this volume provides a detailed look at the major actions of the war, the motivations of its participants, and the deadly consequences of their decisions.

boardgame: Family Guy: Mouth full of BLANKS
description:
Description from the publisher:

Join the fun of Family Guy: Stewie’s Sexy Party Game with the Mouth Full of BLANKS Game Booster! Now you and your friends, along with your pets, your boss, that jerk at work who keeps going on and on about his fantasy team, your favorite bar, and that cool catchphrase you used to say all the time in high school but now looking back you realize it wasn’t that cool, can be a part of Stewie’s Sexy Party Game.

Mouth Full of Blanks is a set of Blank Word Cards featuring every category and character from Stewie’s Sexy Party Game. GF9 couldn’t “go there” but with a Mouth Full of Blanks Game Booster, you can absolutely “go there”. Go ahead, add your own People, Places, Actions, Descriptions, Things and Quotes to the game, we won’t tell!

Description from the publisher:

Family Guy: Stewie’s Sexy Party Game is in full swing, but there’s still one more guest to invite – Quagmire! Add everyone’s favorite deviant to the game with the Quagmire Card Pack. This 80 card pack adds new, inappropriate word cards to fool around with including some of Quagmire’s favorite people, places, things and phrases.

boardgame: Fleurus 1794: The Battles of Fleurus and Mont St. Jean
description:
Description from the publisher:
The Fate of Flanders was to be decided on the Sambre — Republican France against Imperial Austria.

The decisive Battle of Fleurus led to the Austrian loss of Belgium. With the two armies clashing at Mont St Jean, the campaign gave the French Republic additional resources and manpower, opened the gateway to Germany, and the port of Antwerp to the French navy.

Fleurus 1794 is an Expansion Game Module for Napoleon's Last Gamble. It requires the south map from Napoleon's Last Gamble and the southern-extension map from Napoleon's Last Gamble Expansion Kit. The four scenarios cover the battles of Fleurus and Mont St Jean played on the NLG maps. Veterans of TLNB will have an opportunity to examine a key campaign from the French Revolutionary Wars.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players
Series: The Library of Napoleonic Battles (OSG)

boardgame: Captain Scrimshaw's Digg'n Pirate Treasure Puzzle Game
description: A puzzle, memory matching game in which players try to reveal their treasure pieces from nine piles of tiles that form a treasure map.  As players reveal pieces, if they belong to their own color, they play it immediately.  Otherwise they hoard the pieces of the other players to force them to "steal" by taking a random tile in order to complete their treasure puzzle.  The first player to collect and arrange to form their treasure picture wins.

This is a free goodie given at Essen 2016.
It adds 2 new leaders (Raffaello Sanzio & Nicolo Machiavelli) to play with the base game.

The New Edition of Lorenzo il Magnifico already include this promo.

boardgame: Lorenzo il Magnifico: Bonus Card expansion
description:
This expansion was given at Essen 2016 if you preordered the Game.
It was reprinted only in 2021 for events, conventions and as KSE.

The original version of this promo includes 1 rule card and 4 Bonus cards (1 Bonus Card per player).
The new version of this promo include 6 cards: 1 rule card and 5 Bonus cards (so also a card for the fifth player from HoR exp).

You can use your Bonus card once a game (discard it) to immediately make another turn.

The New Edition of Lorenzo il Magnifico already include many promos, but not this one.

Objective: The objective is to be the first player to make an electrical instalation from the switch to the bulb.

The number of cards needed to be the winner without the switch, the bulb and the terminals depends on the number of players.

2 players 15 cards alt least
3 players 12 cards alt least
4 players 10 cards alt least
5 players  8 cards alt least

Components: A deck of 105 especial cards. 10 chips of isolation tape. The cards represent 3 types of cable or wire and shortcircuits.

A simple roll and move game of collecting a set of four tokens from the board. The key is in the cards: in order to collect a token, you must read a card and act what the card says.

The cards are about emotions and feelings, recognising and expressing them. The game is educational and aimed to teach the kids to express their feelings.

This is a slightly simplified and a much smaller version of Ihmevekotin (2009). The board is the same, but with less cardboard decorations and in smaller size. The tokens are slightly different. The cards are very similar.
The fun game in which players open and close the bridges to win the race with their car and boat.

User summary:

In Aflakló the goal is to be the wealthiest player when the market cards deck is depleted. Players roll a die to move around Iceland and depending on the spaces they land on they can buy fish plants, go fishing, draw event cards or sell their catch. Event cards are played immediately, but players also have action cards which can be used when needed, usually to wreck havoc amongst the other players. When players buy fish plants (or land on existing ones) they can go fishing, and then sell their catch later. The price depends on the market cards in the center of the board which are changed when players cross the starting space.

Description from the publisher:
Aflakló is a revolutionary new Icelandic board game where players compete to expand their fisheries empires by sailing around Iceland, catching and selling fish and taunting each other. On every corner danger is near, in the form of changing government, Norwegian fishermen and Greenpeace. Players have to have steel nerves to withstand the pressures and have a chance to win.

The ruins of Ilkannon have been discovered! After being lost for centuries, you now have a chance at learning about the heart of that mythic civilization! Choose your character and begin your adventure into the wilds! But watch out! The curse which brought Ilkannon to ruin is still housed within its walls! Restore the city - but make sure you don’t unleash corruption upon the world!

You play one of four characters: An adventurer, a politician, a scholar, or a mechanic. Each person has their own goals within the city, and each one has their own faults that make them susceptible to various curses. The ultimate end goal is to find four power crystals to revitalize the city or to find one item to bring back to tell of their great discovery!

There are four main stats which each character has: Corruption Resistance, Movement, Restoration, and Ingenuity.  Corruption Resistance is how susceptible a character is to the dark forces still at work in Ilkannon.  This is used mostly during events.  Movement is how far the character can move on their turn.  Restoration is how many tiles a character can restore on their turn.  Ingenuity is used for events, and is based on how well the character deals with certain situations outside of their normal state.  Corruption Resistance and Ingenuity affect how many dice the character rolls during certain events.

The city of Ilkannon is composed of sixteen tiles, laid out in a grid of four by four.  These tiles are double sided, with one side being Wilderness and the other side being the Restored city.

Turn order is as follows:

1) Move or Restore
2) Draw an Event
3) Corruption activates. This only happens once part of Ilkannon is Restored. If no tiles are Restored, Corruption sleeps.

Mythic Wars: Cthulhu Rises, the first expansion set for Mythic Wars, adds 16 new cards (8 gods and goddesses, and 8 creatures) based on the writings of H.P. Lovecraft. When combined with the Clash of the Gods base set, 4-8 players can join together and try to defeat the invading Outer Gods, including Azathoth, Nyarlathotep, Cthulhu, and many others.

Description from the designer:

Players choose one of the five celestial animals to fight for supremacy of the skies - using fireworks! Connect firework tiles to gain territory, break up other firework collections, and battle for the largest section of the night sky.

The game is played with tiles, each tile is broken into four sections and contains four fireworks.  At the start of the game, players are dealt three tiles.  Then they choose the celestial animal they want to play - there can only be one player for each animal.  Players place their tiles in such a way as to connect the most fireworks for their animal together.  Tiles can be placed over other tiles, as long as the tile below isn't locked.

Each turn players:

     Play one tile from their hand.
     Draw a new tile from the pool of tiles.


Play continues until all the tiles have been placed.  At the end of the game, all the fireworks on the board are tallied for points.

The game includes rules for solitaire play.

Description from the designer:

Snow-White and Rose-Red is a fairy tale adventure where you take control of Snow-White and/or her sister Rose-Red. The goal is to save the Enchanted Bear, who once was a prince but has fallen under a vile curse from the Ungrateful Dwarf.

To defeat the Ungrateful Dwarf you need to steal back the treasure he stole from the prince or you need to remove his source of power - his luxuriant beard - with a pair of scissors. Explore the woods near Mother's Cottage, rescue trapped animals, and make sure you never have more than four curses upon you at a time! Will you be able to save the Enchanted Bear and defeat the Ungrateful Dwarf? Or will you become cursed too?

Each turn the sisters travel to different locations around the board to have encounters.  These encounters can lead to rescuing animal companions, gaining useful items, collection treasure, encountering the Ungrateful Dwarf, or encountering the Enchanted Bear.  Watch out for the curses!  Curses make it harder for the sisters to complete their goals!  The encounters at the locations are different depending on which sisters are present, and if both sisters are there.

This game can be played solo where one person controls one or two of the sisters, or can be played as a full co-op where both sisters quest to save the Enchanted Bear.
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Snow White

description:
Description from the designer:

This morning your mother, a superb snow leopard, took you outside to practice your pouncing. What she didn't realize is that a deadly Siberian tiger stalks these mountains too! Find the adult snow leopard before you find the Siberian tiger to win in this game of frolics turned fatal!

In this game, one or two players play a group of four snow leopard cubs who are in danger!  They must find their mother before the Siberian Tiger finds them.  The board is made up of thirty cards, played face down.  Twenty-eight of those cards are mountains, one is the adult snow leopard, one is the Siberian tiger.

On each player's turn, as long as they hold a snow leopard cub in their hand, they may swap that card with any card on the board.  The card they take is revealed.  If it is a mountain, nothing happens.  If it is the Siberian tiger, all is lost.  If it is the adult snow leopard, they win!

If the adult snow leopard or Siberian tiger hasn't yet been found, for each snow leopard cub already on the board, the player can flip face-up one card that is located diagonally adjacent to the snow leopard cub card. These cards are flipped down at the end of the turn.

Once all the snow leopard cub cards have been played, players may retrieve a snow leopard cub card on their next turn by swapping it back out with a Mountain card in your hand.  When playing a two-player game, this means you can start to retrieve your cards after playing two, instead of all four.

This game can be played solo, with one person controlling all four snow leopard cubs, or fully co-op with two players playing two cubs each.

boardgame: Bears vs Babies: NSFW Expansion Pack
description: Bears vs Babies: NSFW Booster Pack includes non-kid friendly cards for use in the Bears vs Babies base game.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Lock 'n Load Tactical: Compendium Volume 2 Modern Era
description:
Lock ’n Load Tactical Compendium Volume 2 Modern Era

The Lock ’n Load Tactical Compendium Volume 2 collates, in a re-edited and updated format, all of the essential Modern-Era LnLT articles, maps and scenarios from every issue of Line of Fire magazine, plus some new content.

In the Compendium Volume 2 you can read indispensable articles on forces and tactics and how to deploy them in games such as Heroes of the Nam, Heroes of the Falklands, Heroes Against the Red Star and Day of Heroes.  Get up to speed on modern tank tactics. Read about the Long Range Reconnaissance Patrols (LRRPs) in Vietnam and then play two scenarios that feature them. Plus, historical articles that feature tactical insights on both Heroes of the Nam and The Bear & The Jackal provide insight and perspective into mastering the scenarios in those games—and, more importantly, in how best to defeat your opponent. And much more.

Scenarios, and more gameplay, are what most gamers want, and there are over 20 in Volume 2 of the Compendium. From a bold rescue mission in the jungle of Vietnam in 1972 to the Falkland Islands in 1982 and the Cold War gone Hot in 1985, there are battles to be fought, and desperate struggles to unfold. With over 100 pages, over 20 scenarios and 1 map, this Compendium has something for everyone who’s a fan of the Lock ’n Load Tactical series’ Modern-Era games. And it’s all right here: One game system, one era. one place!
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
In Sweets!, players each start the game with three sweets cards from the fifty-card deck, with five cards placed face up in the tableau. On a turn, a player either draws a face-up card or the top card of the deck (unless they already have five cards in hand), swap two or more cards with an equal number of cards from the tableau, or eat a set of sweets in their hand to score points.

Once the deck is exhausted, each player eats once, then they tally their points based on the number of stars on the cards they ate. Whoever has the most points wins.

boardgame: Isle of Skye: From Chieftain to King – Adjacency Scrolls
description: This small expansion consists of six tiles to be added to the tile mix. Each of the tiles contain a scroll that scores features only on adjacent tiles. Theses special scrolls are marked with arrows.

Tower Defense is a light, asymmetric game where one player defends a castle from the onslaught of soldiers from the other player. The defender needs to stop the hoards of soldiers while the attacker tries to destroy the castle as quickly as possible.

The game comes with a one game card which represents the playing surface. Players are expected to supply their own coins (10 one-cent coins, 10 two-cent coins, and 10 5 cent coins).

Depending on their placement, the coins have multiple purpose. They either: (1) represent the life points of the castle and the soldiers, (2) determine how a soldier enters the board when rolled by the attacker or (3) show how much damage a soldier will suffer when played by the castle defender.

An expansion to Robogem board game. Contains 24 new cards, 8 new tiles and new rules.

- Repeat statements allow commands to be repeated infinitely or five or three times.
- Conditional statements allow for finer control: conditionals can check if it's possible to move forward, left or right, or if there's a diamond in front of the robot. 
- Special cards allow robots to hijack diamonds, turn towards the closest diamond, jump over obstacles or destroy obstacles.
- Equipment cards include a rocket motor, super powers, extra card powers and the possibility to hijack someone else's robot for a turn.

New tiles include hologram plates that are safe spots for robots.

The new cards allow for more complicated programming.

Monstrology is a fully cooperative card game, which you can also play competitively between two teams, a la 'Charades', suitable for gamers of all ages. You can play Monstrology with 2-8 people. Each round takes about 5 minutes, with a recommendation of at least 2 rounds per player. The game requires both sides of your brain to work together, as you'll have to blend your imagination and deduction skills in order to succeed.

At the core of the game are 84 illustrated imaginary monster cards, depicting some of the most beautiful, strange, unique monsters you have ever encountered. The game also includes twelve taxonomy boards that will allow you to categorize and identify these delightful, unsettling creatures.

A simplified explanation of the game:

1. One player - the "subject" of a monstrological investigation - randomly and secretly selects an imaginary monster from the deck of monster cards.

2. The subject fills out a "taxonomy board" in order to give the other players (the "investigators") a sense of his or her monster.  The subject cannot communicate with the other players in any other way - no facial expressions or hand gestures!

3. The subject places his or her monster in a lineup with other imaginary monsters (i.e. more cards).

4. The investigators attempt to deduce which monster belongs to the subject. Lively discussion ensues as the investigators attempt to convince each other that "this monster looks like he'd prefer flies to gerbils", or "are you kidding? that monster would be terrible at babysitting!"

5. The more investigators who select the correct monster, the more points the team scores.

In general relativity, an event horizon is a space-time boundary beyond which no matter can escape; it is a "point of no return" past which nothing will be the same.

Event Horizon is also the title of this expansion for Exodus: Proxima Centauri. Designed by Rainer Åhlfors and Andrei Novac, it marks a significant step in the Exodus universe!

Exodus: Event Horizon is fully enjoyable when integrated with just the base game of Exodus: Proxima Centauri, but it also works great along with Edge of Extinction, the previously released first expansion for Exodus. Even though all ten modules herein are compatible with one another and can be used together, each represents a new or enhanced experience. While some modules integrate seamlessly, others will surprise and engage you in brand new modes of play.

The expansion also contains two scenarios that provide brand new ways to enjoy the game, including both cooperative and semi-cooperative game modes, a flexible solo-mode, and a scenario that introduces timed game events.

Module Summary



    
         Bonus Actions

         M1

         This module adds additional Bonus Action cards as options in your games.

    
    
         Centaurian Resistance

         M2

         The Resistance fleets have been reimagined to better scale in both threat and reward throughout the game.

    
    
         Centaurian Outposts

         M3

         Centaurians are actively defending their planets against players who exploit their valuable resources.

    
    
         Exploration

         M4

         Surprising events and sudden dangers await those brave enough to explore the unknown worlds.

    
    
         New Planets

         M5

         New and exotic planets provide even more variety and choices as you expand the reach of your faction.

    
    
         Proxima Centauri

         M6

         Our home star makes an appearance in the game and impacts the planetary system with solar wind.

    
    
         Jump Gates

         M7

         Utility structures suitable for both strategic and tactical use as well as emergency response.

    
    
         Communication Satellites

         M8

         With a strong communication network, your ships will gain an advantage away from home.

    
    
         Energy Barriers

         M9

         Natural pockets of energy which can be both a hindrance and a boon.

    
    
         Leaders

         M10

         Employ powerful leaders and benefit from their unique strengths.

    


Scenario Summary



    
         Centaurian Stronghold

         Multi-Player,
Fully Cooperative

         Challenging game mode where the players must work together to eliminate a Centaurian Stronghold megastructure while fighting against relentless waves of Centaurian Warship Squadrons that pursue player ships. No combat takes place between players in the fully cooperative game mode.

    
    
         Multi-Player,
Semi-Cooperative

         Most of the scenario rules remain the same in the semi-cooperative game mode. The players can still collectively lose the game if they fail to eliminate the Centaurian Stronghold. However, both the Stronghold itself and the Warship Squadrons are slightly less deadly. On the other hand, they players are also competing against each other as normal.

    
    
         Solitaire

         True solitaire game mode where you can enjoy the full Exodus experience, with or without whatever expansion content you like.

    
    
         Event Horizon

         Multi-Player,
Competitive

         New challenge mode where the players must attempt to score the most victory points before the planetary neighborhood of Proxima Centauri becomes uninhabitable. Triggered in-game events may end the game a turn or two early, requiring the players to always make optimal use of each turn.

boardgame: To the Last Extremity!: The Siege of Fort Stanwix
description:
To The Last Extremity recreates the 21-day siege of Fort Stanwix in 1777 during The American Revolution. Any number of decisions or events could have altered the outcome, and 'To The Last Extremity' gives players the chance to see what might have been." --from the rule book.

In To The Last Extremity the forces of the fledgling United States of America, consisting of Continental Army infantry and artillery, local militia, and allied Oneida Indian units, face off against the besieging forces consisting of British Army infantry and artillery, Hessian Jägers, Canadian militia, and American Indian allies. Each side receives a deck of cards representing its military forces and the various leaders instrumental in the campaign, such as the American commander, Col. Peter Gansevoort, and the British commander, Brig. Gen. Barry St. Leger, plus many others (including American Indian leaders and Benedict Arnold before he turned traitor!).

Players face off on opposite sides of a period map of the area surrounding Fort Stanwix. At the beginning of each turn, the American player will roll to see if a Chance card is drawn. Chance cards introduce historical events into the game and their effects on one or both sides. The rule book has detailed historical descriptions of each event, which are informational only, but help to bring the history and drama of the Siege of Fort Stanwix to life. Following a possible Chance card draw, players will secretly assign forces and leaders to each of the four sides of Fort Stanwix: north, south, east, and west. How the players choose to distribute their forces will determine the outcome of the siege. Players then reveal their cards. If the British have assigned forces to assault one or more sides of the fort, players will compare the strengths of the opposing forces on that side and roll a die to determine the resultant casualties and whether a breach of the fortress walls has occurred. If the British achieve two breaches on the same turn, they have taken the fort and win the game. If, after 13 turns, the British fail to take the fort, the outnumbered Americans have broken the siege and have defeated the British.

On each turn, the British may also elect to dig entrenchments to protect their artillery, or bombard or skirmish with sides of the fort to effect American morale.

Easily playable in under an hour, "To The Last Extremity" is a simple wargame that provides a great deal of historical flavor surrounding one of the earliest American victories achieved in U.S. history.

boardgame: Camel Up: Grand Prix of the Sahara
description:
This is a Playmat for Camel Up with whirlwind spaces that have special Rules:

The space 6, 7 and 8 on the race track are considered to be whirlwind spaces.

    If a camel or a camel unit leaves space 6, they always move to space 8.
    If a camel or came unit leaves space 7, it always moves to space 6.
    (The arrow of the whirlwind indicate the direction the camels move.)


A camel or camel unit may only move from space 8 to space 9, if at least one of the following conditions is met:

    There is another camel or camel unit on a whirlwind space(6, 7 or 8);
    All other camels are already on space 9 or beyond.


If no condition is met, the camel or camel unit moves backwards to space 7.
From 7 it will move to space 6 with its next step.
If it gets back to space 6 it will move again to space 8 with its next movement.
There it needs to check again for the conditions to move forward to space 9.

Important:
You may not place your desert tile on any of the whirlwind spaces (neither oasis side nor mirage side).

boardgame: No Thank You, Evil!: Story, Please!
description:
Description from the publisher:

No Thank You, Evil!: Story, Please! is a one hundred-card deck stuffed full of evocative art and creative ideas, letting you quickly build hundreds of unique adventures for No Thank You, Evil!

To use these new cards, combine an engaging STORY card with one or more cool PLACE and PERSON cards to outline your quest. Then give your adventure a surprising TWIST, and some cool STUFF. Map and handout cards complete the adventure. The deck is the same size as the cards in the original game, allowing you to use it with the Creature and Cypher Cards from that game.

boardgame: No Thank You, Evil!: Uh-Oh, Monsters!
description:
Description from the publisher:

No Thank You, Evil!: Uh-Oh, Monsters! is an adventure expansion that includes everything you need to run new adventures for No Thank You, Evil!

• Claws and Paws: The Monsters You'll Meet! is a creature book chock-full of monsters, humans, aliens, robots and tons of other walking, talking, fighting, biting creatures you’ll encounter in Storia. Learn more about the Monster Museum, discover quirks, and learn how to make your own monsters.

• Lairs and Scares: Let's Go on Wild Adventures! is an adventure book that offers three brand-new adventures for No Thank You, Evil!, all with fun monster themes.

• 30 new creature cards that match the format of the cards in the original game for quick-and-easy reference.

• 15 creature stand-ups that let you bring creatures to life right at the table!

It's time for the annual Coney Island Hot Dog Show where competitors worldwide will compete for the coveted title of Wurst In Show!

In Hot Dogs, you will need to craft a variety of frankfurters quickly and accurately using your ingredient cards, but you are working with a head chef that is trying to mess you up. In order to gain a point, you must win one round, take a detriment card and then win the next round. You could say you need to win "at your Wurst point"!

If nobody succeeds, the chef scores. Play continues until all players have been chef. To win you have to make 'em fast, make 'em right and do it all before the other players ketchup.

The game can be played with 2-6 players
Each player representing one of the main characters from the "Les Enfants du capitaine Grant" novel by Jules Verne: Mary Grant, Robert Grant, Lord Glenarvan, Major Mac Nabbs, Captain John Mangles and Paganel.

The game contains 60 cards with characters (10 cards for each character), 30 value cards (positive and negative), a world map and two plastic figures.

Based on the number of players, a equal number of characters are chosen (there are ten cards for each character) and only these cards will be used in the game.
All these characters cards are shuffled (the total of the caracter cards used it will be ten multiplied by the number of players). Also the value cards are shuffled.

Each player starts the game with ten character cards and five value cards.

The game is played clockwise. The first player put one card from his hand face down. The next player put also a card from his hand face down and then take the card from the previous player and so on.
The scope is to have all the ten cards from a single caracter.
The first player to collect these cards will gain 2000 points.
Bellow are the points for each place:
1 – 2000 points
2 – 1600 points
3 – 1200 points
4 – 800 points
5 – 600 points
6 – 400 points
After that, each player looks at his value cards to calculate the final score. The points from his value cards (can be positive or negative) are added to the points based on his place.
The player with the most points win the game.

This game is a chinese version of Mafia / Werewolf.

There are a judge and 8~16 players in this game. 
The players consist of Civilians, Killers and Policemen. 
The number of Policemen and Killers depends on the total number of players. 
8~10 players: 2 Policemen and 2 Killers. 
11~14 players: 3 Policemen and 3 Killers. 
15~16 players: 4 Policemen and 4 Killers.

Game Goal Policemen: find out Killers and lead Civilians to vote Killers out of the game. Killers: find the Policemen and kill them when it's getting dark. Civilians: help Policemen to vote Killers out of the game. Never ever help Killers on purpose in the game.

A single promotional card.  The power of the Xenobiologist is that once per game you can look at your own parasite card.

This card was included in Game Trade Magazine #201.

boardgame: Sherlock Holmes Détective Conseil: Oliver Stone
description:
On a brisk Sunday morning at around 10am, as we wind our way around the grounds of the Tower of London, a boy, not more than ten years of age, hails Wiggins with a message:

My dear friends, Oliver Stone was found dead in his home. I think it might be instructive for you to look into this matter. Meet me tomorrow afternoon at 221B to compare our respective investigations, if you wish. S.H.

So begins "The Oliver Stone Case," a single extra case expansion originally published in Role Mag' (#3 - November-December 1990).

Once upon a time, there were no lands, and it was the time for creation. There were two gods — the god of the sea and the god of the mountains — who created the world, but each loved their own creations more than anything else, so they came into conflict.

In Minimal Creation (a.k.a. 小さな創世記), players are these two gods and to end their conflict, they each decide to send one of their followers deep into the other's territory to steal a relic that sits there. Over the course of the game, you lay terrain tiles on the game board to expand your territory and lead your follower, moving it along the terrain to the opponent's temple in which the relic lies. The opponent loses a lot of victory points if you successfully steal their relic, but you also need to place tiles to accumulate small points along the way. How much effort you commit on expanding territory and stealing the relic will determine the outcome of the game.

When a follower successfully reaches the opponent's temple, the temple is removed and the game ends. If you have made any 2x2 squares of your territory, each square is worth one point. The temple is worth points if not removed (the invading follower destroys it). The player with the higher score wins.

Designer's summary

貴方たちは世界を創る海の神と山の神です。ある時二人は争いを始めてしまい、冒険者を相手の領域に送り込み秘宝を奪わせることで決着としようと決めたのでした。
　「小さな創世記」では相手と同一のボード上でタイルをつなげて配置して世界を創りながら、その世界の上で自分の冒険者を導き相手の神殿に向かわせます。限られたアクション数の中でこの二つの行動をどう配分するかで勝敗が分かれるでしょう。
　いずれかの冒険者が相手の神殿に到達した時点で、秘宝を失った2×2のマスがなくなりゲームが終了します。自分が司る地形が2×2マス以上にまとまっていればその面積が得点となり、得点が高い方が勝者となります。

boardgame: Nepal (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride)
description:
This map is an unofficial fan made expansion. Symbols & Graphics Copyright © 2004-2015 Days of Wonder, Inc. Produced with permission of Days of Wonder, for non-commercial use only.

This follows the principles of the basic Ticket to Ride game on a special board with special cards and rules. The object of the game is to gain the most points by travelling various routes and outwitting other players. You will need the train pieces and the train cards from either the US or Europe base games (or you can buy them from the Days of Wonder shop.

There are spanners along some tracks which may help or hinder you. The board does not have any grey tracks or parallel tracks - but there are some options in the spanner pack.

You can climb the mountains for extra points.

There are many new options in the rules: exchange all your cards at the start but keep all the new ones: discard 5 cards and pick up 5 new ones, as your turn: earn extra points: and many more. You can also call time when your wagon stock reaches 3, if you want, giving you the advantage of stopping other players completing tickets.

The nation is in a state of civil war: a secret group of rebels are growing fast in numbers and are determined to overthrow the government and start a revolution. Police forces are tasked to go undercover and identify the rebel leaders, before they have time to gather too many followers in the population.
 Rebels Unite! is a party game for 8 to 20+ players, in which you play as a policeman, a rebel leader, or a civilian. Police officers have to arrest the rebel leaders, civilians try to help them in their task, unless they are turned into rebels themselves by a rebel leader. Rebel leaders have to turn as many civilians into rebels as possible without getting caught, and with their help overthrow the corrupt government and start a rebellion!

Rebels Unite! is a game of deception and hidden information. At the beginning of the game each player is given an identity that is unknown to everyone else. Players will try to convince each other that they are staying true to the government, but in the meantime a rebellion is growing behind the scenes. During the game, players will be able to find out and guess each other’s identities, so it will be harder and harder to get away with lying and playing both sides of the conflict.

10 Female Survivor Models for the Basegame and New Cards.

boardgame: Twin Stars: Adventure Series I – Scenario 01: Escape the Brig
description:
Two cosmic adventurers are in a tricky scenario and must work together. By rolling and manipulating dice for special abilities and combos, you can be the one that guides them to victory.

The guard tosses you into the dark cell and you hear the bolt clank into place. Your cries of "Come back!" are answered only with laughter, and heavy footfalls receding down the long corridor.

As your eyes slowly adjust to the darkness, you realize you are not alone. "We have to escape," the prisoner says, "Now."

In Twin Stars: Escape the Brig, two of you are locked up together and need to break free. You can dig a tunnel, but you need to watch out for the guard. There's also a pick that you can use to assist in your escape, but you need to be careful! You must escape to win. If the guard reaches the tunnel you lose.

Escape the Brig is a scenario in the Twin Stars solo system by Jason Tagmire and Mike Mullins. The game consists of just 3 cards and players will provide their own dice and coins/tokens. Each scenario comes with 2 all-new characters and can be interchanged with others for a very different gaming experience.

This is the first scenario in the Twin Stars family and was originally offered as a stretch goal for Button Shy's Pod-X Kickstarter.

boardgame: Twin Stars: Adventure Series I – Scenario 02: The Quark Star
description:
Two cosmic adventurers are in a tricky scenario and must work together. By rolling and manipulating dice for special abilities and combos, you can be the one that guides them to victory.

You look at each other skeptically before powering up the massive collider.

"Why are we doing this again?" you ask, hesitantly.

Your partner shrugs, but you know the answer. It's always the same with you two. Succeed and get paid, or fail and get vaporized.

The discovery of the pentaquark in the early 21st century shed some light on the conditions inside neutron stars. Now, in Twin Stars: The Quark Star you are trying to create the hypothetical Quark Star, an even more dense state of matter. Calibrate, Test, and Experiment with your particle collider, and Capture 5 Quarks to win. Lose if funding reaches $0.

The Quark Star is a scenario in the Twin Stars solo system by Jason Tagmire and Mike Mullins. The game consists of just 3 cards and players will provide their own dice and coins/tokens. Each scenario comes with 2 all-new characters and can be interchanged with others for a very different gaming experience.

This is the second scenario in the Twin Stars family and was originally offered as a stretch goal for Button Shy's Pentaquark Kickstarter.

This is a boardgame based in Dante Alighieri's literary work "The Divine Comedy". Your goal is the game is to collect souls.
To achieve such a task, you assume the role of one of the main characters. Dante, Beatrice, Orpheus and Virgil are the heroes available in the basic box of the game, up to 4 players can play.

There are no dices involved in this game. Players will be interacting over the board, using their few action points to progress in the game.
Many options are available, you can use your action points to recruit hosts for war, play your cards, declare wars, conquer teritories, stop wars, invoke guardians... The main goal of the game can be achieved following different strategies; and luck always plays its part too.

The dark art style designed by for this game has been mostly done "from and over the paper", her creator Melissa J.G. honors Dante's writtings this way.

boardgame: Host Party: Back to the Eighties
description:
This is a murder mystery party game for 8-14 guests.

Setting / Theme:
Get ready for some teased up hair and Member's Only jackets mixed with a fun '80s story line and trivia challenges! Everyone who is anyone in the 80s will be there, all coming from Hollywood to Broadway through Texas. J.R. and Melinda are throwing this dig to introduce Allegra and her new business. This will be a totally bodacious affair. The guests can chill out and have a blast until that moment when someone gets murdered!

Oh those 80s! What fun years with big teased hair and characterized by a "disco" backlash, Rock and Pop Bands with long hairstyles and citizens with political correctness became popular. Prepare your guests for the 80s murder.

How to Play
Planning:

     Host of the Murder Mystery picks the day/time and location for the game which will take 2-3 hours and finds out how many guests can attend.
     Host creates guest list and assigns each guest one of the characters to play.
     Host sends official invite with their character background to each guest.
     Guests create / purchase a costume based on their character.  Thrift stores are good for this.
     Host decorates and plans for food for evening.


Playing the game:

     As guests arrive, the host gives them their instructions for the first round which will include things to say/do/ask.
     After all actions are completed, instructions are given to guests for Round 2.
     Repeat again for Round 3.
     In between rounds you can eat or have activities based on the theme.


Ending:

     Everyone discusses and says who they think the murderer is.
     Each guest reads their final clue in order leading up to the murderer.
     Winner are those that guessed the murderer.  
     If nobody guesses correctly the murderer wins.

The Nameless ever encroach on the last bastion of Gravehold. To defeat the dark, one must first become it.

The Nameless is an expansion for the cooperative deckbuilding game Aeon's End. This expansion features two new nemeses, and one new breach mage, as well as new gems, relics, spells, and minions.

Getting’ the job done just got more interesting. Announcing the Crime & Punishment Game Booster for Firefly: The Game

The Game Booster adds 40 new cards to the Misbehave Deck, presenting more hassles, hardships and the rare opportunity to those crews that might indulge in a bit of criminal behavior now and then. Of course, when there’s trouble in the ‘Verse, you can be sure the Alliance won’t be far behind, new Alliance Priority Alerts affect the entire game with sweeping new temporary conditions. The Game Booster also adds two new Story Cards and two new Set Up Cards.

boardgame: The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game – The Black Serpent
description:
Description from the publisher:

Hundreds of miles from home. Stranded in enemy territory. Outnumbered. Short on supplies… It's time to be a hero!

The Black Serpent is the fourth Adventure Pack in the Haradrim cycle for The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game. Its sixty cards (which include a new hero and three copies of each new player card) lead several of Middle-earth's greatest heroes along a detour from their homeward path. Even in the arid wastes of Harad's deserts, even after traveling league upon grueling league, and even after just barely surviving their encounters with Orcs, Wargs, and giant spiders—the heroes remain true to themselves and committed to their fight against the Dark Lord.

Accordingly, when your heroes come across an opportunity to strike against a caravan of Haradrim carrying tributes to the Dark Lord of Mordor, they hesitate only long enough to devise a plan. When the caravan camps inside the walls of a dilapidated desert fortress, the heroes and their allies will launch their ambush. But will their tactics prove sound enough to account for the caravan's greater numbers? You'll join these heroes and fight alongside them in the new scenario from The Black Serpent.

description:
There once was a Kingdom where precious gems where the most invaluable treasure, but as every treasure comes with wealth it also comes with tragedies. Wars besieged the kingdom, every habitant desire for these treasures was bigger than the love for peace.

Before the Kingdom perished in the hands of War, the king decided to take part in having the battle end in the best way possible. In Kingdom Duel, there is a modular board which can be modified as you like. You also have Heroes that will help you win and defend your walls from the upcoming attacks. Every Hero has their own set of abilities that can be used against the enemy or to help your own party win the battle.

Rules

boardgame: Paß gut auf und komm bald wieder
description:
There are 4 starting Points (Houses) for 4 different traffic participants: a car, a three-wheeled delivery van, a bicycle and a pedestrian. The first player to reach the wholesale supplier (Grosshändler) and get back to his starting point wins the game. Movement is determinated by a die. The players may choose their own way while minding the traffic rules. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players.

The game was published sometime between 1945 and 1953 to promote Dreiring soap and laundry detergent.

Dance Off: Furry Fury is a competitive card game based on a dance competition. You play 3 cards face down and compare them to your opponent's, utilizing 12 different dance moves.  Keep track of your score because after the 3rd round, the player with the lowest score at the end of the round gets eliminated.
