description:
A quick Tabletop Roleplaying game, Heroes Cove has you choose a race and a class and then you can go on quests with others, or you can work by yourself. The standard game takes you though seven quests, but you can do more or less depending on how much time you want to play. The game comes with 40 quests so you can replay many times without repeats.

You need to go to one of the four shops to gather the needed quest items to beat the pirates, repair the bridge or other tasks needed by the townsfolk. Ultimately, whomever does the best will attract the attention of better trainers and gain more Victory Points.

Each round the players will have two actions. There are several different things that can be done, if you have an item needed for a quest you can contribute that item. You can grab an individual quest or you can go shopping for items.

The game comes with 4 shops, the Tavern, the Blacksmith, the Armorsmith and the General Store. Each quest will require you to visit these different shops to find the items needed. Future expansions will add new shops and new quests!
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: "Ratespaß auf Reisen" is a travel game for children. Questions regarding travelling with a car will teach the children how to act on the street and the meaning of the different traffic signs. The not included Tiptoi pen asks each player a question or gives a task to all of the players like "The first player that finds a red car will win this round and gain 10 points".

From the IndieGoGo campaign

The union of history, art, strategy, entertainment and passion, that’s what M400 was born from. A game of conquest, which revolves around an important and defining historical period: the 1400.

But this century is so much more than just a bunch of years: it’s one of the most magnificent periods in the history of Italy and the European culture. Because it’s not a coincidence if the end of this century marks the beginning of the “Modern Age”.
M400 comes from the idea to bring strategic board games to the next level; neither the name nor the historical references to the XV century are just a fortuity. The century that saw the fall of the last Eastern Roman Empire, the birth of the power of Spain and England, the ascent of the Russian Republics, and the achievements of the glorious Republic of Venice are the ideal background for the birth of this new way to have fun with history.
M400 has a high level of playability with multiple players, and the cards allow an always different gameplay, helping the creation of new and different matches. Every time a player finds themselves in the late Medieval period of the game, they can rewrite important pages of history in new ways, through trades, battles, journeys and revolutions. The challenge and the strategy also allow to create with the opponents new dynamics of gameplay beyond the rules: deceptions, bluffs, silent and secret alliances. The almost total absence of chances and coincidences, linked to the dices, reduces the possibility of a player to win just by chance.
M400 is a game which contains very different elements, elements that can mix, and give the player a unique and unpredictable kind of entertainment. This game is for everybody, and the choice of the Latin makes it reach a new level of universality, just like the one of the world of the XV century.

M400 is a card-driven Risk-like game. A player's turns consists of four phases: 1) draw cards, 2) trade cards with other players, 3) move army, 4) expand. Players use cards to resolve combats and score points by occupying a region (temporary points) and defeating opponents (permanent points). The game is won by the player who first achieve a determined number of points, depending on the number of players.

A mansion in French Quarter has been the center of woe for 30 years. Its taxes are paid, and the power is on, but no one has seen anything in the house for years. Suddenly you have received an invitation that requests you to come for dinner at this address. "I know what you are looking for, and you will find the answers you need here on this night". You have just been invited to play A House in New Orleans A game for 2-6 players. Some will call it a player deduction game or a game with a traitor mechanism, but most will call it horrifyingly intense. 
You are invited to A House in New Orleans come hungry.

The game takes place in an old mansion in New Orleans. Each room contains two item cards face down. Players select unique characters with special abilities and stats. They will move from room to room discovering items. Some may be traps that impede players, some may be special items that improve a character. You may only choose to carry one item, so every room will provide a new discovery and a decision.

Human Goal:
Players are attempting to find all three items that their character requires. When one player finds all three items the game is over.  If all but one player has 2 items, the game is over as well. Each of the collected items has a point value and the points are totaled at the end of the game. You must be careful what items you choose to collect, if the items you collect are of too low a point value a player with fewer items may still win.

Vampire goal:
Did I mention a lonely vampire has disguised themselves among the dinner guests? The goal for the original vampire is not to acquire items themselves, but to "Turn" human players that have already acquired items. You see, nothing tastes as good as hope. If a human is attacked and can’t fend off the vampire, they are now secretly a vampire themselves looking for their victim. If the vampire is successfully fended off the vampire is not “revealed” and may become invisible (hidden player movement).  If the vampire turns a player, the new vampire gains points for the items they have already acquired but give the Vampire that turned them points as well. They do not give their "Parent" vampire points from the new victims they acquire. The closer a human player is to completing their goal the more valuable they are, but the more human players you "Turn" the more competition you make for yourself.  If you wait too long, they may be able to fend you off

The Clock:
The old grandfather clock gongs on the hour, at which time a new event card is turned over. These events are random but become worse for the humans as the game progresses. The humans may skip an event if they place an item that turn. Do you help the other humans even though it may mean losing the game to avoid the next horrible event..it is up to you.

Does it sound simple? It is simple to learn, and as complex as the mind of the players that you play against.

boardgame: Captains of the Golden Age: Captains and Characters
description:
ERRATA: Players can only buy one character per turn.

THE STORY

The infamous innkeeper has a curious grin on his face as he says “Friends,how good of you to visit Smuggler’s Cove inn again! I’ve been quite busy expanding here, let me show you.”

He opens a hatch behind the bar and urges you to step down a ladder into what you assume must be a secret room below. As you descend the ladder the scent of spices, perfume, and other exotic wares you can’t quite place greets you. The room is as exquisite in decoration as it is in fragrance, and inside is a group of a dozen or so outlandish individuals.

“These are some of the friends I met while sailing the seven seas”,says the infamous innkeeper. “Like myself, each of them is uniquely skilled and they’ll gladly work for you.

But their trade doesn’t come cheap; it will cost you pepper...”

HIRING CHARACTERS:

Characters can be bought for a single unit of pepper at Smuggler’s Cove during the harbor phase. Players may always look at the stack of available characters, and when they buy a character they may choose it from the stack themselves. Players may have any number of character cards.

TURNING CHARACTERS FACE-DOWN:

Each character has a unique effect but only functions as long as it is face-up. Characters can be turned face-down in the following four ways:

1-When you board another players’ ship with your crew and win, for each cargo of the losing player you decline to steal you may choose a character of that player and turn it face-down.

2-When you bombard another players’ ship with your cannons and win, for each ship segment of the losing player you decline to damage you may choose a character of that player and turn it face-down.

3-‘The Artful Assassin’ can be used to turn a character of another player face-down.

4-‘The Sneaky Stowaway’ has to be turned face-down when it is used.

TURNING CHARACTERS FACE-UP
Face-down characters can be turned face-up again in the following three ways:

1-In the Republic you may turn all your characters face-up during your harbor phase.

2-In the Orient you may turn all your characters face-up during your harbor phase.

3-At Smuggler’s Cove you may turn all your characters face-up during your harbor phase if you have ‘The Infamous Innkeeper’ and it is face-up.

In The Clockwork Conundrum, after each player receives 3 cards and 3 pawns, a modular board is formed 5 cards wide x 5 cards high (cards are left face down), surrounding a single victory card. All players besides the start player may examine their cards, and place these however desired. The start player may only see his cards after choosing which card to begin with. He places his pawn on this card.

Most cards have a L, M, R, or C in their upper right corner. These stand for Left, Middle, Right, and Choice. When the start player reveals his start card, it determines where the next player places his pawn. This unleashes a chain of events. The last player's pawn placement will indicate the two to four movement options for the start player. (These are indicated by direction markers on each card.) His subsequent move will indicate the next player's move and so on. This can be treacherous given the players don't know the cards they are moving to until they make their moves. It is entirely possible that a player may be forced off of the board, or onto one of the more bizarre action cards.

1) FREEZE: If a player settles on this card, the next player in turn order may not move. Instead whichever card he previously settled on will indicate the next player's movement options.

2) LAVA: If a player stumbles into a lava pit, he loses one of his three pawns and starts over on the start card indicated by the lava card.

3) MIRROR MIRROR: If a player settles onto a Mirror Mirror card, the next player in turn order will move across the board to the card in the mirror position.

4) +PAWN: If a player settles onto a +Pawn card, he adds a second pawn to the start card of his choice. This increases his movement options, since he will still only move 1 pawn at a time. Therefore, his likelihood of reaching his destination is significantly increased.

5) REVERSE: If a player settles onto a Reverse card, the turn order is reversed, and the player who preceded him must move in the direction indicated by the card on which he is currently settled.

The game only uses 37 cards. There are 41 in total. But the remaining cards are not totally useless. While increasing the variety of the modular board, the cards also help to resolve conflicts if a player has settled onto a card where his opponent is currently positioned. Each card, excluding the Victory Card, has a number 1-40. When players are involved in a conflict, each draws a card at random, and the player with the higher card gains the space, whereas the player with the lower card loses his pawn.

Usually any cards revealed are left face-up, but there is also a Memory Variant where after moving to a new location, any cards not currently active are flipped back to face-down.

Due to the unique design of the game, it may also be played cooperatively, as players work together to lead one to the Victory Card.

The Clockwork Conundrum is an intense puzzle game. And due to its featuring nearly 100,000 ways to set up, it is highly replayable. Who will gain the victory in a game where your next moves are triggered by your opponent?

Best of the Worst is the game of life's lesser moments! Do you remember your WORST first date? How about your WORST trip to the dentist or WORST day at school? Sure they all seem terrible at the time, even life-threatening in some cases! But over time, you can look back and there's usually a pretty good story attached to the experience! That's BEST of the WORST! Do your BEST and tell your WORST! For 3-6 players.

Draw a card and come up with your best story to go along with the worst scenario on the card

Tell what really happened or go ahead and stretch the truth if you'd prefer

It's up to everyone else playing to determine if what you're saying is a true story or a bogus story. Every player who tells a story advances one space and gets an additional space for every vote they receive.

Ty plush characters are playing hide and seek. Peek under the coconut halves to find them!
Ty Beanie Boos Coconut features 16 playing pieces that look like colorful coconuts halves. When players turn the Coconut playing pieces over, each piece shows either a blue or pink background color. Careful, don’t turn over the piece that shows the Bat, he’s trying to eat all your Coconuts!

Each turn, players push the die popper, which indicates whether the player needs to find a blue or pink Coconut during their turn. Choose your Coconut and turn it over. If it matches the die, it is yours. If a player turns over the Bat, they must put back one of their Coconut pieces and mix-up the remaining pieces. Collect the most Coconuts pieces to win. The chunky plastic pieces are easy to handle for young players.

Face off against your opponents in a series of challenges sure to test your speed and agility. Actions that anyone can do-it all comes down to who can do it first. Flip It, Stack It, Spell It, Roll It - SHABOOM!.

To play, flip over a card. If it's an Event card, follow the instructions on the card. The illustration on the card shows you which pieces to use and how to use them. The first player to successfully complete the challenge gets to keep the card.

Last Out cards play the same as Event cards, but you simply don't want to be the last player to complete the event, otherwise you have to return one of your collected cards to the deck.

Boom Laws cards don't require any competition, but they do put a restriction on the next Event or Last Out card to be drawn. For instance, you might have to put your right hand on your neighbor's shoulder while trying to roll even numbers using all five of your dice.

The first player to collect 10 cards wins.

A revolution of industry is in full swing.  Everywhere you look, people and animal power are being replaced by steam-powered machines.  Steam rises from every street corner, filling the city with noise and commotion.  It is the industrial revolution at the height of Victorian England.  A new technology called the link is on the rise and you want to profit from it.  The link makes it possible to live in the sky.  People are clamoring for the chance to visit or live high overhead.  You decide to sink all you have into the venture.  It is all or nothing.  Empires of steam documents the rise of your empire as it grows on water, land and in the great expanse overhead.

Start with 5 coin cards.  With each turn, add additional cards thru maneuvers and purchases.  Build your deck to gain more maneuvering and purchasing power.  But wait, the point of the game is not to build your deck, rather its to build your empire.  Grown your empire by placing cards into your empire, but once they are in your empire, they can no longer be used to grow your deck.  Find the right balance to beat your opponents.  Many cards in your empire have a population associated with it.  In the end, the winner is the player with the most population in their empire.

Just be careful, because as you are focusing on your deck and empire, another may be ready to attack with the saboteur.

This is a deck building, card placing game of strategy.

Octo is a press-your-luck dice-rolling game where the goal is to set, and then maintain, a high score.  Once a player has set the score, it's up to the rest of the players to beat it--and then become the holder of the score themselves.

Players start with eight six-sided dice and roll them all at once, then must set aside at least one of the dice rolled.  They may then stop or reroll all dice not set aside.  The caveat is that the same face value may not be claimed more than once per turn; once you've set aside two sixes, for example, further sixes you roll cannot be used.  If you roll and cannot set aside any of the dice due to them matching previously-rolled values, you bust.

Octo is one of several games Reiner Knizia describes in his book Dice Games Properly Explained.

boardgame: Tank On Tank: West Front – Defenders of the Rhine
description:
Tank on Tank: West Front – Defenders of the Rhine is a new expansion to our Tank on Tank: West Front series. In this expansion, Tank On Tank fans can delve into catastrophic armored clashes between the United States and Germany. This expansion includes two maps and many customized scenario rules to add to your gaming experience. Within these ten scenarios, US tanks confront German panzers in devastating battles in villages and over rivers to achieve victory towards the liberation of Europe. Also included for owners of Tank on Tank: East Front is a special bonus scenario, “Patton Strikes West”, which has the US and German forces teaming up in an attempt to push the aggressive Soviet bear back into Motherland. Enjoy hours of added gameplay with this expansion. It’s fun. It’s tactical. Get in a turret and take command!

You must own Tank on Tank: West Front to play Defenders of the Rhine.
family: Series: Tank on Tank (Lock 'n Load Publishing)

boardgame: Tank On Tank: East Front – Red Storm in the Valley
description:
Tank on Tank: East Front – Red Storm in the Valley is a new expansion to our Tank on Tank: East Front series. In this expansion, Tank on Tank fans can delve into catastrophic armored clashes between Germany and the Soviet Union. This expansion includes two maps and many customized scenario rules to add to your gaming experience. Within these ten scenarios, the Soviets confront the Germans in huge armor battles in valleys, depressions and over railroad tracks in order to achieve victory for the Motherland. Also included for owners of Tank on Tank: West Front is a special bonus scenario, “The Bear at the Rhine”, which has Soviet forces attempting to push the US and Germans back to the Rhine River. Enjoy hours of added gameplay with this expansion. It’s fun. It’s tactical. Get in a turret and take command!

You must own Tank on Tank: East Front to play Red Storm in the Valley.
family: Series: Tank on Tank (Lock 'n Load Publishing)

description:
You have all been trapped in a room by the psychopath Enigma. There’s only one means of escape and you must get the correct tool to unlock the exit. Only one person can escape while the rest will perish. Beware as you do not know the other players’ identities and Enigma might just be one of them.

There are 2 ways to win in highly-addictive game of strategy, deduction and deception from the creators of Mystery Manila:

(1) Be the first person to escape
(2) Be the last person left alive

If you are playing the character of Enigma, the only way to win is to kill everybody else.

Each game box contains the following components:

- 7 Character Cards
- 7 Exit Cards
- 7 Tool Tokens

To start the game, each player is handed at random a Character Card with a specific ability. An Exit Card is randomly drawn and placed faced down in the middle of the table. This card is now the Escape Card.

All remaining Character Cards and Exit Cards are placed in two separate piles near the center of the table. All Tool Tokens are also placed individually at the center of the table.

Everyone closes their eyes. While one person counts loudly back from ten, the player holding the Enigma Character Card opens his eyes, looks at the Escape Card, and places it back to it's original position.

In every game of Escape from Enigma, players take turns doing one of the following actions:

- Take an Exit Card
- Take a Tool Token
- Do Character Card's ability
- Bluff and do another Character Card's ability
- Change your Character Card (except if you are Enigma)
- Attempt to escape (except if you are Enigma)
- Pass

To Escape, a player must call out a Tool Token he owns and peek at the Escape Card. If they match, the player wins the game. Otherwise, that player is eliminated.

When a player is eliminated, all his Exit Cards and Tool Tokens are returned to the center of the table, and the game resumes.

A second way to get eliminated for the game is through bluffing. When a player bluffs and is challenged, the defender and the challenger must show each other their Character Cards. Whoever loses the challenge is eliminated and gives the winner all his Exit Cards and Tool Tokens. The winner may also assume the loser's Character Card.

Will you be the survivor of Escape from Enigma?

boardgame: Age of Thieves: Chaos in Hadria
description: Four tarot-sized full art cards mini expansion. Expansion contains new district cards with additional rules, which change the game after one of the players steals the Emperor's Jewel.

The game involves two sets of pieces of dinosaurs: team red and blue.
Each player must try to beat the opponent. The winner is the one who will beat all the dinosaurs from the opponents team or the player who can reach to the base of the opponent with their pawn. Each turn you can move one piece to an adjacent hex, play a special movement card, or launch a little ball from a tiny catapult on your end of the board, trying to knock down opponent's pieces.

In Wyspa Skarbów players take on the role of expedition commanders who received a message about an old pirate treasure that is hidden on one of the caribbean islands. They embark on a journey to acquire legendary riches. 
The player must go through three stages of the game, which in turn will make him closer to the final success. 
On the first stage players search for treasure maps in the island to enable further exploration. 
On the second stage the objective is to find the key, which makes it possible to open the hidde treasure in the third and final stage. 
The winner is the first to overcome all the obstacles
and reach the hidden pirate treasure.

boardgame: Zagor: Odissea Americana – Molti occhi Promo Card
description:
Zagor: Odissea Americana: Molti occhi Promo Card is one of a set of 4 event cards to be added to those in the Zagor: Odissea Americana base game. The cards are simply shuffled in with the base game cards, adding more variety from game to game.

The first 3 italian versions of the cards (Molti occhi, Trampy and Icaro La Plume) were given to the visitors of Lucca Comics & Games 2016, a comics & gameplay festival in Lucca in 2016, and later during other promotional events with the 4th card (Insidie sul fiume). It's one of the first additional cards for the Italian edition of Zagor: Odissea Americana.

Text:

Molti occhi / Many Eyes
Ogni giocatore fuori dal battello sceglie se curarsi una ferita (simbolo) o pescare un oggetto (simbolo) dal mazzo
Any off the boat player chooses either to heal a wound (symbol) or draw an object (symbol) from the deck

Say Cheese is a multiplication board game for students of grade 1 and up. It is designed to get them 14 times more multiplication practice than classroom.

Players take turns pulling a number out of a bag and places it in the play arena. The next player takes another number, checks to see if it is multiple of four (say "Cheese" if it is) and recites its multiplication fact. If correct, they receive all the tokens in play and puts them in their bag.

Game ends when one person has no more tokens and the person with the most wins.

Ocean Raiders is an addition board game for students of grade 1 and up. It is designed to get them 11 times more addition practice than classroom.

Ocean Raiders is a simple roll-and-move game where you roll the dice and move your pawn the corresponding number of spaces. If you land on someone else, their pawn is sent back to the start (they found you in the ocean). First player to 49 wins the game.

Say Cheese Cafe is a multiplication tables board game for students of grade 1 and up. It is designed to get them 14 times more multiplication tables practice than classroom.

Similar to their first product, Cafe is a more advanced version of Say Cheese with the addition of "rotten coins", "dessert coins" and a spinner which sets the "cheese" value to a random number instead of the standard "4" in the base game.

Big Catch is a division board game for students of grade 3 and up. It is designed to get them 8 times more division practice than classroom.

Each player draws a card on their turn with a number that must be divided by the current number of islands in play. A token is placed on each island equal to the result, with the remainders being saved for subsequent rounds.  These can also be used to move from island to island, creating an area-control mechanism.

There is also an included dry erase slate to help the child work out the problems with long division.

The first to get to "Home Island" wins the game.

boardgame: Elder Sign: Omens of the Deep
description:
The murky depths of the Pacific ocean conceal untold horrors in Omens of the Deep, a new expansion for Elder Sign, the cooperative dice game steeped in the lore of H.P. Lovecraft’s terrifying mythos. Based on the popular The Call of Cthulhu expansion for the Elder Sign: Omens app, players can now venture forth from the confines of the museum and board The Ultima Thule to combat the Ancient Ones that have begun to stir in the depths of the Pacific.

The new R’lyeh Rising mode of gameplay features two stages as you first navigate the Dark Waters track and then assemble the Amulet of R’lyeh to stop one of three dangerous new Ancient Ones from waking and devouring the world. Engage the newly introduced mission tokens for hard-won rewards and fend off the dogged Deep One Legion as your investigators move to unknown dangers on the Pacific Adventure cards. The depths of the Pacific are filled with fresh terrors that only the bravest can attempt to seal with the power of the Elder Signs in the Omens of the Deep expansion for Elder Sign!

-description from the publisher:
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet
family: Series: Arkham Horror Files (Fantasy Flight Games)

description:
Deep inside the mine, the investigators have stumbled across something sinister. They have previously encountered fear embodied. They have stared into the depths of madness. They have even fought against a madman who sought to dominate their psyches. But how will they be able to defeat a demon born out of the darkness infesting their own souls? Dagon is coming. And he is not rising from the sea: he is rising from the filth inside your own mind. This is Rise of Dagon, a new expansion for Deep Madness.

Rise of Dagon features three new investigators: the celebrity "single mother" who runs the Leng Corporation, Meredith Waite, her sheltered but oh-so-special daughter, Regan, and a strange writer who seems to have stepped right out of another century, Ward Phillips.

Rise of Dagon is also populated with several new horrors. There's the Deep Ones: mutated, shambling creatures who were once men and women. There's the Hydras: High Priestesses of a dark, malevolent, and very present power. There's the Shadows of Filth: oozing, nightmarish beings who cling to their matching investigator like a bad rash. And then there is, of course, Dagon himself.

Rise of Dagon features several new mechanics, both for good and bad. 

    First of all, Rise of Dagon functions as an ongoing campaign. Search cards, infection cards, filth cards, infection tokens, and filth tokens are carried from one game to the next, and the three scenarios must be played in order. 
    It is very possible to slowly lose your humanity throughout the course of Rise of Dagon.
    there's the new Domain and Enlighten mechanic. When you rest during Dagon, you will take a domain card. These cards are sometimes good, sometimes not so good, but often feature a trade-off: a positive effect for a negative effect. Once these cards are used they aren't discarded. They're just wiped clean, and you can build them again.

When the investigators first came to Kadath, they received a vision of a man intent on helping them defeat the terrors rampant in the mining facility. Little did they know that one of their only allies would turn into one of their greatest threats. This is The Oracle’s Betrayal, an expansion for Deep Madness.

Oracle features three new investigators: the morally-driven detective John Murdock; the morally depraved psychologist Dr. Clarence Branom; and the star of the whole #justiceforhannah movement, little Hannah herself.

New nasties include: the Victims: lost, fractured souls who only want to survive - and who the investigators start out Chapter One as; the Collectors: monstrous diving-helmet-bedecked servants who exist for one reason - to harvest fragments of souls and return them to their abominable master; the Dissected: creatures who might not always be as evil as their horrific appearance may at first hint at. And then, last of all, the horrific mastermind Dr. William West.

The Oracle's Betrayal has several new mechanics tied in to some very unique scenarios. 

    The fusion deck. Fusion cards work by integrating two investigators together.
    William's will, trial, and puppet cards. The puppet's goal is now to kill the other investigators while the investigators will be unable to win the scenario unless they kill the puppet.

User summary:

Mickey's Poppin' Magic is a children's pop-o-matic action game with a Disney theme. Players move pegged stand-up tokens (Minnie Mouse, Donald Duck, Pluto, and Goofy) around the edge of a 3-D plastic popcorn bowl. The playing surface is a series of holes (designed to fit the pegs, of course) with a hidden trigger mechanism underneath. If the pop-o-matic die roller indicates a "popcorn" result the trigger is activated randomly, possibly knocking somebody off the pathway. Players also trigger a random poppin' event when they land on a popcorn space. First player to complete the circle is declared the winner.

Designed for 2, 3, or 4 players ages 4 to 8 years old. No reading is required to play. Spanish rules are included.

The stated object of the game is to "do everything you can to survive out-of-control government, corrupt unions and environmental weenies who want to destroy your business and livelihood." The object of the game is to be the last player with at least one franchise, or to be the first player to complete four circuits of the board and roll "some snake eyes"; failure to roll two ones on two six sided dice on completing four circuits eliminates that player from the game.

As with many games of this genre, the focus is on political satire rather than game play.

Bring the fast-paced Western action of The Lone Ranger to your HeroClix collection! This easy mini-game combines Disney’s new film with the most popular pre-painted miniatures tabletop game for exciting gameplay.

Includes everything new and established players need to get started: Lone Ranger and Tonto HeroClix figures, an exclusive fold-out game board, quick-start rule sheet, scenario sheet, 32 bystander tokens, and dice.

Description from the publisher:

Zen Squire is a 2-4 player card game inspired by medieval marginalia, absurd illustrations found in the margins of medieval illuminated manuscripts. The first edition (released on Kickstarter) includes 72-cards, a 54-card main game plus an 18 card expansion. All illustrations and design elements were produced in pen and ink and hand-painted with watercolor.

The goal of Zen Squire is to collect 1,000 points. Each player begins the game with five cards, drawing one card and playing one card each turn. Cards are played into the area directly in front of each player, this area is your Castle, the total sum of which is your score. Diplomat cards contain positive or negative points. Any player can play Diplomat cards to any Castle. Once your Castle totals to 1,000 points, you win the game.

Challenger and Defender cards contain actions, which can be played to the Discard pile in order for their action to occur. Actions may allow players to draw additional cards, steal cards from opponents, or even swap Castles! Rulers are played to any Castle and pair with a Diplomat card already in that Castle. Rulers cannot be removed by Challenger or Defender cards. Multiple Ruler cards can be paired with the same Diplomat card. Once paired with a Diplomat, Rulers may double that card's points, change a negative value to a positive value, or make that card immune to all actions.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
The funny games of the Badgers is played two different ways:

Come Let Us Build A Cave!

All players try to build a cave together. If they manage to surround themselves on all sides with bushes, they have won. If the Frechdachs, however, takes 11 shrub game pieces during the game - the badgers cannot build a cave any more - so he is the winner.

The Search For The Pine Cones.

And again the game begins against the Frechdachs. But this time, every badger has to play for himself and try to collect as many pine cones as possible. Whoever has found most pine cones is the winner. You only know this when all the pieces are taken out of the game, and the number of the depicted pine cones is counted on the flipped side. Did you win now? Or was it the Frechdachs again?

This game was a promotional item for the foundation "Wald in Not" (Forest in Need), protecting the remaining forests of West Germany. The back of the box has an endorsement from the foundation president Dr. Bernhard Vogel, and two deutschmarks out of every copy sold was donated to the cause.

boardgame: Garbage Day: Gen Con Promo Pack
description:
Pack of "Garbage" given out to attendees of Gen Con 2016.

18 new "con" related cards.

Flirt is a game that gets players winning hearts by "courting" other players for their hearts.

Flirt is best played with friends, and with everyone saying the flavor text, with feelings and with a straight face.

Setup

Each player starts with three hearts of different values and patterns. The aim of the game is to be the first to complete a set of hearts with the same pattern.

Attacking

To attack, "Court" by using Court cards of equal or higher value than the heart you're targeting.

Defending

To defend, block Court cards by using other Court cards of higher or equal value to the Court card being blocked.

Win Condition

The first player that acquires three (3) HEART cards of the same pattern wins. If a player loses all his/her HEARTS, the player is out of the game.

The Mind & Emotions game explores our head and heart. In the beautiful box is a playing board, 120 cards, and a single die.

The game is based on the idea that we all have positive and negative states of mind, and positive and negative emotional states. The playing board has 3 columns with mind states on the left, and 3 columns of emotional states on the right. The positive states are on the top, and the negative states are on the bottom.

There are 2 piles of cards - 60 mind cards with blue backs, and 60 emotion cards with maroon backs. On the top of each card is one of the 12 mind & emotion states. In the middle of the card is a word that is an example of that mind or emotion state.

Each player takes a turn picking a card, and uses the word on that card to add to a developing story that’s interesting and fun.

For example, let’s say the first player picks the word SHY and begins the story: "Ever since Barry was a little boy, he was always SHY and had trouble socializing in groups." 
The next player picks the word: INVESTIGATE, and continues the story: "One day Barry was reading the newspaper about a neighborhood near where he lived. The article described houses that sounded like an excellent investment, and he decided to INVESTIGATE." 
The next player picks the word NEGLECTFUL, and says: "When Barry arrived at the neighborhood, he saw that it was run down, looking as if the owners were NEGLECTFUL of the most basic repairs." 
While telling the story, the players move their pieces on the board trying to get each of their pieces into a top or bottom end zone. We’ll now describe the rules of the game.

2 to 6 players can play. Each player gets two pieces that are placed on the start section in the middle of the board, one on the left mind side, and the other on the right emotion side of the board. Each player moves one piece up and down the mind side, and the other piece up and down the emotion side. The goal is to get each piece in either the top or bottom end zones.

On your turn:

Roll the die to see how many spaces to move either piece. 
Then pick a card from either the mind or emotion pile, and say out loud the category and word you picked. 
If you picked a mind card, move your mind piece. If you picked an emotion card, move your emotion piece. If the word you picked is positive, move your piece up. If the word you picked is negative, move your piece down. 
If the die is 2, 4, or 5, move your piece that many spaces. If the die is 3 or 6, move your piece 3 spaces up OR down. If the die is 1, roll again right away, and on this move you can move up OR down.
So there are two goals of the game. The first goal is to tell an interesting and fun story together. The second goal is to be the first to get both of your pieces to an end zone. The end zones do not have to be reached by an exact number of moves.

Positive-mind cards: 
Analyzing, Interested, Aware 
Negative-mind cards: 
Oblivious, Uninterested, Unaware 
Positive-emotion cards: 
Up, Outward, Warm 
Negative-emotion cards: 
Down, Inward, Cold

You and your friends will enjoy many hours together playing the game of Mind and Emotions!!

"The stolen items will be taken by me... no, us!"
The knights would like to take the honor, ... no, they want just a public interesting. Additionally, they are very sly for all. They can take the credit from other knights for the own.

This game is a collecting card game.
Each player has a card pile with a same card set. Each player draws 5 cards, and places 2 cards on the front from them as scoring cards for all.

Then, each player places one card to the stolen item from a hand clockwise, and the knights pick up them. But please take care of, only the LAST PLAYED PLAYER to the stolen item can take it and get the points.
The winner is the player with most points.

This expansion pack contains 2 models, 2 character sheets (English on one side, French on the other), two Overlord tiles (language independent).


    Valeria


This version of Valeria will still be adept at close quarter combat, however, she will be a more lightly equipped incarnation, faster and more dexterous, with other uses than just combat.


     Taramis/Salome


Then, we have Taramis, the queen of Khauran. She eschews personal combat almost entirely but she brings her personal bodyguards to the table as well as being a great boon to her allies."
But that's not all! Taramis's evil sister, Salome is also playable with this pack as an Overlord minion. They are in fact twins and it is almost impossible to tell one from another, something that Conan discovers to his peril in the tale A Witch Shall Be Born.
Salome is a sorceress of great power and she is able to draw upon the being known as Thaug (a HUUGE creature) to crush her enemies!

boardgame: Montenegro (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride)
description:
This map is an unofficial fan made expansion. Symbols & Graphics Copyright © 2004-2015 Days of Wonder, Inc. Produced with permission of Days of Wonder, for non-commercial use only.

This follows the principles of the basic Ticket to Ride game on a special board with special cards and rules. The object of the game is to gain the most points by travelling various routes and outwitting other players. You will need the train pieces and the train cards from either the US or Europe base games (or you can buy them from the Days of Wonder shop.

There is an extra set of Tourist cards for the cities, each with a value to be earned by visiting that city. These cards are dealt/picked up alongwith tickets in the same quantity and with the same 'keep/dispose' rules.

There are also spanners along some tracks which may help or hinder you. The board does not have any grey tracks or parallel tracks - but there are some options in the spanner pack.

You can scale Bobotov Kuk for extra points.

There are many new options in the rules: exchange all your cards at the start but keep all the new ones: discard 5 cards and pick up 5 new ones, as your turn: earn extra points: and many more. You can also call time when your wagon stock reaches 3, if you want, giving you the advantage of stopping other players completing tickets.

Players start out from Skagway with a “stake” of 1000 ounces of gold. They make their way, with many adventures along the road, to Dawson City and the Klondike Region. Along the road they may be caught in a blizzard, or lose their way along the trail, or they may be attacked by snow blindness, wolves, or chance upon various other adventures and hazards of the trail. At Dawson City players may speculate in various concessions, or they may continue on to the Klondike Region and try to make a gold strike. Once the gold strike is made a player must continue on the trail to the Land Claim Office (with adventures good and bad on this trail, too). Once he has successfully registered his claim and made his way back to his mine he searches for Nuggets of great value. When one of the players has earned his second Nugget the Klondike Adventure is over and players tally their gains from all sources. The prospector who has gained the most gold on his trip is the winner.

Did Indiana Jones rescue slave children from the Temple of Doom before King Kong terrorized New York city? Did Marty McFly first travel back in time in a DeLorean after Harry Potter's parents were murdered by Voldemort? Did the robots Wall-E and Eve meet before James T Kirk became Captain of the USS Enterprise?

In this game, players take turns trying to play fictitious event cards in a timeline. Featuring 234 cards from over 100 of your favorite fandoms from movies, television, books, and video games, Continuity is a unique and easy to learn card game that anyone can enjoy.

Baby GOUGGY is unhappy for different reasons. He may want; a dressing, a toy, pacifier, milk, his ball or take a bath. Determine why his sad and give to him what he desires. Take care to your babies GOUGGY not happy!  Opponents can put down different disadvantage cards on them; booger, dirty bib, pee and even ... a big poop!

GAME PLAY
Each player performs this steps in order :

1. Draw from the draw pile and/or the discard pile for a total of 6 cards in your hand.
2. Place a Gouggy not happy face up in front of you to start a baby pile. Each player may have no more than 3 baby piles underway at a time.
3. Use a subject (dressing, toy, pacifier, milk, ball or  bath) to continue your baby pile.
4. Once you have placed the appropriate subject card on your baby pile, you can place a Gouggy happy on that pile.
5. Place that baby pile face down in your points pile to make room for a new baby pile.


        Slow down an opponent by placing a disadvantage (booger, dirty bib, pee or poop) over a Gouggy not happy. If another player places one of this card on one of your baby piles, you must first remove the card before you can continue building that baby pile. On your next turn, place the appropriate subject (handkerchief bib clean, powder, nose clip) to remove disadvantage. Once you have done this, place the disadvantage and the subject in your points pile.
    


The game ends once the player who draws the last card from the draw pile has taken his/her turn. When the game is over, count your Gouggy happy you have in your point pile. The winner is the player with the most Gouggy happy.

boardgame: The Red Dragon Inn: Pharaoh's Toast
description: Gen Con 2016 Promo Drink

boardgame: The Red Dragon Inn: Gizmo-Grade Oil
description: This additional drink card came as a Kickstarter reward for The Red Dragon Inn 5: The Character Trove.

A promo card for Snowdonia, given out for free at the Surprised Stare Games booth at Spiel 2016.

Only 50 copies were printed, so this card is very rare.

This is the 6th in a series of Annual Games Unlimited Holiday Games and is the followup to Gameshop Express (2011), Gameshop Workday (2012), Gameshop Gladiators (2013), Gameshop Adventurers (2014), and Gameshop Expansions (2015)

To Win: Have the best gameboard collection by scoring the most 
Victory Points (VP) based on your owned games and add-ons.

Setup: Each player receives 1 Collection Tracks card, 
1 $/Shelves/Add-ons card, and 10 markers. Place 1 marker on the 
1 Shelving Unit Track space and 1 marker on the $3 space on the 
$ Track. 4 of the remaining markers will be used to mark Add-ons purchased. The other 4 markers will be used to mark a player’s owned games on the Collections Tracks. The 2 dice are given to the player who was chosen to go first. The first player will be the one who has most recently been to their favorite local gameshop.

Play: Each player’s turn consists of three phases:

Phase 1 Upkeep: Pay the listed upkeep fee on each of your Shelving Units. For each Shelving Unit a player cannot pay the fee for they must move a marker back 2 spaces on a Collection Track of their choice. Players do not pay the Upkeep fee on their first turn.

Phase 2 Dice: The player rolls the 2 dice and they may then reroll any die or dice an additional two times.

Dice are then used as follows: 
(Note: dice faces 1 (red) and 2 (yellow) do not count as Collection Track colors)

A. Move a marker, adding games to a colored Collection Track matching one die color rolled (3 (orange) 4 (green) 5 (blue) 6 (purple)). The amount moved will be equal to 1/2 the value of the other die, rounded up.

B. Move a marker one space on a Collection Track matching one die color (3 4 5 6), and add the other die’s value to your $ Track.

C. Add both die values to your $ Track. Your opponent then moves a marker on any one of your Collection Tracks back 1 space.

For each Shelving Unit a player owns, they may move past the        space (this space shows the first VP scoring amount on each track) on one Collection Track. They may not move past this space on an additional Collection Track until they own an additional Shelving Unit.

Phase 3 Expand: The current player may now purchase 1 Add-on and/or 1 Shelving Unit if they have the money to do so. Pay the listed cost and move the marker down that amount on the $ Track. Place a marker in the space next to the purchased Add-on. The first player to purchase a listed Add-on receives a $1 discount off the purchase price. The dice are then passed to the other player.

Add-ons: 1 Add-on may be purchased by a player each turn. 
The first player to purchase a listed add-on receives a $1 discount off the purchase price. Score VP at game end.

Game End: The game ends when 1 player purchases all 4 Add-ons or
when a player reaches the maximum VP space on one Collection Track. The player’s opponent may then take 1 final turn.

Score each Collection Track you own a Shelving Unit for. Each track shows a VP number in a circle next to certain spaces on that track. Score the highest VP value reached or passed on a Collection Track.

Score additional VP for any purchased Add-ons the player has.

Highest VP score wins!

Description from the publisher:

Plan your battle. Marshal your armies. Eliminate the opponent.

EverZone is a strategic card game set in a distant future. Each player is in command of forces in order to defend his race and expand the control in the universe. Three races (Numan, Slurr and Uthron, most commonly distinguished between humans, aliens and robots) fight for the galaxy on different battlefields: uncharted lands, seas of planets and in the cold space.
The game's goal is destroying the opponent's "headquarters" by reducing to zero his command points (same as life points). To achieve this, players have 330 cards between units, supports and locations in order to build their own forces deck to use in battle against opponents, in the locations determined by each player. EverZone introduces a revolutionary game mechanism to minimize random elements, creating extremely tactical challenges.

EverZone: Colony Assault is not a mere expansion of a game already published some time ago, but it's a sort of EverZone 2.0.
In this new game you will have:

     A new and more complete rulebook,
     330 brand new cards with balanced and refined powers to give you a more exciting gaming experience.
     A stand alone box, so that you won't necessarily need the basic game to play EverZone, but you can combine both of them to have wider strategies.


Besides, a third kind of card will be introduced: the structures.

Structures are paid by your own command points (or life points) and remain in the battlefield with permanent powers for your army until they are destroyed by opposing attacks.

Kebab-matador is base on Nørrebro, Copenhagen, Denmark. Here you have to buy all the stores and shops before the other players, so you can become the biggest gangster boss in town.

Unlike regular Matador (Monopoly) this game contains two alleyways where you can win big or almost lose it all!

The game is made from the idea of Manus Sareen who also wrote the Danish children book series "Iqbaal Farooq", and illustrated by Lars-Ole Nejstgaard.

The game is in Danish, and the first edition contains a free e-book on "Iqbal Farooq - og den sorte pjerrot"

A two-player microgame with 18 cards.

Winner of the 2015 18 cards game contest.

Epic Fights: Kung Fu Masters is a confrontational two-player game, with a quick, very light, tactical gameplay. Two kung fu masters battle each other in 9 rounds, using various techniques and building up combos.

Promo coaster released at Gencon 2016

Spilled Ale:

Place the Spilled Ale side of the coaster on the board. It covers a 3×3 square area.  These are called ‘Ale’ squares.

Any character that moves into an Ale square (and is on the floor) slides until they move off of the coaster, or hit a table, wall, or another character.  This only counts as one square of movement. They may continue moving once they exit the coaster or are stopped.

If they hit a table, they stop sliding.  If they slide into a square with another character, both characters start sliding until they are both no longer on the coaster, or hit a table or wall.

If player is Knocked Back onto an Ale square, they slide as above.

Rules for POKER TABLE:

The Poker Table side of the coaster can be used one of two ways:

As a Poker Table: Use two Large tables to support the Poker Table on the board. It is a 3×3 table, and is subject to all Table rules. It may be pushed, flipped, etc.  The two tables that are supporting it are considered part of the Poker Table.

For Ilicit Gambling: The coaster is placed off of the board. Any time a character has to give reputation to another character, and the Coaster has three or fewer reputation on it, place one Reputation from the bank on to the coaster, in addition to the normal effects of the Action.

If the coaster has four or more reputation on it, the next time a character gains reputation they also take all the reputation from the coaster.

If an action causes multiple characters to give reputation, one reputation per character goes on the coaster. A character may only take the Reputation when it has at least four at the start of the Action.

The COOKS of the CULT OF CTHULHU meet for the annual cooking ceremony of the tentacle stew. Whoever manages to chop the best pieces off the RITUAL TENTACLE will be appointed CULT CHEF for the next year.

INVASION: Free State is a competitive urban tactical combat board game for 2 to 4 players. In each mission, Alien and Resistance factions conduct combat against each other in a desolate district of Annapolis suburbs.

INVASION: Free State is inspired by such classic computer games as X-COM: UFO Defense and Laser Squad.

SIMULTANEOUS PROGRAMMING: Anticipate and outmaneuver your enemy
TEAM VS TEAM: Play with up to two players per side
COVER SYSTEM: Use them or die in the open
MODULAR BOARD: Each mission uses a randomized map every time

Join the fight for the Free State in 2020!

--

They might look alien to us, but they bleed. Believe me — I've seen it. I was sent here to tell you that all over the world people are arming themselves against the alien threat. You must do your part here in Maryland. We're few, but we can win this war — as long as we're united.

Keep your head down… caught in the open, you will be dead.

A small game covering the Rumanian Attack against Odessa in 1941.
Romanian player selects activation chits for his wings and then pull a chit one at a time.  He has three wings and two chits for each wing.  So, he can have six activations in one turn.  But, more activations bring Russian player more command points.  With them he can move or attack with one unit.
Victory condition for the Rumanian is to control or totally encircle Odessa at the end of turn six.

4 pages rulebook
60 counters
one map


Published in Command Magazine Japan #50 in 2003. Map was to be downloaded

Republished in Command Magazine Japan #109 in 2013 with full colored map and larger counters.

ROCKOMAN is a bigger and better version of the famous game ROCK–PAPER–SCISSORS. Fight with ROCKOMAN's superheroes to get the most points.

GAME PLAY
The players take turns going clockwise. On your turn, draw one card from the draw pile and place it, face up, next to the draw pile, in the discard pile. If your card beats the one of top of the discard pile, place all the cards in the discard pile in your point pile (rock beats scissors, scissors beat paper and paper beats rock).

When you draw a super card, place it face up next to your point pile. The super card beat card on the top of the discard pile at any time (rock suoer card beat scissors, paper super card beat rock and scissors super card beat paper).

When you place gun card, good card, sick card, rock-paper-scissors card, ring card in the discard pile, does the specified action to the opponent (take or give 3 cards, take his super card, play the standard game and the winner takes 5 cards from the loser’s point pile).

The game ends when the draw pile runs out. The winner is the player with the most points at the end of the game. Each card in your point pile is worth 1 point.

boardgame: Kodama: Les Esprits de l'Arbre – l'Extension Florissante
description:
Expansion for Kodama.

(translated from the title card)
Contains 28 cards:
- 12 Season cards
- 7 Kodama cards
- 9 mini-Kodama cards

The mini-Kodamas enable you to play the game with kids from 6 years old and up instead of the normal 8 years and up.

Description from the designer:

The year is 1921 and the Academy of Magicians has gathered once again to perform their greatest stage tricks. The competition to win the title of World’s Greatest Magician brings out the worst in these rivals who won’t hesitate at a chance to ruin the act. Fortunately, a good magician always has an assistant at their side and a trick up their sleeve…

Magician's Club is a "Trick-Breaking" game where players are Magicians who play Trick Cards for points. Moving around the table, the other players will try to "break" their trick by decreasing or modifying the Magician's tricks using conjure cards. The Magician can cancel out some cards by using his assistant cards but they are one-time use only cards, so play them wisely. When play returns back to the Magician, they can play one more card and then collect the trick for points (either positive or negative)

After all the players have had a chance to be the on-stage Magician, the points are totaled up. A second chance to score happens at the end of the game by counting up collected cards that have a similar suit. The player with the most points wins!

In Castle Climbers, players lay tiles to build up a fantasy castle that they climb with their hero character. As players scramble up the treacherous castle wall, roll dice to battle the grumpy guardians that bar their way. Use a magic sword or wish token to improve your luck. Win the battle to keep climbing. Lose and you'll drop back down! Special tiles help you climb faster or avoid the enemies altogether! Reach treasure chests to plunder the gems within. Whoever has the most points in gems and climbs the highest up the castle when time runs out, wins the game!

—description from the designer

After the experiences of improvised tank destroyer vehicles such as the M3 half-track with 75mm gun, M6 truck with 37mm gun and the M10 tank destroyer (3-inch gun on a modified M4 Sherman hull), the US Army were keen to produce a bespoke tank destroyer to hunt enemy armour. An order was issued in late 1941 to mount a 37mm gun in the requested vehicle. This was to be upgraded several times to 57mm, 75mm and then finally a 76mm.

The result of this development would be the M18 Hellcat - the fastest US armoured fighting vehicle of the Second World War.

M18 Hellcats served in the invasion of the Phillipines and saw action in Europe during the landings at Anzio in Italy and in Northwest Europe.

Hellcats were also exported to China where they served in an infantry support role.

The Hellcat could be found in the service of several nations following World War II with the Yugoslav army still fielding them into the 1990s!

boardgame: Bushido: Prefecture of Ryu Starter Set
description: The Dragon Clan won the rights to colonize the Westward Isles in a series of duels with their rivals, the Shiho Clan. However, when the Emperors vessel expired, and his young heir too young and inexperienced to concern himself with the pretty disputes at the fringes of his lands, the promise of sole settlement rights died with him. The newly formed Prefecture of Ryu found themselves yet again in conflict with their old enemies, the Shiho Clan. Now, the Prefecture’s grasp of power is weakened, due to the recent Tsunami. Their forces are in disarray, they are fighting on all fronts in the aftermath of the disaster. But still the men and women of the Dragon are ready to take their place at Destiny’s table, uniting the human world by defending it against all threats.

boardgame: Bushido: Cult of Yurei Starter Set
description: In the shadows and dark corners of the world, the worshippers of Yurei are gathering force. The many members of this Cult may not even be aware that they are serving the same dark masters, but serve them they do – the Yurei Bargain, which in its various forms allows control over life and death, has proven a temptation to many different people who would otherwise have nothing in common. In the Cult of Yurei, you may think yourself the master of your own Fate but you are always advancing the unfathomable goals of dark unseen masters. Even a puppet master has someone pulling his strings...

They take the form of evil creatures of myth and folklore. From where they come or why, no-one knows, but one thing is for certain, the wave crushes everything in its path. The Oni and Bakemono demon-creatures that make up the bulk of the Savage Wave appear bent on the destruction of humankind and all that is holy, but they are not mindless – as many opponents have found out at great pain, the Savage Wave is cunning and organized, and it operates according to its own weird and unknowable logic. Some mad hermits and outcasts scholars whisper that this is not the first Savage Wave...

The Ito clan have long been the laughing stock of the ruling clans. Never quite able to understand it is their own flaws which hold their greater ambitions back. Being untrustworthy is synonymous with the Ito their support of the Shiho’s rise to power followed by their treacherous role in the Dragon Wars against the very same clan, highlights their fickle nature. Now though the Ito have gone through a transformation, their alliance with an age old and powerful Kami has imbued them with a new found strength. If it was the Kami that caused the snake like changes or whether it has simply exaggerated the Ito’s natural affinity is unknown. What is sure is that the Ito finally have the power to rise against the other clans and avenge the mockery and mistreatment they have suffered.

Party game for two teams competing against each other.
The game shares its name with the Dutch version of Family Feud, but the game elements have only vague resemblance to that game.
Each game is played in five rounds, varying from a round that plays like Taboo to a round that plays like Time's up.
Points are scored for answering quiz questions (none of them in the style of 5 tegen 5 the tv show). First team to get to 50 points wins the game.

boardgame: Arkham Horror: The Card Game – The Essex County Express: Mythos Pack
description:
Description from the publisher:

The Essex County Express is the second Mythos Pack in The Dunwich Legacy cycle for Arkham Horror: The Card Game. Among its sixty new cards, you'll find twenty-eight player cards, including four copies of a new weakness.

You'll also find a thrilling new scenario in which your investigations lead you outside the town of Arkham. While the train you take may offer the fastest transit to your next lead, it may not offer the safest… When the train rumbles, shakes, and lurches to a halt, you and your fellow investigators must race from your car to the engine. If you can't get the train moving in a hurry, you and all the other passengers may fall prey to whatever unnatural things seem to be crawling, shifting, and oozing their way from car to car, growing ever larger as they do so.

Your adventures on the Essex County Express feature a randomized set of locations that greatly enhance the scenario's replayability, meaning this nightmarish ride is one you'll want to take again and again and again!
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: Arkham Horror – The Card Game
Game: Arkham Horror TCG – The Dunwich Legacy
Series: Arkham Horror Files (Fantasy Flight Games)

description:
This is the original game that was developed into 1893 Cologne by Marflow games. It is set in the same Cologne region of Germany. There are several differences including a differences in the map, stock chart, minor differences in the major and minor company contents, and a more restrictive tile set.

As with other 18xx games, players start with a set amount of money that they then invest in various companies. As the game progresses, these companies grow and earn income for their owners. Technology increases in the form of better trains with progression through more complicated track tiles. When the game ends, the winner if the player with the greatest net worth.

Hushed tales of fantastic beasts under the dark waves have been told throughout the known world for centuries: a giant piranha-turtle with a trident, a sea serpent that swallows ships and crew whole, a bearded dog-lizard with two water spouts on its head... stories so wild as to defy the imagination. While untold sailors have met their grim end by these sea monsters, an intrepid few have welcomed the challenge of discovering them with timeless rumors as their only guide.

In Monstra Maris, 2 to 4 players each control a small fleet of ships, represented by wooden discs, that they sail across the board with the flick of a finger. Their goals are the changing locations on the board where the monsters are rumored to have been sighted. When a player flicks a ship disc onto one of these locations, he or she is awarded points for the discovery and granted an experience token for end-game scoring. Players also hope to avoid the storms and pirates that may come their way, thanks to their opponents. The game ends after one player has discovered twelve monsters, and the player with the most points is declared the winner.

In ワラドの冒険 (Adventure of Warado), players are apprentice witches who want to travel the lands, moving beyond the seas and deserts to discover faraway villages and ruins never before seen by modern man.

Playing simultaneously on turns, players try to lay down cards that show two types of landscapes on them, matching an existing landscape to continue the travels of an adventurer, although some cards will allow an adventurer to jump across great distances in one go thanks to the efforts of a fellow traveler.

Wild cats! This will be the start of the military exercises, which are divided into first and second platoons. Your goal is to suppress the three columns in which the enemy can attack, and you can conquer a column by having more cats than the enemy.

In ノラネコ小隊 (Wild Cat Platoon), each player has a set of twelve cards, with those cards being numbered 1-3 twice in two colors. Starting with four cards in hand, players engage in a placement phase, then an operations phase.

Pyramid Arcade Loonacy is a custom edition reimplementation of Loonacy using the 22 game-specific images from Pyramid Arcade plus the main Pyramid Arcade logo for illustrations at the 72-cards card deck.

The game is a real-time card game where 2-5 players compete to get rid of all their cards by matching one of two images on each card in their hand with the images on the open piles in front of them.

The limited edition print run sold only from the Looney Labs web store. Produced while Pyramid Arcade was in print and announced at the Kickstarter page for Pyramid Arcade.

Jordanian version of Monopoly

The object of the game is to become the wealthiest player through building, renting and selling properties (bungalows and lodges).

It is Giftmas and you, along with the rest of the Grimm family, are engaging in the tradition of sharing presents, wonderful things like cakes, puppies and electrodes.  Will you get what you want, or what you deserve?

In this 18-card game, players each have a character card and two or three gift cards (depending on player count) face down on the table in front of them. When it is your turn you may either swap one of your face-down cards with one elsewhere on the table, or turn a card face-up, possibly activating a special action on the gift cards. The round ends when more than half of the players have revealed their cards or when a bomb is revealed, at which point everyone scores points if they have collected the gifts wanted by their characters.

The twist comes from the bombs: only one of the character scores highly if she collects bombs, but other characters score nothing for the round if they have one.

After an agreed number of rounds, the player with the highest total score is the winner.

boardgame: Road To Infamy: Gangster Booster Pack
description:
This Gangster Booster Pack was a Kickstarter Exclusive. It started with 4 new gangsters but added 4 more via stretch goal.

The Booster Pack contains 1 card with game variations and clarifications and the following new gangsters...


     Goons : When hit by an action, ignore its effect (Once per game).
     Highroller : Start each hand with 7 bids.
     Hitman : +3 Infamy Points for each Gangster you have assassinated.
     Identity Thief : Choose an opponent's Gangster. Identity Thief copies that ability for the rest of the game.
     Rat : -2 Infamy Points (The player with the lowest Gangster bid takes the Rat).
     Saboteur : Discard an opponent's 3, 4, or 5 bid played that turn (Once per game).
     Spy : Pick an opponent. They must show you all of their action bids (Once per hand).
     Swindler : When Laundering/Gambling look at an extra card and discard the one you don't want.


Each of these cards has a KS logo in the bottom right corner.

Dice game with 12 dice using scoring elements from Yahtzee (with extra combinations) and Tutto (points for sets of 1's and 5's) at the same time.

Multiple scoring combinations per throw are possible, splitting the 12 dice into groups.

A bit of push your luck is also present. Players can either stop before all dice are used, scoring their total thus far or continue taking the risk to throw non-scoring dice, losing all the points of that turn.

First player to 100,000 wins.

The Unexpected Mini Expansion was offered to Kickstarter backers in an update just as the base game was getting ready to ship.

Due to the layout of our printing there was a very small amount of space left on one of the sheets. So we went ahead and made a mini-expansion utilizing works of classic Mike Foxall art. AN EXPANSION BEFORE THERE IS EVEN A GAME, YOU ASK? Haha, we say, because we are mad. No, seriously, we had the space - why not use it?

Thus we are proud to bring you the 'Unexpected Mini Expansion'!

Including:

Evil Knievette - The only card that can take things from the Ditch! What a Buddy.
Sensual Simon - This troll who just wants to come in for "a coffee". He's one sexy Enemy.
Story Time - During this Event Uncle Morty tells truly terrifying tales.
Lynched By Witches - Something isn't right with this Ordeal. Shouldn't we be lynching the witches?
Think About Feet - An Effect to make your opponents think too much about feet!

We will bundle it with your game, so do it now before we send your game. There are less of these available than there are Bedlamites, so first come, first AWESOME. Second come, second MISSES OUT AND CRIES IN A CORNER LIKE A MINI EXPANSION-LESS LOSER.

–description from the publisher

2 to 4 players compete to build strong orchestras and earn reputation points by playing concert pieces successfully. The game requires players to balance the need to strengthen different sections of their orchestra with the desire to score points. Concert music pieces reflect difficulty levels required to play successfully. Card drafting and hand management are the primary mechanics in this game.

An audition for Person of the Year for the newest issue of SAY CHEESE Magazine is coming up. A fierce competition is ahead! Only those who can make the most expressions the fastest will qualify. Are you ready? You can be a winner today! Let's go to the audition together! We also strongly suggest you to get your camera ready to catch all the interesting expressions!

One person is chosen to be the expressionist. At each turn, after the expressionist draws an expression card, everyone shouts "Say Cheese!" and the expressionist performs the expression shown the card. After the expressionist performs the expression, the other player guess what the expression is by arranging their dice into their answers. Whoever finishes arranging the dice into his/her answer shouts "Cheese!" Once the expressionist confirms that the answer is correct, the player earns the Expression Card and places it face up in front of him/her.

A small cooperative card game for two players. The objective of the game for two players is to win exactly same amount of treasure (with different card sets) at the end of the game by bidding with their cards. they should discuss a strategy prior to the game, and should not discuss that in the process.

The game was released as a gift to customers of Gémklub, a Hungarian board game publisher and reseller/shop as a commemoration of the 30th anniversary of its existence.
A promo card for Dixit: Revelations.  The card shows a bunny doing a magic trick with the hat.

A boardgame in the theme of Wickie the Viking. 
Eric the Terrible has stolen the valuables of the people of the town of Flake. He hid them on his treasure island. Wickie and Halvar are on a mission to retrieve them.
In the game you play with Wickie ("intelligence" - 1 and 2 moves) or with Halvar ("brute force" - 1 up to 4 moves). They move across the five areas of the island.
Wickie moves slower across the island, but if he can remember the part of the treasure (number of coins) on the card, he is certain of getting that part of the treasure. 
Halvar moves faster across the island, but needs an adequate weapon (viking card of the same color) to collect the treasures of the area he is in.
We play until we have retrieved the entire treasure Eric the Terrible has stolen. Amongst the players, the one that has retrieved the most of the treasure gains the most honour.

A cooperative game in the theme of the Kids' series Night's Watch ("Nachtwacht").

Somebody opened the gates to the underworld. Monsters have free reign. The heroes of the Night's Watch are their to combat them, together.
They patrol through Twilight Town by rolling the "walk die". By being attentive during their patrol (a 1/3 chance roll of the "walk die") they can gather skills (up to 5 skill cards). 
When on their patrol they encounter a monster they have to do a skill test. If they pass (roll 1 of the remaining skills required OR use the proper skill card), they hurt the monster. If they give the monster the final blow, the monster is defeated and removed from the board.  If they fail, they loose health points (1 of 3). Incapacitated heroes can still help the party with their skill cards.
The Night's Watch wins if the monsters are all removed. However, the monsters win if EITHER all heroes are incapacitated OR no more skills can be learned (a skill card must be drawn but all skill card deck is empty).

boardgame: Cats Abiding Horribly: Episode II – A New Low (fan expansion for Cards Against Humanity)
description: A Third party expansion to Cards against humanity

boardgame: Cats Abiding Horribly: Episode III – The SJWs Strike Back (fan expansion for Cards Against Humanity)
description:
A third Cats Abiding Horribly party expansion to Cards Against Humanity.

From the publisher:
"Have you played the game and expansions so many times that by now you’ve memorized all the possible funny combinations? Does the game simply not excite you like it used to anymore? Or perhaps you’re just looking for something a little bit different?

If any of this sounds familiar to you then Horribly Abiding Cats is exactly what you need.

Revitalize your games with 150 new, uncensored, unofficial and anti-politically-correct cards. The deck contains 110 answer, 40 question + 10 blank bonus cards that mix in seamlessly with the original game and expansions.

Printed on high-quality casino cardstock with a linen finish, you are guaranteed to enjoy this expansion for a very long time."

Superfight: The Red Deck 2 — an expansion for the Superfight party game in which players pit unusual characters with superpowers and superweaknesses against one another — adds "one hundred really terrible and likely offensive cards" to the base game.

boardgame: Moldova (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride)
description:
This map is an unofficial fan made expansion. Symbols & Graphics Copyright © 2004-2015 Days of Wonder, Inc. Produced with permission of Days of Wonder, for non-commercial use only.

This follows the principles of the basic Ticket to Ride game on a special board with special cards and rules. The object of the game is to gain the most points by travelling various routes and outwitting other players. You will need the train pieces and the train cards from either the US or Europe base games (or you can buy them from the Days of Wonder shop.

There is an extra set of Tourist cards for the cities, each with a value to be earned by visiting that city. These cards are dealt/picked up alongwith tickets in the same quantity and with the same 'keep/dispose' rules.

There are also spanners along some tracks which may help or hinder you. The board does not have any grey tracks or parallel tracks - but there are some options in the spanner pack.

You can scale the mountains for extra points.

There are many new options in the rules: exchange all your cards at the start but keep all the new ones: discard 5 cards and pick up 5 new ones, as your turn: earn extra points: and many more. You can also call time when your wagon stock reaches 3, if you want, giving you the advantage of stopping other players completing tickets.

Description from the publisher:

No Blank Left Behind !is an adult party game inspired by such games as Cards Against Humanity, Nasty Things and Say Anything. We loved playing Cards Against Humanity, but felt too constrained by the predefined white cards. So we created a game where with the use of whiteboards the answer could be anything your filthy mind could think of.

On the surface No Blank Left Behind appears to be just another Cards Against Humanity clone with a few seemingly minor differences. First players have four statements/questions on each card to choose from. Second rather than be provided a hand of cards with a predefined responses, No Blank Left Behind instead utilizes whiteboards and dry erase markers allowing players to come up with their own responses, limited only by their individual imaginations. And lastly who wrote what answer is not kept a secrete from the judge or as it is know in No Blank Left Behind “The Dictator”. This allows players to lobby for their answer and explain why theirs is obviously the best.

What does all this mean? Well, it means you should never have to ask a lame question. You are never stuck with answer cards that don't fit the question. In-jokes or even the players themselves can become answers and the same answers can be used over and over leading to some very funny running jokes. Which in the end leads to a ton of laughs, many groans and possibly a few gasps. No Blank Left Behind may seem like another Cards Against Humanity clone, but the minor differences makes it a new unique and hilarious party game.
