boardgame: The Lion Guard: Roaring Rescue Game
description:
Description from the publisher:

A Surprise Slides Adventure.

Journey through the Pride Lands, helping friends along the way!

Friends of the Lion Guard need a helping hand to get to Pride Rock, and only Kion, Beshte, Bunga and Fuli can find the way. Race along the Pride Lands' paths, gathering stranded animals, but watch out - the paths can change at any time!

Roaring Rescue is a simple spin and move game for 2 to 4 players ages 3 and up.

boardgame: Beyond the Gates of Antares: Strike on Kar'a Nine
description:
Strike on Kar’A Nine is a new introductory set for Beyond the Gates of Antares containing forces for both the Algoryn and the Concord, with dice, rules and templates plus a fold-out battle mat and cut-out terrain – everything you need to get started with the game.

Thanks to the order dice draw system, every battle is a tense affair with both players constantly being involved in the action. Both epic defeats and narrow victories will be enjoyable when you venture Beyond the Gates of Antares. Prepare yourself for an exhilarating adventure!

The full colour Getting Started book is designed to coach you through, making your first steps as exciting and as simple as possible, with a series of scenarios that gradually introduce the mechanics of the game. Each scenario introduces new rules, culminating in a final battle using everything in the box where you will be able to use all you have learnt.
family: Game: Beyond the Gates of Antares

description: In Ichi Matsu (市松), two players compete to create the largest area of their color by placing tiles of either half-and-half black/white or black/white checkered tiles on a 3x3 cloth board. Tiles can be placed on top of other pieces as long as there are no gaps underneath. When no legal move can be made, each quadrant of your color of the tiles counts as one point. Most points wins.

Promotional tile offered by "Man vs Meeple Season One Kickstarter".

In the game Haspelknecht, when the players acquire the Foreman, they are able to spend 1 victory point in order to activate one of their contract workers.

PRE-ORDER at our official website below!

This board game is a simulation of the spiritual and physical warfare that erupted after the Reformation. It is the only game that covers the specific battles that took place between the Schmalkaldic League of Confessors (the Lutherans) and the Catholic League (Holy Roman Empire) between the years 1532-1555. The setting of League of Confessors begins after the death of John the Steadfast as the leaders of the Augsburg Confession prepare to defend themselves against the impending armies of the Holy Roman Empire. Power and wealth will collide with conviction and courage as two leagues of heroes go to war with sword and the pen!

League of Confessors comes with 4 different classes of heroes.

Commanders – Commanders are the heroes that initially lead your entire army. They are good leaders in battle and are very versatile.

Generals – Generals are good leaders in battle, but that is all they will do. They can advance in their battle abilities very quickly.

Princes – Princes will help build the infrastructure of your expanding territory through the construction of armories, castles, or churches.

Confessors – Confessors do not have the ability to lead troops in battle. They are the only heroes who have the ability to publish confessional writings to convert opponents to their side.

The goal is to win the game by gaining seven Conquest Points. Each electorate territory is worth 1 Conquest Point (CP). The other way you can gain a CP is to fully advance a hero in their abilities. If you fully advance a hero in their abilities, you will also gain 1 CP for as long as that hero is alive. If your league has seven or more Conquest Points at the end of one of your turns, you win the game!

boardgame: Blood Bowl (2016 Edition): The Skavenblight Scramblers – Skaven Blood Bowl Team
description:
A Skaven expansion team with an out-of-the-box team value of 1,000,000 gold pieces. Ideal for playing against either Orcs or Humans in the base game.

Skaven teams are unique with their own stats for agility, strength, etc. in comparison to other teams. Stat sheet included.

Contains:
- 6 Linemen
- 2 Throwers
- 2 Gutter Runners
- 2 Blitzers
- 2 team token/coins
- 2 footballs
- 1 team stats and assembly leaflet

Players lead one of five starter races in this 4X space opera, or create their own unique player made races, as they struggle for galactic control.  Explore new planets, campaign for political dominance, form binding covenants, and use the might of your fleet, all while searching the cosmos for Gaia, the homeworld of an ancient creator race that will tip the balance of power.

Quest for Gaia offers limitless gameplay through technologies, discovery, player made agreements, and racial abilities!  Every game is guaranteed to offer unique and fresh challenges.

During a game of Quest for Gaia, players will undergo multi-phased rounds.  They will start by allocating the resources available to them from technology, planets, and government.  Next, players will draw 7 cards from a communal deck, voting for who will be the galactic Chancellor (first player).  Finally, starting with the first player, they will choose major actions - Explore, Campaign, Market, Covenant, Revolt, Research, Espionage, and War.  The chancellor position is valuable as each of these major actions can only be taken once per round.

Players will also have a variety of smaller, "Minor Actions" to take - Move, Build, Upgrade, and Attack. These actions are very quick and players can upgrade planets to give themselves more actions or discover leaders to give themselves various Minor action options.

Points are earned throughout this process and the first player to reach 24 is the winner!

Choosy is a set collection game featuring 100 dice.

The game starts with all the dice placed in a 10 x 10 grid. Each turn players may choose a matching pair from the outside of the grid, if they have enough dice to make a set, they may bank their set to score points.

The game continues until there are no more matching pairs of dice in the center pool. The player with the most banked dice is the winner.

From the publisher: "NEW CCG From MediaStream Press: Spell Wars is a CCG like no other! It removes a lot of the annoyances of other games, with fast action and just enough luck and randomness to make it truly exciting! Use magic sources to put down summoning circles. Use summoning circles to draw forth minions from 7 different elements: Spirit, Air, Fire, Dark, Earth, Water, and Light. Attack and defend with your minions, using standard d6 dice. Empower your warriors with powerful buffs and enchantments. Strike down your enemies with curses and spells! Defeat your rivals with strategy, luck, and your dice-rolling skills! All you need is this book, some d6, and some counters. All the rules you need to play are included in this full-color book with 270 cards! Unlike other card games where you have to go buy pack after pack to get decent cards, they're all included here! No more tapping, no more waiting for the right card, no more watching the rich kid win because of rare cards, no more boring stats comparisons to watch monsters die... This is an innovative system with a LOT of fun updates to classic card games. Use dice to do combat, always have a hand of cards to play, and no more wasted mana and holding back. This is a SLUGFEST type battle. Check it out now!"

Description from the publisher:

Get Naked! is a silly card game about, well, getting naked.

The game takes about ten minutes to play, and is a fun, light diversion for three to eight players, ages six and up.  The rules are simple enough to teach in sixty seconds.

The game includes a deck of twenty-two clothing cards, fifty action cards, and one instructions card. 
1. By drafting from a hand of 4 cards create your ensemble of clothing cards and place them in front of you
2. Draw 3 action cards.
3. On your turn you play an action card to a card in your ensemble if you can, otherwise you need to play to an ensemble of another player. If this is impossible too, discard your hand and draw up another hand,  every other player draws a card. 
The actions you play are for example unbutton, rip or unzip and are corresponding to their type of clothing.
Nearly every clothing card has special abilities that are triggered through actions or events in the game. Your goal is to be the first one without an ensemble aka the first to be naked.
The art is cartoony in nature and it's by no means an adult only game.

SP Ski jumping is a free PnP solo/competetive sport game that recreates Ski Jumping events. 
The game adopts International rules by FIS.

Set of cards available:
WC Ladies 2015-2016
WC Men 2020-2021
WC Men 2023-2024

No dice are required, the game is driven only FAC (free action cards). The cards manage the 4 phases of a ski jump: in-run, lift-off, flight and landing.
In each jump a ski jumper gains point for the lenght of jump but also for 3 for the 5 marks from Judges and points by wind correction. 
All points are added to gain Total points.

A display sheet, included in game components, helps to drive each phase of jump.

In each jumper card information is printed about statistical lenght of jump, rank, medium mark, nationality and so on.

a Jump sheet is added to register informatio of an event.

Competitions are divided in individual jump events of World cup (2 jumps each event), flying jump events (2 jumps each event) and team events (2 jumps for each team composed by 4 jumpers).

Best for solitaire game.
2 players game is possible combined with strategy cards.

boardgame: Don't Get Bit in the Mead Hall: Zombie Expansion
description:
This is an expansion for "Don't Fall in the Mead Hall" and requires that game to play. The "Allies and Drunk Guys" expansion is not needed but strongly recommended.

There are many stories in the Icelandic sagas about an undead creature called the draugr, or aptrgangr (lit. "after-goer," "again-walker," or "one who walks after death"). Most would call them zombies in this day and age and they have found the Mead Hall! Think you have what it takes to keep playing? Well go and prove your mettle once again. But this time you must fend off zombies as you out drink your Viking opponents.

You will send zombies to other tables, fight zombies, and summon zombies. Just don't fall OR get bit in the Mead Hall!

Each time you get bit the sickness at your table gets worse and you lose a heart. If you get bit when you have no hearts one of your Vikings will turn into a Zombie and stay at your table as a threat.

Also included are two zombie themed Tipsy cards that you can add to your Tipsy deck if you have the Allies expansion.

The Last table with at least one Viking standing is the winner.

Description from the back of the box:

"There seems to be a lot going on down on the farm. The farmer accidentally left the barn door open and now the animals are all loose. With great difficulty he's managed to catch them all but now the barn is in uproar. Can you help sort the animals?

An exciting memory game with lots of animals that can also be played with as a farm set once the game is over.

Players mix up the 32 wooden animals (4 each of 8 different animals) and place them into the 8 barns. Players then take turns trying to find pairs of the animals, until all of the animals are matched. The player who found the most pairs wins.

boardgame: バハムート ゲート (Bahamut Gate)
description:
Fun, fast-playing cooperative game that that reduces the fantasy RPG experience to fighting a climactic boss battle by rolling dice and playing spell cards. Comes with five characters and five sealed scenarios; stands up to replaying well.

The boss stays in an octagon in the centre, and is surrounded by eight spaces that may be occupied by players, or in higher levels fire or minions. Each turn consists of a player phase and a boss phase.

In the player phase, the player on turn rolls 4d6, and rerolls up to 2d6 once. The dice serve two functions: first, they designate through character-specific tables which actions (move, draw spell cards, heal, etc.) or spells you can take; second, the largest set of identical dice determines how much damage you do to the boss.

In the boss phase, the boss rotates either two 45° steps or to face a player. The current player rolls 3d6, discards the high die, and looks up the two lower dice in a table to determine which octants take how much damage.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

boardgame: Steal The Show! Theatrical Party Card Game
description:
Who will be the star of the show? Play as iconic characters as you challenge players to a Battle of Strength or a Battle of Wits using Shakespearean insults and the art of bluffing!

Steal The Show! is a race to collect five comedy masks, and the first player to do so wins. If a player receives five tragedy masks first, however, they're eliminated from the game. In more detail, each player takes on the role of a character. Characters have unique gameplay abilities as well as a strength value (used in a Battle of Strength) and a wit value (used in a Battle of Wits). One location card is always in play and changes throughout the course of the game; location cards modify the rules of the game.

Action cards (Insults) are used during battle to enhance the strength and wit value of the character as well as insert some trickery to mix! Don't forget bluffing! Players can use bluffing techniques to assist with a numerical boost as well!

Players in Floris collect flower cards to create bouquets — but they need to use every card in hand in order to offer that bouquet for points.

To set up, shuffle the deck, lay five cards in the shape of an X, then stack the deck nearby. On a turn, a player can either take the center card of the X, two outer cards in the X (as long as they're adjacent), or one or two face-up cards from the personal supply of an opponent. After doing this, the player either adds those cards to hand or places them face-up in their own personal supply. The player can then add any number of cards from their personal supply to their hand.

If the player can then make a bouquet of all the cards in hand and their cards contain at least five flowers, they can score if they wish. The cards must show all the same flower, or all types of flowers must be represented in equal numbers (e.g., three lilies and three roses), or at least three types of flowers in ascending numerical order (e.g., one daisy, two roses and three lilies). If the player hands in nine or more flowers, they gain bonus points in addition to one point for each flower.

When the market runs out, players score a final bouquet, then lose points for cards left in hand. Whoever has the highest score wins!

It's serious! The earth was hit by a flood! Everyone is flying and running away! It's true!
However, there are still birds left behind on the ground!
What are you doing! Ostrich! Penguin! Kiwi! It's true! It's true!

Each player has a balloon card indicating the pattern of his country, turning one piece per turn and tying it to the straps that comes from his favorite animal.
If all the straps are filled with balloons, that bird will fly away, to the country of the most prolific balloons!
The kiwi has special abilities. Once you acquire Mr. Kiwi, that player can go for balloons in other countries once. So the second half of the game when each player owns a kiwi, is very thrilling!

When Kapybara-san was playing outside, he was confused and became annoyed.
You have the tremendous ability to help him out!
Let's restore Kapybara-san!
What was the original figure like?

The game is about collecting capybara parts and 
Players can take three actions.
· Moving
· Draw cards
· Use Capybara's special abilities
Please select 3 of these and act.

When Capybara returns to their original form, they will use their special special abilities to support them!
Even if you make a new creature, you only draw a special card once. Bonus points are also included.

The game ends once someone has trained six Capybara. The player with the highest score wins.
Using the capability of Capybara well disturbs other players. 
Let's collect Kapybara-san sooner!

_________________________________________________________

どしーん★ぶつかってばらばらになってしまいました。

　カピバラさんたちがお外で遊んでいると
どっしーん★　ぶつかって
ばらばらになってしまいました。

そこへ通りすがったすごい能力を持ったあなた！
カピバラさんたちを元に戻してあげましょう！
あれれ？　元の姿はどんなだったのかなぁ？

概要
　バラバラになったカピバラさんたちを集めて錬成しよう！
　元の姿に戻ったカピバラさんたちの特殊能力を使ったり、新しい生き物を作ったりして、得点を競おう。

Themed on Pixar's "Finding Dory" movie, help little Dory find her way back home by matching shells which become a path back to her parents!

Each player chooses a colored path. The shells are placed face-down on the board.

On their turn, a player chooses one face-down orange and one purple shell. If the pictures on the shell match, they are placed on the player's path. The winner is the first person to build a path to the home space.

Includes:
1 Game Board
48 Plastic orange and purple game shells
2 sticker sheets (stickers must be applied to each plastic shell)

boardgame: Harry Potter: Hogwarts Battle – Spell: Eye of Rabbit, Harp String Hum, Turn This Water Into Rum!
description:
This Game 1 card was available to various game stores to be handed out during demos of the game.

This Spell carries the following ability:

Lose 1 [LIFE]; then gain 4 [INFLUENCE].
family: Game: Harry Potter: Hogwarts Battle (The OP)

boardgame: Don't fall in the Mead Hall: Allies and Drunk Guys
description:
This is an expansion and requires the original game "Don't Fall in the Mead Hall."

This game includes a brand new Action Die that you may use instead of the original on your turn. It also includes a medium-sized bag so you can take the game anywhere with ease. This expansion brings in another table with 5 pawns so now you can play with a 5th player. Also included are 5 brand new Skál cards that you can add to your Skál deck (if you have it) and the optional Tipsy card deck.
 In addition to the drinking, toasting and fighting of the original Don't fall in the Mead Hall, players now have a chance to sober up (passed out Vikings can wake up) by rolling the feast symbol. Every player will sober up by two (the drunk die will go down two). If you roll the turkey legs symbol your table sobers up by two and you choose another player to sober up by two. Vikings can now bring a sword and shield to the battle mat for a fight to the death. It's double the pain if you roll the new double "Skal!". Every tables drunk die will go up by two. Watch out because there's a chance Vikings at your table will get into a fight with each other if you roll the double fist. The Tipsy cards (optional, if you want to use them) add some extra mystery to each round and can make the game go faster. At the end of each round flip a Tipsy card and see if one of your Vikings can't hold his own.

The last table with at least one Viking standing wins.

boardgame: Pathfinder Adventure Card Game: Mummy's Mask – "Portable Sarcophagus" Promo Card
description:
Portable Sarcophagus
Type: Armor

Traits: Light Armor, Construct

Check to Acquire: Constitution / Fortitude 6

Powers:
Display this card next to your location and shuffle your token card into your location deck to reduce all damage dealt to you to 0. When your token is returned to the location, banish this card.

If proficient with light armors, you may recharge this card and a boon that has the Magic trait when you reset your hand.
family: Series: Pathfinder Adventure Card Game (Paizo Publishing)

boardgame: Каркассон: Соловей-Разбойник и Водяной (Carcassonne: Nightingale the Robber and the Water Spirit)
description:
Каркассон: Солове́й-Разбо́йник & Водяной (Solovei Razboynik and Vodyanoy) is a little semi-unofficial expansion (2 tiles) for Carcassonne, made by the Russian editor of the Carcassonne game (Hobby World) and was a present for the participants of the Russian national Carcassonne league 2016.

One tile represents Solovei Razboynik "Nightingale the robber" from the Russian folktale "Ilja Muromez". The second tile represents Vodyanoy, a kind of ghost, only living in water.

Solovei Razboynik
If followers occupy a Road that goes through the Solovei Razboynik tile (or they occupy Roads that are connected to that tile later on in the game), players must move them onto the tile. The followers then remain on the tile until the end of the game. In his turns, instead of placing his follower, the player may return 1 follower from the Solovei Razboynik tile into his supply.

Vodyanoy
When a player places this tile adjacent to one or more tiles on the board, all followers from the adjacent tiles are moved onto the Vodyanoy tile. Followers remain on that tile till the end of the game and get 2 points minus.

Team 1 chooses a card with a word on it.  They give Team 2 a word which rhymes with the chosen word.  Team 2 has to guess the chosen word, but can express themselves only by acting.  When Team 2 mimes the correct word, Team 1 cheers to indicate that they got it right.  Play proceeds with the teams alternating roles.

The team that guesses the most words, wins.

Huddle Up! is a game for 2 players, ages 7+

The aim of the game is to get the most penguins in your color in a huddle, as pieces of the ice flow, they are standing on break away!

Materials
Hex Board with 37 spaces
36 pawns or cubes, 18 Black and 18 White, representing the player’s penguins

Setup
Place the board between the players.
Next starting at the top row, place a penguin one to a empty space, alternating Black then White while skipping the center space on the board.

Definitions
Group – Adjacent penguins of either color are considered a group and a single penguin with no adjacencies are also considered a group.

Play
One Player plays Black the other White.
Decide who starts, turns then alternate.

On a turn,
• Move a penguin away from your starting position, 3 spaces in any of the 6 directions, avoid moving over empty spaces or the center space.

• You must end your move on a space occupied by 1 or 2 penguins.

• You must move if you can, otherwise, you must pass

• No more than 3 penguins in a space.

• If you land on a space occupied by 2 penguins, and one of the 2 penguins is a friendly penguin, your opponent’s penguin is bumped off and removed from play.

If a group is split into 2 groups that is divided by empty spaces, the smaller group is removed from play.

If a group is split into two or three equal size groups, remove the group/groups that do not have the penguin that caused the split to occur.

The game ends when both players pass.

Scoring

Majority controls a space

Total the number of penguins controlling a space.

1 - Friendly Penguin on a space = 1 point
2 - Friendly Penguins on a space = 2 points
3 - Friendly Penguins on a space = 3 points

Spaces controlled by both players are not scored

Objective
Player with the most penguins in the largest connected group in his color, wins

Gawi Bawi Bo is a strategy game for 2 players ages 8+

For ages 8+ 
30 min play time

You move and capture tiles following the Rock, Paper, Scissors rules.

Setup
Place board between the players

Shuffle the 36 tiles face down

Tiles are placed on empty spaces of the board

In turn, flip over and place a tile on an empty space, starting with the outer edge spaces. Then work your way towards the center leaving the center space empty.

You are now ready to play

Stacking Order
A Rock tile can be stacked onto a Scissor tile

A Scissor tile can be stacked onto a Paper tile

A Paper tile can be stacked onto a Rock tile

Play
Players share tiles

Play Rock, Paper, Scissors to determine the starting player

Turns then alternate

On a turn,

Move a tile from 1-3 spaces in any of the 6 directions, not over empty spaces or the center of the board. Then following the Stacking Order end the move on top of another tile capturing it.

Both tiles are removed from the board, they will be used at endgame scoring

Groups
Adjacent tiles are considered a group, a single tile with no adjacencies is also considered a group.

If a group is split into 2 groups that are divided by empty spaces, the smaller group is removed from the board.

If a group is split into 2 or 3 equal size groups, remove the group/groups that did not have the captured tile.

These tiles are not used in scoring.

When no more moves can be made the game ends

Scoring 
Total your tiles using the numbers on the face of the tiles

Runs of two or more tiles only count as the lowest value in the run, like (23,24,25,26)

Subtract any (-1) tiles you may have from your total

Objective
The player with the lowest score wins!

Unwrap special powers this holiday season! Each gift box is a special gift created for Renegade Fans.

Contents:
7 card pack for Lotus
1 promo tile for Lanterns: The Harvest Festival
1 character card for Covert
1 apothecary for Apotheca: The Secret Potion Society
1 board tile for Snow Tails
1 card for World’s Fair 1893
1 choice token for Dicey Goblins
1 card for Clank!
1 Wild Die for FUSE


Lotus: 7 Frosted Lotus Petals - Replace the 7 lotus cards from the wildflower deck with these seven frosted lotus petals. Play as normal!

Lanterns - Snowflake Tile - Remove one other random tile and replace it with this tile. Follow other regular setup rules.

Covert - Kane Character Card - Add to the available character cards. This power can only be used when drawing mission or agency cards from the regular face up options, not when taking cards from the discard.

Apotheca - Tima Apothecary Card - Use the power from the red Apothecary as if it were your own.

Snow Tails - Polar Plunge Track. 2 Sided Track Board - Dangerous Pit: Pick up your speed before the chasm! If you are going less than speed 5 when you cross the line, you will take damage! If you are going to slow, take dent cards equal to 5 minus your speed. If you end your movement in the abyss,, take any necessary dents and then don't refill your hand. 
The Narrows: Follow normal rules, but be careful! This side will require some careful maneuvering!

World's Fair 1893: J. Alex Kevern Promo Card - When setting up, replace one copy of George Davis with this card and follow the rules for George Davis.

Dicey Goblins - Naughty Goblin Token - When setting up, replace one of the regular goblin choice tokens with this token.

Clank - Dire Wolf Promo Card - Add this card to the Dungeon Deck when beginning the game. When it is played, count to total number of monsters that are available to fight. Add this number of Clank! and Swords to your pool for this turn.

FUSE Wild Die - Setup: Add the Wild Die to the bag with the other dice. Gameplay: If the Wild Die is rolled, it is chosen by a player just like any other die. If the # symbol is showing, the die can be placed on any space showing a number. If the color symbol is showing, the die can be placed on any space showing a color. If a space needs both a specific number AND color, the Wild Die cannot be placed there.

The Original Dope Smokers Game

A board game similar in play to Monopoly i.e. Move around the board, passing start, prison and opportunity cards (Freak Out/Contact).

There are 4 concentric circles on the board, Smokers/Dealers/Suppliers/Growers) the aim being to deal your way to the Growers Circle.

Description from the publisher:

Warfighter WWII third expansion. New card deck with German units.

7x PC Soldier Cards
4x NPC Soldier Cards
6x Squad Soldier Cards
1x Location Card
7x Action Cards
14x Weapon Cards
2x Equipment Cards
9x Skill Cards
3x Mission Cards
3x Objective Cards

20 awesome new Canadian themed cards and 20 blanks (to add your own ideas).  The cards include gear Room full of Poutine, the Surprise Whiteout, a Barrel of Maple Syrup, and the Insane Mountie.  These come together to add a little hint of Canada to your game.
family: Organizations: The Game Artisans of Canada

description:
The Saint Nicholas Scoring Board is a Scoring Board for Carcassonne II that was sold by cundco.de exclusively on Saint Nicholas Day. It was limited to a number of 150 items.

The game rules for the Saint Nicholas Scoring Board:

If your scoring meeple ends or crosses the number 6 as last player or the number 24 as first player (numbers are marked with a sticker), you will immediately get an additional turn. 
This means, that after this scoring phase, you immediately draw another tile and take another turn. Once your action is finished it's the next players turn.

From the box:

BRAIN GAME is an exciting memory game.

The cards show well-known landmarks - but each card only shows half the picture.  The object of the game is to find the matching pairs.

To start, place all the cards face down on the table.  Each player in turn turns 2 cards face up.  If they make a pair (they are the two halves of the same picture) that player takes the two cards and scores one point.  If they do not form a pair, the two cards are turned face down once again and the next player takes a turn.

The game continues until all the matching pairs are found and the player with the most pairs wins the game.

boardgame: Scandinavia (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride)
description:
This map is an unofficial fan made expansion. Symbols & Graphics Copyright © 2004-2015 Days of Wonder, Inc. Produced with permission of Days of Wonder, for non-commercial use only.


This follows the principles of the basic Ticket to Ride game on a special board with special cards and rules. The object of the game is to gain the most points by travelling various routes and outwitting other players. You will need the train pieces and the train cards from either the US or Europe base games (or you can buy them from the Days of Wonder shop.

There is an extra set of Tourist cards for the cities, each with a value to be earned by visiting that city. These cards are dealt/picked up alongwith tickets in the same quantity and with the same 'keep/dispose' rules.

There are also spanners along some tracks which may help or hinder you. The board does not have any grey tracks or parallel tracks - but there are some options in the spanner pack.

The 9 bonuses give you extra points.

There are many new options in the rules: exchange all your cards at the start but keep all the new ones: discard 5 cards and pick up 5 new ones, as your turn: earn extra points: and many more. You can also call time when your wagon stock reaches 3, if you want, giving you the advantage of stopping other players completing tickets.

Up to 7 players travel around the world by different means of travel: car, sailing yacht, bicycle, train, horse, airplane and whaler/dog slet. Each means of travel has it's own colored travel route and it's own travel guide. Movement is determined by a die. On some destinations, events hinder or benefit the player. The first player to reach his starting point wins the game.

boardgame: Heir to the Pharaoh: Cursed Expansion
description:
The Cursed Expansion unleashes all of the delicious chaos of a new puppy and a new kitten into your game. Be warned: this addition of curses, chaos, and mummified pets is certain to both delight and dismay!

Includes:
Cursed Expansion Rules
2 Wooden Cursed Meeples
2 Cursed Bidding Cards

Everybody have one of those friends with more than normal knowledge of pop music. Now you have the change to test them.

Object of the game is to score more points which you can win by answering questions about pop music

The game contains 49 question cards, each containing 6 questions and an extra 5 cards with tie-breaker questions

The game is based on the annual Dutch radio and tv progam Top 2000, in which the public votes for the best pop songs of all time (usually Queen's Bohemian Rhapsody).

boardgame: Vikings Gone Wild: Meeple Bros Promo Card
description:
Promotional card offered by "Man vs Meeple Season One Kickstarter".

Hero. 3. Can attack 2 buildings.

Color-matching fun for everyone! Place any card to start. Each card includes different combinations of up to four colors, so be sure to look at your cards closely. How many colors can you match with your next play? Match the most colors, and win! This addictive, make-you-think strategy game builds matching, critical thinking, and addition skills. Includes 40 cards and Activity Guide. Cards measure 3"H; box measures 4½" x 4½". For 2–6 players.

From the publisher's website:

The first in the new Perplexus Micro line! Inside the clear cube is a fun and funky robot that features a winding, moving track for players to maneuver the marble through! Perplexus® Micro was designed to attract the attention of Perplexus fans, as well as draw in new players with its 3" size and smaller price. These collectible games are easy to pick up, but hard to put down!

There are 33 challenges to this 3D maze game.

description:
From the publisher's website:

The second in the new Perplexus Micro line! The clear egg houses a fierce dragon, whose body forms a twisting path for marble maze fun! Perplexus® Micro was designed to attract the attention of Perplexus fans, as well as draw in new players with its 3" size and smaller price. These collectible games are easy to pick up, but hard to put down!

There are 33 challenges to this 3D maze game.
family: Admin: Outside the Scope of BGG

description:
In Heimspiel 2 - 4 Players will be the managers of teams of the German Bundesliga. They buy players for their team to compete in single matches against other players or non player teams.

Every Team has a score. This, the result of a D20 roll and 3 event cards decide, which team has won, lost or if it is a tie.

Between the matches it is possible to buy new players in a silent auction.

In the end the player with the most points will win the game.

You..You really are incorruptible, aren't you? You won't kill me out of some misplaced sense of self -righteousness, and I won't kill you because you are to much fun. I think you and I are destined to do this forever? - The Joker

The Joker is one of Batman's oldest and greatest foes. A mirror of the Bat himself, the Joker stands for everything Batman is against. Chaos, disorder and crime without reason.

Possibly one of Batman's greatest failures when the Red Hood fell into a vat of chemicals, the Joker has plagued Batman, his allies and Gotham City ever since.

Nobody knows who he really is and even the Joker himself has told several different origin stories. If he's going to have one, he would prefer it to be multiple choice.

Seeing murder, chaos and his 'dance' with Batman as one big joke, the Joker is one of the most dangerous men Batman has ever faced. Using countless resources including his own Joker Gas, he tries to take down Gotham and 'Batsy' again and again. Only to end up each time in Arkham Asylum.

During one stay in Arkham, the Joker met Dr. Harleen Quinzel. A psychiatrist that developed an obsession with the Joker that led her to become Harley Quinn and follow her 'puddin' on his journey of insanity.

Some of the Jokers worst crimes include paralysing Barbara Gordon / Batgirl and killing Batman's own sidekick Jason Todd / Robin. Batman has sworn not to kill, but can the Joker push him over the edge?

Using a Joker crew in the Batman Miniatures Game is to bring chaos to the table.

A dangerous man in his own right with weapons like a poisoned knife, one shot gun and explosive teeth, the Jokers true strength comes from his ability to change his crews plans at the last minute with the use of Kaos Agent. This means your opponent can never really know what you are going to do until it's too late.

Having Harley Quinn as a Sidekick can help with the mass chaos as she can even change your opponents plans while her True Love for the Joker means she can become very dangerous if her 'puddin' is taken down. Or if you need some muscle instead, Mr Hammer is happy to help.

The Jokers Clown Henchmen are very basic fighters all armed with a range of different weapons. They are easy to take down one by one, but being cheap to hire, there can be enough on the table to pull off any crime. There are also a few Elite Clowns if you want some Henchmen that are a bit more resilient and hit harder.

The Joker crew has access to all sorts of Free Agents to help them out. Killer Croc adds some serious muscle to the crew when it's needed and is quick to move around to where he is needed via the sewers. Scarecrow can help keep the insanity under control while also messing with the opponents plans further by forcing models to activate first.

Overall, the Joker crew is great for anyone to play. Kaos Agent makes it easy on new players while messing with your opponents plans can be fun for more experienced players.

Contains:

4 metal miniatures:
- Joker
- Clown
- Ringmaster
- Contra-Auguste
Stat cards for the characters

"Ahh... the Batman. While I have an affection for most flying creatures... the winged rat is not among them." - The Penguin

Oswald Cobblepot, known as Penguin to most of Gotham, is one of the best villainous businessmen around. If you want a weapon, he has it. Want someone taken care of, he knows the perfect person to call.

Cobblepot started life teased as a child because of his short stature and bird-like nose earning him the nickname 'penguin' and when his father died of pneumonia, his mother made him carry an umbrella wherever he went which did not help with the rest of the worlds view of him. This led to Oswald becoming a nasty, resentful person that wanted to prove he could be better than those around him while he was growing up.

Cobblepot has a genius level intellect, amazing business sense, he's a tactical mastermind, oddly skilled fighter for his build and has obsessions with birds and his trusty umbrellas.

All of this has made him into the Penguin that now runs most of the crime in Gotham.

The Penguin can out-plan and out-think his opponents with ease but if things ever go wrong he has an entire arsenal of trick umbrellas to fall back on. Everything from guns and grapples to gas, hypnosis and even one that can fly.

His plans, schemes and crimes are often run using money from his legitimate businesses like the Iceberg Lounge and Iceberg Casino. If he needs help, he won't shy away from hiring the best in the world but only if he thinks he will come out better off in the end.

In the Batman Miniatures Game, the Penguin crew is all about money, resources and using them as best as you can to get the job done.

Oswald himself much more dangerous than he looks. Armed with an Umbrella Cannon and Umbrella Blade, he can dish out a lot of damage when he needs to and even has a bulletproof vest under his fine jacket for extra protection. He real strength though is getting his crew and others to do the work for him.

Penguin is Charismatic and a Lord of Business so can hire more Free Agents than most, better equip his own men and even take extra Loot Objectives.

The Penguin thugs are cheap but that also makes them numerous. Throw enough men at a problem and it can be solved. The Penguins extra equipment reserves let him arm his hordes well though. Assault Rifles, Automatic Guns and Grenades are not uncommon to see in the middle of the Thugs.

If the hulking Sickle joins him as a Sidekick, he can also bring along some more Elite Penguin Thugs when the extra skill is wanted.

Instead of hordes of Thugs, Penguin also has the options of hiring almost any Free Agent he needs with his Charisma and $$$ letting him choose people like Deadshot and Deathstroke on a regular basis as deadly assassins.

A couple of very good choices though are Catwoman and Mr Freeze. Freeze adds to the ranged threat of the well armed crew while Catwoman can jump around when your opponent is dealing with all the Thugs. Both also have Loot on their mind so the Penguins extra Loot Objective can help you bump up your VPs.

Overall, a Penguin crew is about strength in numbers. Either the number of bodies you have at your disposal or the number of deadly weapons and skilled assassins you throw at your opponent.

A great starting crew with several options and ways to play for the more experienced players.

Contains:
4 metal miniatures:

- Penguin Emperor
- Royal Penguin
- Blue Penguin
Stat cards for the characters

boardgame: The Next Big Thing: The Game of Entrepreneurship
description:
A board game for people who are interested in being startup superheroes, changing the world, and becoming billionaires. Players take on the role of startup founders to create "the next big thing", bootstrapping using their initial cash savings and superpower skills (i.e. programming, design, sales, marketing, etc.) to create a minimal viable product, gain initial traction, and then go on to raise multiple rounds of capital from investors, increasing the value of their company, but while also having their founder's ownership get diluted.

During the game players will experience many of the ups and downs of the entrepreneurial journey including: increasing revenues while balancing against expenses to grow the company, various significant events (good and bad), and creating an impact that changes the world. There are also multiple paths such as an accelerator path, pivot paths, and in the end (if you haven't gone bankrupt) players decide to either take the acquisition path or IPO path. Along the way players also collect impact points, as it's not just about the money, from sustainability, ethics & diversity, social impact, and health & wellness. The winner is the player who exits ("cashes out") with the most money by creating the company with the largest valuation while still maintaining the most founder's ownership and generating the most positive impact on the world.

description:
A unique take on both the card combat genre of games like Magic the Gathering and Hearthstone, and deck building games like Dominion and Legendary, Andoria is a fast paced game of tactics and diplomacy for 2- 4 players.

boardgame: Sound and Fury: The Battle of Mechili, January 24-25, 1941
description:
On December 9, 1940 the British launched their offensive, called Operation Compass, against the Italian 10th Army in the Egyptian frontier border with Libya. The British were vastly outnumbered, and the offensive’s goals were limited in scope (it was just supposed to be a “five day raid”). However, it wasn’t long before the surprised and poorly-led Italians were in headlong retreat. Following the capture of the Italian fortress at Bardia, the Italian retreat turned into a full-scale rout. All that stood between the British and complete victory were the remnants of the Italian Strategic Reserve, the Sabratha 60th Infantry Division and the Babini Armor Group (roughly a brigade) who were dug-in at the frontier village of Mechili. The war’s first major tank battle in North Africa occurred here as the British vanguard ran into an Italian force that was ready and primed for a fight.

Each copy of Sound and Fury is composed of the following:

•    One 11 by 17 inch map with game record-keeping tracks and terrain identification.
•    56 un-mounted double sided units.
•    One set of 7 page rules.

description:
The game is played with coloured dice on an 8 x 8 board.

64 wooden cubes are marked with a different colour point on each side. Each player chooses one of the six colours and the dice are evenly divided among the players.

The game consist in two phases:

Phase 1. The players alternate turns playing one die on the board and trying to form 4 in a row.

Phase 2. If no player has reached the previous goal the next phase begins. Now each turn consists in turning the dice on the board. The winning condition changes to be 5 in a row.

description:
All the time tiny bugs were seen whenever we played outdoors in childhood. Sometimes by homework, and sometimes by curiosity, we used to catch bugs with friends. But these days, especially in downtown, we can barely see the bugs. Furthermore, some kinds of bug are very rare. Now let’s meet the bugs evanescent and out of sight from us.
The players need to find various kinds of bug as the bug-hunters. We are not trying to collect bugs stuffed and exhibited. Rather, we study and preserve them for the better ecosystem. The conference will be held that everyone pay attention soon. Collect various bugs and be the best bug-hunter!

[Object]
You have to collect various bugs as a bug-hunter. You can collect the bug when you gather all same kind of it in your hand. But it would be a little hard because other bug-hunters also compete for the collection. If you collect the most bugs until the conference(end of game), you will be called the bug expert.

[Game Play]
On your turn, you do 1 action among the 3 actions - Pick, Call and Collect.
Each player holds the cards of bugs and shows their backs to others during play. The backs notices the color of the bug, so players can deduce the cards needed.
By Pick action, you pick only 1 card from specific player.
By Call cation, you call a kind and a color of the bug to specific player. If you success, you get the card and do Call action again.(Until you fail.)
When all cards of same kind of bug are gathered on your hand, you may collect it and get scores.
At the end of your turn, you have to swap a card in your hand with a card in Forest - the array of 3 cards in hidden on the middle of table.(The illustrations of backs are trees.)

[Scoring]
When 7 kinds of bug are collected during play, the game ends immediately. (There are 10 kinds of bug in the game.)
The period of bug hunting has been over, and the anticipated conference is held now. Score 1 point per a card of the collected bugs. (e.g. The bug with 5 colors(cards) scores 5 points.) Sum whole points including the bug collected by items. And the player who has no item card at the end of the game gets -2 points. (It is basic attitude of the bug-hunter to take care of items!)
The player who scores the most points wins a victory.

For details, check the rulebook please.

You and your friends just came back from a very successful night of trick or treating.  Now comes the hard part—dividing up the candy.

Halloween Candy takes place over a number of rounds.  Each round, one player will divide a group of candy into piles equal to the number of players.  The other players will then choose one of the piles of candy, and the candy divider will take the remaining pile.  Cards are worth a number of points.  There are also bonus cards which can give or take away points from players.  At the end of all rounds, whoever has the most points is the winner!

boardgame: ATS Desperate Hours: Three Battles at Kesternich '44-'45
description:
From the publisher's website:

KESTERNICH, BELGIUM, 14 December 1944: The First Battle for Kesternich took place from December 14 to 16, 1944. This battle pitted the 2nd Battalion of the 309th Infantry Regiment and the 2nd Battalion of the 310th Infantry Regiment of the 78th Infantry Division against units from the 272nd Volksgrenadier Division, including elements of the 326th Volksgrenadier Division. This attack was part of a greater attack by the First Army's V Corps in an effort to capture the Roer River Dams that included the 78th Infantry Division as well as the 2nd Infantry Division to the south. The attack by the 78th Division interrupted Hitler's plans for the northern shoulder of the Bulge.

The recent release of Battle of the Twin Villages got your boots into the snow. Now you're going to have to slog forward, if you are the Volksgrenadier player .. or hold on ... if the Americans. Because the dates are 14-16 December 1944. And that means the height of ferocity of Hitler's last gamble, aka 'Wacht Am Rhein' has been unleashed. And every village, every road crossing, where a stand is made can make the difference between a drive to Antwerp, or a final, crushing blow to the tyrant's dreams as he hunkers in his lair. As all good tactical-level gamers know, the decision was always decided on the ground, at the sharp end ... by individual soldiers and squads.

To that end, we bring you ATS DESPERATE HOURS: Three Battle for Kesternich, a brand new historical module with never before published WINTERIZED map and scenarios > what's more, to make things easy for you to get YOUR boots on the ground, a complete set of all the combat counters you need to play are provided in ALL NEW OLIVE DRAB > American Personnel + American AFVs/H Weapons + German Personnel + German AFVs/H Weapons!

Just bring your ATS Rulebook 2014 and system markers > and you are ready to stand ... or smash through as this is a showdown between the 78th Infantry Division and the 272nd Volksgrenadiers ... and only one side will stand ... or fall.

The sun hid itself behind the desert landscape as the Mad Scientist ordered his young office assistant, Savannah, into the lab. Normally she was strictly forbidden to enter. Who was this creepy old man staring at her with such menacing eyes? He pointed. To her horror, she recognized the muscular young weapons expert strapped to a table. He had just asked her on a first date hours ago. He was now a brilliant glowing fireball struggling in his bindings. The Mad Scientist said with a sickly grin, “Marry me or suffer a similar fate”. She turned to run…

boardgame: Arkham Horror: The Card Game – Carnevale of Horrors: Scenario Pack
description:
Description from the Publisher:

The sixty-two card, standalone scenario in Carnevale of Horrors transports you to a city of masks and mysteries. While revelers throng the streets of Venice, masked conspirators advance their sinister agendas, shadows envelop the city, and something terrible rises from the lagoon…

Also playable as a "side-story" that can be added to your ongoing Arkham Horror: The Card Game campaign, Carnevale of Horrors challenges you and your fellow investigators to move swiftly among the masked revelers parading the streets of Venice in order to unravel a dreadful conspiracy. But while the day's festival begins with music, mirth, and merriment, it quickly spirals into abject horror… The Sun vanishes, and screams erupt throughout the city. You want to save as many innocents as you can, but everyone is wearing masks. Who are the villains, and who are the victims? You must race to identify and confront the cultists, lest they and their sacrifices summon a being of unfathomable malice!

Carnevale of Horrors comes with all the act, agenda, location, and encounter cards that you need to enjoy its adventure. Additionally, five player cards—one ally and four Mask assets—enrich your campaign experience after you complete your investigations in Venice.

Requires a copy of the Arkham Horror: The Card Game Core Set to play. This expansion is made available via FFG’s in-house manufacturing.

description:
Fairy Rade is a card game for 2 to 4 players. Each player is the leader of a Fairy Parade, trying to trick unwary humans into becoming part of their procession. Play continues in a series of rounds, until one player has tricked enough mortals to be declared the new Fairy King or Queen!

Through a series of rounds, players take turns leading a trick by playing one of the cards from their hand, and other players must play a card of the same suit if they have one. The highest ranking card played wins the trick, but there are surprises in the form of Royal Favor and Escape cards!

This is an artifact pack that was given to backers of the Leaders of Euphoria Kickstarter campaign. It came in a zip baggie outside of the box and will not be included in the base game, at least for the first print run.

The base game has 24 artifacts and this adds 4 more to increase the variety the ones that appear throughout the game. These are the 4 artifacts:

     Bionic Bones of Flexibility
     Burial Chamber
     Historical Records
     Sundial of Perpetual Uncertainty

1849, years after the first expeditions West, folks continue to traverse America’s frontier to look for new beginnings. You and your fellow pioneers have wandered into an ideal location for a new start.

Seek your fortune in gold or cattle, forge your future by helping build your new town, and when needed, drown your sorrows in a bottle of Whiskey. You are charged with your town’s destiny in The Fate of the West.

Each round, payers will roll their dice, and then take turns placing dice on action spaces. The goal is to become the most influential person in making this new town. You'll be faced with situations that will make you choose the path of the righteous or the dark road of the wicked.

What color hat you end up wearing is your choice. How you influence and grow the town with your fellow players will determine who wins in the end.

Global War On Terror (GWOT) is a low complexity, global geo-political war game for 2-5 players, or just one player using the solo bot cards.

One player controls the terrorist mastermind and his / her lieutenants (subordinates); their identity is unknown. The overall goal of the terrorists is to carry out major attacks on population centres, and to inflict maximum terror to achieve their political, religious, or ideological goals. To achieve this, the terrorists will recruit jihadists (to provide manpower), collect funds (to finance plots), and collect materials (e.g. bio-toxins), all of which are required to launch terror attacks.

The other players in the game control the opposing counter terrorist agents. Their primary goal is to work cooperatively to disrupt terror plots by collecting plot-specific Intel (intelligence). Counter terrorists may also carry out surveillance, drone strikes, and deploy special forces to eliminate jihadists, terrorist cells, and training camps, thereby impeding the progress of their plots.

VICTORY CONDITIONS
The game ends and the winner(s) declared when any of the following apply:
•    The Global Terror Track marker reaches 10 (terrorists win).
•    The counter terrorists identify and eliminate the terrorist mastermind (counter terrorists win).
•    If the Global Terror Track marker has not reached space ‘10’ after ten game turns, the counter terrorists win.

This game has been entered into the 2016-17 war game print and play contest:
https://boardgamegeek.com/thread/1626631/2016-17-wargame-print-and-play-contest

This game won 3rd Prize in the Best Strategic Game category.

Note:

     The components and rules in this game are prototypes for play testing, and therefore subject to change.

boardgame: Sigil: Powers of War – Base Set
description:
Description from the publisher:

Sigil: Powers of War is an action packed fantasy based strategic battle game with 8 customizable factions. It takes all the excitement of dice combat and combines it with pre-built deck construction that allows for multiple strategies for victory. Choose your Sigil, build your army, and prepare for War!

Each player start the game with a Sigil of Power related to their faction of choice, a 5 card starting hand, and a powerful Warlord who can advance your front lines by placing mana generators and pushing back the enemy. These mana generators allow units to spawn at them when summoned and also generate mana on every turn giving the player the power to cast multiple and increasingly powerful spells.

The goal of the game is simple... destroy your opponents Sigil which starts with a 20 point life counter. The first person whose Sigil's life counter reaches 0 loses. This is accomplished by using your Warlord, the action cards in your hand, and multiple units who can be summoned throughout the battle. Combat is strategy based with a touch of luck based on dice combat.

Gameplay is expandable up to 6 players and the base set out of the box can be played with up to 4 players. Multiple game variants and player count game options.

description:

description:
One of the oldest board games series "Gundam", for 2 to 5 people. the SD Gundam was so popular that it became the third piece in the SD Gundam Series of the Patio.  It is content, but the third task is to change the ga rats and become an RPG-like game of "to become one of five warrior gundam, to defeat the ghost that Controls SD gundam world ".

The goal of the game is to defeat the Devil. The Devil appears when you defeat the Devil's load of six places including grasslands, deserts, sea, and gravity fields. Can your warrior Gundam defeat the Devil and find peace?

Introduction
Man can build bridges and castles and towers.  We can refine ore into wonderful tools and machines.  But to truly hunt, we look not to man’s creations, but God’s - the falcon.

Use the tools and skills at your disposal to become a master falconer.  Learn skills, obtain a falcon, take time to train it, then build your reputation with successful hunts.

Game Overview
Each turn the player will decide to take one of the following 5 actions: 
- Go to town (to buy, sell, or barter items, work for gold, or obtain skills), 
- Stay home to build items, 
- Stay home to train a falcon, 
- Stay home to tend to an injured falcon, or 
- Attend a falconry hunt/tournament.

The player will need to balance finances (gold amount) with learning skills, training their falcons, and hunting with their falcons.

Falcon Master is played with a deck of 50 cards, plus about 25 tracking cubes in 28 turns total over 4 seasons.

36 cards are Item cards. 10 cards are Hunt Resolution cards used to determine outcomes of hunts.  4 cards are used to:
- track the turns through the 4 seasons, 
- track player Gold amount, and player Reputation
- hunt
- aid in hunt resolution (reference card)

Winning
A master falconer cares for falcons, hunts them successfully for a variety of prey, and has demonstrated the skills required for falconry.  To win:
- Earn 12 Reputation points, 
- Successfully hunt with at least 2 different falcons, and
- Successfully hunt at least 3 types of prey: dove, Quail, Pheasant, or Duck.

Reputation points are gained by successful hunts, training your falcons, and by gaining skills important to the art of falconry (selling items built with your skills).

Falcon Master was an entry in Boardgamegeek's 2016 Mint Tin PnP Contest, and won the following:
2nd place Best Overall Design (tie)
2nd place Best Theme
2nd place Most Innovative Mechanic.

2 Kings is a 2 player card game played with 2 sided cards.  Each card has 2 military icons (top and bottom of card) and 2 negotiation icons (left and right side of cards). The game is played in 2 phases: collecting your army, then the battle.

In part 1 of phase 1, players draw a card from the 15 card Nobles deck and place them into a diamond shaped grid to form the disputed land.  In part 2, players will move their King card through the land to form allegiances with the nobles by matching icons on the sides of both cards.  At the end of phase 1, each player will have their army of 5 to 7 nobles.

In phase 2, players will create 3 battle lines to battle each other.  The winner is the player who wins at least 2 of the 3 battles.

2 Kings is played with 18 cards, no other components.  The game is completely free of any text, no reading is required.

2 Kings is an entry in the Boardgamegeek 2016 18 card PnP Contest.

It is the year 1173, the Normans have invaded Ireland and King Henry II has laid claim to the island. But beyond Dublin, the old ways still rule and the ancient houses of Ireland hold sway. Take up the mantle of a young chieftain as you work to build your clan, expand your kingdom and gain the loyalty of your fellow Irish. Beyond the Pale tracks the Norman occupation and the Irish resistance over three centuries, spanning the invasion of the Bruce from Scotland, the Black Death, and the Norman retreat to their last vestige of power, the Pale of Dublin.

Beyond the Pale is played over five rounds. Each round players work to expand the influence of their clan by conquering neighboring lords, making alliances with Norman nobles and gaining the loyalty of the Irish people. The player with the largest territory and greatest loyalty at the end of the fifth round wins and is named as the High King of Ireland.

A quick and easy real time dice rolling game for any number of players.

The box includes enough materials for 2 players, but more components can be added.

You'll be rolling, rerolling, and locking dice during a three-minute countdown to grill more food than your opponents.

Each player gets 6 dice with both charcoal faces and food faces. Dice can be locked on the player mat and charcoal added to "cook" that food item, which can then be scored.

Book Bugs is a collectible, deck-building, card game for 2 to 4 players, created by National Library Board, Singapore.

Visitors to any of the participating public libraries can collect Book Bugs cards in three ways:
1. Borrow 8 books from the library to redeem 2 normal cards.
2. Complete a library activity to redeem 1 shiny card.
3. Attend a library programmer to redeem 1 shiny card.

A player deck is made up of 30 cards. These are put in the player's draw pile. The player's play area also has a discard pile and a playing field. Each player starts with 5 cards in his/her hand.

To determine who starts the game, each player draws a card from the top of their draw pile. The player that draws the card with the highest points starts the game.

During each turn, the player starts by playing a card on his/her playing field. The effects of the card played takes place immediately and only lasts one turn. Each player must play only 1 card per turn. The player ends his/her turn by drawing a card from his/her draw pile.

The first player to achieve a total of 20 points wins the game. Alternatively, the game ends when one player runs out of cards in his/her draw pile. All players will then have to count the total number of points from the cards on his/her playing field. The player with the highest total points wins!

Description from the designer:

Osztálytalálkozó is a nostalgic and black-humorous card game from the socialist era of Hungary, where the players are old classmates, and after a long-long time they arrive to their class reunion.

With cards everybody tells what they have archieved since their graduation in trick-taking turns. Who shows the most valuable property wins the turn, earn the points on the cards and activate a special ability of the card category (vehicle, real estate, stuff, food). Every player tries to earn as many points as he can, but everybody has an own line of limit, and if he passes that point, he lives like a bourgeois, the men of state are arriving and he is kicked out of the game. At the beginning of the game the players draw (or choose by bid in the advanced game) a job card which can help to find the best strategy and add extra abilities to the players.

Osztálytalálkozó is a fast-placed card game with the base of trick-taking games, but it adds not only the special abilities on the property cards and the characters but the line of limit. It has been drawn by the socialist state, and it is an other rule what twists the well-known mechanisms.

boardgame: Warhammer Age of Sigmar: Spire of Dawn
description:
The Dawnspire stands at the edge of the Transient Isles. Immense, drifting islands of black stone hanging in the air above an azure sea, they were once the subject of foul sorceries, a powerful Chaos wizard seeking to bring them crashing down. The mages of the Eldritch Council were able to stop this foul ritual, but not before two of the islands were lost to the depths.

To protect the isles, the Dawnspire was created; housing objects of incredible power, it is protected by the most elite warriors the Transient Isles have ever known. Yet cruel eyes glow in the corners of the realms. fixated on the Dawnspire, intent on harnessing its power for their own dark purpose…

Dawnspire is a fantastic box set filled with Citadel miniatures, with a book telling the story of the Skaven Warlord Ripsnikk’s attempt to take the Dawnspire from the Swifthawk Agents who protect it. 74 miniatures are included, split between Skaven and Aelf forces:

Aelf:

-        1 High Warden
-        1 Archmage
-        10 Swordmasters
-        10 Spireguard
-        5 Reavers

Skaven

-        1 Skaven Warlord
-        1 Warlock Engineer
-        1 Packmaster
-        2 units of 20 Clanrats
-        2 Rat Ogors
-        1 Poisoned Wind Mortar Weapon Team
-        1 Warpfire Thrower Weapon Team

The box also includes the complete rules for playing games of Warhammer Age of Sigmar, 2 Warscroll Battalions and 2 Pitched Battle Profiles, one of each for each force. Supplied with round and square bases for each model.

1 versus 1 tactical combat game played on an 8x8 to 12x12 square grid game board. Each side has 6 military units, plus a Fortress to defend, and a Cannon Turret to defend the Fortress.

Set up your units on your edge of the board, then let the battle begin.  Each player's turn is made up of 2 actions: Move, Attack, Heal an injured unit, or Repair and disabled vehicle.

Attacks are resolved by comparing Attack Strength + dice roll  to Defense Strength + dice roll. Defending units may be destroyed, disabled, or sustain no damage.

A player wins the game by destroying their opponent's Fortress or all of their opponent's army.

Iron Infantry was an entry in the 2015 Mint Tin PnP design Contest.

Iron Infantry is a mini game based on the steam punk inspired game "Iron & Brass".

All that's needed to play is the 8 units per player, 2D3 (1,1,2,2,3,3) dice per player and a checker or chess board, or any other 8x8 to 12x12 square grid board.

Each player starts with 14 soldiers and 1 ensign on the 15 spaces between two castles. Movement is one space in any direction. The river can only be crossed on the three bridges. Enemy pieces can be captured by jumping over the piece onto an empty space behind it. Capturing is alowed in any direction but backwards. To win the game one has to capture the enemy ensign or move one's own ensign onto one of the enemy's starting spaces.

On the backside of the game board, there is an "Nine Men's Morris" board.

boardgame: Plan 1919: Fuller's Plan to End the Great War
description:
Plan 1919 is a two-player game exploring the most influential campaign that never was: J.F.C. Fuller's revolutionary plan to use new tank technology to penetrate past the frontlines, forgoing the brutal stalemate of "body warfare" to take out German lines of communication via a "shot through the brain". The 1918 armistice rendered the plan moot, and it was derided by the more traditionalist elements of the British Army. But others were paying attention, and the Second World War saw Fuller's vision realized with terrifying effect by Nazi Germany.

The Allied Player is on the attack, with many tank brigades at his disposal - but will they be enough?  The Germans of this era have few armor units, but can utilize anti-tank units and aircraft to mount a stubborn defense. It's in their interest to prevent and minimize Allied advances while maintaining their tenuous lines of supply and communication.

Units represent corps, divisions, and brigades, moving across hexes that are about seven miles across. The time scale of individual turns is fungible and dependent on tempo, but the entire twenty-turn campaign represents about two months of real-world fighting.

Designer John Gorkowski is no stranger to the Great War, having designed a number of games on the topic at tactical, operational, and strategic scales. When Hollandspiele decided it wanted to publish a game on the subject, Mr. Gorkowski was the first and only designer we had in mind, and his experience with gaming the era results in a plausible, believable, playable version of Fuller's what-if.

Description from the publisher:

Monopoly, the legendary game of risk taking, deal making, and owning it all, and the University of Kansas, the celebrated university of academics, research, Olympic track stars and national basketball championships, team up to create a classic board game for Jayhawk fans everywhere. Take a spin around the board to find the best loved athletes, coaches, buildings and scenes of one of America's great universities. And be prepared to pay big bucks if you land on James Naismith or Phos Allen the inventor of basketball and one of the most beloved coaches in college basketball history.

Grab your friends and family, put on your Jayhawk sweatshirt and enter the halls of KU for the game of your life. The Lied Center, Gale Sayers, Allen Fieldhouse, Wilt Chamberlain or 22 other KU properties can all be yours. Whether a student, alumnus or fan, can you resist the chance to buy KU's finest?

GAME INCLUDES: Game Board Property Cards Play Money and Banker's Tray 2 Dice 10 Game Tokens Chance Cards Community Chest Cards Package of Dormitories Package of Houses

"Out of curiosity... Amelio Gonzolez... stuck a flaming candle... in the protruding ear of... Maria Sanchez... and smiled wickedly."

Uncover an act most cruel by revealing six story elements that together describe the treachery and spell the word UNKIND.

Careful though, your opponents are equally devilish, so you'll need to use your letters, corvid tokens, and perhaps summon a black raven to stop them. 2 to 5 players and plays in about 20 minutes.

The Corvid Tokens can also be used to expand your options and deepen your strategy in Murder of Crows.

OBJECTIVE:
Be the first player to spell the word UNKIND in front of you and then read the Act Most Cruel along the bottom of the cards aloud to win.

PLAY:
On your turn you draw 1 card and play 1 card in front of you in order to spell the word UNKIND.

When played, a card's ability is activated:

     Undo: Pick a player, they must return the last card they played into their Unkind to their hand.
     Need: Draw 1 card and 1 corvid.
     Kill: Remove one letter from an opponent’s UNKIND
     Investigate: Draw 2 corvid Tokens, then look at a player's hand.
     Drag: Collect 1 corvid Token from another player.


Alternatively, a player can acquire a Corvid Token: 

     When casting a spell, a player may instead draw a corvid token. Spell does not occur.
     When drawing a card, a player may instead draw a corvid token.


Countering Card Abilities:
When a card is played, another player may prevent its effect from affecting them (alone) by discarding a card from their hand. The discarded card must have the same number of Crows in the top left corner as the card played.

Using Corvid Tokens

     When playing a letter card, a player may spend corvid token(s), to increase the crow value of the card by 1 crow per token.
     When countering a spell, a player may spend corvid token(s), to increase or decrease the crow value of the card by 1 crow per token.
     Before ending their turn, a player may place corvid token(s) on any empty letter spot(s) in another player’s UNKIND. Players may add to spots with corvid token(s), but no letter spot may every contain more than 5 corvid token.


Corvid Tokens in your Murder

     When there is one or more corvid tokens in your murder, you may only play a letter card into that spot if you over come the total number of Birds in the spot. The player can do this with just a letter card, or it may require additional crows added to the card (An empty spot with 5 corvid tokens, could be removed with a 3 Crow Letter card and 3 corvid tokens). Once overcome, all the corvid tokens are removed.
     A Wild Crow/Raven card may be placed in a empty spot regardless of corvid tokens. The corvid tokens are all removed.


Wild Ravens Cards
In Unkindness of Ravens, each Raven card has a unique ability written on the card.

boardgame: Dream Home: Promo Tokens – Christmas Tree

language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Lore.
...Your Shieldbearer falls, shield broken and stomach pierced by the Sword of the opposing champion. You move your Archer into the frontline – Encouraged by your Priestess, he shoots an arrow at the Magician, covered against melee attacks. With an arrow in his chest, he falls. You watch as the Warlord frowns at the result. The loss of his Magician was not part of his plan. Desperately, he orders his Conqueror to battle to face your Monk

With an empowered, overwhelming strike your Monk puts the Conqueror in the grave, exposing the opposing strategist. Rising to the occasion enters your Champion. He charges the Strategist and with a single swing of his sword the Strategist – The Savages only hope – is decapitated, securing your victory. Icosara is yours.

Game Concept
Edge Is a tabletop card game where two players wage war on each other using cards, dices and tactical formations. The game has a heavy emphasis on strategy and calculated tactics and plays both like a boardgame and a living card game.

It is very easy to get into the game and the rules seem almost simple, but don't let that fool you. The more you get play it, the more advanced formations you and your opponent will come up with. 
Through different tactics and different setups, you will delve into the over 14.000 different ways to set up your army.

Though playing it gives a similar vibe, Edge is not Magic or even a rework of the game. No more arguing about obscure rules or different versions of the same. This game combines elements and inspiration from multiple games, that most gamers will recognize, but the mechanics are utilized in such a way that the game forms it's own new genre.

So choose your tactics and strategy!
How will you choose to play and how will you ensure victory? Do you want to charge at your opponent with brute force, or do you want to slowly lure him into your traps to weaken him before the deathblow. The choice is yours.

Goal of the Game
Most points win!
So in the normal game you will need to accumulate more points then your opponent in a 2 matchs/battles setup.
There are variants but even for those most points win

Resources
This game as stated in the game concept, is about resources. your card/pawns, dices, re-rolls and everything else is a limited resource and you will need to think about when and when not to use them or you will quickly find yourself with any way to protect your army and lose.

User summary
パナマ (Panama) is a investment card game for 4 players only, which uses a deck of traditional playing cards and 20 card stands made with a 3D printer. The rules are available in Japanese and English. (In Japan, the standard deck of Western playing cards is known as a 'Trump' deck. Hence the designer explaining the game as a 'Trump Extension' game.)

Cards numbered A, 2, 3, 4, 5, and two jokers are investment cards. Five cards each are dealt to the players. Cards numbered 6-10 are tax haven cards. Players try to earn positive tax haven cards by playing investing cards.

When you invest on a tax haven card, you place a card stand along a border of the tax haven card and insert an investment card you want to play, so that one player cannot see how much you invest. Up to four players can invest on a tax haven card, and the highest investor earns the face value of that tax haven card. But one pre-determined suit counts negative, and a joker also inverts the sign if involved. You should speculate about hidden cards and try to let negative points go to other investors.

You can co-operate with one of other players by sharing complete information about what you play. But to win the game, you have to part ways in the end.

Jacks, Queens, and King are agent card. Each player has one of these at the beginning of a game, and can use it once for one shot effect, such as reversing order or skipping a player.

Publisher's summary
トランプ拡張ゲーム「パナマ」

トランプ(Playing Card)を
・投資カード　・タックスヘイブンカード　・工作員カード
に分け、場に出されたタックスヘイブンカードの周囲を
投資カードを付属のカードスタンドで立てていき、点数を稼いでいきます。
情報漏洩事務所(Panama paper office)への投資は減点となるため、他人におしつけます。
トランプを立てて置くと、４人の内１人からは必ず絵柄が見えなくなるため
プレイヤーは常に誰を騙すか選択することになります。
協調することで点は増えますが、最終的には出し抜いて１位を目指します。

boardgame: Zagor: Odissea Americana – Trampy Promo Card
description:
Zagor: Odissea Americana: Trampy Promo Card is one of a set of 4 event cards to be added to those in the Zagor: Odissea Americana base game. The cards are simply shuffled in with the base game cards, adding more variety from game to game.

The first 3 italian versions of the cards (Molti occhi, Trampy and Icaro La Plume) were given to the visitors of Lucca Comics & Games 2016, a comics & gameplay festival in Lucca in 2016, and later during other promotional events with the 4th card (Insidie sul fiume). It's one of the first additional cards for the Italian edition of Zagor: Odissea Americana.

Trampy
Cico non tira dadi azione. Nota: Sacrifica un dado azione giocatore
Cico does not roll any action die. Note: Sacrifice one action player die

Icaro La Plume
Quando entra in gioco, Icaro La Plume viene preso dal timoniere. Puoi associarlo a un movimento per spostarti in qualunque spazio. Dopo averlo usato, passa Icaro La Plume a un altro giocatore. Nota: Questo evento non può essere scartato

boardgame: Zagor: Odissea Americana – Insidie sul fiume Promo Card

Text:

Insidie sul fiume / Perils on the river
Scopri i pericoli dietro il battello. Ogni giocatore può pagare un gettone (simbolo) per scartare un pericolo. Se restano almeno:
due eventi: rimuovi segnalino scafo (simbolo) più a destra
una pianta carnivora: il battello subisce danno (simbolo) pari alla velocità attuale
una rapida: avanza il battello di uno spazio e svuota la cambusa
Find out the dangers behind the boat. Each player can pay a token (symbol) to discard a danger. If there are at least:
two events: remove the rightmost hull marker (symbol)
a carnivorous plant: the boat takes damage (symbol) equal to the current speed
rapids: the boat moves forward one space and empties caboose

boardgame: Age of Steam Expansion: Cuba & Sicily
description: Age of Steam Expansion: Cuba & Sicily has two new maps, with a new "Gunpowder" special action delivery to recognize the revolution in Cuba, which is a solitaire map. In the two-player map Sicily, the players struggle against the Cosa Nostra, a third virtual player.
A pre-order bonus for Phalanxx, adding an extra expert starting card. The card gives you an additional, fourth, white "1" die that helps you fulfill card requirements.

Description from the publisher:

A new concept in word games with the great advantage of involving all the players in the fun of the game all of the time. The game can be played by the whole family & by any number of players. The game centers around the planners or plans. These are the 'Crosswords' made up by all the players before the play actually begins. These planners are filled out by each player in crossword fashion on a particular theme or subject e.g. Film Stars, The Sea, Sport etc & the rest of the players have to find the words in these plans - the player with the most points being the winner. A game concept that can grow & even use tiles from other word games !

boardgame: The Vampire, the Elf & the Cthulhu: Edgar Allan Poe
description:
B. - The novel is almost ready, missing only the finale.
J. - Right, it could take place in front of the gates of the Black Land.
H. - Black Land, John? We are in New England!
J. - You are killing the epic poetry Howard, Black Land and a horde of orcs.
